reviews.text: Came here for a toastmasters meeting. Great facilities and ambiance. It looks like an engaging and well operated community Center.
Thank you Veronica for helping me to settle the Singpass matters. Also Karen too for your help re the SG seniors vouchers. These are 2 beautiful, kind n patient souls!

Has a sheltered basketball court that is used for everything except basketball. Ring is missing on one side of the court.

Had so much potential as one of the only few sheltered basketball courts in the area but unfortunately poorly maintained and not used to its potential.

Can you imagine a basketball court that is booked every evening (except Tuesdays) for non-basketball related activities? This is it.

Appealing for CDC voucher, take almost 1 month didn't approve.  Call every single day,the staffs kept give the reason * in- charge is not in the office * . Just wondering when the in charge will be in the office,? And when the voucher will be issued???

I went over to do some collection and update certain issues. I was served by Mr Stanley Tan who was outstanding in his customer handling. He was patient and always answered my questions well and it showed his good product knowledge. He was courteous and a credit to your CC. Kindly convey my thanks to him
Beautiful park in the middle of housing estate in Sengkang. Opened area with plenty of space for outdoor activities and picnics. There is a Tenderbest restaurant nearby. Clean and less crowded.
Love this park. Scenic. Bridges. Ponds. Chickens. Parrots. After a jog/walk, hop in to Tenderbest for a cuppa. Bliss.

Big open space. Terrapins roam free (don't feel, I guess and be careful of where you step). Also monitor lizards. Peaceful, air is so clean and fresh. Love the views and the vibes. Went in the evening so wasn't tortured by the sun.
Very nice relaxing walk not crowded. Prayed for bikes if you got them or you can rent them.
it’s a great place for jogging. The paths are clean, well-maintained, and surrounded by lots of greenery

result.name: WRITERS AT WORK Enrichment - Ang Mo Kio

reviews.text: The writing techniques taught by my W@W teacher are easy to remember!  Makes it easy to know what to write. I'm not scared of my school exam anymore because I finally have a few tricks I can use to start my composition  😎.

Tr Glenn has been caring and helpful towards my son. He is always prompt and constructive in giving feedbacks on ways to improve my son's compo. My son enjoys his lessons very much and he has done so well for his EOY paper. We have witnessed his progress and we are so proud of him.

As a mother of two kids, W@W has been extremely helpful for both of them. It had taught them how to write a fluent composition with interesting storylines, improving their score significantly! I highly recommend W@W as the improvements in my kids have proved that it boosts scores in a snap of a finger.
At W@W, both my English paper 2 and paper 1 have greatly improved and we had many practices especially for our end of year exams.

I appreciate my teacher, Mr Neil Ng who has been constantly trying to help me improve in my English language. He encourages me about the little improvements and I have learnt so much from him for the past 2 years.  If you want to join W@W, I really encourage you to do so as I really enjoyed my time here.
Vegetarian hor fun was nice! Generous portion with a lot of ingredients (veggies and mock meats) at only $5

This is my offday favourite lunch place. All the way from Bedok to here for these 2 foods. This boneless chicken rice honestly is the best. Fragrance rice, thick and juicy chicken, fresh ginger and chili paste, if you want can put black soysauce. Chicken rice closed every Monday. And prefer this 文华coffee stall, not only they provide gau coffee, the boss can remember all regular customers past orders, extreme good memories.

Worth the trip.

A medium sized hawker centre which is clean, spacious and well ventilated. There is always a long queue at the roast meat stall. Tried their assorted roast meat set for one person but was a bit disappointed probably due to too high expectations. The roast pork skin is crispy though. Another stall’s Amaranth soup is quite big portion and tastes good.

Like the bustling atmosphere in the hawker. Ordered the century egg with minced meat porridge and the friend black carrot cake. The porridge was worth the try, but not so for the fried carrot cake which I finds it so so only. Will come back to try more food! :)

Visited 628 Ang Mo Kio Market when my mom's warded at TTSH Rehabilitation Centre which is about 8 minutes walk away.

It's a wet market and food centre which serves quite good variety of food. It gets quite crowded during lunch time and early evening time.

There are also few retail shops that offer really cheap clothes. I bought 12 pyjamas sets from a Chinese seller at $7/set. Material is cotton, thin but not translucent and very cooling for our weather.

Walk down further there's a kopitiam opposite a Chinese temple that offers good food too. I love the clear soup non oily Yong Tau Foo here. I think the stall is called MEI XI.

There are other shops nearby too that you can explore.

❤️

Special thanks to the team at The Serangoon Community Club for their efforts in bringing many events to the community. 👍🏻

The CPR/AED training was not only well delivered, it was very entertaining as well. Thank you for the refreshments too. 😍

Keep up the great work!

A clean, tidy & quiet corner with a relax community atmosphere. The oversize sculpture, Iggy's Pod hanging over the vast atrium is a sight to behold.
There are onsite recycle facilities for electronic  (computers, printers), batteries  and lightbulbs (some LED lightbulbs contains mercury, so please dispose off responsibly).  Facility to replace your Trace Together Token too!
A short walk round the corner is Pet Corner, colourful birds & bird's songs welcome you. There are several coffee shops all within walking distance which cater for breakfast, lunch and dinner.

Facilities are limited, but serves their purposes.
The one I know about are the study room and gym.
Take the lift beside the counter to go to the amenities.
Study room has no wifi, and just some old office tables for you to do your own things. Water cooler is just outside.
Gym is on level 3, with limited equipment.
There is a free weights rack for squats, deadlifts, bench press.
There are free weight dumbbells, resistance machines, treadmills and inclined bench.
Quite a small cozy gym with not many people. Closes at 930pm. See rates and packages in pictures.
Note that non-members who didn't purchase the package cannot come during certain timings.
They can only come when the counter opens, which closes at 6pm.
Go to the counter to exchange for a pass to go to the gym. You need to return the pass afterwards.
Be sure to call in if you are unsure so as to not make a wasted trip!

Like all CCs, they also offer all kinds of services like singpass reset, or classes to sign up for! Check with them for further details.

Bus interchange with bus services Express to Upper East Coast Shenton Way Tampines Clementi Toa Payoh Marine Parade Woodlands Sengkang and Punggol. covered pathway to Ang Mo Kio MRT Ang Mo Kio Hub with plenty of dinning shopping banking Electric supermarkets Electronic library cinemas entertainments groceries sundries postage services community centre and amenities nearby. Walking distance to fast food outlets such as KFC and McDonald's Jing Shan Primary School Anderson Secondary School Deyi Secondary School Maybank Elsie Departmental Store Fairprice Ang Mo Kio Jubilee Square Boardway Plaza Djitsun Mall Ang Mo Kio 3G Centre Community Centre Cheng San Community Club YWCA child Development Centre and Ang Mo Kio Town Garden East with breezy greenery away from busy cityscapes. Friendly cosy comfy welcoming ambience for romantic intimates groups students expats locals visitors and families.

Bus 73 almost always never on time. Yesterday, I waited 30 minutes for a bus that was supposed to only be 9 minutes. Currently, I am in line and I checked the bus app: arriving became 10minutes. It's bad enough that people in this line often go to bus 25 because of how inconsistent the timing is and long the bus takes to get here.

If you cannot make sure your drivers are on time, at least don't lie in the app or on the television.

Bus interchange at the first  floor of Integrated shopping mall walking  distance to Ang Mo Kio MRT with plenty of dinning shopping banking supermarkets library cinemas entertainments groceries sundries postage services community centre education and amenities nearby. Bus services Express to Upper East Coast Shenton Way Tampines Clementi Toa Payoh Marine Parade Woodlands Sengkang and Punggol. Friendly cosy comfy welcoming ambience for romantic intimates groups students expats locals visitors and families.

Bus timings can be a real hit or miss at times. Haven’t been here too often thankfully, was waiting for bus 135 on Sunday and it took a really look time to arrive. Barely any controls here so you get queue jumpers getting ahead.
Quite ok place, leaves much to be desired to get anywhere remotely first world I guess
BUS TIMINGS ARE NEVER ACCURATE AS DISPLAYED ON THE SCREEN. THE BUSES ARE ALWAYS LATE. VERY FRUSTRATING.

Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

The swimming complex was really great.
But there's only one problem...

They don't allow swimsuits with zippers when going to the slides
definitely will come again to enjoy the facility here! sheltered and water playgrounds available to play around.

The toilet cleaner aunty is super rude. Not first time encounter this problem. As level 2 under renovation. Lot of ppl rush to toilet near mcd at level 1. The aunty just mop their leg. We saw the aunty mop the floor and just walk out. We thought she just done cleaning the washroom may be. Then when we walk in, the toilet is full of tissue on the floor and she just ignore and go out. More cleanser required.
Good variety of age appropriate pools. Jacuzzi jets don't really work well at all the spots. Jacuzzi water are constantly cold.
Quite a few different pools for all ages! Remember not to wear swimwear with zipper if you are planning to take the slides!

A nice and modern mall conveniently located at the Jurong East transportation hub, with sheltered walkways linking it to two other popular malls in the area. The mall is spacious, thoughtfully designed with plenty of greenery, and offers a pleasant shopping experience.

Some sections are non-air-conditioned, which can feel warm during the afternoon, but the open concept adds to its vibrant, lifestyle-focused atmosphere. With a wide variety of food and beverage outlets, it’s a great destination for dining, shopping, and leisure.

Mall is around 10 years old located in Jurong east central and beside the MRT station. Good shopper traffic, more home goods, Fnb and lifestyle/services shops such as Nittori, Dreame and Paradise restaurants etc. Toilet cleanliness and  amenities can be improved. Carpark is easy to navigate.
Great place for casual shopping. Good spread of food. Easy connection to the jurong east train station.

As a frequent shopper in the west, I’ve spent countless hours at Westgate, and it has firmly established itself as one of my go-to destinations. Located at 3 Gateway Drive and directly connected to Jurong East MRT Station, its accessibility is virtually unbeatable . The mall is also linked to JEM and IMM via covered walkways, creating a massive interconnected shopping belt .

Westgate truly shines as a family and lifestyle hub. The layout is a mix of sleek, air-conditioned corridors and its signature open-air "Courtyard," which houses popular alfresco dining spots . However, the crown jewel for families is definitely the Westgate Wonderland on Level 4. This fantasy-themed outdoor playground is a massive hit with children, featuring giant interactive structures where they can burn off energy .

Foodies will be in heaven here. The mall boasts an impressive spectrum of dining options. You can grab a quick, affordable bite at the Food Republic food court in the basement or indulge in world-class ramen at Ippudo and next-level Japanese barbecue at Yakiniku Like . For those with a sweet tooth, the newly opened Kind Kone offers fantastic all-vegan ice cream with unique flavors like "Blue Planet," while Flipper’s serves up their legendary Japanese souffle pancakes . Don’t miss the Isetan Supermarket in the basement, which is a treasure trove of premium Japanese groceries and ready-to-eat meals .

On the retail front, Westgate covers all the bases. You’ll find major anchors like a sprawling two-story Uniqlo and a Japanese department store, alongside high-street favorites like Zara and COS . Tech enthusiasts can explore the Samsung Experience Store or Best Denki, while beauty lovers will appreciate the large Sephora outlet .

If I have to levy one major criticism, it’s the notorious parking situation. The carpark has a single, tightly designed entry and exit point that leads to the pick-up/drop-off area, creating absolute chaos during peak hours . Many reviewers report it can take 30 minutes just to exit . My advice? Take the MRT. It drops you right at the mall’s doorstep, saving you the headache.

Despite the driving frustrations, Westgate remains a vibrant and well-maintained mall. With its excellent store mix, incredible variety of food, and dedicated kids' facilities, it offers a complete package that caters to everyone from office workers to young families. It’s a solid 4 out of 5 stars for me.

Right next to JEM but feels a bit more "atas." The outdoor playground on the top floor is great for families with kids. Lots of popular restaurants like Tim Ho Wan and Din Tai Fung here. The link bridge to Jurong East MRT is very convenient and fully sheltered, which is a life-saver during those heavy afternoon downpours.

The park itself is fine if you don't intend to go onto the other side of the waterway because the number of bridges linking the two sides are few and far apart. I am not sure why it is designed like that. We happened to be on the opposite side of the "main attractions" like Waterway Point, SAFRA, etc and we just could not get over easily. I guess if you are aiming to have lots of steps, this is the place to be.
Great place for a family walk. Quite a lot of wildlife and nature you can spot here if you look closely.
Nature's serenity and wonder, 😜 if you don't mind just a quiet morning walk or family walk ☺️🍀😎💕👍🏽🌈

This is my 3rd time here. Although I stay in Jurong West. Took over an hour travel I still miss this place. I hike from Punggol MRT to Lorong Halus Wetland Centre.  On the way we by passed lot of nice place like One Punggol we ate at hawker Center and proceed to waterway park. Along the way u can see few bridge it connecting both side. Easy walk path.
Please see photo enclosed.
A peaceful escape from the city! Perfect for a scenic jog, bike ride, or just a relaxing stroll by the water.

This is one of the well-known hawker centres in Ang Mo Kio – Teck Ghee Square. It has been around for ages and remains a favourite among locals.

Some of my go-to stalls here include the mee rebus / mee siam, sliced fish soup, kway teow soup, fried kway teow, Hainanese chicken rice, and the western food stall. The variety and taste have kept me coming back over the years.

While the food is great, I do feel the place could use some upgrading to make it more comfortable for diners.

Next to the hawker centre is the market, which offers a wide range of fresh produce, and there are a few fruit stalls that are always busy with regular customers.

A classic heartland spot worth visiting, especially if you enjoy traditional local food in an authentic setting.

Good quality, Good value! One of the most reasonably priced stall. Most expensive MSW is $12.50 per kg. Buy 5 free 1.  Durian arrives at about 3.45pm by which time queue starts to form. Unloading and stacking completes by 4.20pm. There are 10 in the queue at any time.
The fish soup was fresh, tender, and cooked just right. Super long queue one. Must patience abit is old old uncle cooking

There are many nice food stalls at this hawker centre. We went there during dinner time and saw many people are queuing at the Fish Soup stall. The BBQ chicken wings stall, fried oyster stall, western food stall are the favourite during dinner time. Apart from that, there is also a stall selling the traditional charcoal steamboat which give you the “familiar old time taste of steamboat”. Will go there again during breakfast time as there are so many stalls for us to try out. A nice neighbourhood hawker centre within walking distance from AMK Hub.
Many hawker stalls including a wet market. Pricing is reasonable. There is a very good fruit stall that sells all types of fruits with reasonable pricing also. Plenty of parking at the HDB car park.

A great place to makan after hikes at nearby reservoirs.

Used to be a very popular hawker centre but for some reason, some stalls have closed down and are still vacant.

That said, there's still quite a few gems in it.

+ Clean and well-maintained.

- Quite a few vegetarian options.

This hawker is along Upper Thomson Road even though is called Sembawang Hill Hawker Centre. The front car park is always full with very limited slots. The next is beside Esso Station. Food wise, seems to be a bit run down hawker, maybe due to not close proximity to HDB. The Cantonese chicken rice and char siew stall delivered a classic authentic a plate of  char siew rice with nice soup. With price of $4, it is worth it. The tender char siew is not common even with well roasted outer layer.

Delicious yet affordable Char Siew and Roasted Pork Rice ($5) or Wanton mee ($6) . Char Siew slightly saltier (3.5/5), Roasted Pork was crispy and thick cut (4/5), wanton mee was spribgy and thin (4/5). The only drawback is that the Q may take 15-20 mins as the uncle does everything.

Not a big traditional food centre, but it is clean and well maintained.

For vegans and vegetarians, there are stalls serving delicious food such as Pawon Vegetarian (Indonesian/Malay cuisine) and Delisnacks, which offers traditional local snacks like You Tiao (fried dough sticks). Other stalls also provide meatless options, including fried carrot cake and traditional toast sets.

Prices in this food centre are reasonable and not expensive.
It’s a great place for a satisfying meal or simply a nice coffee break.

Visited this hawker centre on a Saturday morning around 9.30am and it was already pretty crowded! It’s a small, cosy hawker centre with just two rows of stalls, but there’s a surprisingly good mix — noodles, rice dishes, and plenty of breakfast bites like curry puffs, popiah and fritters. There’s also a clean, well-maintained washroom and lots of sinks, which is nice.

I originally queued for Jing Ji Fishball Noodles, but the line barely moved... after 30 minutes, it only went from 12 people ahead of me to about 7–8, so I gave up. Ended up trying Hui Wei Fishball Noodles (Unit 01-04) instead and honestly, no regrets! Their signature soup plus dry mee pok combo is one of the best I’ve had :)

Also tried the popiah, carrot cake, kaya toast set and taro toast from various stalls. All were decent, nothing particularly memorable; but the fishball noodles? I’d definitely make a return trip just for that ☺️

(Taking 1 star off for how inaccessible this hawker is if you don't drive 😭)

Quiet, underrated gem in the northeast. Punggol Park has a really chill vibe, especially around the big lake in the middle. Great for evening walks, light jogs, or just sitting down to unwind after a long day.

Nice mix of facilities too. There’s a Tenderbest nearby for easy bites, a gym pod if you want a quick workout, plus water coolers and vending machines around for convenience. You’ll also spot exercise stations catered for the elderly, which is a thoughtful touch. There is a nice woodball corner as well and also an open area for football.

Plenty of shaded paths and open spaces, so it never feels too crowded. You’ll see families, cyclists, and even people fishing by the pond. Simple, not overly fancy, but that’s part of the charm.

If u are in Hougang / Sengkang area, and wanted a park to stroll or exercise.  Try this Punggol Park.
Pls note that although it called Punggol Park, it’s actually in end of upper Serangoon, Hougang area.
It’s a smaller to Bedok Reservior Park, but it’s wide enough for most outdoor exercise.
Peaceful and tranquil.  U can have a drink/ food at the eatery: Tenderbest.  They have both indoor AC and alfresco dining area.

Also, if u want to walk/ cycle more, it’s just 5mins walk away to the Serangoon Park Connector.  Which will lead u to Kallang southwards or Northwards will lead u to Punggol Point or Changi Pt via Pasir Ris PCN.

Actually, It's a Nice and quiet park with a running loop around a pond.The park with a lot of place to rest chair benches. It's shady with huge trees and bird chirping. The place is Great for walking, jogging or biking. You can come here anytime u want some peace and to de- stress through a nice and scenic walk in the park. At Night time the lights around the pond makes for a cool background. Overall a good place to bring the family and have some exercise. 🌳🌱🌿🍂🍃🍀🪺🌸💮🐦🐦‍⬛🚴🏃🚶🧎🤸

attended a 3 day wake at the oratory of the Church of Nativity recently. On one of the evenings, I went for a walk in the park. It was pleasant to be in nature, to reflect on the life. The park was however crowded in the evening with people exercising, cycling. walking their dogs and having picnics. Love the trees.

It's a mind-blowing park for spending time with nature ,any time can go and spend time in peace , best place for morning time exercises and walking or running , meditation etc etc . Early in the morning the feeling here is very nice and quiet , the public toilet is also here ,
One wedding machine is also there to take your favourite drinks .

result.name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library

reviews.text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

The National Library is conveniently located with good traffic access from around the island. It’s a comfy cool place to be if your want to study, research and just reading. You may spent hours but no worries about hunger as the are many choices of food in the area.

The Albatross File exhibition at NLB is a fascinating showcase of Singapore’s separation history, featuring declassified documents, oral histories, and immersive multimedia displays. It brings to life the tense years leading up to independence, blending archival records with dramatized storytelling.

2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

Both my children were taught by teacher En Tong, and they really enjoyed her lessons and showed clear improvement during the time with her. While my younger child did not do as well as expected, En Tong genuinely did her best and was very dedicated and supportive. The centre staff are helpful and approachable as well. I would be happy to enrol my youngest child with her again in the future and hope my last child will get the chance to learn under her one day.

Before joining learning point, one of my initial struggles was answering comprehension questions accurately. Learning Point provides great tips and compre techniques which has helped give me a sense of direction whenever i come across compre!

Through the programme, I have definitely seen improvement in terms of knowing how to answer the questions, as well as developing a stronger grasp of the fundamentals when it comes to continuous writing in a cohesive, structured manner.

What makes LP so special is the learning environment! The lessons are engaging and enjoyable, and the ‘current buzz’ segment in every lesson really helped me broaden my general knowledge and strengthen content development. Continually, the class encourages students to write freely and develop their own individual voice!

I also want to add that my teacher Mrs Lee Jo Ann was consistently supportive and encouraging not only academically, but also in checking in on students’ overall well-being, making learning fun and enjoyable at the same time. I think this holistic approach rlly makes LP meaningful and empowering.

My child has been with Teacher En Tong for the past year for P3 Math and we really appreciate her dedication in teaching, putting in extra time and effort to make sure the weaker students could follow up with the class. My child really enjoyed her classes.
Would be better if the centre is more understanding and flexible with their terms and conditions though..

First of all, I would like to express my gratitude to Mr. Elden. As a Korean, I am not good at English, so I have been very worried about my daughter Erin's English learning. As I met a good academy called learning Point and a good teacher, Elden, I was surprised and very grateful for my daughter Erin's English ability increasing day by day. Currently, my daughter is P3. I will trust and follow the teachers of this academy until I become a grade above P6.
Thank you teacher eldon and learnin point !
- Lim jongtae (erin’s dad) -

Happy Teacher’s Day!
As a parent, I just want to extend my deepest thanks for the care, dedication, and guidance the teachers have given to my child through the tuition sessions. The support has made a tremendous difference — not just in academics, but in my child’s overall confidence and attitude toward learning.

We’ve seen the progress, the growing curiosity, and the renewed motivation, and we know that much of it is because of the teachers' patience, encouragement, and consistent effort. They’ve been more than just a tutor — they've been a mentor and a positive influence in our child’s life.

Wishing you a well-deserved Happy Teacher’s Day, filled with the appreciation and respect you’ve so truly earned.

Warm regards,
Yang Dazhi

result.name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Ang Mo Kio AMK) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Student Care

reviews.text: My P1 daughter loves EduFirst- especially Teacher Precia and Teacher Yanxin who have been so kind, loving and patient with her. She has benefitted tremendously not just from their enrichment and tuition but as a growing girl, she has also learnt a lot of virtues from them. In her Chinese especially, she has developed a much stronger interest and proactively initiates the language with me at home. This shows a lot! The school is very accommodating and understanding, and gives constant and quick updates. I am informed even when she does not like a certain meal and the cook will make the most effort to adjust the meal so that my daughter is fed for lunch. This is a great environment for my daughter both professionally and academically. I would recommend EduFirst AMK to any parent!

I attended Mandarin classes at EduFirst AMC for a year. My teacher, Precia, was wonderful, and I liked her teaching style. She is incredibly kind, patient, and always encouraging. She explained everything to me in detail, and I feel I learned a lot. I always went to her classes with great enthusiasm and will miss her very much.

I am very grateful to the EduFirst  for helping my child pass the AEIS exam and get into a local school now (Yew Tee Primary). Now, I am getting my younger son back at EduFirst to prepare him for KET and AEIS exam so he can get into a local school. I highly recommend EduFirst if you wish to get your child into a local government school in Singapore. He takes AEIS English Tuition and Mathematics tuition here for the exam.

I have seen a significant improvement in my child’s grades after enrolling in this learning center. The teachers are approachable and take time to explain difficult concepts. The flexible scheduling also makes it easy to fit into our busy routine. Highly recommend it to anyone looking for extra academic support!

My kid has been attending since P3.
So far so good. He's looking forward to go Studentcare most of the time. Communication with the teacher/in-charge is usually prompt too. Activities/outing planned during school holidays too.

The Seletar Mall is one of those neighbourhood malls that quietly gets the job done. It may not be the biggest or flashiest, but it is clean, well laid out, and easy to navigate. The tenant mix is practical and family friendly, with a good range of dining spots, enrichment centres, and everyday essentials. It is especially convenient for residents in the Sengkang and Fernvale area who want everything within walking distance.

The mall never feels overwhelmingly crowded, even on weekends, which makes grocery runs and casual meals much more pleasant. The cinema and supermarket are reliable anchors, and there are enough cafes to sit down and unwind. Parking is generally manageable, and access via the LRT makes it fuss free.

Overall, it is a dependable heartland mall that serves its community well. Not a destination mall, but definitely a comfortable and convenient one.

Has been ordering the food from food panda and it is the first time to eat at the actual outlet.

The dishes served are very consistent to those that delivered to my home. The delivery bag, the utensils, the food containers shows that they are customer focus.

The vermicelli ordered is really plentiful but is nice. The cabbage is a must order. The red bean soup is nice.

The services are generally good, ma y things are self services.

The only thing which is not a 5 stars is that the service crew could have exercise some flexibility. We are back there for another meal yesterday but the crew tuck us to 2 seater, which is not wrong but consider our barang barang, we requested a bigger seat but was flatly rejected mentioning this is the only seats left where there are 2 four seaters around. It is only when we are exiting out, the crew then offer a 4 seater which is a bit too late.

The service standard fall short compare to our first visit when the bosses are around.

Perhaps it is a new crew? It is our first meal of the day, we will like some comfortable space to enjoy our meals before the dinner crowds swamp in Seletar Mall.

Ahoy! If only every mall had a playground like this hidden gem. We were thrilled to discover this pirate-themed wonderland—packed with fun for kiddos of all ages!

With 3 long slides, a mini merry-go-round, and even a toddler-friendly swing, there’s plenty to keep those little ones busy.

👧🏻 Toddler rating: 8/10. Everything to love about this place for wobbly toddlers, plus it’s semi-shaded!

👉 Parents’ note: Going up can be daunting, and the climbing net is best suited for older kiddos.

Follow us @slideseekersg on Instagram for more playground reviews!

Seletar mall is the nearest to my house.

The aircon is not cold most of the times. You will often see baby cockroaches in their washroom.

Otherwise, it is not bad for a neighborhood mall.

Hope the Management can do something about the aircon and cleaniless of the toilet.

Most of the things at under one roof.
I mostly like their food court.

AMK Hub is one of the most convenient and practical malls in Ang Mo Kio. The location is excellent, as it is directly connected to Ang Mo Kio MRT station and the bus interchange, making it very easy to reach by public transport.

The mall has a good mix of shops, dining options, supermarkets, daily essentials, and services. It is suitable for families, students, office workers, and residents living nearby. Whether you are looking for groceries, a quick meal, shopping, banking services, or just a place to walk around, AMK Hub has almost everything you need in one location.

I like that the mall is very accessible and easy to navigate. There are many food choices, from fast food and takeaway options to cafés and restaurants for proper meals. It is also a convenient place to run errands, meet friends, or spend time with family.

Although the mall can get quite crowded during weekends and meal times, the overall environment is still lively, clean, and well maintained. The air-conditioning is comfortable, and the shops are arranged in a way that makes it easy to move around.

Overall, AMK Hub is a reliable and convenient neighbourhood mall with a good variety of food, shopping, and essential services. It is definitely one of the best places to visit in Ang Mo Kio for everyday needs, meals, and casual shopping.

AMK Hub is a really convenient neighbourhood mall, especially since it’s directly connected to Ang Mo Kio MRT and the bus interchange. It’s super easy to get to, which makes it a popular stop for quick meals or shopping. There’s a wide range of food options here—from food courts with local and international dishes to plenty of fast-food and restaurant chains. It’s not as exciting as the bigger malls, but it’s great for everyday needs and casual dining.

A good, classic neighbourhood mall with food options that leave you spoilt for choice. A rectangular-shaped mall with basements mostly eateries, Level 1 & 2 mostly shops, and Level 3 is mostly educational centres.

Went to AMK Hub for lunch. ate at that Malaysia Boleh. There are many varieties of food here suitable for your lunch or dinner...My hubby always eat the cray pot rice which comes with chicken and salted fish. That day we ate that fish ball black noodles. This store add some tasty dark sauce to their noodles which gives a different taste to it's noodles. Taste very yummy. I had their fried tang hoon. Same store...They have varies type of fried noodles which you must tried. Not bad too. We always end our meal with a bowl of Chendol which is classic with all Malaysia Boleh outlets...They definitely have this menu in all their outlets without fail...AMK Hub is filled with varieties type of food on their 3 floors esp basement 1 and basement 2...a full line of snacks and food for you to choose from. Try coming here for your meals...no regret....I have had enclosed photos of our meals that day...Enjoy!!!!
Lots of shops and less shopping shops.only essential shops are available like daily use stuffs. More favorite thing attracted me it the play area next to jollie bee food court

I've been a member of Fitness First AMK branch for a while now, and I must say, it's been a game-changer! The staff are incredibly friendly and always go above and beyond to accommodate my requests. What's impressed me most is how they've gotten to know me so well - they often anticipate what I need without me even having to ask! Their warm smiles and welcoming attitude make every visit a pleasure.

My fitness journey has been a success, thanks in part to the supportive environment here. I've lost around 15kg in just 4-5 months, and I'm loving every minute of it! The subscription has been totally worthwhile.

A special shoutout to Fafa - she's amazing at what she does! Her welcoming and approachable personality makes working out feel less daunting and more enjoyable. She's always eager to help and has been a great motivator for me.

If you're looking for a gym with great service and a friendly vibe, I'd highly recommend Fitness First AMK branch! 🌟

Happy to work out at this gym! The receptionists and staff are super friendly and always ready to help. The equipment layout is nice and easy to use, not messy at all. There are also lots of classes, and the instructors are very kind and guide you well, even if you’re a newbie. I always feel comfortable coming here 😊 and keep trying to come even my schedule are very tight 😅
Love the black coffee and lemon water as one of my favourite here. Also enjoy for using sauna, feel very relax and I love to use it whenever I have time 🥰

Fully equipped gym in a convenient location. Right in the heart of AMK Hub. Free weights and various weight machines for different fitness levels with different training areas. Many different types of group classes are available too (like zumba, yoga, bodypump, etc). Comes with towels, shower and sauna facilities. Bring your own workout attire though - this branch does not provide it. Can get crowded during peak hours. Warm and friendly staff, like Mithran, who are always welcoming and accommodating. This post was made during an off-peak period.

I had a really great experience at Fitness First, thanks to Atticus. He was very knowledgeable and patient, taking the time to introduce us to the different types of courses available. From strength training to group classes and wellness-focused programs, he explained everything clearly and made it easy for us to understand what suits our goals best.

What I appreciated most was that he didn’t rush us or push for anything. Instead, he focused on helping us explore the options and find what we would genuinely enjoy. His friendly and approachable attitude made the whole experience comfortable and enjoyable.

Overall, Atticus provided excellent service and made our visit worthwhile. If you’re new or considering joining, having someone like him to guide you really makes a difference. Highly recommended!

Awesome front counter staff at AMK FF. They are tired working long hours but still keeping their smiles for members answering any queries and complains coming their way. Keep up the awesome work guys! (Mithran, Sumatra, Iman, Yamuna, Fafa)

I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.
Visited in the late morning before the weather got too hot , historical park with heritage buildings & a gallery of history of this place was event fully refreshing .

​"I used to love the wooden badminton courts at Keat Hong CC, but they recently updated the flooring (around 11 June 2026) to a wood-style carpet overlay. It looks great initially, but it is incredibly slippery. A few other players complained about it, and after playing a couple of games, I felt it too. It definitely doesn't feel safe to play on. I really hope management considers changing it back to the original flooring!"

It seems the staff at Keat hong cc should undergo retraining. When I was there at the counter inquiring abt the basketball court back in March before the renovation, it seems no one know why the court is not allowing public to use on a good Monday afternoon. There’s always a schedule on when the basketball or mic is being blocked and not open to public. If the schedule is not being followed, why put up?

Not only that, the male customer service staff is very rude. Not sure if this facility is for public to use or for the staff to stop public to use. Also not sure why the staff is allowed to be rude to the public when the key roles and responsibilities for a customer service staff is to be the bridge between public and PA.

Especially after a Friday evening event. The court is always unusable on the following Saturday as the hoop is always raise up and no one is at the centre to bring the hoop down. I’ve been there for quite afew Saturday afternoon to bring my kids down for bonding time and getting disappointed.
The CC was just like a mini sports hub. Bowling with arcade dance hall, rooftop sports facilities, makan place like B.K, starbucks. Mini Waterplay under the MRT/LRT tracks.... 👍👍
Came for the STUDY PODS don’t have, remove le but they left tables at the place the PODS used to be. Sad

Large CC, mostly go there for the pod, easy to access for those who stay nearby

Lots of pods but with free WiFi unlike other pods and plenty of tables at same level if pods are unavailable

result.name: My First Skool at Blk 229 Ang Mo Kio

reviews.text: An outstanding preschool with excellent management and a team of passionate educators. The principal leads with great professionalism and heart, creating a positive and safe learning environment for all children. We’ve seen tremendous development and confidence in our child since joining. Truly a five-star experience👍

Excellent preschool with dedicated teachers and a well-structured curriculum. The staff truly care about each child’s development, and communication with parents is always clear and supportive. Couldn’t be happier with our choice!

I decided to enrol my girl in this branch because of the teachers’ experience and attention to detail. My husband and I appreciate how the principal addressed our concerns and supported the teachers. Best decision for choosing this centre for my girl! Thank you!

Ms. Tina is a highly experienced early childhood educator with excellent problem-solving skills. She is a passionate and sincere Principal who cares deeply for both children and staff, showing warmth and kindness in all she does. Teachers here all cheerful and lovely always.
Definitely 5 ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Amazing school! Safe, friendly, and full of love for the students

Was here for an Edusave award ceremony at the MPH, which is clean and has aircon. The open air area is also sheltered and has a huge fan so it's not hot. There are also counters to perform administrative stuff like collection of vouchers etc. There's also a Starbucks here if you wanna chill. There's a 24/7 fitness here too.

First time booking a pickleball court for play on Tuesday (20 Jan, 8-9am) and when we arrived at the CC, the only hall that we saw was dark and empty. As the counter was not open, I called the main PA hotline and requested for assistance. The staff tried contacting someone from the CC to no avail. He said he would escalate the matter. When I asked if we should just “cancel” our court instead of continuing to wait, he told me to wait for his update. We left eventually and there was no further callback at all.

We returned to the CC at 1pm and checked with the counter staff, only to find out that the pickleball courts are not located within the CC itself, but at the HDB block opposite, on the 2nd floor. When I informed the staff that there’s no notice indicating that the courts are at a different location, the staff replied that their badminton players all know that the courts are opposite. He even asked if I’m staying in the area.

From booking, to the email acknowledgement, there’s no indication of any information stating that. Even at the CC itself, there are no notices giving directions to the courts. Another staff then came out and added that the information on the poster did not say that the courts are in the hall within the CC itself and that the opposite block is part of Bishan CC.

Totally appalled at the attitude of the counter staff, who seemed to imply that it’s our fault for not knowing where the courts are.

The volunteer staff there were very polite and provided clear, correct guidance. I’m very grateful for their service—it solved my problem and helped me save a considerable amount of money.
Ann Tan

It’s a cool place to hangout or wait for a friend to meetup. It’s covered so no issues during rain. There is free water refilling, toilet, and Starbucks too if you need coffee. There’s a lot of exercise activities in the morning for seniors, some activities during the day, and cheering squads use it for practice in the evenings. It’s a bustling place and I am glad to be living nearby and can go there anytime.
Nice location. Very convenient as its adjacent to the MRT station and Bus terminus. There is a small Starbucks and the local Post Office in the same building

Really like this library. There are many types of books and magazines to choose from, and it even has a second floor. The place is bright and clean. Staff are friendly and helpful. There is also a toilet and a baby changing diaper table, which is very convenient.

Clean, pleasant and quiet - truly a sanctuary for anyone who appreciates peaceful moments.

It's about a 15-minute walk from Ang Mo Kio MRT station, but I genuinely enjoy the stroll past the old shophouses along the way.
Very nostalgic.
Big, quiet, selection of books is awesome. Not as many desks as compared to other libraries. So if you're looking for a place to study, pls go elsewhere like punggol and don't hog the desks here.

A nice public library. The library was quite secluded, and is about 1km walk from Ang Mo Kio MRT station. The library is 2 storeys high, and it's quite a quiet library with fewer patrons during off-peak hours. There were quite a number of empty tables at the Adult Fiction Section and power points were quite readily available. There was a water cooler at the 2nd storey. The place is highly recommended for students and readers who want a conducive environment. However it was slightly inaccessible and had some problems navigating from outside. But overall, I had a pleasant experience. Would come back again.

My rating: 4/5
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

The Ang Mo Kio library is very easy to find once you use Google maps. I think the library is an OK library, not horrible, not perfect. There are two levels. The first level has a children's section and adult fiction books. I'd say the adult fiction section is actually really nice. I read through a few of the books and I took multiple pictures to basically remind myself to check them out again.

The children's section isn't really a room; it's just some shelves out in the open. The second level has young adult books. The selection had some books that I've already read, and a lot that I have on my To-Be-Read (TBR).
There are some Chinese magazines, English fiction books, and Chinese books. More in the corner, there is a space where there are multiple black seats spaced apart. The second level has two tables with computers originally on it, and 6 seats for each of the tables.

There is a quiet reading lounge, and at the time I was there, there weren't that many people. But the layout is really nice because there are 9 seats where you can use your phone, read, and listen to an audiobook. But there’s also a few desks with seats where you can read, and maybe use your computer.

Overall, I think that the Ang Mo Kio Library is very well organized, spacious, has a nice quiet reading lounge, nice children’s section, and a good place to use your computer and read.

Junction 8 shopping mall is located at bishan mrt station(yellow red line).this shopping mall is crowded everyday especially on weekends which gets more crowded.this mall attracts people for the 1.foods:lots of different foods from fast foods,snacks, restaurants,foodcourt, flavoured drinks.
2.clothing shops
3.accessories shops/jewellery shops
4.fairprice supermarket
5.mrt station yellow n red line is in the mall itself at the basement.
6.popular bookstore for everyone
M POSTING AS MANY PHOTOS AS I CAN FROM BISHAN JUNCTION 8 SHOPPING MALL...HOPE EVERYONE LOVES IT 😍
REALLY GOOD PLACE TO EAT N SHOP.
Good shopping experience here. Good mix of shops. MRT station & Bus Interchange at door step.

first time here, pretty cool mall with lots of shops to browse and well-ventilated not stuffy. toilets are clean too.
best part is a lot of food and bubble tea selection

update: love the mofusand deco, the mall is very festive and perfect for chinese new year vibes with the cute kittes.
Nice place to do shopping , Uniqlo, Skechers, Nike, adidas all mall shops members aa good to give great service to customer

Came over to try out its newly revamped Food Junction, saw several interesting stores on the way. The Tamjai Samgor noodles house, the Subway lookalike Seragoon BBQ & Curry, as well as catching mofusand as collaborating partners

The mutation orchid garden is truly stunning and unique, filled with rare and vibrant blooms that capture attention at every corner. Each orchid displays unusual colors, intricate patterns, and elegant shapes, creating a magical atmosphere for visitors. The garden is carefully arranged, allowing guests to walk through and admire the beauty up close. However, the strong sunlight makes the visit slightly tiring, especially during midday. Despite the heat, the breathtaking scenery and peaceful environment make the experience worthwhile. With a bit more shade or a cooler time of day, the visit would be even more enjoyable and relaxing for everyone.

Been here many times.  Still think it is a good place to relax.
The garden is very well planned and full of greenery.
There is also a new indoor garden air-conditioning space where you can enjoy completely different species of orchids.
If it rains, there are many places to hide.  There are also drinks vending machines.
Even if you need to pay in the park, I suggest you come.  It's worth it!👍

Absolutely spectacular! These garden were Beautiful and very well put together. Clearly a lot of thought has been put into the displays. They also had spots specifically set up for photo opportunities which helps to reduce clogging on the paths. There are bathrooms and places to get refreshments near by. There are also shaded areas throughout the gardens where you can shelter from the rain or sun as needed.

Well manicured and maintained, this place is. The design of this part of Botanic gardens takes you on a journey through meandering pathways and cooling glasshouse. Certainly worth the entrance fee. Orchids are one of the most intriguing flowers. As the old adage goes necessity is the mother of evolution, these orchids are perfectly evolved to benefit from their symbiotic pollinators. Some uses trickery to entice the male species of insects to land on the stigma, some make the landing pad comfortable for a nice sip of delicious nectar. Amazing!

Absolutely stunning the highlight of our trip for me. An incredible number of beautiful orchids in a lovely landscaped and meandering garden. Entry is just 5 dollars making this easily one of the most affordable things to do in Singapore, and absolutely worthwhile. You could easily spend a day wandering the orchid garden and the surrounding botanic gardens

result.name: Cheng San Market & Cooked Food Centre

reviews.text: A lively neighbourhood wet market & hawker centre in Ang Mo Kio serving the surrounding HDB estates. The market sells fresh seafood, meat, vegetables & other daily ingredients, while the cooked food centre offers a good variety of affordable local hawker dishes. It’s especially busy in the mornings with residents doing their grocery shopping or stopping by for breakfast & coffee. A typical heartland market with a friendly community vibe.

This Lor Mee standard dropped le.
After 3 minutes, the gravy turned watery.
The taste is different now. I have always been patronising this stall.
Will try the laksa again, when I am there.

Nice variety of affordable hawker food. My fav here is the teochew porridge. Steam fish, the authentic teochew fishcake, fried anchovies are my top faves. There's a good range of other local favourites like herbal soup, bak chor mee and roast meat rice.

My husband and I wanted to buy a fried spring roll and a fried sweet potato so asked that to pack it separately as we are not sharing. Lady boss said this was not possible, if we want to pack it separately, we would have to pay $0.10 more. So I went separately and was trying to buy the fried spring roll. She firmly said no plastic bag as my husband who was had bought earlier was counted as together. She offered me paper instead.
Our point of view is if we are eating separate food item, why should our food be put together in a bag. Even if we queued up to buy a second item, she said my husband and I are counted as one so no plastic bag. What is the logic? We have bought many times from this company but different outlets, why is she different? So if I want to tabao some for my mother that is not living with me, I have to bring my own plastic?

Been enjoying this for years! After the market renovation, they tweaked the menu slightly.. my go-to BCM now even comes with 2 fried wantons. Leaving this review on National Day because local food like this truly brings me so much happiness. Thank you for serving affordable and delicious food!

NEX is a big, convenient mall that’s hard to beat for errands or a casual day out. It’s right by Serangoon MRT and the bus interchange, so getting in and out is easy. Inside, it’s bright, clean, and well-air-conditioned; even on weekends when it’s busy, the walkways are wide enough that you can still move comfortably.

Food options are plentiful, everything from quick bites to sit-down spots, and the food court is efficient with staff constantly wiping tables, so turnover is fast. Shopping is just as varied, with fashion, beauty, tech, and a large supermarket, which makes it great for one-stop “get everything done” runs. Families will appreciate the clear signage and clean restrooms, plus plenty of places to take a break if you’re with kids or older folks. Overall, a reliable place to eat, shop, and cool off, practical for a lunch stop and pleasant enough for an unhurried weekend stroll.

Huge shopping mall connected to Serangoon MRT. Has all outlets that you need for shopping and food.
Very accessible. Can get a bit crowded on weekends.

Probably the busiest, most bustling mall in the heartlands. It’s a decent size with a pretty good tenant mix, but word on the street is that the old Isetan spot is being turned into a bunch of boring tuition centres and a Kiztopia. If that’s true, we’ll probably be visiting a lot less.

There’s also been a massive proliferation of PRC restaurants in recent years, which makes us even less inclined to frequent the place. We might drop by when Chin Mee Chin finally opens, but we aren’t going to visit as much as before.

P.S The elevators are always under maintenance or not working.

Good Location Shopping!!!
But saw such behaviours of youngsters is totally embarrassing and flabergusted, giving others no place to walk via bulldozing in a row.
Saw them few times doing this in 2025 and 2 times in 2026. Video was captured when I changed my handphone and was trying out it's functions.
The worst is, this group have been doing this bulldozing walks everywhere they go, even Tourists has been complaining on TikTok regarding such behaviours when they visited Singapore.

The management of NEX mall (Serangoon MRT) must be fast asleep. A couple of their escalators (to L2 & B1, see photos) have been out of service for quite a few months and no repair work has been initiated. These escalators are covered with cladding and nobody has been spotted working on them! Rather than have them fixed, signs are instead posted to direct customers to other (working, for the time being) escalators. This smacks of apathy and a pathetic lack of professional pride & customer care. Whoever is at the top needs to deal with it - unless, of course, he/she/they also can’t be bothered. In which case, the problem is endemic.

Dear Anchorvale CC, I am rewriting to thank a staff member for his kindness and willingness to help.

Last Saturday (11/4/2026) at around 9pm, I went for a run with my parents. After running, I was unwell and had to sit on a bench, outside the Anchorvale CC reception office. My parents were accompanying me when a staff, which I later found out was called Sun Hui, gave me a sweet, in case I had low blood sugar. Later, he also gave me a canned drink. The food helped and I felt better.
I, as well as my parents, want to express our gratitude to Sun Hui the staff for not being afraid to stand up and lend a helping hand. They made our day that night.

As my eyes were closed at the time, I was unable to see clearly. However, when I later went into the reception office to ask for his name, two other staff members also checked on my condition and kindly reminded me to take care. Their concern was equally appreciated.

I thank Anchorvale CC for their amazing and compassionate staff.

I am writing to bring a matter to your attention regarding the accessibility of the facilities at our community club.
Recently, I have observed that the restrooms are frequently locked during the morning hours. I personally experienced a situation where I required urgent access to the facilities only to find them unavailable, and I have witnessed several other members of the public facing the same frustration.
As a center intended to serve the residents, it is disappointing to find such basic and essential amenities closed to the public.
A community club’s primary mission is to serve the people. Restricting restroom access, especially during standard morning hours(6am), creates a significant barrier for residents.
If the closures are due to safety concerns or convenience issues? I urge Mr Lam to look into alternative solutions. This might include:
* Increasing security patrols.
* Installing better lighting or CCTV outside the toilet.
* Adjusting cleaning schedules to ensure facilities remain open during peak morning activity.
* Or whatever reason
Access to clean and functional restrooms is a basic necessity. Denying this access feels dismissive of the needs of the public who visit the club.
I m not sure if Mr. Lam oversees the operations here, and I am hopeful that under his leadership, these operational hurdles can be resolved. We look forward to seeing the community club return to being a fully inclusive and accessible space for everyone.
Thank you for your time and for your commitment to improving our community. (If you ever read here)

Great place to be at, well located at the heart of Sengkang - Anchorvale district with amazing amenities like sports centre, swimming pool. It also has a wide variety of family friendly food options like Macdonalds and Pasta mania. It's a really good weekend location. Plus it has atm for all your cash needs!

A great place to keep fit & have a joyful time with your family. There’s an indoor swimming pool for a sheltered swimming experience… check out the swimming lessons for kids by “Dream Team Swimming School”.
