There’s also Climb Central for indoor rock climbing and Splash-N-Surf for kids, making it great for families and those looking for active fun.

Love this lib. Looks so new and design so contemporary. As with all the new libraries, the design is thoughtful with good reading areas, working areas, charging points. These days I love going to the library because it is such an enjoyment experience. Nestled within heartbeat@bedok with supermarket and f&b. Building has its own car park. Water dispenser avail just outside lib. Lib spread two floors with internal staircase.

I was surprised to find tourist reviews for such a niche place! This must be the kind of spot people visit only if they’re on an extended trip to Singapore and have the time to explore a community library with no real scenic appeal.

The library is located on the 2nd and 3rd floors of the community center (CC). Taking the escalator is much faster since the cylindrical lift is extremely inefficient—you might have to wait up to 5 minutes for a ride! The upper floors also host other activity facilities, so even on weekdays, there are quite a few people queuing for the lift.

The overall environment here is definitely not as quiet as a university library. You’ll encounter a mix of people, including uncles whose phones aren’t on silent mode and those who talk loudly on their phones.

The 3rd floor is even noisier because it houses the children’s reading area. The noise from kids running around often drifts down to the 2nd floor, making it less peaceful as well.

Sofa seating is almost always occupied—even if there’s no one sitting, personal belongings will be placed there to reserve the spot. There are usually available seats in the hard plastic chair area, though.

If you plan to spend a long time studying or reading here, you might want to bring a long-sleeved coat, as the air conditioning is set to 18°C. For residents dealing with noisy construction at home or in public spaces, this library can be a good escape. Although it’s not particularly quiet, it’s much more peaceful compared to the noise pollution in residential buildings or on the streets.

For visitors/tourists looking to take aesthetic photos, this might not be the best choice. The design of the bookshelves here isn’t as visually appealing as those in the Orchard Road Library. However, if you’re out of places to visit and just want to relax, this spot offers a glimpse into the daily lives of locals and their community atmosphere.

I visited the NLB Bedok library today to use one of the public PCs. While I was using it, the computer froze and I encountered some difficulties. I sought assistance from the staff, Miss Norsiah , who responded immediately and resolved the issue confidently and efficiently.
I would like to take this opportunity to express my sincere appreciation and thank her for her prompt, professional, and excellent assistance. Her helpfulness made my visit a pleasant experience. Once again, many thanks.

Bedok Public Library staffs is extremely kind and willing to help. It is also we'll designed from interior to exterior. I liked  the library's Teens Study and Reading Area,Early Literary Area,Multimedia Stations area and Magazine Reading Area the most I would strongly encourage you to visit this Library if you want to get BookBugs 3rd release cards as I got 35 Buwan,54 Sisik and 57 Liraes here with only 85 times.The BookBugs dispensing machine and stamp booth is at Level 3 of the librarynear the borrowing station (At level 3 main Entrance).It is also very convenient as it is at Heartbeat@Bedok, which is just 10 mins walk from EW5 Bedok station and there is lift service from level 2 to level 3 if you want to go to the early Literacy area (level 3) from the adult area (level 2) .I would like to visit this library again.

Clean and conducive atmosphere for reading and studying. Most of the spots were occupied by students who seem to be camping there , and elderly reading news paper. As a first time visitor, there are few staff to assist and level 2 entrance were closed. An impressive catalog of books (including other foreign languages ones). There seem to be seminar rooms for special program .

Incident Report – MRT Train (Paya Lebar to Bedok)
Date: 28 October 2025
Time: Around 10:00 AM

This morning, at about 10:00 AM, I boarded the train at Paya Lebar station. I had a team meeting to attend and was required to take the minutes. Unfortunately, my earpiece was damaged after I dropped it earlier, so I could only play the meeting audio at the lowest possible volume.

While I was trying to join the meeting quietly, a man sitting two seats beside me suddenly shouted at me. I explained to him softly that I had already turned the volume to the lowest setting. However, he continued to raise his voice and scold me.

Before alighting at the next station at Bedok, he continued shouting. I had to log out of my meeting and ended up missing an important discussion. As he was about to leave, I noticed him turning sideways and taking several photos of me . I could see that his phone camera was on, and he appeared to be snapping my picture repeatedly without my consent. I already log of the meeting he still continuously want to find problem !

3+ level mall in the east of Singapore. Easy access from the train station. Walk down across and up from the station exit. Lots of food and retail shops. Also some outdoor traditional hawker stalls next door.
Bedok MRT station is spacious, clean and good air ventilation. It is a busy station and located next to shopping mall and hawker centre. Numerous buses also pass by this station.

A busy station surrounded by various amenities, such as a shopping mall, hawker centre, town square, and others. Airy design makes it well ventilated, which is a strong point in Singapore's hot weather.

This isn’t a review on the transport itself, it’s about my recent experience with one of the security at the station.

I had lunch at Bedok Mall and I wanted to head back to the airport via the MRT and my sister & I were stopped by the security/auxiliary police for a “random” luggage check. It didn’t seem random at the time because I saw there were other people bringing luggages and they weren’t stopped. Both of us felt targeted as if we did a crime. I have always enjoyed coming to Singapore and I’m always here for concerts and most recently the Grand Prix but this experience made me feel that this is racial profiling. The security of entering/exiting the country is so strict so what of crime could we possibly commit?

I’d still come to Singapore but maybe not stop by the neighbourhood areas to avoid being targeted again.

Still having the authentic Kampong feel. Drinks tend to be on the sweet side. Sort've a Malaysia feel to it.

The place used to have about a fourth of the shops occupied by hawkers selling Chinese dishes. Recently though, many of those shops were taken over by Malay hawkers, to sell those same 'chinese' dishes.

Is that a monument to integration, or a monument to self segregation though? Not too sure, something to think about.
I personally like this place as there are only local food with local price, amazingly enjoyable. We got roasted chicken rice, teh alia, sugar cane juice and kuay teow which were all so good.

Fabulous food and the best stalls are the ones with long queues such as the halal kway teow and Hokkien noodles and the satay! And all with the breeze from the seaside makes this dining experience totally enjoyable! And the teh tarik is amazing!

1st time visiting this food centre, a lot of halal options, didn't try all the shops, tried the mee rebus with Satay, taste wise about blant for both the stay gravy and mee rebus gravy.
Epok Epok shop mee Soto was awesome, I found the gravy a bit on the salty side maybe due to my taste buds, epok epok was fresh and hot but found the flour a bit hard to break, felt more like biscuit. Ccarrot cake was ok, bought back the wanton noodle to eat, taste wise was normal.

Almost all stalls sell some form of satay dish. As always, tables almost full of patrons. Not wheelchair friendly place. But quality & selection of food had vastly improved. (Together wth the price hike.) Still best!
Sector is optimal for mobility drills. Good vantage points, but expect heavy crosswinds. There's hawker centre nearby. Good spot to replenish calories.

One of the best gravel experience I had and the view is not bad! Will recommend visiting it during sunrise or sunset.

Also part of PCN.

Large park with many local residents around that area walking and jogging. Seats are available around this park. There's playground for kids as well. There's also a canoe area in a separate building nearby and it's called "Passionwave". There's toilet facilities too. Quite a number of parking space available for those who came from far. Many companies and schools like to hold their events here as the space is spacious and it is convenient to come here for the bus stops are nearby too.

Well maintained park with walking paths around the reservoir. The sandy path by the water takes more effort than the shady paved path running along side. A good 5km round the park. Different gradients on the paved path add to a good workout. Encountered squirrels and a lone otter too.

Beautiful spot for a walk or jog! Bedok Reservoir's lush greenery and picturesque views of the water make it an ideal destination for outdoor enthusiasts. Well-maintained and clean facilities too. It’s always a nice 5 km walk !

Bishan MRT station in the Circle line it is Station Number 15.
It is connecting one side with Harbour Front and another side with Marina Bay.
Same MRT connected with North South Line as Station Number 17.
It is getting connected with one side Jurang East another side with Marina South Pier.
It is well maintained. It is very neat and clean.
There are clear  signs for each and every directions.
No question if confusion.

---

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Bishan MRT Station is one of the most convenient and well‑connected stations in Singapore. Serving both the North–South Line and Circle Line, it makes transfers quick and easy. The station is spacious, clean, and well‑maintained, with clear signage that helps commuters navigate smoothly. It connects directly to Junction 8 mall and the bus interchange, adding to its convenience. Facilities such as lifts, escalators, and ticketing machines are in good working order, and staff are approachable when assistance is needed. While it can get crowded during peak hours, the overall experience is reliable. A dependable hub for daily commuting and shopping.

---

The base of my reviewing operations.

The station is usually well maintained and kept clean by the staff. During peak hours can get a bit squeezy but usually got enough space for everyone. Must give credits to the station staff here though, they're always willing to help out and maintain order in the station after big events where everyone is rushing to change lines.

During peak period, I felt very unwell and stopped at one of the benches to rest. One of the staff noticed that I was feeling unwell. She prompted other staff members and even got a wheelchair after I communicated that I felt too ill to move to the sick bay. I was then moved to the sick bay area and someone accompanied me throughout in case I needed anything.

AKA Staff were super vigilant and friendly when I fell ill despite it being peak period

good layout of the station, but be prepared for extra large crowds during peak periods such as the mornings and evenings. Expect to wait for multiple trains during super crowded timings to even board the train.

here is an image of what the station looks like

Had Supper here for the very first time. Loved everything about this place. The atmosphere, the music, the food, Chef Badrul and I assume there is another Chef as well, nailed it on the food we ordered, especially since we asked for the pasta to be spicy. And the portions are really generous. Thank you Chef Badrul and Chef.

Shahe was very welcoming from the start, made sure we had all we needed, and giving us chili flakes without any request, was a really nice touch. Also, he saw us taking selfies and offered to help us take photos. Really great service here. Also, as a bartender, the drink, PassionFruit Mango Earl Grey Tea, was off the roof! Thank you Shahe.

Joyce took over, and we ordered a little too much and she was really nice to help pack our food, even suggested separating certain things. Checking on all our food and overall dining experience. Thank you Joyce.

I love the attentiveness of the team. Overall really satisfied. I would definitely come back.

Dropped by here for a quick lunch on a weekdays. Still have seats but I still highly recommend to reserve the seats via Google. The food portion is great and we literally can share one course! The weekday lunch is affordable. The roasted garlic is chef kiss. Thank you for this amazing place!

Food was absolutely delicious and flavourful. The seafood aglio olio and Turkey bacon chicken ham pasta was delicious.

Their iced teas were amazing as well with a unique touch and ingredients to it.

The ambience and overall atmosphere of the restaurant was very good and lively. Staffs were very friendly and attentive to our needs.

Overall a very good experience and highly recommended place for family and friends

This place is worth the hype! Found them through TikTok while searching for a place to celebrate my friend's birthday! The staff are really nice! And were really cute playing the birthday song and providing candles and lighter for the cake!

I recommend their carbonara turkey bacon and tomato vegan pasta! Both dishes surprised me and my friend with their quality!! Not too heavy on the cream for the carbonara, and for the vegan pasta was one of the best vegan version I've tried. For drinks, their premium drinks are really nice especially the strawberry coconut, it has such a smooth feeling and the coconuts really complements the strawberry Earl grey!!

We'll be coming back!!

This is a very pretty cafe near the Bishan Sports Complex. The exterior is decorated with a mini camping feel and the interior is resort-like. You can sit on the camping chairs while waiting for that cup of coffee or food.

Food wise, portions are good for the price paid. We ordered the
. Lamb Chops $24.90
. King Prawns with Tom Yum Pasta $20.90
. Fried Chicken & Waffle $16.90
. Prime Cheese Burger$16.90
. House Salad $9.90
.
The Fried Chicken & Waffle and Tom Yum Pasta were our favourites, especially the pasta. It was a little spicy but the prawns were huge and fresh. The cheeseburger and lamb chops were ok.

The kids ordered the Rainbow Paradise and Salted Caramel milkshake at $9.90 each. They came with a huge scoop of ice cream on top. It is fillling and sweet. I think it is a better idea to share.

This place exudes relaxing vibes so its a good place to chill out over a casual meal.

Weekday Lunch Sets are available too.

quiet cozy and big library, 4 lvls and a cafe downstairs. Theres a programme rm to study in, which i did, or for u to browse a huge variety of books! borrowed a couple books and had a drink at the cafe. overall it was amazing, plenty of lounge chairs too, if you want a cozy spot to read or work if you cant find a table in the programme room! Librarians were friendly and helped me when i couldn’t locate the toilet! Love this, by far my fav library!!! 🫶

Great selection of books.

There's a 40-50 year old balding guy with a bright shoe and a bright watch who will go around harassing boys. Be aware!

I love this library a lot.
And I love its clean toilets even more.
Hehehe. Book selection is quite good, better than some libraries out there.

I went to find children books and return books. I was looking for return station but didn't notice it was outside cause it was small sign there. The children books section is at basement 1. I went to level 2 cause there is on pathway going to level 2 then took the elevator back to B1.

Very clean, tidy and has many reading space. Quiet and absolutely comfortable to spend some times for reading. From bishan mrt exit A, go through to the junction then we can see library on the right side of traffic  area. Beautiful ❤

Clean, new, and convenient MRT train station. It's a great addition to the Upper Thomson neighborhood, especially the many eateries, shopping mall, residential estate, and church. You'll just need to be a little mindful of the exit that you're heading towards, lest you get lost.

🟫 Upper Thomson MRT Station/  (TEL) line
___________________________________
🟦 This is a clean, spacious and modern MRT station on the Thomson–East Coast Line.➡️  This was my first visit and I was impressed with how well it connects to the surrounding areas.

🟨 There are 5 Exit points at Upper Thomson MRT station.

🟧 The station exits ( Exit 4) opens up to the charming row of old shophouses along Upper Thomson Road, which are now filled with cafes, dessert spots &  small shops — perfect for exploring.

🔴 Exit 1 ➡️ leads directly into Thomson Plaza ( popular mall), giving commuters sheltered & convenient access to the mall without needing to cross busy roads.

🟩 The station is brightly lit and easy to navigate, making it a pleasant stop for both locals and visitors.
********************************

🟧 Travel Tips:
*******************
🟧  This station sits between Bright Hill and Caldecott stations on the TEL, making it an easy link to the Circle Line at Caldecott Station.

🟧  Several bus services stop nearby along Upper Thomson Road, so it’s convenient to transfer between bus and train.

This MRT Station is TE8 along the brown Thomson East Coast Line (TEL) 🚆🟫 Quite a new and lovely station that is really clean, with excellent infrastructure all around. Plenty of escalators and lifts for those that need this too. Multiple exits with clear directions, with friendly staff to assist if needed. Love that this line connects to the East. Lots of eateries around this station too!
The newly built subway. I bring my little “subway fan” to experience it. The facilities are very complete, bathroom is very clean. Just as good as the other modern MRT station around the island.

The ladies toilet is very dusty. Every time I  enter the toilet, the cubicle door side or top of the door , toilet holder and full areas  are full of dust. I am wondering whether is the Mrt do not have cleaner to clean the toilet ? It is not smelly though.

We would like to express our heartfelt appreciation to the entire team at Mindchamps Bishan for organizing such a memorable concert experience for our child in class K1P 2025.

We could truly see the dedication and hard work the teachers have put in to guide and encourage the children, and to create this valuable exposure for the kids.

A special shout-out to Jiang Laoshi and Ms Sharmila, who have devoted so much time and patience to guide the children, and most importantly, to ensure the kids enjoy the process.

Thank you for creating such wonderful memories for the children. We are truly grateful to be part of this community.

My K2 son has graduated!
The graduation concert was a blast! It was well choreographed and the teachers put in a lot of effort to train the students well to perform their lines. I am genuinely impressed how the students could remember their lines, actions and the sequence in which to do so with their friends, for a 3 hours concert no less. It is very much different from other school's ceremony and very impressive.
Thank you to all the staff of Mindchamps Bishan for the hard work and dedication to make it such an enjoyable concert for the whole family.

We attended the Family Day Extravaganza organized by Mindchamps Bishan. Although the weather was scorching hot, it did not deter us from having loads of fun. A lot of effort have been put up by the teachers to organize amazing race, game booths, snacks and ice pop bars etc. The kids had a fantastic evening playing and mingling with their classmates as well as watching the fabulous fireworks at the backdrop at an exclusive private area around silver garden.

The teachers and school staff put in a lot of effort to make each event meaningful and enjoyable to the champs and parents.  Thanks Mindchamps Bishan and the entire team letting us enjoy a special moment of togetherness in school with the champs.

MindChamps @ Bishan has been a wonderful part of our family’s journey. Both of my children have been attending classes there over the past few years, and we’ve been consistently impressed with the experience. We love the genuine care, warmth, and dedication shown by the teachers. They create such a positive, nurturing space where our children feel safe, supported, and genuinely excited to attend school every day.

We’ve seen our kids grow and develop in so many ways, and that speaks volumes about the encouragement and individual attention they’ve received here. They’ve become more confident, curious, and socially engaged, and they’ve truly developed a love for learning that we believe will stay with them for years to come.

We also really appreciate the school’s consistent communication. From regular updates to detailed progress reports, we always feel informed and reassured. The photo updates we receive weekly via the school app are one of the highlights of our week—they give us a heartwarming glimpse into our children’s daily activities and interactions.

We’re truly grateful to the amazing team at MindChamps @ Bishan, and would wholeheartedly recommend them to any parent seeking a nurturing, engaging, and high-quality early childhood education.

Timbre+ One North is a great dining destination in Singapore's One North district, offering a vibrant atmosphere that appeals to a wide range of visitors. Whether you are meeting colleagues after work, catching up with friends, hosting a casual business discussion, or simply looking for a good meal, it provides a welcoming and comfortable setting.

One of its biggest strengths is the variety of food available. With options ranging from Indian and Chinese cuisine to continental dishes and local favourites, there is something to suit almost every taste and preference. This makes it an excellent choice for groups with diverse food interests.

The venue is clean, well organized, and efficiently managed, creating a pleasant dining experience even during busy periods. The lively yet relaxed environment makes it suitable for both working lunches and social gatherings.

Good food, reasonable prices, and a wide selection of cuisines make Timbre+ One North a place that offers excellent value for money and is well worth a visit when in the area.

It’s like hawker food but abit pricy by $1-2

Variety is limited imo. Theres a Thailand, Malay, Chinese, Korean and some food trucks do burgers and fried chicken.

Food is decent but not exactly wow. The space is huge and many seating area, I’d assume it’ll be crowded during lunch hours from the offices nearby

Ambience is lit.. there is a live band too, but it’s pretty loud and hurts the ear. Good if you prefer not to hear each other in a conversation. A lot of alcohol people there, more to chill and enjoy the music.

Interesting vibes but not for everyone
Open air food court with live band on Friday evening. A little too noisy for me but can see the crowd were enjoying themselves much and with very good vibes. Highly recommended.
Nice hawker scene near one-north mrt. Good variety of food with some modern healthy options to cater to the younger working demographics. Interesting beer taps, an option for day drinking

Found this pancake eating place,  looks very nice, and usually pancake dish like this unable to find in HDB state, even restaurant don’t sell like this.  Will give a try next round when we re- visit this place.  The shop is just opp if Nanyang Poly main gate.

Mustafa Centre is an incredible shopping destination! I always find exactly what I need there, and the variety of items available is truly amazing. 😊 The atmosphere is lively, and it's a great place to explore. So many unique finds! 🛍️ Love it! ✨

A 24/7 Shopping Paradise with Great Prices! 🛒🇸🇬
Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5)
Mustafa Centre is one of the most famous shopping malls in Singapore, and for a good reason. It’s open 24/7, making it incredibly convenient for anyone to shop at any time of the day or night.
Why I recommend it:
• Best Prices: Almost all goods are sold at very reasonable and competitive prices.
• One-Stop Shop: From electronics and jewelry to groceries and souvenirs, you can find everything here. It’s the go-to spot for foreigners and migrant workers to buy gifts and essentials for their families back home before vacation.
Important Tip for Shoppers:
I highly recommend checking the expiry dates on all food and consumable items before you buy. Try to ensure they have at least one year of validity.
My Experience: I’ve had issues multiple times where I bought items in bulk, but since the expiry date was too close, I couldn't finish them in time. This is a big waste of money, so please double-check your basket before heading to the cashier!
#MustafaCentre #SingaporeShopping
#LittleIndia
#TravelTips
#BudgetShopping #SmartShopping

I have been to Singapore several times, but it was my first time going there.
I really regret that I didn't go there before.
There are many snacks, accessories, cosmetics and so on.
As a tourist, I could have reasonable souvenirs.
I will go there next time when I visit Singapore.

Mustafa Centre is one of the most unique shopping experiences in Singapore. Located in the heart of Little India, this massive multi-level store literally has everything — from electronics and perfumes to groceries, souvenirs, jewelry, clothing, and even travel essentials.
What makes it even more impressive is that it’s open 24 hours a day. Whether you need something late at night or just enjoy browsing without crowds during off-peak hours, it’s incredibly convenient.
The store can feel a bit overwhelming at first because it’s packed floor-to-ceiling with products and narrow aisles, but that’s part of its charm. Prices are competitive, and you can often find great deals, especially on chocolates, cosmetics, and electronics.
If you’re staying in the Little India area or nearby, it’s definitely worth a visit — even if just to see how massive and diverse the selection is. A true Singapore experience for shoppers.

Mammoth! You name it and you'll have it. It spans across the street and variety. Reasonable prices and lot of variety. They have every retail brand under the sky here. Electronics, apparels, grocery, fresh produce, jewellery what not. Hats off to the management. If you are looking for best prices in Singapore this is the place to go.

Great way to be able to come here by train easily from Sengkang to visit the temple. Train frequency a little long on weekends, unsure about weekdays. I am waiting around 7 to 8 mins for train intervals.

Your Staff, who can be clearly identified as from their SMRT uniforms, are ALWAYS SMOKING near the elevator around Sin Ming block 406. It is illegal to smoke under HDB void decks, entrance ways and covered shelters. When they do this, the smell lingers in the area for a very long time and also gets trapped in the nearby elevator. It paints an unsightly scene for SMRT Staff. Please respect the residents living around and train your staff to go to designated smoking zones to do their bad habits.

I think TE7 is about the best gift for residents staying around the station, the workers and staff at midview city, students from Ai Tong School as well as visitors  to the Bright Hill temple and Bishan Park.

Thank you.

Clean, quiet
Not super noisy
Idk why someone would recommend bc its just a travel point but yes its nice place
I am happy whit ddL maps

Saw a poster at the MRT station at Outram Park 🚇 and spent a few minutes reading it. It was very touching. Jia you, Junle!

Not sure if the story is real or not, but I hope to see more posters like this everywhere — stories about the elderly, parents, couples, and children. Stories that remind us of love, family, and life. ❤️

Caldecott MRT station is one of the calmer, cleaner stations I’ve used, and it’s honestly a nice change from the usual crowded ones. The place is bright, tidy, and easy to move around in. The layout is simple, the signs are clear, and the platforms feel spacious instead of cramped. Even during busier hours, it doesn’t get overwhelming.

One thing that really stands out is the staff. They’re friendly and actually helpful — not the kind who just point vaguely and carry on. If you’re unsure about directions or have a card issue, they take the time to explain things properly without rushing you. I’ve seen them step forward to assist people who look confused, which is something not every station does.

Escalators and lifts are usually in good condition, trains come regularly, and the whole station has a very calm, organised feel. It’s not flashy or loud, but everything runs smoothly, and the people working there make the experience even better.

Overall, Caldecott is a peaceful, well-maintained station with staff who genuinely care about doing their job well. Great spot for a stress-free commute.

🟧Caldecott Station ⬛️ Exit 4 (✅️ I always wait here for Feeder Bus 235 to bring me home to Toa Payoh East).

😁 As of early 2023, I've fully mastered knowing all the passageways, exits, entrances of the escalators etc of Caldecott Station. ✳️ I will not be lost again.

🟧 In the beginning, I was a tad confused when the Thomson-East Coast Line opened at Caldecott Station and more passageways, exits, etc ,all opened up. 🙏🏻✔️But now, I am okay. Not confused anymore.

At Outram Park MRT station, especially with my family. I were there with my parents to support my brother for his appointments at Singapore General Hospital (SGH) and the National Heart Centre Singapore (NHCS). It's a testament to how well the SGH Campus is integrated with the public transport network, making what could be a stressful day a little bit easier.

I loved going to Outram Park MRT Station (EW16) today because of how convenient it was. The two photo updates I shared perfectly illustrate why this station is so helpful. The "Exit Directory" is a lifesaver, clearly listing all the major institutions within the SGH Campus. I can see at a glance that both the National Heart Centre Singapore (NHCS) and Singapore General Hospital (SGH) are accessible via Exit 6 and Exit 7.

The second photo, the "Locality Map," reinforces this. It shows a detailed layout of the entire SGH Campus, making it easy to see the direct, sheltered walking paths from the MRT station to the various hospitals. It's a well-designed system that ensures visitors and patients can navigate the area with ease, even if they are unfamiliar with the layout.

With my brother's checkup at National Heart Centre Singapore (NHCS) today and his appointment at Singapore General Hospital (SGH) yesterday, the convenient access from Outram Park MRT must have made a big difference for my family. The clear signage and well-laid-out campus truly minimize the hassle of getting around, allowing my family to focus on what's most important: my brother's care.

In short, my experience was great because Outram Park MRT station and the SGH Campus are a perfect example of a well-planned and user-friendly system, where every detail is considered to make a potentially difficult journey as smooth as possible.

Conveniently located in Toa Payoh rise with a few bus connections (3 bus connections from Toa Payoh town) and a well connected mrt infrastructure! Travelling to different parts of the island via public transport, such as the various bus services, Circle Line and Thomson East Coast Line has been more convenient than ever!

This place has been around since we were young. And it is known to host many shops that sells the products for military, police and such.

Lets dive in!

Food:
There have been changes with some maintaining their business around. Most of the time, many would come for tulang or mamak dishes. We kinda like the western food here that is around recently.

Service:
Many shop owners will call you for their business, part of their attention seeking modus operandi. But dont worry bout them, as all seats in this area can be for anyone, thus no dedicated table for any booth.

Atmosphere:
Having been around here for years, we do feel the charm and miss the memories we had while younger here. There have been some works on the place and its way cleaner now compared to the past.

All in all, we definitely enjoy coming over, even for window shopping at the top floors. We applogise there are limited photos of the shops around as we were too hungry actually 🤭🤭

Besides the ever-popular claypot rice & Hokkien prawn mee (which had a long queue despite the odd hours), try out Dragon Curry’s Nan ru pork cutlet curry rice! Sweet curry with a lemongrass taste, thin pork cutlet but quite tender.

Wen Li Taiwanese’s Pig intestine mee sua set was tasty too, & comes with crispy salt & pepper chicken! For dessert, go for 75 Ah Balling’s Tang yuan with peanut soup / almond milk.

IG @pingguo301

There are actually three levels at Golden Mile Food Centre. Most will visit the 1st and 2nd level where many good and popular food stalls are located but little people visit the 3rd level where shops selling military merchandise are located. Do pay a visit while you’re there to see the many interesting merchandises on display.

Not able to fairly gage the food here because it has lots of food stalls I'd only tried the glutinous ball peanut soup that tasted just like my mom's home made peanut soup yum2. Generous portion of peanuts! And 6 glutinous balls or tang yuan made of 5 different flavors you can choose from at SGD3.50 wow! I ate 2 bowls and I came back again for another 2 bowls! I brought company hahaha~ 😋😋

A Sarawak friend i brought thinks the auntie made the 5 flavor glutinous balls herself because the thickness of the tangyuan were not even or same with other dessert stalls.

Personally I still love peanut and sesame flavors best. But I'm pleasantly surprised by green tea or matcha flavor. My friends love the yam flavor. I like red bean paste too but peanut and sesame are sweeter and crunchier with something to bite I needed more of those 2 flavors or I'll get a 3rd bowl. 🤣🤣

This Halal stall next to Ashes Burnnit, fried delicious Kway Teow Goreng. The noodle is abit moist if anyone like it fried this way. The prawn is cut in smaller size & fresh too. Portion is good for heavy lunch at S$4.50, however is a little chilly, so prepare a cold drink in hand.
I had try their Laska Johor before which is creamy & different from SG laksa.
Stall mainly served seafood dishes, No beef & mutton.

Jan’26, revisited the stall and managed to photo the stall name. Ordered the Mee Goreng also Kway Teow Goreng and she take time to fried it. The taste remain same as last eaten but this time less spicy. The Mee Goreng is slightly on a wet/moist dish that differs from traditional Indian mee goreng with potato.

Hidden away in the Rifle Range greenery, Temasek Club honestly feels like one of those underrated chill spots in Singapore. The whole place has a peaceful resort vibe with lots of nature surrounding it, making it great for family gatherings, weddings, casual dinners or even just escaping the city for awhile.

The atmosphere is probably the biggest selling point here. Many visitors mention the relaxing ambience, lush views and spacious environment that makes it feel less hectic than most clubs in Singapore. There is also a decent Cantonese restaurant within with very nice menu options.

One thing to note is accessibility. If you are driving, parking is manageable and convenient. But if you rely on public transport, getting there can feel slightly out of the way since the club sits deeper into Rifle Range Road.

On 6 March 2026, I had the privilege of visiting Temasek Club during a Taiwan–Singapore exchange meeting. The venue is beautiful and well maintained, creating a welcoming and refined atmosphere. The service was professional and attentive, and the food was excellent. It was a very pleasant and memorable experience.
Nice and clear place and the foods are damn good 👍

Great facilities that are well maintained and super clean, probably one of the cleanest clubs I have ever been to. Ample car park spaces available. Lots of function rooms with the food served of good quality. Helpful staff also. Quite far in from Bukit Timah Road so not the most accessible club public transport wise.

We visited SPIZE restaurant during weekend and we have 20 pax and did not have reservation. The staff was very helpful and served us with 4 tables. And told us order Asian food will serve faster. Think they know we all are hungry. The food was delicious and big potion.

we were so happy to find Sheng Shiong aupermarket where we xan come to grab ingredients for our dinner on the way home after spending an afternoon shopping in IKEA. this super is very well stocked withe fresh produce from fresh seafood, to pork, chickens and tofu, mushrooms, carrots, kai lanand Japanese leeks and roast pork. we got got all we needed here amd were so happy. it is definitely a great place to drop by when you need to buy ingredients for Asian cooking. good prices and customer service too. kudus to Sheng Shiong.

I am a regular customer at your Commonwealth outlet and frequently purchase the Penguin Durian Ice Cream.
I would like to share some feedback regarding the freezer display. Every time I buy the ice cream, I have difficulty pulling out the carton/box because there is a metal strip or bracket along the side that obstructs it. As a result, it takes quite a bit of effort to lift the box out, and I'm concerned that customers may accidentally damage the packaging or even injure their hands while trying to remove it.
Since this happens every time I visit, I hope you could look into whether the freezer rack or metal divider can be adjusted or modified to make it easier for customers to access the products.
I enjoy shopping at Sheng Siong and appreciate the wide range of products you carry, which is why I wanted to bring this to your attention. Thank you.

Spacious, well-organised and good pricing. This outlet is quite extensive and varied. There is a good selection of groceries and several interesting finds. It is linked to multi-storey carpark so rain is not an issue plus it’s 24 hrs! Only issue is no trolly return at the carpark so best is to drive down to the pick-up bay in front of Sheng Shiong (sheltered) to upload groceries.
Medium sized supermarket in the neighborhood. Good collection of items. All payment options available. Price is right too. Very near to CommonWealth MRT station
Big branch with lots of variety of items. Famous for its fresh meats and fish. My mom loves this store to cook all her awesome Peranakan dishes!

Lower Seletar Reservoir Park is such a peaceful and scenic spot in Singapore! 🌅 The waterfront views are beautiful, especially during sunrise and sunset, and the park is very well maintained. It’s perfect for evening strolls, jogging, cycling, or just relaxing by the water. The jetty and heritage boardwalk add a special charm, and the cool breeze from the reservoir makes it even more refreshing. A great place to unwind and enjoy nature — highly recommended! 🌿✨

10/10, would absolutely paddle back for this again. 🌅🚣‍♂️

The sunrise kayak + breakfast tour was such a peaceful, feel-good start to the day. Ali and Shiva were fantastic, friendly, funny, and genuinely informative. They made everything feel easy and welcoming, and were always ready to help while keeping the whole experience light and enjoyable.

It’s one of those rare activities that’s genuinely for everyone. Easy to pick up, surprisingly calming, and somehow makes you feel like you’ve got your life together before 9am. Paddle a little, enjoyed a lot, then reward yourself with breakfast by the water… what’s not to love?

Seas the day, eat your way through it, repeat. Try it!

The park is beautifully maintained, with a delightful variety of flowers that add color and charm to every corner. Each pathway feels inviting, filled with vibrant blooms and natural scents that make the walk feel almost therapeutic.

The gardens are especially peaceful—perfect for an evening stroll after a long day. Whether you want to unwind, enjoy nature, or simply spend some quiet time alone, this park offers the ideal atmosphere. It’s a serene escape from the noise of the city, and I’d highly recommend it to anyone looking for a calm and scenic place to relax.

A Serene Escape, Even in a Light Drizzle

The Equatorial showers in this heartland park is a gem, especially in light rain. I recently went for a walk as a gentle shower began, and it was a surprisingly perfect experience. The rain kept the crowds away, and the sound of drops on the canopy and the reservoir's surface was incredibly peaceful.

The best part was watching the water itself. Each raindrop created a cascade of tiny, expanding ripples across the entire reservoir. It was a mesmerizing and deeply calming effect, turning the whole scene into a living, moving work of art.

It’s a wonderful reminder that you don't need to travel far for a moment of tranquility. A short, refreshing walk here, even under a light rain, is a perfect way to reset.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Lower Seletar Reservoir Park is such a peaceful and scenic spot in Singapore! 🌅 The waterfront views are beautiful, especially during sunrise and sunset, and the park is very well maintained. It’s perfect for evening strolls, jogging, cycling, or just relaxing by the water. The jetty and heritage boardwalk add a special charm, and the cool breeze from the reservoir makes it even more refreshing. A great place to unwind and enjoy nature — highly recommended! 🌿✨

Ammar Ibn Nizamudin worked part time at NTUC grocery store, and his service was highly commendable. He consistently demonstrated professionalism, responsibility, and a positive attitude while attending to customers and handling his duties

I often go to NTUC Hillion, and NTUC has always been my go-to supermarket since childhood. However, I had a really shocking experience there recently.

On 1 November 2025, around 10.30am, there was this Chinese lady (I’m quite sure she’s a PR) handling and arranging the fishes. She spoke to customers in a very unfriendly and unhappy tone, and was talking loudly with another staff member, which made me quite uncomfortable.

When I handed her my prawns, she literally threw them onto the weighing machine. While waiting for my fish, she took her own sweet time cleaning, then disappeared for a bit before continuing — all while I was standing there waiting for about 10 minutes (and there were no other customers). Not a single “sorry” or “thank you.” Simply no basic courtesy at all. Literally she is like throwing the fishes and arranging them unhappily.

Honestly, I will never buy fish from NTUC Hillion again if this is the kind of attitude shown. I’ve also noticed that NTUC staff in general seem to be getting ruder lately — it wasn’t like this before. Too many so-called “foreign talents” with unpleasant attitudes. I do understand that everyone has their own personal issues which we may not know about, but when you’re at work, you’ve got to wear a different hat. That’s just part of the job when dealing with customers.

Do take a look at the other reviews too — many are about poor service. Such a shame. I’m an observant customer, and I can see the difference — Sheng Siong staff are definitely friendlier, happier at work, and more proactive. They even promote items sincerely without us asking — and because of that friendliness, we actually end up buying!

Attached is the lady in case you are curious.

Bought two packs of rice in store (by 榮记) S$4.95 + S$3.80.  Packings looked nice, portions normal, some bones 鸡肉和鸭肉很多骨，义烧蛮硬，chicken not tender compared to 榮记own store at 680143,  if compared to the store at 680016.... good to try for this first time only.   A little bit warm,  store did not provide heating when asked. Tried to make hot at home, chilli and sauces provided are good though.  Overall,  can only say, lacklustre.  Hope to see some improvements.   18/09/2023 Mon 1400 Singapore

This NTUC Fairprice is one of two NTUCs in the area - the other being at Bukit Panjang Plaza. However, this one here is 24/7 and is strategically located at an integrated transport hub and at an integrated development with residences above it. So, how could it not do well. While I do shop here fairly often, I am sometimes disappointed by its lack of stock. Otherwise, it is a convenient supermarket to visit.
Just wanna express my appreciation to Rasy for being always friendly, helpful and approachable during my visit to ntuc on last Sunday. Thank you!!

Watching Commonwealth MRT Station rise from the ground years ago is a memory etched in grit, defined by enduring endless clouds of construction dust and grinding gridlock along Commonwealth Avenue. It required immense patience from the neighborhood, but seeing that sleek, elevated station finally open felt like a hard-won victory for the community. It completely transformed daily commutes, seamlessly linking our quiet enclave directly to the rest of Singapore.
The landscape around this familiar transit landmark continues to evolve rapidly. The two primary schools and one secondary school that once framed the station—filling the morning air with the bright laughter and energy of generations of students—have now been demolished. Seeing those familiar buildings vanish feels deeply poignant, marking the bittersweet end of a cherished chapter in local history.
In their place, the land is being cleared to make way for high-rise public housing, injecting fresh life and new families into this mature estate. While it is tough to witness the nostalgic landmarks of our youth fade away, Commonwealth MRT Station stands firm as an anchor through it all, evolving from a disruptive construction site into the vital, reliable spine of a constantly renewing neighborhood.

nearly have to walk down 2 flights of stairs ! Lucky to meet the nice cleaner who stopped me and directed me to the other hidden invalid toilet at the other side of a  store entrance. The place is also covered by banking teller machines.
New stall Veggie stall at morning: mee hoon, brown rice + others veggie 🥕 for your selection.
The MRT station is split into 2 sides: One with a service counter, and the other with nothing. Had to walk to the other building to top up my ez-link card with cash.

Commonwealth MRT station has become my regular visit station. With Koufu food court nearby,  I would not hesitate to alight and walk into Koufu food court, just to spend my late afternoon snacking my delightful favorite snack of set B meal that comes with a french bun, two half boiled eggs and a cup of strong aroma coffee, really make my day happy!
Looking at the front scene, the opposite supermarket, with throngs of people moving in and out, stopping and choosing their choice of goods, how I wish to have a day, just to sit and laze around to immerse into this warm environment while enjoying my delicious snack.

We like this hawker because it is clean and airy. The few nasi lemak stalls always have long queues. The other stall that we like is the popular Mee Soto stall. Besides Mee Soto, we also order their mutton tendon soup which uses the same soup for Mee Soto. So if you are craving for their soup but don't want carbs, their mutton tendon soup is a good alternative. They are also very generous with the mutton tendons.

A small hawker centre compared to others   I've visited but there was still amble choice. Tho many of the stalls offer the same or similar foods. The tables were clean and the vendors were friendly and kind. I ordered chicken and rice and the vendor actually came over and offered me some of their lemon chicken which they were famous for as a freebie! The food was all affordable and delicious and the drinks were huge and refreshing. The toilets were clean too.

One of the smaller but popular hawker center, perhaps due to its location just across the road from the Botanical Garden. Aside from a few hawker centers in places like Little India, this is perhaps one with the highest concentration of Halal (Malay, Indian Moslem, with one or two Javanese) stalls in SG. There are also a few non-Halal stalls as well, such as the chain chicken rice and Hokkien mie.

Went there a few times during visits in the area for breakfast and lunch. The nasi lemak stalls seem to be always having long lines in front of them. Tried the Sutra (?) Nasi Lemak, the rice and otah were well-spiced and marinated, although the ikan bilis was somehow wasn't as crunchy as what I expected. The Indian rojak was pretty nice, with spicy peanut sauce.

The cleaners from Primech A&P are exceptional; throughout our time here for about 2 hours, they were cleaning non-stop, keeping the place amazing. Always energetically and with smiles on their faces. Made our eating experience wonderful. Will definitely be back
Chill, laid back food centre, with mulit-racial food stalls for everyone. Honoury mention goes to the ice kachang, perfect for the hot weather like SGP.

This is a nice club with adequate facilities such as a swimming pool, villas, chalets and BBQ pits. Open to the public. It has a very rustic charm and feels like the Chijmes building / Raffles hotel when you go in. Food options here come with a great view, great taste and a greater price. Still, I think it is definitely worth a visit if you are in the Changi village area.
Nice play for a staycation. Beautiful sunrise and a shallow 25m lap pool with jacuzzi features are the highlights of the stay.

Rustic, charming, and nostalgic. It has a peaceful, old-world feel that’s hard to find these days. The housekeeping staff were excellent! Friendly, polite, and genuinely helpful. Even the staff managing the parking area were courteous and pleasant, which really added to the overall experience.

Unfortunately, the front desk service did not match the quality of the place.

We were attended by a staff member named Serena. Her service was borderline acceptable, and there was a very concerning incident during our stay. While we had already rented the place and were in the middle of our private event, she brought external visitors into the unit to “view” the place for their upcoming event, mentioning they were her family or family friends. Regardless of who they were, this was highly unprofessional and inappropriate, as the space was already paid for and in use. Guests should not be subjected to unexpected interruptions during a private event, especially when she enter the rented compound with visitors on a buggy, which can reasonably be perceived as an intrusion into a paid, private space.

Another staff member, Wani, was even more disappointing. She was unreliable, abrupt, and lacked basic phone etiquette. Her manner of speaking came across as impatient and reactive rather than calm and service-oriented, which is not acceptable for a front desk role. Communication felt disorganised and dismissive, making interactions unnecessarily stressful.

It’s a shame, because the location and supporting staff are wonderful. However, the front desk team especially in guest-facing roles, clearly needs proper customer service training, particularly in professionalism, communication, and respect for guest privacy. These two individuals felt like a poor fit for an otherwise lovely and well-maintained property.

If not for the housekeeping team and supporting staff, I would have rated this stay three stars due to the poor front desk service.

I truly hope management takes this feedback seriously, as the place itself deserves much better representation.

Clean rooms, nice place. Need to book as soon as possible, as many would want to book it. BBQ grille was good - need to pay extra though.

Not the smoothest booking website.

Ground floor could use a larger space.

We stayed in the Family Suite Plus for three nights and were very disappointed after last night.
Other than housekeeping, there was almost no service. The only facility we used was the swimming pool for one time, and when we asked the front desk for information, we were told to search online ourselves.

On our last night, a group of guests downstairs had a loud party that went on all night. The soundproofing was extremely poor, and we could hear constant laughter, shouting, and whistling. We couldn’t sleep at all.

We tried contacting the front desk and security, but nobody answered. Despite the house rule stating no gatherings after 10:30 PM, nothing was done.

A sleepless night completely ruined our holiday. Very disappointing experience.

The airport is exceptionally clean, modern, and equipped with world-class facilities, making travel and transit very comfortable for international visitors. The arrival and departure processes are well organized, creating a smooth and efficient travel experience.

We were also impressed by the friendliness and helpfulness of the staff, who made travelers feel welcomed and comfortable throughout the journey.

This memorable trip became even more enjoyable with Aruna -Selalu Jalan Bersamaku, always providing smooth and meaningful travel experiences.

Thank you, Changi Airport, for the outstanding service and hospitality.

Very good option for affordable, safe and secured transport to/from Changi airport. It’s reliable and you’re protected from rain unlike taking buses where bus stops are lacking of covered shelter for rain. The down point is that the train network doesn’t connect well and you have to at least make 1 exchange.

Easiest way to travel from Changi airport to the city. You have to buy MRT card to use the metro. Price is 10 SGD where 5 SGD is the balance.
You can use credit card also but note that some visa cards sometimes decline. Better to bring 2 different credit cards.
Do try the Changi journey at 3rd floor of Jewel...games and high tech gadgets..also find out why Changi was selected and not Seletar, Kallang or Paya Lebar for Singapore's airport location.
One of the best experiences. Singapore is a beautiful place to go and one cannot miss exploring the airport. Every corner is beautifully designed and well planned.

One of the best national libraries I’ve seen, with a large number of books to rent. 24/7 book drop, a lot of magazines available to read, plenty of quiet, comfy and beautiful areas to read in, and that’s all just the basement level – there are 15 floors all with their own content. Floors 5, 10 and 15 have green areas if you’d like to be in a forest-like environment while reading/working/studying. If you’re nearby, surely a great place to visit!

Perhaps the largest public library in Singapore. Hard to find a librarian for assistance, and bookings for seats are required if you do not want to be asked to leave just because someone has booked the seats. A really stupid concept for those who would casually come into the library if they are in the area. Throughout the years, NLB has done nothing to enhance the library experience. What the public has been slapped with are rules and regulations which makes visiting the library a chore.

Came purely for the kids section of the library and it sure lived up to our expectations. The little one thoroughly enjoyed himself amidst the aquarium themed library and the endless literature around the area. Very thoughtfully, they also offer floor cushions where you can take and just find a spot to read and chill out on. The hit area with the deck chairs is the central of the kids section but was unfortunately pretty much always filled with people. Great place to spend the weekend letting the kids immerse themselves. Parking is also pretty reasonable at $1.20 per hour on Sundays.

The Central Public Library in Singapore is a vibrant space blending innovation, design, and functionality. Located at the National Library Building, it offers an extensive collection of books, digital resources, and themed spaces catering to all age groups. Highlights include the eco-friendly My Tree House, the world’s first green library for children, and its lush ambiance. The library’s modern facilities, cozy reading nooks, and seamless technology make it an ideal place for study or leisure. Regular workshops and events add to its community spirit. A hub of knowledge and creativity, it’s a must-visit for book lovers and lifelong learners.

Today, I attended Emma Memma's Performance at Drama Centre. I grew up watching Sesame Street and Hi-5, and of course, The Wiggles. So when I learned that Emma Memma, formerly of The Wiggles, was performing, I was really excited.

What I find especially meaningful is the way Emma Memma and Elvin Melvin connects through sign language. For that, kudos to them.

Some children can only sing and communicate through sign language — yet they’re often overlooked in theatre shows. That’s why Emma Memma’s performances stand out: she reminds us that inclusion matters, and every child deserves to shine. (Catch her in action on youtube.)

Drop by our libraries or check out the NLB online portal and mobile app! You’ll find a huge variety of e-books, e-magazines, audiobooks, and lots of other exciting features. Truly one of the best resources you can enjoy in Singapore 🙂

Have you seen the sky-bridged library, on the upper floors, rising 16 storeys high? Designed with floor-to-ceiling glass panels, it welcomes natural light into every corner, creating a warm, open atmosphere. Look out for the greenery that softens the sleek architecture, blending nature with structure to offer a calming, inviting space.

Changi Point Ferry Terminal serves as the charming, nostalgic gateway to Singapore’s rustic offshore escapes. Nestled in the quiet, scenic eastern corner of the mainland, this bustling maritime hub offers a wonderful breath of fresh air long before you even step onto a boat. The terminal itself is incredibly clean, well-organized, and efficient, instantly putting you in a relaxed frame of mind for the adventure ahead. It is famously known as the main jump-off point for the iconic bumboats heading to Pulau Ubin and the pristine coastal shores of Pengelih in Johor, Malaysia.
What makes this terminal truly special is its unique, old-school coastal charm. Watching the traditional wooden bumboats bob gently in the water creates an immediate sense of anticipation and wanderlust. The process here is refreshingly casual—you simply line up, wait for a boat to fill up with passengers, and embark on a breezy, scenic sea crossing.
Its location is also incredibly convenient. Right next door, you can explore the vibrant Changi Village Hawker Centre for a plate of legendary nasi lemak, or take a peaceful stroll along the beautiful Changi Boardwalk to catch a stunning sunset. It is the perfect place to kick-off a flawless weekend getaway.

Changi Point Ferry Terminal is a peaceful and well-organized gateway to Pulau Ubin and nearby islands. The terminal is clean, easy to access, and offers a smooth ferry experience with beautiful sea views.
A great starting point for nature lovers, cyclists, and families visiting Pulau Ubin. The surrounding area is calm and scenic, making the journey enjoyable even before boarding the ferry.
The ferry services are convenient, and the overall atmosphere is relaxing compared to the busy city environment.
Highly recommended for a day trip and outdoor adventure.

Changi Point Ferry Terminal provides a charming communal gateway to the island. The boat ride is smooth and scenic, though departures occur once twelve passengers board the well-worn vessel—a quaint ritual enhancing the journey. As you set off, panoramic views of the shoreline and distant hills unfold under a cooling sea breeze. The terminal itself is clean and functional, with clear signage and seating. Convenient amenities and authentic ambiance make a solid four-star experience.
My childhood playground in the 70s. From town take bus number 2 all the way here to catch a ferry to Ubin

The ferry to Palau Ubin Island is a simple pleasure in Singapore. While most attractions in Singapore are high tech, ultramodern and expensive, the ferry to Palau Ubin Island is a reminder of simpler times when Singapore was an area of fishing villages and rural lifestyles. I think this Singapore is more to my liking, to tell the truth.

Pro Tip
Upon arriving at the terminal, go downstairs to wait for the boats to depart. When enough people have gathered (around 10), a boat will head out. You won't have to wait too long.

The nearby hawker center is highly recommended for a reasonable dining alternative (dining options on the island tend to be limited and expensive)

My absolute go to mall for all-things sports and the typical outlet stores! Especially Nike, Puma, Adidas and Asics.

Changi City Point is one of those underrated mall spots that quietly delivers, especially if you are into sportswear and sneaker hunting.

Prices can be pretty attractive if you catch the right promos, especially for past season items. The Nike store usually has the widest selection and best deals, while Asics is great if you are looking for proper running shoes. Fila and Puma have some nice lifestyle pieces if you want something more casual and trendy.

Mall itself is spacious and not too crowded, which makes the whole shopping experience way more chill compared to town. Good spot to spend a couple of hours without feeling overwhelmed. There are also alot of good food options to be honest and also cafes to just sit down to chill after shopping for too long.

Very good shopping mall with lots of cafe and restaurant selection. All at affordable price. It's also filled with quality factory outlet for sporting products. Bought 1 crocs 2 nike 2 adidas and 2 sketcher shoes in one day with factory pricing discounts. What a steal.
Only issue is toilets need improve.

I would like to commend the mall management for their prompt response to customer feedback.

I shared feedback regarding the temperature in the mall 30 mins ago and I was pleasantly surprised that within a short period of time, the air conditioning was noticeably improved. I could already feel the mall becoming much cooler and more comfortable.

It is very encouraging to see a management team that genuinely listens to shoppers and takes action. This shows a strong commitment to providing a pleasant shopping experience for everyone.

Thank you to the management for being responsive and attentive to customer feedback. Great job and keep up the excellent work!

Recently, the mall has been noticeably warm and uncomfortable, especially during peak hours. The air conditioning does not seem sufficient, making the shopping experience less enjoyable for both shoppers and tenants.

What is particularly concerning is that the warmer environment appears to have attracted flies inside the mall, which is not something visitors would expect in a shopping centre. This affects the overall cleanliness, comfort, and customer experience.

I hope the management can look into this issue and consider lowering the air conditioning temperature or improving the cooling system. A cooler and more comfortable environment would greatly enhance the experience for shoppers and help maintain the mall’s standards.

A Unique Mall

On the outside This looks like another run of the mill mall. However, given its positioning - the mall serving the Changi “CBD” and residents living there but at the same time this isn’t exactly down town - you get a unique mix of high end stuff that is highly discounted. Some famous brands have their factory outlet in the mall.

I’ve always enjoyed coming to this mall because there are things that I would actually buy here because many things are discounted and definitely worth the buy.

This place also has numerous restaurants and coffee places when you need to take a break from the shopping.

Overall, a mall that has evolved excellently to serve the needs and wants of customers who go to this mall.

Changi City Point

5 Changi Business Park Central
1, Singapore 486038

I would like to provide some information about the CCP. If you're in the vicinity of CCP, the nearest MRT station is Expo. CCP is a shopping mall that offers a variety of shopping and dining options.

You will find numerous eateries and restaurants, as well as a grocery store, particularly NTUC FairPrice Xtra, which is excellent for purchasing daily necessities. The prices are reasonable, making it a great place to stock up on bulk items if you're driving.

For tourists visiting Singapore, there is a large hotel and service apartment called Dorset. Guests can conveniently check in here, as it is easily accessible from Changi Airport. The nearest MRT station is Tanah Merah, and tourists will need to take one more stop to reach Expo MRT Station.

With the train station just a few minutes from the hotel, guests can quickly travel to the airport and explore various attractions across Singapore, such as Bugis and Orchard, following their check-in.

Best regards,
Chris
17 Feb 2026
A much cleaner park than I remembered it was 20 years back. It’s really windy till I developed a running nose in the mid afternoon. Great place to chill as not so crowded like east coast park.
Quite nice place. Just the beach needs a bit of cleaning. Saw people fishing and wind surfing with sails. And even a bird catch fish. Not to mention airplanes taking off from Changi airport.
It's a great park, nice clean beach and plenty of shade to relax.. As it's close to the airport you will always see flights landing non-stop!

A nice place to be quiet, reflect, and be with loved ones, bustling with children and people cycling. There are also people fishing, selling things, everything is orderly and nice, not far from Changi Village when I was here, ill come back here in the future.
Lovely beach, gets deep fairly quickly, very popular  with wind surfers. There were no swimmers in the water during our visit but it was quite choppy. The water was body temperature  warm.

Love the wide variety of food and at most stalls you can really taste the years of experience and perfection of the dishes.

I enjoyed the chicken hor fun to the max. The chicken was crispy and seasoned perfectly and the noodles got to sitting the rich broth that was cooking for hours already. Loved every bite of it! Finished it off with an iced milo and it made my day after a long day of traveling.

It has the feeling of a classic Asian food court, but its not very well connected and you'd better get there by cab, if one is willing to take you. Not all shops are open everyday so it makes sense to check out before what you might wanna have

May 2026 — I revisited this market and food centre, and despite the passing years, it feels much the same. The only noticeable improvement is the larger, cleaner public toilet.
As the gateway to Pulau Tekong and Pulau Ubin, and with the bus terminal located right next to it, this is an important stop for visitors to shop and enjoy a meal before continuing their journey.
The atmosphere still carries a strong old kampong spirit, and many of the food stalls here are well known. I look forward to returning again soon.

Wanton mee was good. There was a very similar competitor right opposite with the same decoration and all. Maybe we had the right wanton mee! Chicken wing was not there. It was supposed to be a rather simple dish I supposed but it went so wrong.
I cant say much, only had 1 order of a very small serve of bad oyster egg, starch filled and very soggy. It's so horrible I'm appal this lousy shop is still running. Cost $6 throwing out $3.

Ah.... Changi Village Hawker Centre, the land of nasi lemak. Fullstop. Thats right, Changi Village Hawker Centre has nothing much going for it. This hawker centre has so many stalls serving the same thing that you will cringe(yes, looking at you nasi lemak). Problem is, the nasi lemak isn't even good, and no serious foodie will recommend a nasi lemak stall here to a foreign tourist. A nasi lemak chicken wing set here cost $5, which is more expensive than most places. Taste is seriously lacking compared to other places and some stall dont even have ikan bilis. The best nasi lemak i have tasted here is Mizzy, and they have a whooping google rating of 1.9 when I reviewed them. They now have a rating of 2.0(no need to thank me). I can't seriously recommend this place to anyone.

I am a Simei resident and working mother of a toddler attending KidsCampus on the 4th floor of Simei CC expressing my great frustration over this building’s seeming reluctance to activate both lifts on Mezzanine level (ground floor) in the morning before 9am. Even after they did, the access to 4th floor for one lift was disabled today.

Management of Simei CC, please be duly informed that the parents of KidsCampus children NEED access to both lifts between 8 - 9am on all week days. Most of us are working parents and this is the rush hour where we bring our child/children to school before rushing to work. Every second counts so please make sure both lifts work properly during
this timing. If you cannot fully understand the situation, I invite you to see and experience it for yourself by coming earlier to your workplace in the morning from 8am onwards.

It is already challenging enough being a parent in Singapore. We are constantly pressed for time, exhausted, things are expensive and we have limited options for childcare. Please help us out by making our days and life smoother by activating both lifts for our use.

This was extremely interesting and very informative on the different varieties of butterflies and moths. The guides were excellent in their dialogue, describing the various species and the measures being taken to ensure their survival.
The butterflies are free to fly around and will occasionally land on you, if you’re lucky.
This is well worth a visit.

OMG Butterfly garden is an absolute must as yes inside is humid, but absolutely worth every second as butterflies are just fluttering every corner. Keep your eyes everywhere from under a leaf, on a branch, on the fruit, or even on your shoulder because there's going to be one. Now it would be best to do this during daylight time, I was cutting it close to sunset so I'm not too sure how the 24hr work here, but you have to really just live in the moment here. Also, don't forget to walk up the stairs for a bit more of a higher view. I absolutely just can't believe how many species thrive here.

Flying to Shanghai this afternoon, and since I arrived at the airport early, I decided to explore around Terminal 3. That’s when I discovered the beautiful Changi Airport Butterfly Garden 🌿

Located at Terminal 3 Departure Level 2, this little garden honestly feels like a mini tropical paradise inside the airport. The moment I stepped in, butterflies were flying all around me 🦋✨

The space is filled with greenery, soft natural lighting, and a super relaxing atmosphere. It’s such a nice place to slow down before a flight instead of just sitting at the gate scrolling on your phone.

Definitely one of the most unique airport spots I’ve visited so far. If you’re flying through Singapore and have extra time before boarding, I highly recommend stopping by!

Amazing to have a butterfly garden in the midst of an airport, Changi has really solidified itself as a top airport hub by installing various attractions within the airport beside just shops and eatery places. Of course it's not as expansive as Gardens by the Bay at MBS, but nevertheless it's still impressive with very clean and great educational posters explaining the life cycle of a butterfly. You will encounter several species of butterflies. Definitely worth a visit during transit just make sure you don't miss your flight.

The idea of creating a natural environment for butterflies inside the airport is truly beautiful, and I appreciate the effort to bring nature closer to travelers. The garden looks peaceful and educational at first glance.

However, it feels very sad to see many butterflies lying dead on the floor and stuck in the safety net. When butterflies try to escape or fly freely, they get trapped in the net and eventually die. This was quite disturbing and took away from the overall experience.
While the intention is good, I sincerely hope the management reviews the safety design and takes better measures to protect the butterflies’ lives. A place meant to celebrate nature should ensure the well-being of these delicate creatures.

Upper Changi MRT Station is quiet, clean, and located near SUTD and residential areas. Spacious design, good connectivity on the Downtown Line, and convenient for students and residents. A calm, well-maintained station!

Quiet and empty MRT station, nothing inside.

A decent MRT station located along Changi Road. From this station you can take bus number 24 to Changi Airport or number 2 to Changi Beach or Changi Ferry Terminal to Pulau Ubin. This station is clean and not always crowded.

​⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
​Jewel Changi Airport is an absolutely breathtaking masterpiece and easily the most unique shopping destination I have ever visited! From the moment you step inside, you are transported into a futuristic, lush, tropical paradise right in the middle of the airport. It easily deserves a full five star rating.
​The variety of stores is phenomenal. Whether you are looking for high end luxury brands, exclusive local boutiques, or fantastic dining options, the shopping experience here is simply unmatched. It is a true shoppers dream with super great shops catering to absolutely every taste and style.
​However, the absolute highlight is the magnificent indoor waterfall. It is a monumental, awe inspiring sight that leaves you completely speechless. The atmosphere is elevated even further by the incredible live entertainment and talented performers who bring an extra layer of magic to the entire space. Watching the spectacular light shows alongside these captivating theatrical performances creates a truly unforgettable experience for all ages.
​Jewel is not just a mall; it is a world class attraction surrounded by thousands of beautiful plants. If you have a layover or are visiting Singapore, this is an absolute must see. Highly recommended for everyone seeking pure magical adventure!

I recently had the opportunity to visit Jewel at Changi Airport, and it is truly one of the most impressive airport destinations in the world. Located at Jewel Changi Airport, this place is far more than just a terminal connection — it’s an architectural and experiential masterpiece.
The highlight, of course, is the breathtaking HSBC Rain Vortex, the world’s tallest indoor waterfall. Seeing it cascade from the glass dome ceiling into the lush forest below feels surreal. The surrounding indoor gardens create a peaceful, almost magical atmosphere that makes you forget you're inside an airport.
Beyond the iconic waterfall, Jewel offers an incredible mix of high-end shopping, diverse dining options, and entertainment attractions. The Canopy Park upstairs is perfect for families and travelers who want to stretch their legs between flights. Everything is clean, modern, and extremely well-organized — which is exactly what you would expect from Singapore.
What stood out most to me was how Jewel transforms travel stress into an enjoyable experience. Instead of just waiting for a flight, you actually want to spend time exploring.
If you ever have a layover in Singapore, make sure to plan extra time just to experience Jewel. It’s not just an airport stop — it’s a destination in itself.

Jewel Changi isn't even just an airport anymore—it’s a whole destination! You can easily spend an entire day just hanging out and roaming around because there is so much to do. It honestly feels like a fun tourist spot rather than a place to catch a flight.
Getting through immigration was an absolute breeze. It was super smooth and fast, and the ground crew members are incredibly helpful and friendly if you ever need directions.
If you love shopping and eating, you'll be obsessed with the crazy variety of cool stores and restaurants here. It is also a massive win for families because there are tons of activities to keep the kids fully entertained. They will definitely not get bored!
The absolute best part? You have to check out the giant waterfall at night. The water and light show is jaw-dropping and totally mesmerizing. If you are traveling through Singapore, make sure to set aside a few hours to tour this place. It’s an amazing experience!

The Jewel Changi Airport is one of the most impressive airport attractions in the world and feels more like a luxury nature-themed destination than just an airport terminal. From the moment you enter, the glass dome structure and greenery create a calming and futuristic atmosphere that immediately stands out.

The main highlight is the Rain Vortex, the world’s tallest indoor waterfall. Watching the water cascade from the ceiling through the lush indoor forest is truly breathtaking, especially when the light and sound show begins in the evening. It’s a perfect spot for photos and relaxing while waiting for flights or simply visiting Singapore.

Inside, the Shiseido Forest Valley offers walking trails surrounded by plants, trees, and cooling indoor air. It feels peaceful and refreshing, especially compared to a busy airport environment. Families, couples, and solo travellers all enjoy walking through the multi-level gardens.

There are also plenty of shopping and dining options, ranging from affordable food courts to high-end restaurants and cafes. The design makes it easy to explore, with clear walkways and comfortable seating areas throughout.

For families, there are fun attractions like the Canopy Park, play areas, and interactive spaces that keep children engaged. The whole environment is very clean, safe, and well-organized.

Overall, Jewel Changi Airport is not just a transit hub but a destination on its own. It perfectly combines nature, architecture, shopping, and entertainment, making it one of the most memorable airport experiences in the world.

This MRT station is a shining example of modern technology and convenience! From the sleek architecture to the state-of-the-art ticketing systems, everything is designed for efficiency and comfort.

The platform is spotless, with clear digital displays showing train schedules and real-time updates. The station is also well-ventilated and features energy-efficient lighting, which adds to its futuristic vibe.

One of the best things is the seamless integration of facilities – plenty of escalators, elevators for accessibility, and even charging stations for electronic devices. The station also boasts great connectivity to nearby amenities like shopping malls and parks, making it super convenient for commuters.
