🏬 Suntec City – 4.5 stars

Suntec City is one of those malls in Singapore that feels huge but still easy to walk around. There’s a good mix of shopping, restaurants, cafés, and even office spaces, so it’s always lively but not overwhelming.

What I like is the variety — you can find everything from affordable eats to nicer dining spots, plus fashion, lifestyle stores, and specialty coffee places. It’s very convenient if you’re spending the whole day in the area.

The layout is quite spacious, and it connects well to nearby attractions and MRT, which makes it practical for both locals and tourists. It’s not the most luxurious mall in Singapore, but it’s reliable, comfortable, and has everything you need in one place.

Overall, a solid shopping and dining destination. Great for casual hangouts, meetings, or just walking around and exploring. ✨

A complex of Fountain of Wealth, Convention & Exhibition Centre, Shopping Mall & 5 Towers of Business Centres.

"Fountain of Wealth
(The heart of Suntec City)
Visit the heart of Suntec City - the stunning Fountain of Wealth, a symbol of wealth and life. Recognised in 1998 by the Guinness Book of World Records as the World's Largest Fountain, the bronze ring of the fountain is designed based on the Hindu Mandala (or "universe") and is a symbolic representation of the oneness in spirit and unity, representing the quality and harmony of all races and religions. Soak in the positive “qi” energy at the fountain by encircling the fountain clockwise three times, stretching out your right hand over the water, as you make a silent wish and let the wonders unfold."

​If you're looking for the most scenic library in Singapore, this is it. Located on the 3rd floor of VivoCity, library@harbourfront is a masterclass in modern, functional design.

The Singapore Recreation Club (SRC)
Review on 20/1/2026

The Singapore Recreation Club (SRC), founded in 1883 and set along the iconic Padang, is one of Singapore’s most historic social‑sports institutions. Its clubhouse offers a wide range of sports, recreation, dining, and event facilities that continue to serve a vibrant and diverse community.

The club’s most striking feature is its grand entrance, framed by two sweeping curved staircases. This graceful, ceremonial design creates a sense of ascending into a place of heritage and dignity. Against the backdrop of the Padang and the Civic District, the entrance stands out as a timeless and memorable architectural landmark.

Private club in central district with roofed warm and cold swimming pool, restaurant, bar with live band and cafe.
Cost environment for private gathering and easily accessible by MRT via City Hall station
Dinner with an old friend at the SRC'S Chinese Restaurant. Reminiscent of the old days in Singapore, with traditions alive and well. Very pleasant evening 😊😋

Our family had a wonderful visit to the Singapore Recreation Club (SRC) during their open house! The location right by the Padang is fantastic, and we were impressed that the facilities are truly top-notch. The pool looked inviting, and the entire club was clean and prestigious. It's clear why it's a desirable spot, but be aware that it is indeed for members only. We really enjoyed our temporary visit and appreciated seeing the high quality of the club.
Not a member but, their facility is quite complete. From dance, restaurant, chill drinking club, outside chill area, car parking, swimming pool, gym, to pickleball court

Raffles Place is the centre of the Financial District of Singapore, with many Banks, Insurance companies, and other financial-related firms has their offices located here. The district features some of the tallest buildings and landmarks of the country, and a clear view to this is when you look from Marina Bay Sand piers.

If you're going to Lau Pa Sat via MRT, this is the nearest station.

Raffles Place in Singapore is a captivating destination that effortlessly blends historical charm with contemporary vibrancy. Having explored this iconic neighborhood, it is with great pleasure that I award Raffles Place a well-deserved five-star rating.

The centerpiece of Raffles Place is the historic Raffles Place Park, named after Singapore's founding father, Sir Stamford Raffles. This lush green oasis provides a welcome respite amidst the bustling cityscape. The well-manicured lawns, tranquil ponds, and elegant statues create a serene ambiance, inviting visitors to unwind and appreciate the beauty of their surroundings.

The architectural marvels that line Raffles Place showcase the city's dynamic spirit and modernity. Skyscrapers tower above, housing global financial institutions and multinational corporations. The gleaming glass facades and innovative designs exemplify Singapore's status as a global financial hub. As I strolled through the streets, the juxtaposition of historic buildings and futuristic structures offered a unique visual tapestry, symbolizing the city's ability to blend tradition and progress seamlessly.

Raffles Place is not just a business district; it is a cultural and culinary haven. The neighborhood boasts a wide array of dining options that cater to every palate. From trendy cafes and fine dining establishments to local hawker centers, culinary delights await at every turn. Indulge in a gastronomic journey, savoring diverse cuisines from around the world or sampling delectable local dishes. The variety of dining choices ensures that every culinary craving is satisfied.

Shopaholics will find themselves in paradise at Raffles Place. The neighborhood is home to luxury boutiques, high-end malls, and unique specialty stores. From fashion and accessories to electronics and home decor, the retail offerings cater to a range of tastes and preferences. The experience of shopping in Raffles Place is elevated by the impeccable service and attention to detail that Singapore is renowned for.

Raffles Place is also a transportation hub, making it easily accessible from all corners of the city. The efficient MRT (Mass Rapid Transit) system connects Raffles Place to various parts of Singapore, ensuring convenient travel for both residents and visitors. The well-planned pedestrian walkways and underground passages further enhance accessibility, allowing for seamless exploration of the area.

Beyond its commercial prowess, Raffles Place holds historical significance. The district is steeped in colonial heritage, with notable landmarks such as the Old Parliament House and the Asian Civilisations Museum in close proximity. History enthusiasts will appreciate the opportunity to delve into Singapore's past, gaining insights into its cultural roots and evolution.

In conclusion, Raffles Place is a captivating neighborhood that offers a blend of history, modernity, and cultural experiences. Its stunning architecture, culinary delights, and accessibility make it a five-star destination. Whether you're a business traveler, a culture enthusiast, or simply seeking an unforgettable urban experience, Raffles Place in Singapore will leave an indelible impression, showcasing the city's remarkable ability to embrace progress while cherishing its heritage.

The best station on the MRT. Has something of a classical train station feel.

Make sure to check out the murals near the entrance showing the area in older times.

Gardens by the Bay is truly an amazing place and definitely a must-visit attraction in Singapore. The entire area is beautifully designed, extremely clean, and very well maintained, making it a wonderful experience for visitors of all ages.

The Flower Dome and Cloud Forest offer a unique opportunity to see plants and flowers from different parts of the world. During our visit, we also experienced the Tulip Feast, where we saw many beautiful varieties of tulips in full bloom. The colorful display was absolutely stunning and added a vibrant touch to the overall experience. The indoor waterfall inside the Cloud Forest is especially impressive and creates a refreshing atmosphere. The Supertree Grove is another major highlight, and the night light show there feels absolutely magical with the music and colorful lights.

The whole place is spacious and easy to explore, although there is a lot to walk around, so it can feel a little tiring after some time. Still, the beautiful views and attractions make the walking completely worth it.

There are many perfect photo spots throughout the gardens, especially during the evening when the night view looks stunning. Souvenir shops are also available if you would like to buy gifts or memories from your visit.

Overall, it’s a peaceful and beautiful place to relax, enjoy nature, and experience one of Singapore’s most iconic attractions. Highly recommended, especially for first-time visitors.

Gardens by the Bay is truly a highlight of Singapore and absolutely not to be missed. I’d highly recommend getting a combo ticket for the Cloud Forest and Flower Dome—plan for about 2-3 hours to cover both.

The Cloud Forest is spectacular, almost Jurassic Park-like in parts, and enjoyable for both kids and adults. There’s misting at specific times, so it’s worth checking those in advance. A great way to explore is to head to the top level first and then make your way down, stopping at the different exhibits along the way.
The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, showcasing flowers and plants from around the world, with plenty of photo-worthy corners throughout. There’s also a nice gift shop with garden and flower-themed souvenirs.

I also got a separate ticket for the OCBC Skyway and it was 100% worth it. I went up right at 7:45 PM during the light and sound show and got to watch it from the top—an incredible experience. You can stay up for about 15 minutes, and while there’s an elevator, lines to go can get long.

For food, the Jurassic-themed food court nearby is a good option for dinner. Overall, it felt like a well-spent ~$60 to explore all three places. I’d suggest going after 3 PM and spending the entire evening there to fully enjoy the whole area.

Gardens by the Bay is absolutely magical and one of my favorite spots in Singapore. The Supertree Grove is stunning, especially at night when the light and sound show begins—it feels like stepping into a fairy tale. I loved walking through the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest; both are beautifully maintained and full of unique plants and flowers from around the world. The pathways and gardens are perfect for leisurely strolls, and there are so many spots to take photos or just pause and enjoy the scenery. It’s peaceful, inspiring, and feels like a green oasis right in the middle of the city. Whether you’re a nature lover, a photography enthusiast, or just looking for a relaxing escape, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit.

During my recent visit to Singapore, one of the most memorable places I explored was Gardens by the Bay. This remarkable attraction is a perfect example of how nature, technology, and sustainability can come together to create something truly extraordinary.

From the moment I entered the gardens, I was impressed by the sheer scale and beauty of the place. The landscape is meticulously maintained, featuring a stunning variety of plants, flowers, and themed gardens from around the world. The iconic Supertree Grove is undoubtedly the highlight, with its futuristic structures creating a breathtaking skyline, especially during the evening when they are beautifully illuminated.

I also had the opportunity to visit the conservatories, where the climate-controlled environments showcase unique plant species from different regions. Walking through these attractions felt like traveling across multiple ecosystems in a single day. The attention to detail, cleanliness, and visitor-friendly facilities were outstanding.

What impressed me most was Singapore’s commitment to environmental sustainability, which is reflected throughout the entire garden. Despite being located in the heart of a modern city, the gardens provide a peaceful escape where visitors can relax, take photographs, and appreciate nature.

Whether you are traveling with family, friends, or exploring solo, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit destination. It offers a unique blend of natural beauty, innovation, and world-class design that leaves a lasting impression. My visit was truly unforgettable, and I would highly recommend it to anyone planning a trip to Singapore.

Gardens by the Bay is one of Singapore’s most impressive attractions and a place that keeps rewarding repeat visits. What started as a quick visit to the Supertrees has grown into an entire day of exploration, from the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest to the outdoor gardens, lakes, sculptures, and hidden corners scattered throughout the grounds

The Cloud Forest remains the highlight for me. Walking through the cool mist, towering ferns, orchids, and prehistoric-looking plants feels like stepping into another world. The elevated walkways offer fantastic perspectives, and the constantly changing displays make every visit feel fresh. The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, with seasonal floral exhibitions and meticulously maintained landscapes

Beyond the conservatories, there is so much to discover. The Supertrees are iconic, the outdoor gardens are peaceful and surprisingly diverse, and even simple walks around the lakes reveal interesting plants, wildlife, and photo opportunities. I particularly enjoyed wandering through areas that many visitors overlook, including the lily-covered ponds and the themed garden displays

Another pleasant surprise was discovering that the Conservatory Gift Shop leads into a complimentary Jurassic World Experience, which added an unexpected element of fun to the visit. Combined with convenient access via MRT, dining options such as Satay by the Bay, excellent facilities, and world-class landscaping, it is easy to spend an entire day here without getting bored

This station, Nicoll Highway CC5, is an underground station serving Circle line. It’s a very convenient stop to go across to Beach Road via the long overhead bridge and it is also a convenient stop to go Suntec City, Kampong Glam area and City Gate.

Wonderful station along the Circle Line, between Stadium and Promenade stations. Beach Road buildings are easily accessible via the overhead bridge that is connected. On the other side is easy access to the F1 race track too, which I used for the Singapore F1 Night Race this year. Really clean station too!
There are two ATMs inside Nicoll Highway MRT station, POSB and OCBC.

Nicoll Highway, is much better thought then its name, why because is both accessible for people in wheelchairs, also it is a place like mini or like a East Cost or West Cost beach, is so nice relaxing place to have a stress relive or just like to have some peace and quite for sometime....you should go and feel it

Nicoll Highway only collapsed once, and boy, did it make a bang! I must say, it provided the ultimate thrill ride for an otherwise uneventful commute. Five stars for the unexpected roller-coaster experience.
Bayfront MRT Station is the quintessential gateway to Singapore’s most iconic modern landmarks, functioning more as a polished underground lobby than a mere transit stop.

Bayfront MRT Station is the quintessential gateway to Singapore’s most iconic modern landmarks, functioning more as a polished underground lobby than a mere transit stop. As a cross-platform interchange between the Circle and Downtown Lines, it offers exceptional connectivity, allowing commuters to switch lines seamlessly without navigating complex tunnels.
​The station’s design prioritizes passenger flow and aesthetics. Its direct underground links to Marina Bay Sands, Gardens by the Bay, and the ArtScience Museum are a masterclass in urban planning, saving visitors from the sweltering tropical heat. The station interior is spacious, well-lit, and spotlessly clean, featuring the charming "When the Ships Come In" mural by Lee Wen, which adds a touch of local artistic heritage to the modern transit space.
​While the station can get incredibly crowded during weekends and peak tourist hours, the wide concourses manage the footfall well. Signage is abundant and clear, making wayfinding easy even for first-time visitors.
​Verdict: Bayfront is efficient, futuristic, and incredibly convenient. It is an essential, high-quality transit hub that perfectly complements the world-class attractions it serves.

First time, first day in Singapore. Receive assistance from 2 security guards (diections only), friendliness from a stranger, and great service from station personnel. What's not to like. These initial interactions helped my wife and me to fall in love with this city. What a super first impression! Then again I have spent years in NYC and, loved it, until this  Singapore visit. There is no comparison regarding cleanliness, bathroom availability, noise, temperature comfort, orderliness, efficiency,etc, etc.
Andy-in-Ormond, FL, USA

The MRT station near Gardens by the Bay. You can reach it through the Downtown Line (blue) or the Circle Line (yellow). I love the way they put maps at every intersection inside the station, it’s really helpful for first-timers like us. The signage is clear, and it also lists nearby locations, so you just need to find the name of the place and check which exit to take on the map.

The station area feels really lively, with lots of hobby communities like dance groups, anime fans, and there’s even a piano you can play! The distance to Gardens by the Bay is super close. There’s also a water refill station nearby!

Excellent MRT station, which is part of the very usable,  safe, clean, and easy -to- use MRT system in Singapore.
Added benefit is the wonderful dancing by local girls practicing and perfecting their dance routines  in front of the long mirrors in this MRT  tunnel.
Final take.....enjoy the MRT rides and the entertaining locals buying some great dance moves 👌 👏 .
Recommended.

Tucked within the vibrant heart of Tekka Food Centre, 蒸好味 Steamed Fish Cuisine is a quiet ode to the beauty of honest, well-executed cooking.

What sets this humble gem apart is its unwavering commitment to freshness — each fish is live-caught for the day, delivering a delicate sweetness that simply cannot be replicated. Gently steamed to perfection, the flesh remains tender and succulent, elevated only by a refined touch of ginger and scallion that enhances rather than overpowers. Every bite speaks of clarity, balance, and respect for the ingredient.

Equally noteworthy is their steamed egg — a silky, comforting masterpiece crafted from real eggs cracked fresh on the spot, not pre-mixed liquid substitutes. The result is a custard-like texture that is impossibly smooth, rich, and deeply satisfying.

In a setting as bustling as Tekka, this stall stands out not through extravagance, but through precision, sincerity, and an unwavering dedication to quality. A true hidden indulgence for those who appreciate the quiet luxury of simplicity done exceptionally well.

The food never disappoints—every bite hits the spot. While the service is decent, there’s still room to elevate the overall experience. If you want to enjoy it at its best, come before lunchtime… unless you don’t mind a long, hungry wait in line.
And once you’re seated, don’t hold back—order the duck webs, pig ears, and duck wings. Paired with an ice-cold beer, it’s the kind of combo that keeps you reaching for more.
One visit is all it takes—you’ll already be planning your next.

The first time I tried the food stalls here was on a Sunday afternoon. It was crowded and I got overwhelmed. I came back on a Wednesday and had tremendous service even though we barely spoke the same language.  Food was delicious. I found a clean spot to sit and eat and soak in the atmosphere.

Actually went there to try again the famous 545 whampoa prawn noodle. Saw this stall (several stall from 545). An old amah alone selling. Surprisingnya the food taste good. And worth the price for a bowl like this.

Did a market walk at Tekka Centre — a true slice of Singapore life! 🇸🇬

First built in 1915 as Kandang Kerbau Market, this Little India landmark has evolved into a vibrant multi-use hub:

Ground/Level 1 – bustling dry/wet market & food centre with fresh produce & multi-ethnic eats - spoilt for choice!

Level 2 – heritage shops with colourful textiles & hidden gems

B1 – convenient parking below

My Favorite stop: Alhajar Latifa (#01-116) — where curry spices are freshly blended just for you. Always greeted by cheerful & pleasant staff.

Will be back to explore more of Level 2 soon!😻

Staff is just ignoring you. Asked for a menu at the bar two times - the lady said “give me a minute” and never came back. And it’s not because they’re super busy - they just don’t care. This is simply disrespectful

Restaurant have several hit and miss cuisines but the most recent visit had me trying the fish and fries. It was really good and affordable. The fish was not overly battered and there were thick flakes of fish within that is light and fluffy!

Singapore Swimming Club has a great selection of Indian food and have always enjoyed the food, especially someone who is very discerning. Having said that, off late the quality of paneer dishes seems to be off. The paneer tikka is no longer soft, creamy and flavourful. I think they may have changed their supplier or switched to using frozen paneer. The management should look into it and bring back the original quality paneer.

Been coming here for years since I’ve been a kid. Always love how homely and family-friendly this place is. The eateries are fantastic, especially the cafe and Chinese restaurant. The club sports facilities are excellent, I’ve used the pool, tennis courts, bowling alley, and gym before. With the Katong Park station almost ready nearby, it will become even more accessible.

Went there for the second time as my aunt is their member. The first round tried their lunch buffet. Decent taste in many varieties. This round tried their fried hokkien mee. The taste was perfect, the portion was big. But wait for my local coffee for a long time. This really needs to improve

I alighted and later boarded here today (5 March 2026). Earlier, I alighted to visit the nextdoor via Exit A, Sim Lim Square. As I live in the Hillview area, this Downtown Line station is such a convenience to me to go to the Rochor area and Little India - that is, without having to change MRT line. I also noticed that this MRT Station is quite deep underground but getting to the ground level is facilitated by two sets of ascending (or descending) escalators. This MRT Station appears well maintained and clean.

🟦 Rochor MRT station
11 Rochor Canal Road
● Singapore 188505

🟦 Rochor MRT Station (✅️ DT13)
*************************************
🔵 This is my first visit to Rochor MRT Station. ● This underground Downtown Line station features a spacious concourse and 2 main exits ( ➡🟧  Exit A and Exit B).

🟦 Exit A leads you to Rochor Canal Road, near Sim Lim Square, Burlington Square, Fu Lu Shou Complex, Albert Complex, LASALLE College of the Arts, and nearby shops and hotels. ✔️ It’s the busier primary exit with escalators & llifts.

🟦 Exit B opens toward Sungei Road and nearby residential blocks, Masjid Abdul Gafoor, Rochor Centre, Sim Lim Tower, and the vicinity of Tekka Place/Indian Heritage Centre ( ➡️ 🟡 Bus stop “Bef Rochor Station Exit B”).

🟡 I took Bus 57 from Blk 27 Toa Payoh East and alighted at “Bef Rochor Station Exit B” ----which is a short walk to the station entrance.

🟢 Overall, this is a well-connected and convenient station with good access to shopping, cultural spots and buses.

➡️ 6 Feb 2026 / Friday afternoon
MRT Station nearby SIM LIM tower

It's pretty straightforward, if you're studying or working in the area. If you're looking to shop for electronics, you'd likely need to pass through this MRT station, as it brings you directly to Sim Lim Square. The station is relatively elderly-friendly too, as there's Bencoolen Street nearby.

This MRT station is within walking distance to many busy areas, situated just beside a few, namely Sim Lim Square to get electronics and devices, Laselle College of the Arts and opposite the Rochor stretch of malls, Tekka Place, Burlington Square Shopping Mall, Indian Heritage Centre. It is also near to Sim Lim Tower, NAFA campuses, OG Albert complex, Bencoolen mall and various hotels. The station’s internal is designed artistically. Overall nice and new appeal.

Easiest way to take bus to Woodlands checkpoint using Causeway link cross border bus. Only SGD 4.80.

There is also sbs transit and SJE bus operating from this terminal

Accessibility: 400 meter to Bugis MRT (EWL/Green Line/ DWL Blue Line) and also can access to Jalan Besar MRT (DWL/ Blue Line)
To get to JB from here is easy. If you want to sit in the bus, line up on the right side but if you don’t kind standing, line up on the left side.

Why is there nowhere to sit?
Now some days later after arriving at Kuala Lumpur’s beautiful bus terminal, the comparison to Singapore’s terminal is extreme. KL’s is like a modern airport terminal, Singapore’s Queen Street is more like a empty boot sale

Overall, the transportation itself was smooth and we had no issues with the ride. However, our experience at the station was quite disappointing due to the attitude of one of the staff members.
We were traveling with a young child and accidentally walked into the station area because we did not notice the signage, which was quite small and easy to miss. Instead of guiding us in a calm and helpful manner, an elderly staff member raised his voice at us in a way that felt unnecessary and unfriendly.
We completely understand that there are rules to follow, and we had no intention of going the wrong way. A polite explanation would have been much more appropriate, especially considering we were with a child.
We hope the staff can be reminded to communicate more kindly with visitors. A little patience and understanding can make a big difference in creating a welcoming environment for everyone.

The terminal itself is easy to find, but my experience with the security staff was very poor. While crossing a zebra crossing with no buses entering or exiting, a guard revved his engine at me. He then aggressively asked if I could speak English and if I was "blind," pointing to a sign. When I pointed out it was a zebra crossing, he shouted that the rule was only for bus drivers.

According to Singapore's Highway Code, pedestrians have the right of way on pedestrian crossings. The guard's aggressive and incorrect interpretation of the rules makes pedestrians feel unsafe. Management urgently needs to retrain its security team on basic traffic rules and respectful communication.

One of Singapore's most convenient MRT interchange stations. Clean, well-maintained, and easy to navigate with clear signage. The station provides smooth connections between multiple MRT lines, making travel across Singapore fast and convenient.
The facilities are excellent, with lifts, escalators, and barrier-free access for all commuters. Trains are frequent, and the station is generally well organized even during busy hours.
Highly recommended for both daily commuters and visitors exploring Singapore.

date of visit: 2025-11-21

2 charging points spotted at the purple/ north east line and quite dirty, no cleaning done.

the charging point further from the ticket office does not work, no electricity when plugged in the phone charger.
All people favorite place is outram park and special its hub and metro junction to reach anywhere easily all the location like blue, purple, red, brown line

Outram Park MRT Station is a major transport hub in Singapore, offering seamless connectivity between the East-West (EWL), North-East (NEL), and Thomson-East Coast Lines (TEL). The station is spacious, clean, and well-maintained, reflecting the efficiency of Singapore’s MRT system.

What stands out is the clear signage and accessibility features, including elevators and escalators, which make it easy for everyone to navigate. It’s also conveniently located near key spots like Singapore General Hospital and Chinatown, making it a great starting point for exploring the city.

During peak hours, it can get crowded, but the trains run frequently, so the wait is minimal. Overall, a fantastic and essential MRT station that enhances commuting across Singapore!

Thank you kindly to the many kind and helpful people at Outram mrt today 13/2/2026. I fainted and two bystanders came to assist me, ensuring I was ok and being looked after by staff before they went on their way. The crowd marshal lady at EW line reacted very quickly and called for help. Abdullah (sorry if I got your name wrong) and another lady staff made sure I recovered well and had safe plans to get medical help, constantly checking on me and offering milo and biscuits. May God bless you all for your kind actions today 🙏

One of my favourite AF in town. Split over 2 floors, this is the slightly troublesome part, so bring your keyfob with you at all times. 3rd floor is most of the machines and some half-racks and smiths. 4th floor has 6 full racks, powerlifting platform, dumbbell area with db up to 50kg and 5 benches, and full cable jungle setup. Unique machines like belt squat and combined high row/ chest press combined machine. There is a functional area on 5th floor with another cable stacks.

Overall well maintained and clean. They use a nice essential oil air freshener as well. Showers on the lower floor. I like to drop by for my workout when in town as they have almost everything you need 👍🏻

Well equipped gym with good crowd. Easy access in good location. Wash rooms x 2 for each gender, one on each level.  One point though, sign is for gym users (your customers) and the language is crude and unsuitable.

Simply a more restorative way - thank you for returning the weights for other users - would be more professional.
Highly recommend! The equipment is top-notch, and the entire three story facility was impeccably clean. The customer service is also fantastic, Ahmad was welcoming and super helpful.

Anytime Fitness Bugis is easily one of the best gyms in the area. The space is clean, well-maintained, and equipped with everything you need — from free weights and functional equipment to a solid range of machines and cardio options. The staff are friendly, helpful, and always make the environment welcoming, even during busy hours.

I especially love how spacious the gym feels despite its central location. It’s easy to find a spot to work out, and the overall vibe is motivating but not overwhelming. The members here are respectful, and the atmosphere feels safe and comfortable for all fitness levels.

The 24/7 access is a huge plus, and the location is super convenient, just a short walk from Bugis MRT. If you’re looking for a clean, accessible, and well-equipped gym in the heart of Bugis, this is definitely the place to go.

One of the best branches of AF I’ve visited. It’s clean, the equipment are all new and the gym is very spacious, spanning across 2 floors (3 and 4).

I especially appreciate the variety of equipment and number of racks available, compared to other branches.

The only issue is it’s good location, which can cause it to be very crowded most of the time.

Haven’t had any encounters with the staff yet but I assume they are nice as per AF standard.

Everything is amazingly well managed right from the entry to the exit. The environment inside feels like stepping into another world — cool, misty, and beautifully designed. The lush greenery, rare plants, and the stunning indoor waterfall create a magical atmosphere the moment you step in.
I would strongly recommend pre-booking your tickets through Klook to avoid wasting time buying tickets at the entrance. It makes the process much faster and smoother. If you're lucky, you might even get a good discount there, which is definitely a bonus.
The Jurassic Park–themed section was absolutely amazing and added an adventurous vibe to the visit. The dinosaurs looked so realistic, and kids absolutely loved it — they were thrilled throughout.
For me, this was truly a lifetime experience. I lived every bit of it and enjoyed each moment inside. A must-visit attraction in Singapore! 🌿🦖

I visited the Cloud Forest at Gardens by the Bay, and it was an amazing experience. The place is incredibly cool—literally and atmospherically—with a refreshing climate that makes it very comfortable to explore.

The variety of plants is impressive, featuring rare and exotic species from tropical highlands around the world. One of the highlights is the massive indoor waterfall, which creates a stunning visual and adds to the calming environment. The entire conservatory is beautifully designed, with elevated walkways that let you explore different levels and enjoy unique views.

It’s a large area, perfect for walking around at your own pace. Despite being a popular attraction, it still feels peaceful and relaxing, making it a great escape from the busy city.

Overall, the Cloud Forest is a must-visit in Singapore—perfect for nature lovers, photography enthusiasts, or anyone looking for a serene and unique experience.

We visited the Cloud Forest during the Jurassic World: The Experience, and it made the entire experience even more special and memorable.

From the moment you enter, you’re welcomed by the breathtaking indoor waterfall, which instantly sets the tone. The whole space feels like stepping into a misty rainforest, with cool air, lush greenery, and a peaceful yet dramatic atmosphere. We visited during the misting time, which added an extra layer of magic—the clouds and mist moving through the space made everything feel almost unreal.

The Jurassic World exhibition was very well done and blended naturally into the environment. The dinosaur displays were impressive and fun to discover as you walked through the different levels. It added a sense of adventure, especially as you move along the elevated walkways and explore the dome from different heights.

There was a short wait to go up when we visited, but I can imagine it getting quite busy and the lines becoming longer during peak times. Still, once inside, it’s very easy to take your time and enjoy everything at your own pace.

Everything is beautifully maintained and thoughtfully designed, with plenty of unique photo spots throughout. Between the dramatic waterfall, the misty atmosphere, and the interactive exhibition, it’s a place that feels both relaxing and exciting at the same time.

I would definitely recommend visiting, especially when special exhibitions like Jurassic World are happening—it truly makes the experience even more immersive and unforgettable. 🦖🌿

The Cloud Forest dome is more impressive than the Flower Dome. The vertical garden and waterfall are incredibly tall. After exploring the ground floor, you can take the lift up to the very top, then walk your way down via the Cloud Walk. Even though it’s high up, the Cloud Walk doesn’t feel scary at all. Definitely worth a visit.

Even more impressive then I'd thought. This dome inside is awesome inspiring, so vast, with the plants reaching up the the top through the mist. The dome is cool and relaxing atmosphere, plenty to look around and loads of space, so it never feels busy. There are lots of paths, and then a lift up to the top - we were mid morning so no queues, but after we came back down there looked to be a wait. The view down from the top is also very impressive - including out of the dome towards the gardens and 'trees'.
The addition of the Jurassic world dinosaurs added to the whole experience, very well done and strangely fitted on perfectly.
Overall definitely worth a visit.

Tiong bahrh plaza is ok to me...I would not say I liked the plaza or loved it...has a few shops such as uniqlo, decathlon,miniso,kkv...dine in options such as McDonalds,fkc, long john silver's,4 fingers crispy chicken, toast box,kaya toast n other more dining food options...take away options such as stuffed,Dabba street, polar puffs n cakes,mr bean,Tori Q,wok hey,munchi pancakes...n lots more take away options...this plaza also has Watsons, guardian,the body shop,beauty language,Venus,spa,hair salon, Chinese medicine centre...tiong bahru plaza is not a shopping mall but a plaza therefore not much to shop around...

Your friendly neighborhood joint for the residents living in this area. Super convenient as it right at the MRT station.
You can find everything over here !!! Especially food ! High risk of over eating people ! Beware!
Love this mall. But hope management can do something about all these illegal bikes that drive up to the entrance of the mall which is very dangerous to all the elderly here.

Tiong Bahru Plaza is a great mall in the heart of Tiong Bahru.
It houses FairPrice Finest, Don Don Donki, Daiso, a Kopitiam food court, movie theatre and many other shops.
With plenty of food and beverage options, and easy access via MRT and nearby bus stops, it’s a convenient place that can satisfy all your daily needs.

We visit this coffee shop at Tiong Bahru. I love the chicken rice stall. A new western stall just opened; the pork chop dish was good. The dishes are reasonably priced. A variety of food to choose from. Can be noisy at times. And while you there, visit the fruit stall for they sell good sweet fruits. Enjoy 😋!
Malls and five-star hotels. There’s the Michelin-row of restaurants. Chinatown SG is the best area to stay. Clean and not crowded.

Very centralized. Food plentiful, hdb public housing nearby facing City skyline offices. Good mix of shops food merchandise massage parlors at People's park complex. MRT a walking distance to rest of the stations islandwide.

Chinatown MRT Station (NE4/DT19) is a vibrant and centrally located transit hub in Singapore, serving the North-East Line (NEL) and Downtown Line (DTL). Situated in the heart of Chinatown, it provides easy access to one of Singapore’s most culturally rich and historic neighborhoods.

The station features eye-catching design elements that reflect the heritage of Chinatown, with artwork and motifs inspired by traditional Chinese culture. Its proximity to iconic attractions like the Chinatown Heritage Centre, Buddha Tooth Relic Temple, and Sri Mariamman Temple makes it a popular stop for both tourists and locals.

Chinatown MRT is surrounded by a variety of dining, shopping, and entertainment options, including hawker stalls, souvenir shops, and boutique stores. It’s also well-connected, offering seamless transfers between the NEL and DTL, making it a convenient starting point for exploring other parts of Singapore.

Chinatown is so alive with lots of stuff from China.  Scarlett shop is specializing in Chinese snacks , when jiabao is a famous Chinese dumpling， steam bread place with authentic flavour opposite to OG department store.  Pu Mian Er Lai not only provide takeaway Chinese pastry but also provide dine in service with noodle, home made Chinese dishes.

A small, narrow and yet busy MRT station where Exit facing one of the popular dining steets in Chinatown.

The traffic flow is heavy, here is the hub of gathering and Chinese food. So be ready for non-rest rush.

We stayed nearby the Tanjong Pagar MRT station and it’s so convenient to go there the Changi airport from here. Just take the Pasir Ris line from Tanjong Pagar to Tanah Merah. From Tanah Merah you board another direct to Changi Airport which is the end stop. The experience was good and not too crowded. Everything was seamlessly connected.

I love how this MRT Station "opens" up at the basement level of the "very happening" Guoco Tower in the Central Business District. I also love how well connected this MRT Station is to the nearby Tanjong Pagar Plaza area where there is an abundance of very good and reasonably priced food at the hawker centre and eateries thereat. And to mention as well, a couple of nice hotels in the nearby vicinity.

Mansion7 is one of the best Chinese dining experiences I’ve had in Singapore—memorable food, exceptional value, and truly outstanding service.

Every dish was thoughtfully curated to suit our tastes, thanks to Alex, the restaurant manager, who has an incredible ability to understand his guests and recommend dishes we instantly fell in love with.

The abalone with crispy edges coated in salted egg yolk was a revelation—crispy and chewy at the same time. The prawns in mango sauce were bright, appetising, and beautifully balanced. The pork chop, served with tomato sauce and wine, was unbelievably tender. The Beijing Duck was a visual masterpiece, presented on a picturesque plate and expertly wrapped for us—no awkward DIY wrapping, just pure enjoyment. The shark’s fin soup was so flavourful! It had a generous portion of ingredients like mushrooms, fish maw and thick crab meat!

The finale was unforgettable: a durian puff with sesame crystal skin and Mao Shan Wang filling, dramatically revealed with dry ice. A true visual and sensory delight.

What truly touched me, beyond the food, was the service. Alex stayed attentive throughout the evening, respectfully giving us space during deep and meaningful conversations, and even stayed beyond closing time to ensure we were comfortable and happy.

This is white-glove service at its finest in the F&B industry. Mansion7 delivers not just a meal, but a complete, thoughtful dining experience. Highly recommended.

Fantastic MRT station sandwiched with all the Commercial Buildings & Hotels.
Many Night Life Activities here.

Great location of a train station, well connected to central business district, good food places across singapore, 🇸🇬 also historical places, old- school market (Tanjong Pagar plaza) and many fitness studios, dance studio, for the fitness community. Also a lot of tasty food options within the train station and just a short distance outside of it.

Bugis Street is one of the best places to visit in Singapore for shopping, street food, and local vibes. The area is always lively and full of energy, with many affordable fashion items, souvenirs, snacks, and accessories to explore. Really suitable for tourists or anyone who enjoys walking around and discovering unique shops.

There are also many food stalls and nearby cafés around Bugis, making it a great place to relax after shopping. The atmosphere becomes even more interesting at night with the bright lights and busy crowd. Convenient location, easy to access by MRT, and a nice place to experience the modern and local side of Singapore at the same time. Highly recommended for shopping lovers and first-time visitors to Singapore.

I absolutely love this traditional market. It sells all kinds of goods, modern and traditional items, food and drinks, souvenirs, and various trinkets.

There's also a large fruit stall outside. If you're staying at a nearby hotel and craving fruit, this is definitely an excellent choice, incredible value and extremely fresh.

The food options here are overwhelming. The best way is to eat while you walk, and you can definitely eat until you're full. After you're full, there are still all kinds of desserts and drinks to enjoy.

This place can easily rival traditional markets and night markets from around the world. It's highly recommended for a visit!

The street is always bustling with energy, filled with countless small shops and stalls offering trendy clothes, accessories, souvenirs, and much more at affordable prices. It’s a great place for budget shopping and picking up unique items.
Even if you’re not planning to shop a lot, just walking through the market is an experience in itself. There are also plenty of food stalls and drink options around, so you can enjoy local snacks and refreshing juices while exploring.
It can get quite crowded, especially in the evenings, so be prepared for that. But overall, it’s a must-visit place in Singapore for shopping lovers and anyone who enjoys vibrant street markets!

We went to check on the souvenir prices and among all souvenir places we’ve been, we think bugis street has the best prices already. Super affordable and not so much crowd. You can haggle a little and have some food and snacks that you can try around while shopping.

Bugis Street is a lively and bustling shopping spot that a lot of visitors recommend for a fun and budget-friendly experience. Many people love how wide and varied the shops are, with everything from affordable fashion and accessories to quirky souvenirs, making it a great place to explore and shop without spending too much.
The street food stalls and snack options add to the vibrant atmosphere — you can easily spend some time tasting local bites while browsing through the stalls. It does get crowded, especially in the evenings and on weekends, but that’s part of the energetic vibe here.
Overall, it’s a great place for casual shopping, good street food, and vibrant street life — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

Bugis Street ek bahut lively aur bustling shopping destination hai jise kaafi log budget-friendly shopping ke liye recommend karte hain. Yahan shops ka variety wide hai — saste fashion, accessories, souvenirs aur unique items milte hain, jis se shopping explore karna fun ho jata hai.
Street food stalls aur local snacks bhi yahan ka vibe aur enhance karte hain — shopping ke beech mein local bites try karna easy aur enjoyable hota hai. Thoda crowd hota hai, especially evenings aur weekends par, lekin yeh Bugis Street ka energetic charm hi hai.
Overall, casual shopping, good street food aur vibrant atmosphere ke liye ek badiya place — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

🔵 Downtown station ➖️  2 platforms here
_____________________________________________
⬛️ Downtown line to Bukit Panjang ( Platform A)
⬛️ Downtown line to Expo Station   (Platform B)

🟦 Downtown MRT Station 🔵 (Downtown Line)
____________________________________________
🟦 Downtown Station is an underground MRT station with 2 platforms going towards
▪︎ East ( Expo station) &
▪︎ North-West ( Bukit Panjang station)

🟡 The layout is straightforward & easy to navigate, with clear signboards and wide walkways.

🟡 There are 7 exits (▪︎A, B, C, D, E, F, G) here that conveniently connects to IOI Central Boulevard Towers, Marina Bay Financial Centre, Asia Square, Marina Bay Suites, Marina One Towers, The Sail, Lau Pa Sat, and the surrounding office buildings, making it accessible for those working in the CBD ( financial district) area.

🟠 Overall, it's an efficient and comfortable station to use.

⚫️ Dated 2 March, 2026 Monday / CNY 14th day

1. If you're transiting from Downtown MRT (DT17) to Shenton Way MRT (TE19), take Exit E. Once you're outside, walk in the opposite direction, and you'll see Asia Square – a clear sign you're heading the right way. There are plenty of signs along the route guiding you to Shenton Way MRT.

2. If you're transiting from Shenton Way MRT (TE19) to Downtown MRT (DT17), take any of these exits: 1, 4, or 5, and walk along Marina View. You'll reach Downtown MRT after a short walk. Exit 2 works too, but you'll need to cross the road.

I'm not sure if this is the most optimal route since it's my first day transiting from the Downtown Line to the Thomson-East Coast Line, but I hope this helps!

Connected to the Downtown Line. This station is located in the middle of the financial hub.

Marina Bay Link Mall is located beside the MRT station. Marina Bay Financial Centre, Asia Square Tower 1&2 and Singapore Exchange Limited are within walking distance from the MRT station. The station is crowded during the weekdays.

Good place n always happening area
Cheers n fun toast always crowded with lots of food choices

Descending into the depths of Downtown MRT station feels like entering a subterranean wonderland, with its impressive architecture and tasteful decorations. From here, I made my way to Lau Pa Sat, drawn by the tantalizing aroma of diverse cuisines awaiting me. While the journey was long, the reward of sampling delectable dishes at Lau Pa Sat made it all worthwhile, from fragrant laksa to succulent chicken rice. However, I encountered a setback when I suffered from food poisoning after dining at a particular store, emphasizing the importance of choosing dining establishments carefully.

In one of the busiest places in Singapore, stands a Community Club that has been around for 65yesrs.

Lets dive in!

Location:
As its name implies, it is in Tanjong Pagar. Coming to this place, one needs to note that it is on a 1 way road.. nestled between Pinnacle@Duxton and Yan Kit Playfield.

Parking:
Right in front of the Community Club is a small parking area where there are limited lots. Alternatively, there are some who parked in the housing estate nearby.

Upkeep:
The whole place looks very clean. The upkeep and maintenance of the whole place seems to well managed. From the outside, from someone who is not from arond the area, will definitely be impressed at the area as a whole.

All in all, this is one of the oldest Community Club in Singapore. We definitely will recommend you to come over as well and enjoy the visit to Yan Kit Playfield. A location rich with history lays beside the CC.

Cheers!

My friends and I booked and paid for the use of the badminton court for 2 sessions which is equivalent to 2 hours. We were told that we could not open any doors except the main entrance(which was already open). The badminton court has many doors, infact all around it. We only asked for a small portion of it for ventilation but they said no. Not recommended to book this facility, hardly any ventilation!

The facilities are clean, well-maintained, and easily accessible. I especially appreciate the effort they put into making events inclusive and engaging for people from all backgrounds. It’s clear they care deeply about the community they serve- from kids’ activities and tuition classes to senior programs. If only the rest of the cc can have the same level of recreation clubs that would be ideal.

Very nice neighbourhood with good restaurants, heritage shophouses. You can find birds of paradise icecream shop, Dentalis - 5 star rated dental clinic, Ma Maison french restaurant, Shake Shack and famous Kok Sen in the area

This residence is very convenient, with shops in the front and a garden in the back.
its in the middle of town area

First experience with the MRT in Singapore. Once we finally found the entrance it was quite easy to navigate. Many small restraunts in this Bugis station, even a 7-11 where I got a SLURPEE. Loved using the MRT. Very clean and safe.

Singapore’s MRT system is clean, efficient, and extremely reliable. Trains arrive frequently, stations are well maintained, and signage is clear, making it easy for both locals and tourists to navigate the city.

The network covers most parts of Singapore, with convenient connections between lines and seamless transfers to buses. Stations are generally equipped with lifts, escalators, and clear announcements, making travel accessible and comfortable.

Overall, the MRT is one of the best public transport systems in the world—fast, affordable, and stress-free. It’s the easiest way to get around Singapore.

Bugis MRT station is very different now it has becomes the interchange for the Downtown Line.

It also have many more facilities and better connected.

Crowded, but serves its purpose well.

One of the key public transport or mrt station in Singapore connecting travellers to bugis area having lots of malls and restaurants. It’s a happening place. The transport route linked this station to important hubs like the airport and the downtown, as well to many other tourist attraction areas. One thing I suggest to improve is to make the connectivity network more complete by having a covered pedestrian to the nearby queen street bus terminal. This will really make this mrt station get connected well with the bus networks including the crossborder routes.

Bugis MRT Station (DT14/EW12) Review

Located in the heart of Singapore’s vibrant Bugis district, Bugis MRT is a key interchange station on the Downtown (DTL) and East-West (EWL) Lines. It provides seamless connectivity to business hubs, shopping malls, and cultural landmarks like Bugis Junction, Bugis Street, and Kampong Glam.

The station is well-maintained, clean, and efficiently managed, with clear signages for easy navigation. Peak-hour congestion is common, especially at the interchange area, but crowd flow is generally smooth. Escalators, lifts, and tactile flooring ensure accessibility for all passengers, including the elderly and those with mobility needs.

Facilities include convenience stores, ATMs, and ticketing machines, while exits lead directly to major malls, office buildings, and bus stops for further connectivity. Overall, Bugis MRT is a modern, convenient, and strategic transit hub in central Singapore.

I had a great experience with my personal coaching training. My coach Henry had helped me improve my strength and build muscle, especially my shoulder strength, which has improved significantly. My coach provided personalized guidance, including home workout routines that made it easier to stay consistent.

The gym environment is also a big plus, it’s a boutique gym with more privacy compared to typical commercial gyms with memberships. If you’re looking for a private training environment and a dedicated trainer, I highly recommend looking for Henry at Revolt.

This is the first time I started using trainer as i take a plunge into my fitness journey and I couldnt have chosen an better person than Hans. He balances intensity and fun very well and is also mindful that he doesnt push me into full exhaustion during the session. The Gym is also very well managed (apart from odd days of no hot water), clean and i never had to wait for my turn to use any of the facilities/gym equipment.

Shasha is an outstanding trainer. After a long break from exercise, she helped me rebuild a consistent routine with perfect pacing and endless encouragement. Her focus on form and adaptability made me feel safe and motivated. I’ve regained my confidence and actually enjoy working out again! Highly recommend, especially if you're getting back into fitness.

I’ve had a great experience training at Revolt, and have already noticed my changes. My mindset toward exercise has improved significantly, and I’ve genuinely started to enjoy exercising.

My trainer Henry has created training plans tailored to my level /goals and has also provided helpful diet / nutrition advice. I’m very grateful for the guidance and encouragement, and would recommend Revolt and Henry to anyone looking to improve their fitness in a positive and sustainable way.

I’ve been training with Hans once a week over the past few months and what I appreciate most is his focus on proper form and technique, which has helped me train safely and effectively. He takes a realistic, sustainable approach to fitness and works with my individual goals rather than pushing unrealistic expectations.

I’ve visited Bugis Junction quite often with my girlfriend, and it’s always been a great place to spend time together.

What makes this spot special is the unique mix of a modern mall and a street-style shopping experience under a beautiful glass roof. It gives a nice open-air vibe while still being comfortable. There are plenty of shopping options, from affordable brands to mid-range stores, and a wide variety of food places to explore.

The location is super convenient, especially with direct MRT access and easy connection to Bugis Street, so there’s always something to do around. It’s a perfect place for casual dates, shopping, and just walking around enjoying the atmosphere.

It does get crowded at times, especially on weekends, but the overall vibe and energy of the place make up for it. Definitely one of our go-to spots in Singapore.

My family and I were here yesterday (29 DECEMBER 2025) for lunch at the "adjacent to this shopping mall" InterContinental Singapore hotel, and for some after-lunch dessert at the Miam Miam French Japanese cafe at level two of the shopping mall itself, and to catch some Christmas spirit - albeit, Christmas Day had already passed. I always love coming to this shopping mall during the daytime to especially walk at its ground level, along the wide corridors with the clear see-through roofing high above them - basking in the daylight and yet doing so in air-conditioned comfort. I also love, of course, the many shops and eateries at this shopping mall for their variety and for their quality.

This place has changed many times and I’m very unfamiliar with it already. Used to grow up here as my parents used to sell Hakka cuisine at where bugis+ used to be. (I think??) the fountain no water by the way!! Anyway I used to attend Pets Tseng first album release in Singapore here too!!

located near little india and rochor , bugis is a nice area to stay in, u can easily find halal food here.
Area is cleaner than little india or Geylang ,
food are quite affordable here and u can visit the famous bugis junction if u are in area

Bugis Junction is a wonderful place to shop its complete place for whatever you are looking for. I believe I bought a luggage here a high quality luggage at that. Clothes are many to choose from, souvenirs and many more. There is a food court adjacent to this place as well to rest your feet from shopping all day. One of the best places to shop and get bargains. While everything here is great and all please to not be misled at the tax free signs in shops. Many are not tax free. Shops actually need to be accredited by the airport for you to claim tax free rebates. Shop til your heart drop!

I want to send my appreciation to the MRT staffs on duty today. They were very helpful and supportive when an incident happened at 230pm and took action immediately to help. Really thankful to have people with such big hearts

Small but complete and convenient mrt station. Directly connected to some buses routes. Shops, restaurants, low cost accommodation, parks just across the street. Some restaurant are included directly at the mrt station. Clean and comfortable. Clear signage.
Very good MRT station. Not available at other stations but this one has the value checker and also the cute robot but it was resting 💤💤💤
Very clean. Good, they give some space to seat while you waiting. They have many informantion boards with 3 languages, so useful for foreigners. There is a timer for the up coming MRT too..

Kallang MRT (EW10) is one of those "old but gold" stations. Not as fancy as the new Downtown Line or Thomson-East Coast Line stations, but very functional and steady.

​The Good Things:
​Convenience: It's an elevated station, so the breeze is quite shiok when waiting for train. If you going to Stadium for concert or football, this is the "secret" alternative. Got one long sheltered walkway all the way to Sports Hub, no need to worry about rain or sun.

​Food & Amenities: Outside the station got many things. If hungry, just walk a bit to Geylang Lorong 1—plenty of good food like the famous beef kway teow or frog porridge. Inside the station also got Mr. Bean and Old Chang Kee for quick bite.

​Accessibility: Right next to the Lorong 1 Geylang Bus Terminal. Very easy to switch from MRT to bus if you heading to Upper Boon Keng or Geylang area.

​The "Can Improve" Part:
​Crowd: Wah, if got Jay Chou or Taylor Swift concert, please be prepared. The station will be super packed. Even though got the bridge, the queue to enter the gantries can be quite scary.
​Vibe: The station looks a bit "uncle" already lah, standard 80s/90s design. Everything clean but just looks slightly dated compared to the newer lines.

​Verdict: Overall, a very reliable station. Clean, well-maintained, and connects you to some of the best food spots in SG. Just avoid the peak hour "concert crowd" can already.

result.name: Geylang Serai Market and Food Centre

reviews.text: fantastic family outing on a Sunday morning; bustling atmosphere, and authentic local energy. The upstairs food court is a standout, serving a wide variety of delicious dishes. Added benefit of clean, well-maintained facilities.

It has a great collection of Malaysian and halal foods with some of the stalls being frequently mentioned in my food research as some of the best. There's also a market on the ground floor and what looked like some clothing shops next to the hawker center on the second floor.
It's also a cool hawker center to check out because it wasn't touristy at all. I was the only obvious tourist I saw there but I got there about an hour before it closed so maybe it's different earlier in the day, I dunno.
One of the oldest markets in Singapore. Now, very nicely renovated. It has two sections: one is the agri-food market, the other is a hawker center where you can eat cheaply and well.

One of the most authentic wet markets and hawker centres, you can get the most variety of fresh vegetables and spices available. And prices are one of the most competitive and reasonable options available. Check out their 2nd level food selection and also sundries and clothing section for the traditional and trending fashion of the Malay racial diversity.
Favorite place to eat on the weekdays except Monday because its closed for cleaning. I wish I can moved to the flats around here . Sinar pagi & Gerai Obek Nenek. 🫰🫰🫰🫰🫰

The nature side of this township, walking distance from the mrt. Good place to relax for people in this area. Nothing extraordinary but the big trees are giving good shades plus filtering the air around. Worth a small walk if you’re at this area.

Kallang Riverside Park is quiet, open, and comforting in a way that feels unforced. The long walking paths beside the river, the soft breeze, and the wide sky create a calm space where everything slows down. It’s not flashy or crowded—just peaceful, steady, and grounding.
This place feels perfect for solo walks, clearing your head, or listening to music while watching the water move. At sunset, the sky turns gentle and warm; at night, the lights reflect softly on the river, making it feel intimate and reflective. It’s the kind of place you go to when you don’t need noise—just space.

I came here because there were some people said that we could see the otters running around this area and I also saw the sign that otter might cross by the pedestrian way. However, the lucky is not mine that day as I didn't see it coming, but I saw a turtle swimming under the bridge and there were groups of fish swam in the middle of the river. Really enjoy the long walk around this riverside park.

Kallang Riverside Park is a peaceful and scenic riverside park that offers a great escape from the busy city atmosphere of Singapore. The park is well known for its beautiful waterfront views, clean environment, and relaxing walking and cycling paths along the Kallang River.

It is a popular spot for jogging, cycling, photography, and evening walks, especially during sunset when the skyline views become very impressive. The park is also connected to Singapore’s Park Connector Network, making it convenient for long-distance cycling or casual rides around the Marina Bay area.

Visitors can enjoy the calm atmosphere, fresh air, and open spaces while watching boats pass by the river. The location is suitable for families, couples, solo travelers, and fitness enthusiasts. There are also seating areas and resting spots that make it comfortable for relaxing after exercise or sightseeing.

The park becomes especially beautiful at night with the surrounding city lights reflecting on the water, creating a very peaceful urban scenery. Overall, Kallang Riverside Park is highly recommended for anyone looking for a relaxing outdoor experience, cycling route, or scenic riverside walk in Singapore.
Best place to cycle which connects to Marina Bay sands, Evening time is soothing..  Best time to cycle is on weekend evening..

MacPherson MRT is very convenient and nice station. I use it almost everyday and never had any problem. It’s connected to both Circle Line and Downtown Line so changing train is fast and not confusing. Signs are easy to follow.

Station is clean and got lift and escalator, so good for old people or if carry heavy bag. Around the station got many shops and food places also, very useful. Overall it’s a good station and I like it very much.

I liked the colour blocks theme in Downtown line.

They are bright and looking great and brings rich ambience to the station.

Rest of the facilities are like any other interchange, but no covered walk way to bus stops. Especially exit B bus stop.

Passed by here for interchanging. The MRT station is immaculate, as expected. Toilets have also been at the same clean standards.

Not really a lot of people, and it was very accessible to other MRT lines from here, as well as the paths to interchanging. Tranquil atmosphere too! Wonderful MRT.
Great infrastructure! I seldom use blue line but it's definitely a good route from bukit panjang all the way to Sungei bedok.

I came to MacPherson MRT because there are 4 temples located at Arumugam exit. After praying, I went to the mrt station to exit another side to buy 4D and Toto. Nearby has a nice Coffeeshop to drink coffee and eat your meals😊
Greatly appreciate the manpower deployed to assist commuters regarding the tunnel strengthening works.  very helpful mrt staffs stationed along the cc n ew line to provide direction. TYVM
Though the interruption is chaotic at times, we need to bear it as I feel that once when tunnelling work completes in April 2026, the MRT service would be gradually more seamless.
The linkage between plq mall and paya lebar square. An inter change that links to many places. Crowed on weekend
Grab & Go traditional Italian bakery treats and coffee from 7 am

Less populated place and area with very good understanding people around. As you come out you get to have food in subway and other hotels. You have giant supermarket near by. Lift access available. Less crowded place.

There is a drop off / pickup / taxi point, very convenient for drivers to pick up passengers. The bus stop is a short distance away, so will not caused too much hassles when you need to wait a little while. There are space for about 4- 5 waiting  vehicles, but I think it is meant for taxi. This road is not too busy mostly, great for passenger pickup without worrying about lack of waiting space. I give this a 5 star.
Big shoutout to the staff of Ubi mrt. My daughter ran into a small mishap and they helped her. Thank you Lili and Firdaus!
Ubi MRT station is surrounded by HDB housing estates, shops, factories and offices. During certain hours, it can be crowded with commuters. The place is clean.

Went there to buy my medical supplies from Fu Kang Healthcare Pte Ltd but it was so difficult to locate the destination.  Confusing signage. Tried to ask around but no one seemed to know. I'd a tough time searching and finally met a kind lady who was able to show me the correct direction with the help of Google map. At first, I don't really know how to use the Google map but after this round, I have learnt and appreciated this technology.
The toilets in the Ubi MRT station are very clean. Thanks to the cleaners. Station is big and quiet perhaps I go there during non peak timing.

I was at this MRT Station today (21 NOVEMBER 2025) alighting thereat to go to a lunch venue sort of nearby at Aljunied Avenue 2, and embarking on my journey here afterwards to go home about three hours later. I was very impressed with how strategically located this MRT Station was - near to so many good eating places in Geylang / Aljunied. And there were even some good food options at the MRT Station itself - in particular, a Muslim eatery thereat.

​This is a nice MRT station, conveniently located in the heart of all the hustle and bustle. Everything inside is clean and properly systemized, making for a great travel experience. The one thing to note is that the neighboring area can get a little messier, which is expected given Aljunied's industrial nature.

Aljunied MRT Station (EW9) is a convenient stop on Singapore’s East-West Line, offering easy access to both residential and commercial areas. The station is clean, well-maintained, and efficiently managed, like most MRT stations in Singapore. Its location makes it a gateway to nearby attractions, local eateries, and budget accommodations.

The surrounding area is vibrant, with a mix of traditional shops and modern amenities, providing a glimpse into local life. Connectivity is excellent, with frequent train services that make it easy to travel across the city. Overall, Aljunied MRT is a reliable and practical transit hub for both locals and visitors.

Bustling MRT station in one of the most interesting district of Singapore, Geylang. The station is very spacious with car parks, bike park, surrounding with shop houses and restaurants.

We stayed in Geylang on our last visit and it is very convenience to as the Station is just a few hundred meters from our hotel, and it’s the same line back-and-forth to the Changi Airport. Love the place, and whenever we alight from the MRT, the station makes us feels like home.

Neat and small (2exits) above ground MRT station closest to Geylang. This is the last one before Paya Lebar interchange and second above ground station coming from the city. Plenty good eateries around with ample parking space in front of the station. Lot of bike rack space as well. Only minus is that as it is above above ground, the platform is open space and there is no AC so it can get hot at times, but luckily the trains are pretty frequent.

Quiet CC near Kallang MRT.

Even though this library is not as big as Woodlands and Jurong East Regional Libraries, it still carries plenty of books and it’s spacious enough too with plenty of comfortable seats.
I also like the more relaxed vibe here with plenty of plants.

My rating: 4/5
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

I wish that the Geylang East Library was closer to where I stayed so I could go there more often. The library has a nice location because it’s just 5 minutes away from the MRT. The library has 2 levels. On the first level there is the borrowing books section, and right across the hall there is a children books section. The children’s section is one of the best children’s sections I’ve seen. When I had walked in I noticed immediately that the room was not tiny. The children’s section is a good size for children because some children are very hyper, so if they want to find books that they are interested in they can just keep walking around because the room is a very nice size, and find books. The second level has Cooking, Singapore Collection, Fiction, Non-fiction, Business, and YP (Young People) books. I like it when libraries have books on display so I can maybe browse through them and that’s what the Geylang library had. There’s also this nice long sofa that multiple people can sit on and also a nice place to use a computer on. There are also a few seats and smaller tables in the middle close to the sofa and that’s really nice for having a pile of books and just reading for the day. Overall, I think the Geylang East library is a very nice place to study, use your computer, and read for the whole day if you’re up for it.

Air quality in this library is really bad. I don’t think it’s suitable for kids/teens. Carpet floor collects so much dust, should be replaced. This is my go to library in my pri school days, but now I come out of it having a sore throat due to a sensitive nose
The library has a dedicated children's section that is welcoming, engaging and safe. The kid's area is designed with bright colors, comfy seating and fun decorations.
A pure reading encounter, with no shopping centres to distract kids.  Kids are calm and people are not anxious.  Such a pity it’s going to get demolished soon.

For a station that sits at the heart of a major commercial hub, the crowd control here is top-notch. I appreciate that despite the high volume of people from the nearby offices and retail outlets, the flow is kept organized through effective barricading and staffing. It’s a great example of an MRT station that stays functional and safe even during peak hours.

quite good but now circle line got problem so got some train delay and sometimes need to take the next train. if u take the mrt on green line from paya lebar towards pasir ris around 7:20pm then will have nice blue hour view from the mrt window

We recently visited Paya Lebar, and it is quickly becoming one of the most convenient and well-developed areas in Singapore for both business and leisure travelers.

The area features modern developments like PLQ Mall and SingPost Centre, offering a great mix of shopping, dining, and everyday essentials. It is clean, well-planned, and less crowded compared to central tourist areas, making it a comfortable location for visitors.

One of the strongest advantages of Paya Lebar is its excellent transport connectivity via Paya Lebar MRT Station, which connects both the East-West and Circle Lines. This makes it extremely easy for travelers to move across Singapore using MRT, taxis, or private transport services.

At HolidaySG, we often assist guests staying in the Paya Lebar area with Singapore attraction tickets and transport services, including city tours, private transfers, and ferry bookings. Its central connectivity allows travelers to easily plan visits to major attractions such as Marina Bay Sands and Gardens by the Bay without any hassle.

We have also coordinated with guests in this area for tours and ticket bookings, ensuring smooth pickup arrangements and seamless travel experiences across Singapore.

Overall, Paya Lebar is a smart and convenient choice for travelers looking for strong connectivity, modern facilities, and easy access to tickets, tours, and transport services in Singapore.

— Team HolidaySG
Singapore Licensed Travel Agency

Singapore’s MRT stations are exceptionally clean, efficient, and well-organized, reflecting the city’s commitment to seamless public transport. The signage is clear, and announcements are made in multiple languages, making navigation easy for locals and tourists alike. Most stations are air-conditioned and equipped with essential amenities such as escalators, elevators, and ticketing machines. Some central stations also feature retail shops and food stalls, adding convenience for commuters. However, peak hours can get crowded, so traveling during off-peak times is recommended for a more comfortable experience.

Paya Lebar MRT Station is a major interchange in Singapore connecting the **East-West (EW) and Circle (CC) lines**, making it a convenient hub for commuters traveling across the city. The station is modern, spacious, and well-signposted, allowing smooth transfers between lines. Platforms are wide and clean, with ample seating and clear train arrival information. Accessibility is thoughtfully designed, with lifts, escalators, and barrier-free access for wheelchair users and families with strollers. The station is integrated with **Paya Lebar Quarter (PLQ)**, offering direct access to shopping, dining, and office spaces, which adds convenience for both daily commuters and visitors. Peak hours can be crowded, but train frequency helps manage passenger flow efficiently. Overall, Paya Lebar MRT Station is a clean, well-maintained, and highly functional interchange that plays an important role in Singapore’s MRT network, combining connectivity with practical amenities.

Old Airport Road Food Centre exceeded my expectations with a great variety of Singapore heritage dishes. I also loved the creativity behind many stall names referencing places like Chong Pang, Kallang, and Blanco Court — adds local charm.

• Old Airport Road Fried Hokkien Mee (#01-99) – 4/5: Wet-style Hokkien Mee with decent wok hei, generous prawns, good value.

• Famous Old Airport Fried Oyster (#01-101) – 3/5: Soggy omelette, overcooked prawns, not worth $10.

• Chong Pang Huat BBQ (#01-90) – 3/5: Expensive, small portions, hygiene issues.

• Jin Fried Rice (#01-63) – 5/5: Fragrant mala fried rice with pork chop — excellent, must-try.

• Chuan Loo Bee Hokkien Food (#01-111) – 2/5: Tough pork ribs, odd sauce, disappointing.

• Whampoa Soya Bean (#01-68) – 4/5: Solid traditional desserts; soya drinks slightly diluted.

• Unkai Japanese Cuisine (#01-88) – 5/5: Generous salmon bowl, wallet-friendly, satisfying.

• Western Barbeque (#01-53) – 3/5: Chicken chop okay but lacks flavour; queues are long.

• Famous Kallang Airport Fried Oyster (#01-55) – 2/5: Oily, overwhelming, still hunting for a better version.

• Salt (#01-128) – 4/5: Aglio Olio with teriyaki chicken — decent but slightly pricey.

• Yi Ji Fried Prawn Noodles (#01-73) – 3.5/5: Carrot cake decent, noodles not outstanding.

• Xin Mei Xiang Lor Mee (#01-116) – 4/5: Double Fish Lor Mee decent; broth mediocre, some service issues.

• Heritage Tanglin Puff – 5/5: Singapore institution since 1952. Freshly fried puffs with generous fillings — curry potato and curry chicken are standouts. 6 for $12, great value.

• Ah Yee Hong Kong Roasted (#01-77) – 4/5: Three-meat combo rice ($10) — char siew well caramelised, roast pork had satisfying crackling, but roast duck was chewy and rice was dry. Chilli a nice touch. Would love a chicken option on the menu. Worth a visit for the char siew and roast pork.

Overall, a must-visit for variety and heritage flavours, though some stalls are hit-or-miss. Will keep exploring and updating!​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Old Airport Road Food Center is one of popular places for breakfast and lunch.  There are many nice food around such as Lor Mee, Hokkien Mee, Prawn Noddle, Chicken Rice, Roasted Pork and Duck, Western Food, Wanton Noddle.  There is also a new NTUC at level 2.  This food Center can be quite packed during peaked hours.  If you are around Kallang area, I will recommend you guys to drop by …

Great hawker centre but it can get a little stuffy. Try to grab one of the round tables by the entrances or near the ventilation areas for more space and cool air.

Loved the lor mee! Char koay teow and prawn mee we’re decent too.

If you’re getting ais kacang or cendol, get it from the popular stall in the middle to avoid disappointment!

Very nice and patient uncle who runs this stall. I always thought sugar canes all tasted the same, until I tried this. Happened to find a seat in front of this stall and decided to just buy from him. The sugar cane was really fresh and had a clean taste (there was not that sugary aftertaste). Uncle was also telling me that his is not soaked in sugar syrup and yet it was still naturally sweet! Highly recommended!

I think $2 for a cup, extra for lemon.
One of my favourite food centres. Great selection and food. Easy to get to via Bus 36 from Changi. Due for a nice power cleaning though.
Nice park for cycling walking running. Everything was well organized except lots of rubbish by the beach during my visit.
Views of morning sun is amazing. Good tracks for running. However, it can get crowded so users of the park should be mindful when moving around.
Great relaxing place also there is a restaurant just across from the park for a rest and F&B
