Nice park, the lookout tower is nice however is often covered in trash and migrant workers or homeless people sleeping on the stairs. Then again migrant workers are sleeping anywhere they can in Singapore given there bad dormitory accomodations. Be nice if they didn't litter so much

This one is a hidden master piece in the eastern line. Five minutes walk through a subway from Marine Prominade and you enters a lush green ecosystem combined with loads of eateries, ample green fields to run, play, party and enjoy with friends and family. A perfect family place in Singapore. Going farther it attaches with the East Coast Park beside the beach
A must visit place

We had a wonderful experience with Coach Alice. She is incredibly kind, patient, and truly understands how to guide young children based on their individual needs and learning pace.
A strict or fierce coach doesn’t always mean they understand a child, but Coach Alice knows how to build confidence while helping children improve their skills. She adjusts her teaching approach to suit each child, making every lesson enjoyable and productive.
In just 8–12 lessons, we could already see a significant improvement in our child’s swimming. More importantly, our child became much more confident and looked forward to every lesson.
Thank you, Coach Alice, for your dedication, encouragement, and genuine care. We highly recommend her to any parent looking for a coach who is both skilled and compassionate.

Coach Brandon is incredibly kind and always taught at my child’s level. Thanks to his patient and encouraging approach, my child was able to overcome their fear of the water. His coaching was outstanding. He was also very flexible with lesson scheduling and took the time to explain everything step by step, making each lesson both comfortable and enjoyable. Highly recommended!

SwimRay has been accomodating to our requests. Lessons were flexible. My daughter who is 4yo gets cold and cough occasionally. It was fuss free to request to cancel and make up the class. It took us a while to find a female coach and lucky that SwimRay was able to provide for us. Thanks to Coach Kai Ni who was able to keep my daughter engaged and focused during the class. Lesson objectives, outcome and payment credits were clear and transparent on WhatsApp chat. Highly recommended to any parents who are looking for swimming coach in private settings. :)

Coach Deep was excellent with our child. We saw a clear improvement in water confidence from the very first lesson, and the progress since then has been very encouraging. He is patient, calm and great with children. Highly recommended!

The SwimRay team was earnest and helpful in matching me with a female swim coach. The first one didn't work out, but the next one did, and brilliantly. Coach Nafizah laid out lesson plans, tracked my progress, and patiently worked with me to achieve my objectives. In the process, she helped me to overcome years of mistakes I didn't even know I was making. It was a productive and enjoyable journey to have learnt an important life skill and picked up useful tips along the way. Thanks team!
The place was very comfortable and just nice for our event. The people in charge was very accommodating and open with some requests. Not to mention helpful and friendly.

Potong Pasir Community Club stands out for its high land-use efficiency, successfully integrating essential services (POSB AutoLobby, 24-hour Sheng Siong, KFC, SingPost) within a community framework. This coexistence creates a complex security perimeter.

​The presence of high-traffic commercial tenants necessitates robust physical security. I noted clear sightlines and effective lighting in the transition zones between the CC facilities (like the unique Archery Academy) and the commercial areas, which is a key tenet of CPTED (Crime Prevention Through Environmental Design).

It probably has the cleanest toilets in Potong Pasir neighbourhood. However, it can consider updating directories and signs on the premises. Newspaper reading corner is usually occupied by seniors and multi-purpose hall users. Advisories could be pasted on the tables for fair and considerate usage.

​A highly functional, securely managed community hub that serves as a benchmark for neighborhood-centric integrated design.
Call PPasir always dont answer calls. Sleeping on their jobs. Wkg hrs 10 am to 2200 hrs. Workers there warming up their seats n collecting pay on time.😂

Hi, I would like to complimemt Mr Jimmy for his customer sevice at Potong Pasir C C👍🤝😀😊🙏🙏...today.
(15 Nov 2025)
Around10 :15 am+ who help us today to reset our helper's Singpass.
First we went to him, he tried can not..he told to approach digital team at lvl 2 also can not.
Then we approched Mr Jimmy second time again he thinked out off the box..together with his lady colleague was also there during that time..and the Singpass was reset.
We really appreciate their customer service attitude.👍😀☺️👌😀🙏🙏

Technical Boxing class. Every Wed & Friday. 7 - 9pm.
$64 for 10 classes.
https://www.onepa.gov.sg/courses/technical-boxing-healthiersg-c027057962

HIGHLY RECOMMEND. Rain or shine. The class still goes on.

Just need to bring your own:
1) Boxing Gloves (12 oz for 55 kg & below. 14 oz for 70kg & below. 16 oz for 90 kg & below. 18 oz for 91 kg & above)

2) Hand wraps

3) Mouth guard

4) Skipping Rope

Both of my kids have been with this preschool since their infant days, and watching how much they have grown over the years truly means a lot to us. The teachers are incredibly warm, caring, and dedicated, and we always felt that they were very well taken care of.

The school provides many opportunities for the children to explore such as through excursions and their isteam program, and during K2, we could clearly see how thoughtfully they prepared the kids for Primary 1 — from daily life skills like managing their belongings and personal hygiene, to strong academic foundations in phonics and Chinese literacy.

We are very grateful for the love, guidance, and effort from the teachers. Highly recommended to any parent looking for a nurturing and well-rounded preschool for their child. 💛

Our son joined Cambridge Preschool @ MacPherson at just 10 weeks old and is now moving on to playgroup at 18 months. From day one, the teachers and staff have been nothing short of amazing. They cared for him like their own, celebrated every little milestone, and created a safe and joyful environment where friendships naturally blossomed.

We’ve watched him grow from a tiny infant to a confident, happy toddler, and we couldn’t have asked for a better start to his school journey. The teachers’ dedication, warmth, and professionalism shine through in everything they do.

As we reflect on our time here, we honestly have nothing but gratitude—it’s been a wonderful first school experience that has exceeded every expectation. Thank you to everyone at Cambridge MacPherson for giving our son such a beautiful beginning.

A Truly Wonderful School Experience
As expats, choosing the right school for our son was one of the most important decisions we had to make—and looking back, it was truly the best decision we could have made. From the very beginning, the school welcomed us warmly and made us feel supported every step of the way.
The care and attention given to our son have been outstanding. It’s clear how professional and dedicated the entire teaching staff is. Special thanks to Teacher Nell and Miss Sharon—their kindness, patience, and genuine care made a world of difference in our son's daily life and in ours as parents. We always felt heard, understood, and truly part of the school community.
The rest of the teachers have also been amazing and played a key role in his learning journey. Each one brought something special to his experience. The environment is not only educational but also incredibly nurturing, with everyone being genuinely sweet and supportive.
We are so grateful to have found this school—it has made a meaningful impact on our family’s life, and especially on our son's growth and happiness. Highly recommended to any family looking for a place where their child will be cared for, challenged, and celebrated.

We don’t post photos only for privacy of the children ! However you can see how impecable is the facility when you go there !

My daughter has been with Cambridge @ MacPherson since infant care, and now she’s graduating from K2. Over the years, we’ve had the privilege of witnessing the dedication, warmth, and professionalism of the teachers at this centre.

The educators here are truly wonderful. They genuinely care for each and every student, creating a nurturing and safe environment where children can learn, grow, and thrive. Their commitment goes far beyond just teaching — they invest their time, energy, and hearts into making sure every child feels supported and valued.

We are deeply grateful for the patience, love, and hard work the teachers have shown throughout the years. Thank you for being such an important part of our daughter’s early learning journey.

We would strongly recommend Cambridge @ MacPherson to friends and family!

Our kids started at N1 and N2 in 2023, and our older one will be graduating with the K2 2025 class. So glad that our 2 kids enjoy the school bus rides and going to school at Cambridge!

The main strengths we see are:
(1) high school hygiene (our kids fall sick way less here),
(2) consistent staff retention (both our kids’ main teacher have been with the school for a number of years, and our kids love the teachers and staff. Pretty cosy and stable environment!),
(3) very strong curriculum (lots of STEAM activities, and even optional exposure to robotics competition, wushu competition, overseas cultural trip).

Physical play is also important to us. We see that the school’s indoor playground is a hot fav, and there are lots of nature walks and regular school outings.

We are forever grateful to Teacher Lynee, Guan laoshi, Lin laoshi, Teacher Nell and Ms Sharon for watching our young ones and guiding them in a warm and loving manner 🥲! You have been so important in helping us bring up our children!

One of the greatest gifts a school can give a parent is peace of mind, and Little Atlas Preschool in Geylang delivers this daily.
The campus security protocols are robust and professionally handled, but more importantly, there is a palpable culture of "watchful care."
I’ve noticed that, Arrival and dismissal are organized and secure, ensuring every child is where they need to be.
The emotional environment feels safe; the staff fosters a culture of kindness that makes Matteo and Eliza feel protected from the inside out.

We are so glad to have enrolled our girl into Little Atlas. All the teachers here are very bubbly, caring and takes good care of the kids. The curriculum involves reading, discovering, exploring and thinking skills. These have made my child love reading alot and have bring out the creativity in her. The consistent updates from teachers also helps us alot in understanding how she have been behaving, coping and learning in school.

We are absolutely thrilled with the care and education our daughter has received at Little Atlas Preschool. From the moment we enrolled her, we felt welcomed by the warm, nurturing staff who are truly dedicated to the growth and development of the children. The teachers are not only knowledgeable but also incredibly patient, kind, and attentive to each child's individual needs.

Our daughter loves going to school every day! She has gained so much confidence, developed important social skills, and is always excited to share what she's learned. The curriculum is engaging, age-appropriate, and encourages creativity, curiosity, and a love for learning. It's clear that the staff take great care in creating a supportive and safe environment where kids can thrive.

We also appreciate the communication from the school. Whether it's through regular updates, parent-teacher conferences, or fun events, we always feel involved and informed about our daughter's progress. Little Atlas Preschool has truly become a second home for her, and we couldn't be happier with the experience.

After trying a 3 other preschools, we've finally found the right fit with Little Atlas. The teachers are warm and genuinely engaged and care for the kids. We love getting the weekly updates with real candid photos of our toddler fully participating in the activities - no more forced smiles for the camera like some of his previous school. The curriculum seems to be keeping him challenged and interested. Overall, a really positive experience compared to our past preschool struggles.

A Fantastic Kindergarten Experience!

We couldn’t be happier with our experience at Little Atlas Preschool. From the moment we walked through the doors, it was clear that this is a place where children are nurtured, valued, and encouraged to grow.

The teachers and staff are warm, caring, and highly skilled. They genuinely take the time to understand each child’s unique needs and personalities, creating a supportive and inclusive environment. Our child has not only gained a love for learning but has also grown socially and emotionally thanks to the thoughtful attention and encouragement provided here.

The curriculum is both engaging and well-rounded, blending structured learning with plenty of play-based activities.

Angela is very patient in explaining the term and conditions with me. As I’m first time in a gym with her cheerfulness make me less anxious. Thank you Edmund for explaining the detail on the package so clearly, very helpful and patient.

Fantastic 20-session journey – Highly recommend Fitness First Paya Lebar!

I recently completed an awesome 20-session journey at Fitness First Paya Lebar and cannot recommend this gym enough. The equipment is modern and kept in excellent condition, and the locker and shower facilities are good and provide everything you need for a smooth workout transition. My experience from day one was seamless; Mae provided a very professional and thorough briefing during registration, and the reception staff are consistently pleasant and welcoming. A special thank you goes to my personal trainer, Charlotte, who is truly an exceptional physical trainer and an expert in her field. She is incredibly knowledgeable and patient, taking the time to explain the proper mechanics of every machine and ensuring I understood exactly how to use them safely and effectively. Her friendly and encouraging approach made every session highly productive and enjoyable. If you are looking for a supportive environment and expert guidance to reach your fitness goals, I highly recommend.

Really grateful to have trainers like Nazree at Fitness First. He’s very experienced, knowledgeable and professional, but also friendly and easy to approach. Even when you’re not his client, he’s always happy to give advice and help out.

I used to attend his BOOM class and it was one of my favourite classes — fun, energetic and very technique-focused. Hope he brings BOOM back again!

Now attending his Hyrox class and I’m impressed by how attentive he is. He remembered my right ankle injury and always gives me options for the workout. You can really tell he cares about members’ safety and progress. .

Highly recommend Nazreen to anyone looking for a dedicated and supportive trainer! 💪

Came here for my fitness assessment and had a great experience. The gym and lounge area were very clean and well-maintained, and the front desk staff were friendly and welcoming.

Special mention to Charlotte, who attended to me during the assessment. She was patient, friendly, and very well-versed in the exercises and movements. Even after I shared that my upper body strength was weaker, she remained encouraging and supportive throughout the session. Overall, had a really positive experience with her!

I’ve been training at Fitness First Paya Lebar for the past two and a half months, and it has been a very positive experience.

A big part of this is thanks to my personal trainer, Charlotte. She is incredibly helpful, patient, and always fully focused during our sessions. She takes the time to ensure I’m using the correct form, explains each exercise clearly, and adjusts the workouts based on my progress and condition.

I really appreciate how attentive and encouraging she is—it makes each session both effective and enjoyable. I’ve definitely seen improvement since I started, and I feel more confident in my workouts.

Overall, I’m very happy with both the club environment and the quality of training. Highly recommended!
The place is very quiet , The MRT station should put some chair .
New setup bag scanning system. Crew are polite. Feel comfortable while doing the scan process.
Efficient way to go home
Looks nice. Reminds me a bit of the mrt at changi.

First time to visit the station and it was just a few meters from where I stayed... the amenities are new... the train is a bit smaller than the other trains from other lines... but I enjoyed every ride I had with it... the staff are accommodating always there to assist...

Macpherson Community Club, is located at 400 Paya Lebar Way, Singapore 379131.
We thankful, applause & respect Advisor to MacPherson Ms Tin Pei Ling Grassroots & Organiser inplace various Courses, Events, Activities, Care, Wellness, Life Long Learning program for young & elderly to develop, embrace social & culture value💚
Art of living, Colors of life - An Art Gallery spark residents of inspiration to enrich to explore meaningful journey 🛤️🧭 Merry Christmas & Happy New Year🧭🎄☘️

There is a basketball court there which is in quite good condition. However, the badminton court is bad. Firstly, the floor is slippery and dirty. Net got hole inside and not in good quality. Very noisy in there. Damn stuffy, no circulation of air. Can  you believe it, the court is exposed to the outside, got wind, a big hole in the wall. Basically impossible to play there. constant wind and slippery court. Waste of money sia. You come here you come for the cafe or to play basketball. Just dont play badminton here. Alsi in a very inconvenient location. Except that it is near a very small hawker centre. Not near Macpherson MRT, need to take bus from there to reach here. Overall a bad experience.
Nice NDP n Diwali decor !
Big CC with badminton courts which are also used as paddle courts in the day time.  Downside is that the floor is kinda tiled and it's not good for the knees.  No shower facilities.
Easy access, esp if driving. Only come here annually for my New Notes for CNY!! Pop up DBS/POSB atms are a life saver

This is my first time at Ci Yuan Hawker Centre, and I visited on a Sunday evening at 8 pm, which is a public holiday. As a result, most stalls were closed or closing. Despite that, I was impressed by the variety of food options available here.

I look forward to returning another time to explore more!

This is a newer hawker centre located on the ground floor of Ci Yuan Community Club. It’s cleaner & more organised than the older ones, with a good mix of food choices without too many repeats. Prices are still affordable & the overall atmosphere is relaxed - less crowded & not touristy.

I don’t live nearby so I hardly eat at Ci Yuan hawker center. Today I’m in the mood to eat some “unhealthy” kind of foods that I normally skip. So I had hokkien noodle (01-16), a duck drumstick (01-12) and fried cabbage (01-11).  Finish my lunch with chendol. I enjoyed myself. Please try out these food. My best experience so far eating at Ci Yuan hawker center. I find that the food is getting better over the years. Recommended 💕

Newly open stall "文哥潮州鱼•蒸好吃 (no English name) yet.

Tried item C: Bitter Gourd with Egg soup ($4) + Dry Hor Fun ($1.50)
= $5.50 (Total) reasonable price.

Hor Fun (dry) with black sauce (mild sweetness), fried shallots, spring onions, mini pork lards (maybe chopped very small pc - you can ask for more if you like).
You can add more chilli paste or chopped chilli padi if you like more spicy kick 🔥 🥵  Shiok & Sweat! 💦

Bitter Gourd Soup MUST TRY!
Really generous ingredients like:
- thick & tender pork slices (maybe pork collar)
- minced pork (no fats surprisingly)
- pork balls (texture not hard when bite)
- brown strips (can someone enlighten me what it call?) thanks.
- bitter gourd not bitter (I'm a person who don't fancy bitter gourd, but it really hit my taste buds - good try for me!)

This soup really warms my heart and makes me feel missing my mum's hot soup...wow!

Met a friend there who also recommended me to try their Salted fish & peanuts pork patty & Vinegar Pork Trotters.

So I will come back again to try & share with you all again!
Clean, spacious and airy hawker center with a variety of food options. We had taliwang nasi lemak, chef wang fried rice, ccf and the sliced fish pao fan from a zi char stall.
Pretty old compared to other bus interchange. But has decent food options. Bread shop, indian cuisine and snacks.

A Malay male bus driver driving 107M to Hougang Interchange on 18/12/2025 (probably reach interchange at 11:20+pm) was very rude!  I may or may not have forgotten to tap my card when I board the bus, and after a few minutes away from the bus stop that I boarded he suddenly shouted at me angrily “EXCUSE ME, YOU GOT TAP YOUR CARD OR NOT??!!” And the bus was lil bit pack at that time, I don’t even dare to turn my head cuz ik I’ll be embarrassed from others passengers staring at me lol Why didn’t he NICELY tell me that I forgotten to tap my card when I boarded the bus?? And must wait till he drives halfway till the next bus stop? Like at least give me a “hello, I think you forgot to tap your card” instead of shouting right at me 🤮👎🏻👎🏻

Their bus timings aren't accurate, much to people's inconvenience. Seems like Bus 329 departed by 7.16am, even though it was scheduled to come at 7.18am. Now I have to walk to the other end of this U-shaped interchange just to take 116.

took a bus at sengkang area @ arnd early 8+pm
161 bus driver was soso sweet ,  saying “ thankyou byebye “ at every single stop even tho bus came from woodlands ..! THANK YOU TATS RLY SWEET

Frustrated with the inconsistent timings for bus 102. I’m not sure if it’s due to a lack of buses, or if the scheduled bus driver is lazing off, but it’s not a good look when a bus is scheduled to depart at a certain time but doesn’t. I’ve experienced this on a few occasions and it’s really frustrating to see that the bus is supposed to depart once every 15 mins but in reality, skips a bus once in a while (leading to 30 mins intervals instead)

Met an extremely helpful young man working at Tai Seng Mrt station by the name of Kai Yong. He spent a fair bit of time explaining to me how to get to my destination due to the underground works affecting the train direction at the moment. Super amazing service! I’m deeply impressed

I want to compliment a crowd marshal by the name of Ricardo! He is a perfect example of excellent service where he rendered a great help to me just now by giving me an accurate and precise direction for me when I got lost in the station! He is very patient in his explanation and is always smiling even though i threw temple at him initially due to my flustered manner! He even helped me to carry my heavy luggage up the escalator as there isn't any lift at the exit.
Nevertheless I am very blessed to have met him and many thanks to him so that I can meet up with my family. Please help me to extend my thanks to him and I hope there are more such crowd marshals like him.
Thank you Ricardo and all at Tai Seng Mrt station!

key amenities at taiseng station
1️⃣ lift located after exiting the gantry where it is exit A/C
2️⃣ toilet located after exiting the gantry where it is exit B
3️⃣ ATM (POSB/OCBC) located after exiting the gantry where it is exit C

I was rushing for work and this morning encountered train delay and disruption. 3 trains in a row people were asked to alight at Tai Seng and wait for the next train. This caused delays to my journey and disappointment with the service.

Worst train station to be at during peak hours. At around 6-640pm, you can’t even get on the train, especially towards Bishan. Be prepared to wait for at least 3-4 trains during that time. People will just push their way in just to board the train.
Beware. But some hack for anyone who read this, there will be an empty train at 621pm. You can board that train to avoid the squeezing.

This was renovated recently and there is now a childcare to help cater to the demand in the area. There is an eldercare centre on the fourth level to cater to our rapidly ageing population. There is also a sheltered basketball court so that hoopers can hoop even if it is raining.

We're here for vaccination for kids.

They are very systematic.

Everything prepared,  staffs are alert.
Thank u for it.
Most kids have accompanied parents.

I let the young one comes for him to feel what is going to happen to his eldest brother after the vaccine cause next is his turn in a few weeks.

The place is wide and open.

Highly ventilated.

Tiled badminton courts

What I like:
- Air-conditioning
- Ample of rest space
- walking distance from bus stop
- convenience store beside the court

What I don't like:
- There is a big fan in the middle of the hall, can't lift shots too high when playing badminton
- the net stand is too wide, resulting in 1 court having to offset the net position awkwardly

Went for vaccination here. It was a good process, quick and all in order. Took about 40 minutes for the queue and the waiting. The place not that huge for sport it seems. Just one basketball field inside.

There is a small store inside fully unmanned with gate. Will try next time again when visiting here.

The carpark was closed that day. I don't know how big or if it is fully functional carpark. Need to park at the hdb carpark before reaching or the one after which longer walk.

Very nice hall.  Was there for vacinnation.  Signs leading to the ticket STn to get queue number was non existent.
I had to ask the staff who were very helpful.
Why not post signs clearly to help ppl along.

It's an MRT station that works for the surrounding neighborhood. It's clean, well structured, and connected to Heartland Mall. Is it any unique, no. Does it work as a public transportation facility, yes. If you're coming through Kovan or Hougang, you'll likely pass through this.

Just be mindful of which exit you're heading towards, or else you may be walking around blindly and end up walking a fair bit further.

Connected to the North East Line. Heartland Mall is located beside the MRT Station.

The Kovan Market and Food Centre is also within walking distance from the Mrt Station. Nice local food can be found there.

One of the stations along the North East (purple) line which connects to a shopping centre (Heartland Mall) and Hawker Centre. There’s enough ancillary services within the basement - $10 haircut (Okinawa), OCBC and UOB ATMs, convenience shop (Buzz). Control station is always well-staffed with friendly and helpful captains. Handicap friendly and well-air conditioned too. Toilets are available at the ground level with taxi stands and passenger drop off areas on both sides of the exits - one just outside Kovan Residences, and the other outside Heartland Mall. Very convenient overall - both drop off areas and taxi stands/bus stops are sheltered across too. You can get almost everything you need here at Kovan - shopping, food, electronics, tuition centers, entertainment (bowling), fitness (anytime fitness), clinics, spas and much more. Many famous eateries around as well - Lola Cafe, Punggol Nasi Lemak, Yaowarat, Chicken Pot, etc. You will not run out of options for sure.

Near the place where I'm staying.
Very convenient as expected from sG mrt colored lines😁

(The comfort room needs a little bit of cleaning)

Situated just after Serangoon and before Hougang, Kovan MRT is one of the northern station on the North East (Purple) MRT line. Notable landmarks here include 3 condos, 1 mall, 1 hawker center and numerous eating places.

People come to Kovan mostly for the food or to catch a bus to Tampines. It's tough to enter the train here during peak hours, as it's right smack between two concentrated places.

There are 3 exits at this station and the toilets are above ground. There is also OCBC ATM.

Visited this community club for a swim on a Tuesday and it was a really pleasant experience. The crowd was minimal, which made the whole session feel relaxed and unhurried. There are two pools available—one indoor and one outdoor—so you can choose depending on your preference or weather. There’s also a feature pool that has just been renovated and recently reopened, and it looks clean and refreshed. Overall, it’s a nice, well-maintained spot for a quiet swim without the usual crowd. There are lifeguards on duty to keep us safe!

This swimming complex have slides and water features
2 large full length swimming pool. One of the pool is under shelter.
Locker is available for valuable item storage.
Parking is available for car, motocycle,  Bicycle.
Next to food court and there are food available together at the sport complex.
There will be swimming classes and may be crowded some of the time.
Limited table and chair around the pool while there is a stand next to the open pool.

This swimming pool finally reopened after more than 3 years of closure.

It now has 5 types of slide rides, with Rainbow dash being the only multi-slide ride. They are all MUCH milder than the ones in Wild Wild Wet.

The lifeguards are pretty strict about height limits:

Purple slide (Cotton Candy Bowl) - 1.3m height limit

orange slide (Sugar Rush) - 1.3m height limit

Green slide (Jelly Lime Loops) - 1.2m height limit

Pink slide (Jellybears Swiggle) - 1.2m height limit

The lifeguards are also very particular about wearing of "hard objects", even watches by adults/parents. Yet, ironically, I see authorized influencers being allowed to wear GoPro cameras!!

There are mini paddle boats for children 4yo and above, with minimum weight limit 30 kg, and requires adult supervision at all times.

Water guns are NOT Allowed !!

Toilets are clean.

There's no more food eatery at the balcony area near the entrance.

The swimming complex is quite big and has 4 adult water slides. Note that jewellery such as earrings or necklaces are not allowed, and swimsuits with zippers are prohibited. Kids need to be at least 120–130 cm tall to use the slides.

It gets very crowded on weekends, especially at the kids’ water play area. The adult slides were fine, just some queuing, especially for the longer slide. The Sugar Rush slide was really fast and fun.

The biggest downside is the changing/shower rooms. Around 5:30 PM, the queue gets extremely long, so it’s better to go earlier if you want to avoid the wait.

It was a super bad experience at the sliding pool. My two girls was not allowed to take the slide just because their swimming wear got a zipper at the front. Going down slides shld be by sitting and sliding down. How in the world the front zipper affect the slide down process. Really disappointed with the system enforced. Really disappoint the kids on their school holiday.

result.name: Arty Learning @ Hougang | Enrichment Classes for Kids

reviews.text:
Just one month into Artylearning, and we are already seeing encouraging progress. The recommended bridging classes have been very helpful in supporting a child who was initially confused with alphabet recognition, reading, and phonics sounds.

We are truly grateful for the patient guidance and interactive activities that continue to spark interest and build confidence in learning. The session videos and feedback shared after every class also help parents better understand the child’s progress and learning journey.

Thank you to the teachers for making learning engaging and supportive!

Dear Arty Learning School Team,

​We are writing to express our sincere gratitude and to highly commend Teacher Evf for her exceptional dedication and impact on our son.
​He is learning so much and truly thrives under her guidance. He comes home every day talking about how much fun he has at school and eagerly shares everything he learned.

​We especially appreciate Teacher Evf’s patience and creativity. For instance, when he needs a little extra time to focus on his writing, instead of showing frustration, she turns it into a fun game by saying "Wakey wakey!" This brilliant approach keeps him incredibly engaged and excited to learn rather than discouraged.

​Because of the safe and wonderful environment you provide and the fantastic impact Teacher Evf has had on his educational journey, we would absolutely love for him to continue learning under her in the future.

​Thank you, Teacher Evf, and thank you, Arty Learning School and all the staff for making the classroom such a joyful, supportive, and creative place!

My daughter Hazel started her journey with Arty at the age of 4 year old. She enjoyed every lesson. We like that they incorporate fun play sessions to have the kids engage in the lessons. I can’t see why any child will dislike attending classes here. My daughter is 6 this year, and she just graduated from Arty in March (year 2026) . I am happy that she now enjoy reading books to us and actively trying to pronounce any words she encounter. I can’t wait to send my younger daughter there to learn when she turns older. Arty Learning @ Hougang.. you are truly amazing :)

Both my kids have been with Arty Learning since they were 4 years old, and I couldn’t be more grateful for the wonderful learning experience they’ve had. My children have become confident readers, and it truly shows. My elder child, who is now in Primary 1, transitioned to formal schooling with ease. He has had no struggles with reading, spelling, or understanding his worksheets and instructions. This strong foundation in literacy and comprehension was built through the years at Arty Learning. I would highly recommend Arty Learning to any parent who wants to give their child a confident start in their learning journey! Special thanks to Teacher Naz and Teacher Joyce for their dedication and care in my children's learning journey!

Visited this language centre to enrol my 6-year-old for beginner Mandarin classes. The classroom was tiny, barely fitting 3-4 students, and the noise from the neighbouring unit was so distracting. No proper soundproofing. It felt more like a playground than a proper learning environment. The chairs were uncomfortable, the space was cramped, and there was no dedicated waiting area for parents. My kid didn’t enjoy it and kept saying it was “too loud” and “boring.” Not suitable for young learners at all. Definitely not worth the fees they’re charging.

SBS is soooo good at managing the crowd. Peak hour 8:45am on weekday, only 1 lane inwards and 5 lanes outwards. Handicap is also used by aunty exiting. 1 vs 6 lanes. How? Everyone late for work altogether, such a perfect transport service.

Clean, well maintained. There are places to sit, lift and wheelchair accessibility.
This is for purple line, the last station is for punggol, and other side is harbourfront.
The last train is arround 12:30.
Exit b is for mall and hougang central and a exit can take you to bus interchange.
Train frequency is 5 min.

Nicely quiet MRT station at the North East Region of Singapore on the NE line (purple line). Hougang Mall is just around the corner. Staffs are friendly and helpful and their toilet is easily accessible.

Helloride & Anywheel bicycle-sharing were irresponsible parking which caused inconvenience for others. LTA SHOULD TAKE ACTION AGAINST THESE IRRESPONSIBLE RIDERS‼️ I took a lot of these irresponsible parking @Hougang MRT Stn bicycle parking bay with my Samsung Z4 fold(hung after rebooting). I shall post more of these irresponsible bicycle parking 🅿️ in future.
Cheap n nice

Glad to see that my girl thoroughly enjoyed practicing and participating in the performance. The venue though far, its facility and stage capability is good and brings out the efforts of the children's practices and talents.
My 2 sons studied in Kinderland Hougang. All teachers there are very kind. They all love them

We have placed 2 of our kids at Kinderland Hougang. The teachers and staff are very loving and genuinely concerned about our kids. The bilingual programme is also very effective and the kids enjoyed their time in school.

“We absolutely love this kindergarten! The teachers are kind, patient, and truly care about every child. Our little one has learned so much and is excited to go every morning. The environment is safe, nurturing, and full of fun activities. Highly recommend!
The teachers at Kinderland Hougang are kind and delicated. And provides very useful feedbacks to help my kids improve

We are truly impressed with the Chinese New Year celebration organised for the children at ELFA Hougang. The teachers’ performance of the lion dance was filled with enthusiasm and joy, creating an engaging and festive atmosphere for everyone. It was heartwarming to see the children joining in with shouts of excitement and dancing along, fully immersed in the celebration.

The art and craft activity with calligraphy was another highlight, allowing the children to have even more fun while exploring their creativity and connecting with the traditions of the celebration.

Overall, it was a wonderful experience for both the children and parents. Thank you to the school and teachers for making this celebration so memorable and enjoyable! I love ELFAAAA

We’ve had such a wonderful experience with this preschool. The teachers are warm, nurturing, and genuinely care about the children’s wellbeing. My child has grown so much in confidence and independence, and always comes home excited to share what they did in school. We truly feel supported as partners in our child’s development. Highly recommend this preschool to any parent looking for a caring and engaging early learning environment.

We are glad to engage my daughter first learning journey with Elfa. Wide range of enrichment activities, opportunities and exposure for the child to explore and pick up soft skills on top of curriculum schedules.

Annual year end concert is a heart felt appreciation to testify their growing up journey. Thank you Elfa for walking with us.

Awesome performance put up by the children, made possible with the guidance and patience of ELFA educators! It’s really heartening to see the growth in the children and more so when they genuinely enjoy putting up the concert! Thank you for the effort, Teacher Pat and all the teachers of ELFA!

Elfa preschool provides the children an enriching experiences to explore themselves through interactive learning. The enrichment classes like abacus and speech and drama also provides the children to prepare them to speak up confidently and challenge them mentally.

Had tried 88 prawn noodle at #01-56

Old school taste. Dried noodle is tasty, very spicy but shiok. Remember to ask for less spicy if you are not so hot.

25 years never come this hawker centre. Heard  just renovation. Impressed with the food choices. So many good Chinese and Malay food. Wanton mee, kolo mee, yong tao fu, chicken rice, tutu kueh. Clean and well ventilated. Parking maybe hard but can park at kinetic mall and walk over.

I discovered this new stall after the recent renovation of this very popular food centre. The steam fish extremely fresh and deliciously prepared. One of the best I have eaten which is so value-for-money for a healthier meal in food centre.

There are plenty of food options available, from malay to chinese and many others, making it a great spot to explore different flavors in one place. I also tried the famous putu piring, which was soft, a little bit sweet, and fragrant. The place can get quite busy but still worth to try.

Came here craving teh tarik, satay, and tu tu kueh — and all three wishes came true!

☕ Teh tarik was fragrant, smooth, and nicely pulled.
🍢 Satay was juicy, well-marinated, and smoky with delicious peanut sauce.
🍰 Tu tu kueh was soft, warm, and freshly made — comforting and nostalgic.

Perfect place if you want a mix of classic local flavours in one visit.
Will definitely come back again 👍

My daughter attended Tr Xiao’s P6 Science class. Mr Xiao drilled the use of keywords and conceptual understanding across various science topics in a few months.

Her results soared from AL7 from P5 to AL3 in prelims. Her confidence was at a high by the end of prelims. She also mentioned her classmates in Mr Xiao’s class mostly scored between AL1 to AL3 by prelims. It reassured me that she’s in good hands for her PSLE prep.

My girl scored her best results of AL2 in PSLE. We are grateful to have found Tr Xiao and his team of supportive teachers. I highly recommend Edify for those who are looking for tuition class.

My son joined Edify’s teacher Ko weekly science lessons for P6 (2025) and attained AL2 for his PSLE. Teacher Ko is an excellent teacher who teaches science concepts very well and he is always happy to answer any questions whenever in doubt. Also big thanks to Teacher Xiao for his weekly sessions on MCQ online. They are excellent educators who are clear of the concepts and will explain them in details. I have also enrolled my younger son to Edify. Thank you very much.

It was a rainy day, and the traffic lights on the opposite side were not working. We called the admin, and they were very kind and helped bring my kids safely to their tuition centre. I was truly touched by their care and kindness towards the students. Thank you, Edify.

We've had a very positive experience with Edify Kovan.  The teachers are passionate, approachable and care about each student's progress.
My 2 daughters have shown great improvement in their academics.
The facilities are clean, safe and well maintained.
Overall, we are very happy with our choice and would highly recommend other.
Thanks Teacher Ko, Teacher Shaun and Teacher John

The teachers here are familiar with the syllabus because they were ex-MOE teachers. This gives us a peace of mind knowing that the teachers know what they are talking about. Why pay for inexperienced tutors who charge an arm and a leg.

They are also dedicated and encouraging, often going the extra mile to help students understand the concepts.

The PSLE journey can be tiring and frustrating but the teachers at Edify, in particular Tr Ko and Tr Shaun, have made it more bearable.

Thank you for the ride, Edify.

Dear Management Team,
I am writing to share a truly exceptional experience I recently had at your store.
While shopping for meat at GIANT Food, I was warmly welcomed by an outstanding employee whose name tag identified him as Mr. Youn Sang. From the moment I approached the counter, he made my entire experience feel special and valued.
What impressed me most was his attentiveness to everyone around him. No customer in line was left unattended. Mothers with children were acknowledged with patience and kindness, and every single person received a warm, personalized greeting. His professionalism, positive energy, and genuine care for customers truly stood out.
It is rare to encounter someone who takes such pride in delivering exceptional service. Mr. Youn Sang made my shopping experience not only pleasant but memorable, and I felt it was important that your management team be made aware of the remarkable job he is doing.
Please extend my sincere appreciation to him. Employees like Mr. Youn Sang are a tremendous asset to your organization.
Sincerely,
Jay.C

Worst hypermarket experience. Only three checkout counters were open on a Saturday night, which naturally led to long queues. We waited about 40 minutes just to check out. If the store is short-staffed, it should reconsider operating 24 hours. Giant, please learn from Sheng Siong.

This is one of the best mega warehouses in Singapore to shop on the East side as it carries many brands of food, household n electrical items . The layout is good where one can shop in comfort. There is a food court that has a nice range of food where one can indulge after the shopping. A great place to stock up essentials for home as the ample parking is convenient to trolley ones purchase to the car. One can find wonderful bargains as the store holds promotional product brands that are a steal. Happy shopping , happy family.

Today, January 12, 2026, at the Giant food court, I ordered *mee hoon* soto from the Nasi Padang stall, asking for an extra chicken add-on for $2.00. After I collected the food, it was not hot and contained only about one tablespoon of chicken. I asked the lady, who sounded Indonesian, about it. She was not happy when I asked her if the $2.00 chicken had been added. She pulled the bowl, and not wanting to create a scene, I just pulled it back and left. I'm very disappointed with her attitude; at the very least, she could have explained nicely. This will be the last time.

Final Giant Hypemarket at Tampines ✔️✔️✔️✔️✔️👍👍👍

Giant Hypermarket at Tampines is ideal for one-stop grocery needs if you live in this area. There is a wide range of electronics, kitchenware, groceries, a semi-wet market, fruits, and vegetables. The prices are reasonable. There are also food stalls serving plenty of local dishes. Halal and vegetarian options are available and typically served until around 9:30 PM. Additionally, there is ample parking space with free parking available. If you live in this area, it’s an excellent place to shop for your daily needs.
Chris
17 Sept 2025 11am

✔️ Review of Hougang Mall
90 Hougang Ave 10, Singapore
538766

💙💙💙 I'm excited to share that I was selected as the winner of the Water Wally & Sally Plushie giveaway for Singapore's World Water Day 2026! To claim my prize, I visited Hougang Mall, which gave me the perfect opportunity to revisit and explore the area, bringing back cherished childhood memories.

Having recently moved to Tampines, I traveled to Hougang Mall, which took about an hour. The trip was a nostalgic journey back to my younger days when I often shopped there.

Hougang Mall provides a wide selection of stores that cater to the needs of most local residents.

It houses numerous shops and recently opened Food & Beverage outlets, such as NTUC FairPrice, BreakTalk, a food court, various restaurants, pharmacies, an electronics retailer (Harvey Norman), jewelry stores, beauty salons, spas, hair salons, and apparel shops.

The mall tends to be busy daily and can become quite crowded during weekends. It also regularly hosts events and promotional activities.

With its inviting ambiance and friendly staff, shopping at Hougang Mall offers a pleasant experience.

It is a must-visit destination for those nearby seeking convenient and diverse shopping options—a great place to unwind and refresh.

Visit Hougang Mall, a true Heartland Mall, and enjoy a fun overall experience.

🙂 All essential items can be found here without the need to travel to the city.

Chris
17 April 2026

Nice neighborhood mall with almost everything you need. Food options are plenty and the prices are quite reasonable, which is always a big plus.

The air conditioning is also very good. Very important, because shopping without good AC is basically cardio.

Only downside is that it does not connect directly to the MRT station. That would make it much more convenient.

Overall, a good mall for daily needs, food and a comfortable break from the heat.

Newly Upgraded Hougang Mall with many New F&B Outlets. Spoilt for choice, variety Authentic European & SEA Food Stalls here. Vibrant & POSITIVE ENERGY ENVIRONMENT Shopping Mall here. Can't wait for the New Renovated Hougang Library at Level 4 coming June/July...hopefully.
There is Value Shop & other Reasonable priced groceries & snacks.
Entertainment Game Station, NTUC Fair Price, BreakTalk, Wok Hey, Pet Lovers, Pharmacies, Jewellery shop, Beauty, Facial, Nail polish, Spa & Hair Salon.
Harvey Norman, Data Shoes & other brands. Enjoy your One Stop North East Hougang Central HG Shopping Mall here.

Hougang Mall is a vibrant shopping destination in Singapore, offering a mix of local and international brands. The mall's cozy atmosphere and friendly staff make shopping a pleasant experience. With various dining options, including popular food courts and cafes, it's a great place to relax and refuel. The mall also hosts regular events and promotions, adding to its appeal. While it can get crowded during peak hours, the overall experience is enjoyable. A must-visit for anyone in the area looking for a convenient and diverse shopping experience

The new renovated mall definitely gives it a fresh look! However, I don't understand why they shut down the escalator from level 1 to level 3. I see elderly folks struggling to climb up and down. For those of us who can walk, it's not a big deal. I see people who are able-bodied waiting for the already small lift, while people who really need it are waiting too.

Food was absolutely Nom Nom! This is the 2nd time I’ve been to Lola’s (1st was around 3 years ago), and I’m glad to say that the quality and service is still top notch!  The hojicha oat latte is a sure must get cuz it’s just that good hahahah. For the sides, Brussels sprouts were well seasoned, and chicken wings were crispy and meaty. For the mains, the pasta was al dente and flavourful, and the salmon was really nice as well. The pistachio strawberry tart was super yummy as well!!! It was quite busy when we went but the food was pumped out of the kitchen surprisingly fast, around 5-10mins after ordering, everything came.  10/10 will recommend coming back to try new things!!! Be sure to get a reservation cuz walk ins had to wait for quite awhile!

We had heard that Lola serves pretty good food, and we've passed by several times, always seeing the place packed.

This time, we visited at around 1.45pm on a weekday, but it was still a full house. We took a queue number, with two groups ahead of us, and waited about 30 minutes before being seated.

Overall, the food is decent by Singapore café standards. Considering the quality, taste, and portion sizes, it's reasonably priced and offers good value for a café in Singapore.

The desserts were nice too, especially the tiramisu.

Some feedback on the food: the salmon was quite fishy (I'm a salmon lover, and I found it fishy too). The Brussels sprouts were rather oily and over-fried, with the inner layers also being fried, leaving them bitter and greasy.

As for the Strawberry Pistachio Tart, the combination felt a little off. The strawberry and pistachio didn't complement or enhance each other's flavors. Instead, when eaten together, it had a strange aftertaste that reminded us of plastic or an electrical taste—we couldn't really describe it, but it felt quite off.

Other than that, the overall food, dining experience, and environment were good. I would recommend visiting during the non-peak period between 3pm and 5pm. Outside of those hours, the restaurant is usually very busy.

Had a truly delightful dining experience at Lola’s Restaurant @ Simon Road ❤️

From the moment we stepped in, the warm ambience and cozy atmosphere made it such a relaxing place for family gatherings and casual catch-ups. The service crew were attentive, friendly, and made us feel very welcomed throughout the evening.

The food was beautifully presented and packed with flavour. The seafood dishes were fresh and satisfying, especially the creamy prawn and mussel combination paired with toasted bread — rich, comforting, and perfect for sharing. Their pasta dishes were equally impressive, with generous portions and bold flavours that kept everyone wanting more.

Not to be missed were the desserts 🍰✨
The waffle dessert with ice cream and cheesecake bites was indulgent yet balanced, making it a sweet ending to a wonderful meal.

20th Apr 2026.
Brunch at 1030hrs, soon after opening, there are already diners.
Ordered the pulled pork with an extra avocado & miso salmon.
The pork is shredded, easy to chew. Missy had the salmon.
Meal was washed down with Hojicha latte, same for missy with Chai latte. Quite a small cup, but enough.
Signed up for member which came with a foc cake & 10% off the bill on birthday month. Very nice touch.
The young staffs are customer friendly, even offered to take photos for us.
Nice place.... Quiet ambience except for the two ladies who chatted nonstop at a volume louder than the rest of the diners.

Nice place to have a simple dinner with friends. Love the food, dessert n beer. Miso salmon grain bowl, fish & chips, Tuscan Seafood Bowl & chicken wings were what we ordered. All were yummy! Dessert - carrot cake n lemon meringue tart : reduced sugar would b better. Overall, nice place!

My review is mainly on the hawker centre in the community club. This is one place where you can find reasonably priced decent local food such as Malay, Indian and "Chinese" food such as bak chor mee. You can expect to pay about S$7 for a main course and a drink. The place has plenty of fans so it's actually quite a nice place to sit down and enjoy a cup of coffee. For obvious reasons, it does get crowded during lunch hours but during off peak hours, it's a good place to chill.

Quiet in the morning. It was a Tuesday. Many food stalls at the hawker centre were closed. Food lacks variety. It is disappointing that the food vendors  inside the hawker centre do not accept CDC vouchers. The community club does not offer a variety of courses to cater to the community. Mainly exercise courses like zumba and yoga.

Management please look at your dishwashing crew. After washing, both cutlery like metal spoons and fork are super oily.

Also, they don't wash it properly. Many a time I have found bits of leftover curry and other stains on supposedly clean utensils.

Do better.

The place has a big hawker center with wide variety of food choices at reasonable prices. Ample sitting space and car parking space.

Good Oyster Omelette, Wonton Noodles and Handmade noodles.

There are also a few small eateries on the first floor.

The hawker centre is quite clean and well-maintained, which is a plus. However, not all the stalls are open. I have visited three times recently, and each time many stalls were closed. It was disappointing not being able to eat what I wanted. On the bright side, it’s not crowded and seats are easy to find. Overall, it’s comfortable to dine in, but the limited number of open stalls affects the experience.

result.name: Star Learners @ Hougang Central | Preschool & Childcare

reviews.text:
I have been a parent with Star Learners @ Hougang Central for the past 4 years, and my child has grown up in the centre from a very young age. Over these years, I have witnessed changes within the school, from changes in principals to changes in teachers. As parents, I think it's natural that we don't particularly like staff turnover because we value stability and familiar faces for our children.

However, over time, I've come to realise that change is something we cannot always avoid. What matters more is how the centre manages these changes and continues to provide a positive environment for the children.

One thing I have consistently observed is the steady leadership of Ms Audrey. Since late 2023, she has done a commendable job bringing the team together, maintaining a positive culture, and ensuring that the quality of education and care remains consistent despite the changes.

I would also like to thank the wonderful team of teachers and staff – Zheng Laoshi, Teacher Eunice, Teacher Wiyah, Xiao Yu Laoshi, Teacher Sue, Yi Ling Laoshi, Teacher Aliyyah, Nana Laoshi, Teacher Titus, Liu Laoshi, Auntie Xiao Wei, Auntie Yong and Teacher Juliana. While not all of them have taught my child directly, we interact with many of them during morning drop-offs and afternoon dismissals.

These daily interactions may seem small, but they leave an impression. The teachers are always warm, approachable and welcoming, and they make the effort to greet the children and parents. It gives me confidence knowing that the entire team knows the children and genuinely cares for their well-being.

Of course, there are teachers who have taught my child directly and have played an important role in my child's growth. But what stands out to me is that the care and positivity extend beyond the classroom teachers. There is a strong sense of teamwork amongst the staff, and it creates a nurturing environment for the children.

Over the years, I have seen my child grow in confidence, independence and social skills. The teachers have not only supported my child's learning but have also guided character development and encouraged positive values. As a parent, that is something I truly appreciate.

Thank you to Ms Audrey and the entire Star Learners (Hougang Central) team for your dedication, patience and care. It has been a meaningful 4-year journey for our family, and we are grateful for the love and support that our child has received throughout the years.

Thank you for all that you do. Please know that there are parents like us who genuinely appreciate your efforts, and we will always be behind you, supporting and cheering the team on.

My child has been with Star Learners @ Hougang Central since 2023. Since the change in leadership, my wife and I have noticed a significant transformation in the centre’s culture and the range of activities offered.

For example, there are now many more field trips than before. The windows are open and the blinds are up, as parents, we truly appreciate the transparency, as we are able to walk by and look in. Previously, the windows were closed and we were unable to see what was happening inside.

Ms Audrey stepped in at the end of 2023 and has remarkably turned the centre around. As parents, we communicate with other families, and all of us have witnessed the positive and meaningful changes she has brought to the centre. Together with her team of teachers, she has cultivated an environment that is positive, authentic and genuine.

My wife and I are very grateful to her and to the wonderful team. We highly recommend Star Learners @ Hougang Central to parents who are considering enrolment. Since Ms Audrey took over, the centre has consistently been full, with a waiting list in place, so do not hesitate.

Thank you to Star Learners @ Hougang Central for the heart and dedication you have shown in caring for the children.

The school provides a safe, friendly, and nurturing environment where children feel comfortable and happy. We truly appreciate the efforts of the teachers in making learning fun and meaningful for the kids.

Thank you to the entire school team for taking such good care of our daughter and helping her grow with love and confidence. We highly recommend this school to other parents.

As the year ends we'd like to take a moment to express our gratitude to Principal Audrey and all the teachers and staff at Star Learners Hougang Central, for being such great partners in educating and caring for our little one.

Our child joined PG at 18M (shout out to Teacher Ivy and Ee Leng Laoshi whom she fondly remembers and talks about from time to time) and progressed to N1 this year (shout out to Teacher Joana and Shi Min Laoshi, whom she's formed a strong bond with) and we saw such development since then! We really appreciate that Principal Audrey and her teachers are approachable and update on her progress even outside of the routine term reports.

Lastly, special shout out to Aunty Siow Wei for her gentleness and care for the little ones especially those who have a harder time during drop off (our girl used to be one of them but now she's ok!). Thank you Star Learners Hougang Central team for your dedication in all that you do!

I wanted to take a moment to express my sincere appreciation for the exceptional learning environment your school provides. The dedication of the teachers, the well-rounded curriculum, and the emphasis on fostering both academic growth and personal development have made a significant positive impact on my child What stands out most is the genuine care and enthusiasm educators bring to the classroom. Lessons are creative, engaging, and tailored to meet diverse learning needs, while open communication between staff and families ensures everyone feels supported. The school's commitment to inclusivity and values like kindness and resilience truly prepares students not just for academic success, but for life beyond the classroom. Additionally, the range of extracurricular activities, encourages students to explore their passions and build confidence. The facilities are well- maintained, and the emphasis on safety and community creates a welcoming atmosphere for all. Thank you for your tireless efforts in nurturing such a positive and inspiring educational experience. Keep up the incredible work! Warm regards, mother : jasmine Nguyen (ayden ang) K2

This is very great neighbourhood library with librarians who are very observant and go the extra mile. Especially I thank Ms Fazilah 9 Nov 2025 at 7.30pm. She goes the extra mile fuss free, efficient, responsive and responsible. She checked the return conveyor belt and took effort to accompany me downstairs to search shelf and all displays thoroughly. She is very detailed to get both call number and shelf number. She found the book at display within very short time and went back to her routine duty which impresses me with her work ethics.

Before Ms Fazilah helped me I tried to get the book as search shown available on shelf but cannot find. I approached another white hair lady who situated there. She gave up very fast after just finding from the usual numbered shelf which I told her I already done. Then just quickly dismissed me and gave me excuses impatiently that it may be on wrong shelf. This is although I explained I am very sure it should be somewhere in library because not many people in library and I saw no one using the book. So the previous staff did not help me much she did not even look at special display areas...

Thank you so much Sengkang library for having MS Fazilah such a rare, helpful exemplary staff who exhibits great time management and problem solving skill. I thank her for resolving patiently and quickly without excuse and not dismissing customer request.

We recently took our little one to Sengkang Public Library, which is conveniently located right inside Compass One. It made for a really easy family outing, as we could grab a quick bite at the mall before heading up. The library itself is quite spacious and felt very clean and well-maintained, spread across two levels. Our child absolutely loved the Early Literacy Area; they have some really engaging displays and the book modules, designed like traditional fishing baskets, were a nice touch that caught our attention. It’s a bright and welcoming space, perfect for little hands to explore.

While the children's section can get a bit lively, as expected with so many excited kids, there are plenty of other quiet spots throughout the library if you need a calmer environment. My wife and I appreciated the wide selection of books for all ages, and I even spotted a dedicated 'Tween Space' which will be great for our child in a few years. There are ample seating areas, so it's easy to settle down with a stack of books and spend a good hour or two. It’s definitely a solid choice for a family afternoon out, combining learning with comfort.

What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

The first thing I noticed that drew my attention when I walked in was this little Harry Potter castle, which was on top of a bookshelf. The second thing I noticed was that they had a huge selection of audiovisuals.

Once you walk a bit more towards the left, you’ll see a few bookshelves, and close to those bookshelves is a long white table. And near the white table there are 2 seats close to a window. I think that area is a really nice spot for reading, studying, and using your computers.

If you want straight, you’ll see comics, and a teen section. In between 2 bookshelves, there are 2 seats, for safe distancing. There is also a magazine section.

There is also a quick charge area. Near the quick charge area there are a few shelves of adult fiction.

Overall, I think the Sengkang Library is a nice library for spending a long duration of time with your computer, or having a book you want to read. You can also study in the area of the white table and 2 seats near the window.

This is a two storey library and that easily makes it better. If you feel like the children section can be loud, you may find other reading spots. Conveniently located inside the mall with lots of reading books for small children. Lots of library staff too to remind the children to keep quiet while inside the library. My daughters love to hang out in this library after school.

Located within Compass One shopping mall, the library covers an area of 2,136 square meters, New areas in the library include the Early Literacy Area where families can read and bond together, the Tween Space targeted at children aged 10 to 14, and a dedicated Adults section for quiet reading.  It was officially opened on 30 November 2002, comprising of 2 levels - it is convenient and accessible in the shopping centre.

This hawker has many good stalls and the place is airy enough. Our favourite is Zuzu, a Turkish stall whose butter rice and kebabs are excellent. Apparently they have two restaurants in Arab Street by the same name. The nasi lemak stall is also good with reasonable price but note that they tend to close anytime they like. Normally, they are closed on Fridays and Mondays. There are two soya bean stalls here, the lesser known one is cheaper. There is a stall selling home made fish cake which is very delicious. And the teochew porridge stall has a wide selection of home cooked dishes.

This place car park is crowded even though there are seats in hawker centre. The exit out to main serangoon garden way is always jam. Other than transport, there are crowd in nasi lemak stall, prawn noodle, pork soup and bread stall. Not always have all kinds of local delights.

I would give 10 stars if it is possible. I highly recommend trying the gong bao ji from the zi char store. The portion was generous, the caramelized onions complemented the chicken. It is as if the hawkers go through cryptobiosis on non opening hours to preserve their adenosine triphosphate energy solely for cooking. The price is also reasonable, not cheap nor expensive.

Seng Kee Bak Chor Mee is a must-try — the noodles are springy, the mushroom sauce paired with the chilli-vinegar sauce is flavourful with just the right kick, and the rich, comforting broth ties everything together perfectly. Easily one of my favourite bak chor mee stalls in Singapore. You can even check if their latest happenings on their Instagram @sengkeebcm

Serangoon Garden Market is a popular place.  Most people will come for breakfast and lunch.  Most of the popular stalls will close after 2-3 pm.  Famous stalls with Pig’s Organ Soup, Duck Rice, Kway Chap, Lor Mee, Western food .. the place is very crowded during weekends (breakfast and lunch).

result.name: Cherie Hearts @ Charlton (Most Reviewed Preschool In Kovan)

reviews.text:
I had the privilege of being an ex-student of Cherie Hearts @ Charlton, and even a decade later, the experience remains incredibly special to me. The school environment was always warm, welcoming, and thoughtfully designed, a place where I genuinely felt fun, encouraged, and excited to go every single day.

What truly made the experience unforgettable were the teachers. Educators like Ms Wang Li and Ms Brands Ng were not just teachers, but mentors who showed genuine care and patience. I still remember how they took the time to guide me through challenges, celebrate small wins, and constantly encourage me to speak up and express myself. 10/10 would recommend

Visited Cherie Hearts @ Charlton and was so impressed! The teachers are caring and patient, and the small class sizes let every child get full attention. Love how the kids learn through fun activities like messy play, water play, and outdoor learning. You can really see how strong their foundation is — no wonder ex-parents say their kids adapt so well to primary school. 💕

This is a kindergarten filled with love. The teachers are very patient and caring toward the children. After joining, my child adapted quickly, gradually developed a strong sense of security, and even started to enjoy going to school.
The school places great emphasis on the children’s all-round development, and communication with parents is always prompt and responsive.Overall, it is a kindergarten we truly trust and feel comfortable entrusting our child to. We are very grateful to all the teachers for their care and dedication.💗❤️

Our kid has been ultra sticky to us since birth and used to hate going to school / crying every day etc. especially when he was in a previous childcare center..
Since he joined Cherie Hearts @ Charlton, he has started to open up to his teachers and friends and now even gets excited to go to school when he wakes up in the morning!
He came a long way from dreading school to being excited and enjoying school, and we want to thank all the teachers and staff at Cherie Hearts for their hard work and love! It really is a very loving school and they genuinely care for each child's wellbeing.

Very dedicated teaching & management staff. Communication/feedback with teachers and management staff is easy. Homely friendly preschool. The school makes the extra effort to have a variety of learning activities e.g. excursion, community activities.

From Zhi kai
I highly recommend this tuition centre for anyone looking to see real improvement in their learning. The teachers are incredibly dedicated, patient, and knowledgeable, always taking the time to ensure every student understands the concepts clearly. Lessons are well-structured, engaging, and tailored to suit different learning styles.

What truly stands out is the supportive and encouraging environment—they don’t just focus on results, but also build students’ confidence and motivation to learn. Communication with parents is also excellent, with regular updates on progress.

Thanks to their guidance, I’ve seen significant improvement in both understanding and performance. A truly outstanding tuition centre—definitely deserving of 5 stars!

We’re extremely happy with the Science tuition at this centre! My son has shown great improvement in both his understanding and confidence, and we’re so proud that he achieved AL1 for his WA1 🎉
The teachers are patient, engaging, and really know how to explain concepts clearly. Lessons are structured well, and they focus not just on memorising, but truly understanding the topics. My son actually enjoys attending classes now, which says a lot!
Highly recommend this tuition centre to any parent looking to help their child excel in Science. Thank you for the dedication and support!

I am writing this review to express my sincere appreciation towards Good School. I am an express student who took the A math O Level examinations in 2025. At the start of my secondary 3 A math journey, i found myself struggling to keep up with the content taught at school as A math is a very complicated subject and it did not help that my school teacher was rushing through the topics. Hence, I made a choice and decided to seek help here at Good School. My tutor, Mr Zhang helped me thoroughly with my studies. From patiently explaining the complex topics to answering all my burning questions , i knew i could always count on Mr Zhang to help me understand them. Aside from the tutors, Good School also has many facilities such as the study corner to help provide a conducive study space even before class starts. If I were to retake O Levels again, i would no doubt choose Good School!

Mr Tan’s class has made a noticeable difference in my child’s English. Since joining his lessons, her confidence and language skills have improved a lot. Mr Tan is a friendly, patient, and dedicated teacher who truly cares about his students’ progress. He creates a supportive learning environment that keeps students engaged and motivated. I highly recommend his class to any parent looking to strengthen their child’s English foundation.

When I was in primary school, Mathematics was always my favourite subject. I used to score full marks in almost every test. However, once I entered Secondary 2, things became more complicated, and I began to fall behind. For my final-year exams, I even failed, almost having to repeat the year. That was when I found Mr Zhang and Good School Learning Hub.

From the very first lesson, Mr Zhang planned out the steps I needed to take. He made sure I rebuilt my basics and developed a strong foundation before moving into Secondary 3. In school, I often struggled to understand the teachers’ explanations and could not grasp the different methods taught. But Mr Zhang explained everything in his own clear and magical way, which made learning so much easier.

What made his lessons special was the way he taught — always creating a friendly and light atmosphere. He often used real-life examples or casual chats to make things interesting, and I can honestly say I always looked forward to his classes. As someone who usually hates studying and finds it hard to sit still, his lessons were the one exception — they never felt like a chore.

Joining Good School and being taught by Mr Zhang was truly one of the best decisions I’ve made. With his guidance, I went from failing to consistently scoring As. My parents and I are both deeply grateful for the effort he put into teaching me. More than just a tutor, he has been someone I could talk to and rely on for advice, and he continues to keep in touch even now that I am studying in the UK.

I genuinely appreciate everything Mr Zhang has done for me. His lessons have not only helped me academically but have also made learning enjoyable again. I could not have asked for a better teacher and mentor.

Iestyn John, Anglo Chinese School (Independant)
if you are looking for KKKL bus, can directly search KKKL Kovan on Google maps. Btw, this is the correct place, the bus and the operator uncle will call you soon. He can sense you are taking the bus.

Pickup location for KKKL Bus bound to Kuala Lumpur, there are a lot food options, has McDonald, and several restaurants near by, we were looking for currency exchange but was not able to find one. Find Uncle operator for confirmation and other instructions, he has a placard

This is the terminal for buses to melaka, kl, and more. If you were also confused by the 0 info that the bus operators give on the exact location, you're not the only one.

The guy who directed me to the melaka bus didn't have any signs too (he can just kinda tell that you're going to Msia).
Good bus stop located in Former Bus Interchange, service 53M and 115 are able to use old sawtooth berths. Boarding Area also has seats for commuters to rest on while waiting for their bus

Formerly Hougang South Bus Interchange was closed since 2004. Services are relocated to Hougang Central Bus Interchange.

Currently it now it serve as bus stop for some services, public carpark and private bus terminal for express buses.

Nice and quiet.  Library staff are helpful. But mobile data signal by Simba ( 1 bar inside, 2 -3 bars outside. not sure about the others) is really poor. Couldn't even login to "checking and secure my seat booking", thus the booking got canceled,  and went to someone else.

Queenstown library is a heritage building, rich in its own history. Spacious with two levels, kids area is on the first floor. The second floor for adults is really quiet with nice view over a green park.

building's exterior may look unassuming or even a lil spooky but the inside is really cozy and homely! lovely space, limited tables but plenty of seats

if you really want a table, they have a table booking system you can use to ensure you have one when you get there!

Nostalgia a plenty in this well preserved library that houses many childhood memories for some.

Located in the quiet side of Queenstown facing sky residences @ Dawson, short walk from Queenstown MRT, a cozy library to spend your time here.

2 levels served by a lift & stairs, lots of reading areas and I particularly enjoy the level 2 quiet reading room facing the greenery.

There are lots of nostalgia and nice touches to this library.

Take your time to explore, read, borrow or exchange. what a privilege!

As of April 2026: It is a old school library with the kinda of nostalgia you get when you visited a library in the 2000's.

Most of the Library's floor space is dedicated to books with working space being rather limited. It's not very convenient to access either as you gotta walk abit from the MRT station and not much amenities nearby.

The tables tend to get booked out until like 7pm ish. With patrons playing musical chairs where someone who has a booking asks those who didn't make a booking to move.

Interesting mix of patrons, with elderly men being the majority and somehow quite a few expats.

For anyone who's experienced the thrill of a National Stadium event – be it an electrifying concert or a nail-biting sports match – Stadium MRT Station (CC6) is your gateway to the events held at the Singapore National Stadium.

Located within minutes walk from the stadium, convenience is just the beginning. This station consistently stands out for its well maintenance, cleanliness, spacious, and excellent ventilation, ensuring a comfortable experience even on the busiest event nights. What truly sets Stadium MRT apart is its unwavering efficiency. You'll always find additional staff on hand, guiding foot traffic and managing crowds with a seamless flow. They even go the extra mile, posting last train schedules upon your arrival, so you can plan your exit with peace of mind.

And then there's the "mini after-party" – a unique and brilliant crowd control strategy implemented after concerts. It transforms what could be a chaotic rush into a surprisingly organized and even enjoyable experience. BIG KUDOS to the station management team for the commitment to a smooth and pleasant journey for every event-goer.

Stadium MRT Station is a modern and well-designed station that serves as a key gateway to Singapore Sports Hub. The station is clean, spacious, and clearly signposted, making navigation easy even during large events or peak hours. Its wide platforms and multiple exits help manage crowds efficiently, especially on concert or match days. Accessibility is thoughtfully planned, with lifts, escalators, and barrier-free access for wheelchair users and families with strollers. The station’s sleek architecture and open feel add to a pleasant commuting experience. Connectivity is convenient, with sheltered walkways leading to nearby venues, parks, and waterfront areas. While it can get crowded during major events, train frequency is usually increased to cope with demand. Overall, Stadium MRT Station is reliable, efficient, and well integrated into its surroundings, making it a comfortable and practical stop for both daily commuters and event-goers.
Just want to say a shoutout to our SMRT staff at stadium, Hisham who assisted me today, around 7.25 to 7.30pm. Really appreciate his kindness and willingness to help. Kudos to him!
