Excellent, love at first sight, the very first impression of taking escalators down the train boarding area was a feeling of going down to a swimming pool themed of it’s unique green coloured floor ceramic tiles, just love this vibes, the place was discreetly cleaned and neat, it’s my first time dropping by here , the connectivity to Kallang leisure Park was only stone throw never seen such convenient and well planned and thought of, link between a shopping mall with covered walkways, u have done it so well SMRT, kudos to you guys.

One of my favourite MRT stations in Singapore! Always really clean and well maintained, which makes for a great first impression especially for foreigners visiting events around the Sports Hub or Indoor Stadium. Also sheltered walkway to the Kallang Wave Mall and Kallang Leisurepark. Always plenty of helpful staff around, especially for big events like the ongoing Taylor Swift concert so people know where to go. I love that their staff also make sure commuters on wheelchairs can board the train safely.

Leisure Park Kallang is a laid-back shopping center with just the right amount of traffic—not too crowded, making it comfortable to explore.
It offers a bit of everything: a kids’ arcade, tuition centers, a bowling alley, a food court, and numerous restaurants and cafés.
Overall, it’s a good place to visit if you prefer a quieter, more relaxed atmosphere away from the usual mall crowds.

This mall, especially when viewed against the backdrop of its more prominent neighbors, is relatively underwhelming.

While it boasts a good selection of restaurants and food options, the overall shopping experience feels somewhat haphazard in my opinion. It's certainly not the first mall that comes to mind when you're looking to do some serious shopping.

Given that the primary patrons of the Kallang/Stadium area are typically athletes, sports enthusiasts, or concert-goers, I can only envision them visiting this mall predominantly for its food offerings.

Very near MRT, the shops here always opened and closed forever more like pop up stores as business has been bad with weak traffic after the open air carpark has closed for the temp swimming competition but when it is over since late 2025, the structure seems to be left there rotting. The level 1 food court is nice, very big supermarket at B1 Sheng Song. Open and shelter carpark lots available. Many eateries with Burger King as the only fast food and a KTV outlet beside it. Level 2 is very boring apart from a bowling allery. Nothing much for tourists to shop or see.

Walking distance to Kallang Wave Mall.

Awesome supermarket Sheng Siong experience! 😊 Rarely do I rave about supermarkets, but this one nailed it. Went on a Friday late morning - no crowds, spotless, HUGE, and super well-stocked. Layout's beautiful, service is on point (just like any other SS), and parking's a breeze. Can't ask for more, what a gem!

Quite an underwhelming & empty shopping mall located near Stadium MRT. This mall has many empty shops, questionable empty spaces, as well as rooftop which is only accessible to the management office workers. I see a need for this mall to touch up on its space management and administration to make it more vibrant and increase the number of visitors

Awesome running track with canopy! You can go easy or treshold, anything you like to go that day! No worry of raining. The colorful track also boost the feeling. Although a lil bit “effort” to go here, but it is worth it!

We attend the Monster Jam 2025, Epic trucks, but Pit Party value gap — no freebies for kids.

We paid extra for Pit Party access and walked into… a merch zone. No free keepsakes at all—just items for sale and a small colouring table. For a premium add-on aimed at families, that’s a miss.

What the Pit Party should include (at minimum):
•	Free autograph cards or mini posters for each featured truck
•	Sticker sheet or Pit Party “passport” kids can stamp at each truck
•	Lanyard/badge (souvenir access pass)
•	Foam earplugs for children (practical + appreciated)

If nothing physical is possible, at least offer a QR code for printable posters/cards and a digital pit scavenger hunt with a small prize pickup.

There’s also a two-hour dead zone from pit close (4:00 pm) to show start (6:00 pm). Schedule some pre-show content—driver Q&A, trivia on the big screen, or early-seat entertainment—so families aren’t just waiting.

On-track: the stunts were fantastic.

Audio: the PA was muddy and unintelligible for interviews/commentary—in the pit, at our seats, and around the concourse. Needs proper EQ and coverage.

Safety: our exit staircase had two failed lights. One staff member with a torch stood by the second; the first was unlit and unattended. Please fix lighting and staff both points.
Great place! Visited OCBC Arena for Singapore Smash 2026. The staff was reliable and helpful, especially Shafiq from the gym who helped me understand the gym and opening hours! 谢谢
Best place to be at in Singapore for sporting events. State of the art facilities and good crowd control throughout.

Good functional sports facilities hub and stadium. Transportation is terrific with the MRT station next to the stadium and also bus stops nearby. The stadium was well-constructed for large scale events and the effects were spectacular. Unfortunately it was raining when I was attending a concert. Instead of using the sheltered walkway, due to poor planning and organization of the organizer, the spectators were made to queue in the rain for bag check. This resulted in unnecessary hassle and unpleasantness of the experience 😒

This MRT theme is Old Singapore art paintings.

Take time to observe, we could see four to five old Singapore paintings showcased.

The one at  train level is the big art showing Sim Lim towers and old Auro Rickshaws. These are very heart touching and remembering our old Singapore life style.

This MRT has a small Backery and Cloth Store as well inside.

One thing must to say, we need to go down 5 levels to catch train means this is one of the deepest MRT in City.

The ramp / walkway leading up to the escalator is so dirty and looked like it have never been clean before with so many stains left for months or even longer than that. Upkeep of cleanliness here is 1 of the worst among most MRT stations. Passenger service is one of the worst here.

Singapore's MTR and transportation system is not a question at all. The train were quite new and well maintained, clean inside and fewer passengers. The MTR system map is also accessible online. Jalan Besar station were near cheaper hotels and asian cuisine. Please follow my fb at Cybercode Shan and Shan Cabillan on Tktok for more travel updates!

Beautiful clean environment.
There's a shop called Stella, Super comfy leggings and palazzo pants. Bought few 3/4 leggings and nice tank top. Material is super cooling and best for this Sg weather.

Nice very calm place.
We can buy jewels, cloths, fruits and many more there.
It is nearby little India and Serangoon Rd, you can vist temples and more fun over there.
This community has quite a big multi purpose hall and the concrete yard next to it. Good to hold an event there.

My old dad went to enquire about CNY 8 course dinner for 28th February event and was told off by a aunty lady that there are no such events!!! Was shocked as the banner posted by whampoa community clearly stated tickets will be on sale in this cc....Is my dad being told a lie?very upset that the staff generally are very rude and careless for the elderly living here! I hope involved personnel will look into it

A Community Centre with space for the
young and old.

Convenient courts for badminton! The place was clean, and easy to find. Patrons are respectful, and ended their sessions at the allotted time, no additional admin process required. One minor downside, is the spot lighting above the courts can make it hard to see the shuttlecocks flying through the air. The toilets are passable, but not the best for changing in/out of sports wear.
Proud of this community club nearby my HDB Block. This is a wheelchair friendly and the staffs are helpful to elder people and you can ask for any assistance 🐕‍🦺🙂

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

We had a wonderful stay at Lavender Hotel in Singapore for 3 nights and 4 days. The hotel was very clean, comfortable, and well maintained. The staff were friendly, welcoming, and always ready to help with any questions.

The location was excellent, with easy access to public transport, restaurants, and nearby attractions, which made exploring Singapore very convenient.

Our room was clean, comfortable, and had everything we needed for a pleasant stay. Overall, we had a great experience and would definitely recommend Lavender Hotel to anyone visiting Singapore. We would happily stay here again!

There are constant leaks from the ceiling and escalators are always under repair. Snaking queues are not uncommon. Manually driven trains are rarely on time and have the same push-pull effect as in taxis of Singapore, no easing into gas or brakes. Most delays are not captured on MRT uptime metrics either.

From Lavender station green lane, you will arrive at the Changi Airport.

lavender MRT Station is quite near to the Airbnb where we stayed last October 17-21, 2025.

Not even a flagship station, this is nice. Clean, everything worked, directions were clear for even a first-time user. Teller who issued my card was helpful. Trains were timely and clean. No problems using the system at all. Add extra funds to the card when you buy it, you will go through the initial money quick.

Went there to take the bus for Kuala Lumpur. It's close to the hotel boss and it's a busy place. There are a few shops and restaurants there to do some window shopping or to have a quick bite. As we were waiting for the bus but unfortunately there was no such seating arrangements for the passengers. Our waiting time was more than 40 minutes, and we enjoyed the surroundings.

This club offers a wide range of facilities, making it the perfect spot for both relaxation and active recreation.

🏊 Swimming Pool – The pool is well-maintained, clean, and spacious. Perfect for both casual swimmers and those looking to get in some serious laps.
🎳 Bowling Alley – A fun and well-kept facility with great lanes, making it ideal for both beginners and experienced bowlers.
🎾 Tennis Courts – The courts are in excellent condition, and booking was smooth. Great place to enjoy a game with friends.
🧗 Wall Climbing – A highlight of the club! The climbing walls cater to different skill levels, and the instructors are friendly and helpful.
🎉 Function Rooms – Well-equipped and spacious, making them perfect for private gatherings, meetings, and celebrations.

The overall atmosphere of the club is fantastic, with a welcoming staff and well-maintained amenities. Whether you're here for sports, relaxation, or social events, CSC has everything you need. Highly recommended!

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Civil Service Club @ Tessensohn is a really cosy and warm recreational place for Singaporeans to have bonding sessions and exercise. I look forward to visiting Tessensohn more often! The place is clean and spacious

Plentiful of facilities including a Chinese restaurant and games room on level one. There is also a bowling alley. The parking lots are sufficient and signages are adequate to help you find your way around. The club is nicely lit up with a  cheery ambience.

Very rude old male staff in red. Name starting with A****s. Unprofessional and disrespectful. Tone like talking down to you. Scowling and hostile. First time coming across someone so contemptuous like you are beneath him. This kind of staff with bad attitude needs to be held accountable. Club reputation  down the drain.

Good community club which has badminton court, swimming pool, cafes and much more.
There are 4 badminton courts.

Small station, big convenience! Excellent local market with economical rates and fresh fruits. Conveniently located Value Dollar shop right outside Exit B. The food court is a highlight, offering plenty of tasty Chinese food options and a good mix of retail shops.

Boon keng MRT is the best place to stay for student S Pass and E pass people because nearby is gurudwara and little india. Many india and foreigner visited to this place for street shopping and delicious 😋 food

Boon Keng MRT Station (NE9) is an underground station on the North East Line (NEL), serving as a vital and highly accessible transportation node for the historic Kallang and Boon Keng neighborhoods. Named after the prominent 19th-century physician and philanthropist Dr. Lim Boon Keng, the station beautifully anchors an area where rich heritage seamlessly meets modern residential convenience.
The station features three strategic exits that open up directly into the vibrant heart of the surrounding estate. Exit B and Exit C are highly popular transit points, placing commuters right at the doorstep of the bustling Bendemeer Market & Food Centre, traditional shophouses, and public housing complexes. The layout is clean, efficient, and well-designed, allowing for a smooth and fast flow of passengers even during peak commuting hours.
* **Culinary & Retail Gateway:** Provides immediate, sheltered access to legendary local food options, morning markets, and everyday neighborhood amenities.
* **Art in Transit:** The station's interior features the artwork *Metamorphosis* by Lim Poh Teck, which uses bright, geometric shapes to symbolize the area’s continuous growth and vibrant transformation over the years.
* **Seamless Connectivity:** Offers an effortless, direct connection to major city centers like Dhoby Ghaut, Chinatown, and HarbourFront, making it a perfect hub for both daily commuters and weekend food explorers.

Boon Keng MRT Station is a clean and efficient transport hub located in the Kallang area, offering convenient access to nearby residential neighborhoods and amenities. The station is well-maintained, with clear signage and helpful staff, making it easy for commuters to navigate. While it’s not as bustling as some of the major interchange stations, its accessibility to buses, shops, and eateries adds to its practicality. The surrounding area has a local, laid-back vibe with wet markets, hawker centers, and small businesses nearby. Overall, Boon Keng MRT serves as a reliable and straightforward stop for daily commuters and those exploring the heartlands of Singapore.
A nice MRT stop with a good location and shops and restaurants nearby; it also has a long underground connection to the HDB south on Serangoon making it easy to connect without going outside.

The library is always an ideal sanctuary for those who appreciate some peace and quiet.
It has been years since my last visit, and it now appears to have expanded, with an entire upper floor dedicated to the Children’s Section.
Clean, well-maintained, and serene, it’s a place where one can truly enjoy a sense of calm and peacefulness.

Choa Chu Kang Public Library at Lot One is a very modern, clean, and well-designed library that is conveniently located right next to the MRT station and inside the mall. The interior feels bright and welcoming, with spacious layouts and comfortable areas for reading and studying

I especially liked the contemporary design touches and the creative displays throughout the library, which make the space feel more vibrant and inviting. Despite being in a busy suburban mall, the library still maintains a calm and relaxing atmosphere

Pleased with my visit here and how clean, modern and spacious the library is. Plenty of seating areas and even a level 5 kids area to entertain the kids. Great selection of books and many assortment of seats to accommodate many people. I also liked that borrowing a book via the NLB app was such a breeze!

Choa Chu Kang Public Library is a well-designed and cozy space for readers of all ages. The library has a good collection of books, magazines, and digital resources, along with plenty of comfortable seating for studying or reading quietly. The layout is modern, spacious, and family-friendly, with dedicated sections for children and teens. It’s also very convenient since it is located inside Lot 1 Shopper’s Mall, making it easy to drop by after shopping or dining. The staff are polite and helpful, and the environment is peaceful — a perfect spot for learning, reading, or simply relaxing.

I think that the Choa Chu Kang Library is very spacious, especially when I walked in. Once you walk around the fiction shelves, you’ll notice that there’s this seating area of 6 chairs around this round table. That’s a good place to work on your computers, study, and read.

Once you see that, just walk towards the window and look left and right, you’ll notice there’s this long table connected to the wall, and multiple people can sit there. That’s a really nice place for reading, studying, and working on your computers. The view is also nice when you look out the window, depending on where you’re sitting though.

They have this specific Malay section of books, and there’s 3 small sections that are kind of curved in, and about 4-5 people could fit in there. Once you walk a bit farther, there’s this section with teen books, and you’ll have to go down a few steps, but it has a really nice section.

Once you’ve passed the teen section, you’ll see stairs going up. If you go up those stairs, you’ll see the children’s section. First you’ll see the family and parenting section; then to the right, you’ll see the children's books.

Overall, I think the Choa Chu Kang Library is nice and spacious, and has a good selection of different books. Nice area for sitting. A good place to read, study, and work on your computers.

The Star Vista is one of the more spacious and pleasant malls in the Buona Vista area. I like the open layout, high ceilings, wide walkways, and the large central atrium, which makes the mall feel airy and less cramped compared with many other shopping centres

There is a good variety of restaurants and cafes here, ranging from casual meals to dessert places and family-friendly dining options. The recently opened Yo-Chi also seems very popular, with a long queue and lively crowd, adding more buzz to the mall

Another plus point is The Star Performing Arts Centre, which makes the place more than just a regular mall. It is a convenient spot for meals before or after events, and the overall environment feels clean, spacious and comfortable

Right next to the Buona Vista MRT station.
Convenient, airy ,spacious with 3 solid floors of eateries, cafes ,restaurants & supermarket.
If you are a owner of a weak mind you will die of over eating here !
In Chinese they say 多元化选择性障碍症 !

Christmas season is here again!
Children would think of Santa Claus, Christmas tree, snowman, Christmas gifts...
Christmas is about the birth of Jesus Christ, born in a wooden stable. But we often see a different scene. A very cooling beautiful white Christmas.
Still, this seaon is a good time to bond, to love, to share...
My family,relatives and I feasted in 'The Star' at Bouna Vista. Good food, good atmosphere, good environment
see the snow falling together. Wow! Great feeling.

Star Vista: An architectural landmark with a unique layout.

I visited the Star Vista at 2 PM on a weekend, and the mall was lively and crowded with people. From a distance, the building is very impressive with its bold, highlighted design.

The Layout & Shops:
Once inside, the layout of the stalls feels a bit more "legacy" compared to the modern exterior. There is a large staircase on the first floor which is a great spot to sit and rest for a while. The mall has a very diverse range of shops—from a Christian bookstore to a specialty bakery ingredient store.

The Location:
It is very conveniently located near the MRT station.

The Verdict:
While the interior layout is a bit unique, the building itself is stunning. It is definitely worth a visit if you are passing by the area!

beautiful uniquely designed building. pretty for some, ugly for others, no middle path haha. the food are are good. agood mixture of fastfood cafe food, restaurent and street food like fun toast and Pontain Wanton noodles. Japan home and Red man has a big shop here.Good that there is a CS Fresh here too.

It’s March 2026 when I write this. There is upgrading work going on, it will probably go on till mid 2028.
The public toilets have been refurbished, new entrance gantries installed. There are two lifts so much easier access for wheelchairs and those carrying children

MRT station and bus interchange with connections! LRT connects NS line to DT line at Bukit Panjang. Coverred walk ways connecting with Lot One shopping mall. Big field with alot of activities that I can do there, the gym is usually packed and it is right beside a swimming pool so I can cool off after a gym session. However, the distance taken to walk there is very long as it is very far away from woodlands mrt so that's why it's not a 5 star.

Excellent place to do your laps. They have a nice locker room, lockers for hire at 40 cents a go, benches to lay down on or place your stuff. The life guards are always watching out for you and I felt safe doing my weekly swim there. Do follow the ground rules though to go with the flow.

Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve is one of the best places in Singapore to experience nature in its most authentic form. From the moment I entered via the Neo Tiew Crescent entrance, I was surrounded by lush greenery, peaceful trails, and the soothing sounds of wildlife

The reserve is exceptionally well-maintained, with clear signage, wide boardwalks, and excellent visitor facilities at the Wetland Centre. The elevated wooden walkways make it easy to explore the mangrove forests while enjoying beautiful views of the surrounding wetlands

One of the highlights of my visit was observing mudskippers hopping across the mudflats among the mangrove roots. It was fascinating to see these unique creatures thriving in their natural habitat. The reserve offers plenty of opportunities to spot wildlife, so keep an eye out for monitor lizards, crabs, and migratory birds

I was also impressed by the Mudskipper Pod, a beautifully designed wooden observation structure that blends seamlessly into the environment. It provides a cool and shaded resting point with panoramic views over the mangroves and the Johor Strait. The architecture adds a unique touch to the overall experience without detracting from the natural surroundings

A refreshing escape into nature! We enjoyed a slow and peaceful walk at Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve on May 27, fully immersed in the calm beauty of the wetlands. The walking paths were beautifully lined with lush trees, creating a naturally shaded and cooling environment that made the stroll especially comfortable even during the warmer hours.
The tranquil surroundings, gentle sounds of nature, and fresh air provided a relaxing break from the busy city atmosphere. Along the way, we appreciated the well-maintained trails and the opportunity to observe the rich biodiversity and greenery up close.
It is a wonderful place for a leisurely walk, nature appreciation, photography, or simply to unwind and recharge. Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy a peaceful and scenic outdoor experience in Singapore.

A beautiful and peaceful escape into nature! The mangroves, fresh air, and rich wildlife make every walk feel special. Perfect for a relaxing stroll or bird watching—especially in the early morning or evening.
Stay curious and keep your eyes open… you might spot a giant monitor lizard crossing the path or even a crocodile resting in the water. A little adventure awaits you here!

My family took a late afternoon walk down to Sungei Buloh during low tide today to unwind, and it was a great way to clear our heads. The air had that familiar, heavy scent of salt and damp mangrove mud, and with the water stripped back, the exposed api-api roots and glistening mudflats completely changed the landscape. We spent a while just watching the giant mudskippers drag themselves across the silt and bicker over territory, which is always entertaining. The low water also meant the local birds were out in full force. We managed to spot a Great Egret freezing in place before spearing a small fish, and a Grey Heron standing completely still in the shallows waiting for Archerfish. Finishing up on the main bridge during the golden hour was the highlight, looking down into the shallow river channel at schools of Halfbeaks skimming the surface under the evening sky was incredibly peaceful and a solid reminder of how nice it is to have this kind of wilderness right on our doorstep.
Really nice nature walk. There is one bus that goes there. Can spend about 3-4 hours there. You get to see the swamps, facing JB, lizards, fishes, crabs, birds etc.

as all other mrt in singapore, yew tee is disabled/wheelchair friendly and yew tee square and yew tee point is a stone's throw away. easily accessible to convenience stores, NTUC supermarket, there is a fruit shop just outside the mrt station which sells a variety of fruits! overall clean and well maintained.

无法表达的情味, not about the look but rather the vibes. yewtee is special not because of physical built environment but rather the lack of it. Being often labeled as the worst precint in Singapore, the reality is that the lackluster environment is what makes yewtee shine. In our busy society, the most valued trait of a home is one that is quiet,normal and ordinary. 懂得知足才幸福

The location of Yew MRT station is considerably superior. It is reasonable to live nearby. Have numerous stores near the MRT. Two fast food outlets in a hawker center lot (Subway, macD, stuffD). There is a supermarket in the mall right underneath the MRT as well. There are also medical clinics and stores beside the MRT.

One of the main connectivity points with an LRT and interchange, CCK mrt station, is only one stop away.
The location of Yew MRT station is considerably superior. It is reasonable to live nearby. Have numerous stores near the MRT. Two fast food outlets in a hawker center lot (Subway, macD, stuffD). There is a supermarket in the mall right underneath the MRT as well.

This is a Redline MRT 4th station from the from Jurong East station This station provides the service likeDBS, OCBC, Axis ATM Machines , Fair Prices finest, And a Food court with 24hrs Mec-D, and the entrance provides one up and down escalator,two side staircase, and the centre of the entrance have the Lift it’s provided a wheelchair access with the security…

This place truly exceeded my expectations. The service was professional and timely, and the staff was very cooperative throughout. The environment was friendly and peaceful, and cleanliness was outstanding. I felt comfortable and well treated during my visit.

result.name: PlayFACTO School @ Woods Square | Student Care Woodlands

reviews.text:
I visited Playfacto Woods Square last year and met Ms Celine and Ms Wind. I love how cosy the centre feels and how amicable the ladies were.

I placed my boy in for adhoc care. On his first day, the teacher fetches him from the lobby and brought him to the centre safely. During the day, they also updated me how my boy was coping.

When I picked my boy, he shared that he like the teacher Ms Lee Lee and the student care very much! And he finds the food delicious too!
(I guess better than mummy cook 🤣)

As a parent, I feel assured that my child is in good hands and that he receives kind attention and care from the teachers. Thank you to all the staff for your heart ❤️ work in ensuring the children have the most comforting and nurturing environment after school. 👍👍👍

I recently had the pleasure of visiting this beautiful center managed by a friendly and cheerful family. From the moment I arrived, I was greeted with warm smiles and a genuine sense of hospitality.

The center itself was lovely with lots of natural light from the ceiling to floor windows, and the interior design was tastefully done. It created a warm and inviting atmosphere that made me feel right at home. The attention to detail was evident in every corner, from the carefully selected decor to the comfortable seating areas.

Overall I must say, it was a delightful experience

Had a very pleasant experience at the PlayFacto Open House @ Woodsquare this morning. Ms Celine and her team of teachers have been very welcoming and shared comprehensively on the curriculum at the centre. Such a warm and cosy envrionment that we decided to sign up immediately. :)

Excellent service right from the pre-registration phase, answering all inquiries promptly and giving sound advice throughout. Looking forward to my children joining them tomorrow onwards. Been to the place in person and it's very child friendly and cozy.

You should send your primary school kids here! This place is all positive vibes the real stars are the teachers and the centre director Ms Celine. They’re super friendly and warm, provide excellent updates on the progress of the little ones, and genuinely care about the kids. The place feels like a modern playground‑slash‑learning studio, with big sunny rooms and a really cosy vibe that the kids love so much. You have to see it for yourself! The teacher‑to‑kid ratio is also perfect and the little ones get all the help they need and finish homework before pick up. You’re paying for peace of mind at this centre, knowing your kid’s happy, learning, and super well cared for!

A bustling neighborhood mall! This mall is very convenient since it's directly linked to the Clementi MRT and bus interchange. It’s got everything you need across several floors, from a variety of shops and banks to a supermarket, food court, and even a library. It’s the perfect all-in-one spot for locals to grab a meal or run errands.

The Clementi Mall is a very convenient small-to-medium sized heartland mall, especially because it is directly connected to Clementi MRT station and the bus interchange. This makes it one of the easiest malls to access in the west, and it naturally gets very crowded on weekends and during peak hours

The tenant mix is practical and useful, with food options, shops, services, a supermarket, pharmacies, and casual dining places. It is not a huge destination mall, but it works very well for daily needs, quick meals, errands, and meeting up with family or friends

The lower floors can feel quite packed, especially near the MRT entrance and popular food areas. The upper floors are noticeably less crowded, so it is more comfortable if you want to walk around or browse at a slower pace

The Clementi Mall: A very convenient and modern shopping hub.

I visited around 7 PM on a weekday. The mall is directly connected to the MRT station, making it extremely convenient for a quick stop or a dinner visit.

The Shops & Atmosphere:
There is a great variety of shops and restaurants, including a section with many food stalls that feels like a mini-market. I was very surprised to find a massive library right inside the mall! It’s a great place to browse and borrow books in a modern setting.

The Food & Culture:
One "minus point" for me is that the mall mostly contains international brands and chain stores. I didn't find many small, local shops, so it might not be the best place if you are looking to explore traditional Singaporean culture.

The Verdict:
It is a perfect, air-conditioned spot for practical shopping and dining, but it feels more like a standard modern mall than a cultural destination.

This mall is small but very high-traffic because of the students and the MRT connection. It’s functional and has all the basics like a big library and plenty of quick-service food. If you want a more authentic experience, the Clementi 448 Market nearby is where the "real" West side food is, though it’s always hot and noisy.

Clementi mall is a place my family would go almost every weekends.
A place where we enjoy most of the local food at B1.  The recent visit at the B1 food area is disappointed, the indoor tempetature made us stay away of the poor aircon ventilation.

Visited this relatively new indoor playground at Mandai on Sunday with my 5-year-old, and here’s some takeaways for fellow parents.

Firstly, be prepared for crowds. It was very packed, so I’d strongly recommend buying tickets online in advance and avoid weekends. They do a pretty casual check for grip socks at the entrance, so make sure you and your child are already wearing them or be ready to purchase a pair on the spot.

Once inside, note that shoe storage is located on the 2/F, which can be a bit inconvenient when it’s crowded. That said, the playground itself is beautifully designed. All the themed zones are creative, vibrantly colorful, and really engaging for kids.

Given how busy it was, I was relieved to see a few staff members stationed around the play areas helping to supervise and manage the flow. Still, with the number of people inside, it’s important to keep a close eye on your child at all times.

Overall, great concept and design, but the experience would be much better if crowd control was improved. I’d suggest the management consider limiting entry or better regulating the number of visitors at a time for safety and comfort.

came on a weekday to make use of the unlimited playtime and so that it wont be so crowded!

the place is beautifully decorated with animals and nature, love the immersive/interactive displays (like art science museum ), the space is big and aircon so nice to run around on a hot day! plenty of seats for parents to rest!  toilets look clean!

downside:
1. they made parents buy grip socks even though there is nothing for adults to climb or run in. most other indoor playground are ok with adults in normal socks.

2.  my 6yr old was bored after 1 hour! i think its more suited for toddlers below 5? the slides were very few and climbing parts were not too exciting.

3. the shoes. a bit weird to have to carry ur shoes all the way to the center to put at the shoe racks. i guess it's because the exit is at the opposite end of the entrance!

4. not worth the price if i cant keep them entertained for 2hrs!

One of the largest indoor playground in Singapore. Perhaps because its new netball the equipment is clean and functioning and very well maintained. There is also ample staff in blue to help maintain safety and order. Friendly and vigilant staff. Especially on the slightly more risky elements like the web and the slides.

The weekday all day play is excellent value for money. My son happily stayed there for 4 hrs. There is plentiful shoe racks, places to keep bags and toilets too.

I would happily consider getting the annual pass just to come here every month on a weekday.

Although it’s designed as a playscape for kids, my friends and I (in our late 20s) absolutely loved it. From the artful illustrations and animations to the tactile textures and thoughtful details crafted for every type of child (literally and figuratively), it was an immersive, creative, sensorial and educational experience from start to finish, and we had a blast learning through play. Kids would love Curiosity Cove for sure. It’s nothing like the usual indoor playgrounds. They even have dedicated calm zones for kids and parents who need a short respite from overstimulation. For adults, it’s a great place to heal your inner child! 😆

Great place for young children to explore, learn, and play. The activities are engaging and educational, and the staff are friendly and helpful. My child had a wonderful time and was excited throughout the visit. A nice destination for families looking for a fun and meaningful outing.

In 2024. The shuttle buses fee :

From Zoo Back to Khatib MRT station

😱$2.5  For adults and children aged 7 and above
Children aged under 7 travel free.

The Singapore Zoo is truly world-class and an absolute must-visit for animal lovers and families.

Nestled in lush greenery, the zoo is well-connected and offers a serene, natural setting that feels like stepping into the wild.

The open-concept enclosures are exceptional, giving animals plenty of space to roam while allowing visitors to get up-close views. It’s clear that the zoo prioritizes the well-being of its animals, creating habitats that closely mimic their natural environments.

The orangutan exhibit is a standout feature, with these amazing primates swinging freely above you. The Rainforest KidzWorld is perfect for children, offering interactive exhibits and play areas. The feeding sessions and keeper talks are informative and engaging, giving visitors a chance to learn more about the animals.

There are multiple dining options within the zoo, ranging from snacks to full meals, and many are family-friendly. The breakfast with orangutans is a unique and memorable experience worth trying.

The zoo is well-maintained, with clean pathways, plenty of rest areas, and stroller rentals available for families with young children.

Tips: Arrive early to explore the zoo at a relaxed pace and catch the animal shows. Don't forget sun protection and comfortable walking shoes.

Overall, Singapore Zoo offers an educational and enjoyable day for visitors of all ages, combining conservation and entertainment seamlessly. A must-visit attraction in Singapore!
138, 926 and 927 buses stop here.
Now zoo provides non stop shuttlebus to Khatib MRT. Costs u only $1.

Awesome place with many facilities and some F&B establishments. Adventure HQ is fantastic for kids and adults alike, with a huge play area with many obstacles and slides, as well as climbing and bouldering areas. There's also a gym, a swimming pool, water park, though I haven't tried those.

Ample parking, and affiliate prices if you're a SAFRA/CSC/Passion card member.
I was attended by a Fitnessworkz staff (Syarf, if I did not spell his name wrongly). He is patient & shared good information. Gym is good & have quite a few Hyrox equipment. Good place to workout.
Highly recommend Deluxe Mancave for private gathering. Equipped with the necessity and able to create cosy ambience. Staff is very attentive and offer the help you needed.
Came here to collect Real Run 2025 race pack on the 2 October 2025. Just nice the 15th year of me joining HTNS Real Run

First time to this place. Went for a wedding function.
Love it. The Ballroom space is remarkably BIG and Spacious.
The carpark is also 👌 for it is so wide and good room space for cars NOT to squeeze.
Btw, look around and I guess this is quite a new place. All things are well organised and well maintained.
Bus captain dalvinder singh from mandai bus depot is the best
Worst depot after bulim,only 1 bus svc to go to tis depot.Pity the bus captains.Poor management too.Better think twice b4 joining as a bus captain.
Fun place
I will miss you SMRT
Very big bus depot

A good bowl of Lormee should have that thick, gooey yet smooth gravy and packed with flavour. Unfortunately, this Lormee at Yishun Park (早碗见面 Lor mee & Satay Beehoon) , gravy was diluted and has no umami taste.

Even after adding vinegar, garlic and chilli, it’s still pretty one dimensional. The only saving grace was their fried fish, nicely fried, soft and still has that slight crunchy exterior.

Damage: $4.80 (small bowl)

Family went there for lunch on a Saturday at around 1130H. It was not too crowded, and we managed to find a table without needing to wait. Parking was okay too.

The hawker centre was big and airy, we didn't feel hot. The tables were not so clean, and no one seemed to be going around to wipe the tables too.

We ordered (1) fish sliced soup, (2) Ming jian Kueh, (3) kids nasi lemak meal, (4) ice kachang.

Food is pretty much the standard stuff... just two things to say - (1) Shrinkflation is real - could have more fish slices in the soup, and (2) nasi lemak rice was fragrant but the chicken fillet was dry and tasteless.

Overall, still prefer Canberra hawker centre for it's range and quality of food!

I would like to introduce a delicious Pao Fan at Stall 01-35A if you are dining at Yishun Park Market, 51 Yishun Ave 11.

Look for Chef Kelvin — he is very polite and courteous. The fried fish is freshly fried, and every grain of rice is fragrant and perfectly cooked.

Highly recommended if you are in the area!

Honestly, this is the best food I've ever tasted. You won't find this kind of food anywhere else but in Türkiye or Lebanon.I recommend everyone try this new and sophisticated food with delicious flavors.

I stopped by for breakfast after spotting their Bubur Kampung, which looked too good to pass up. It didn't disappoint the flavors were delicious and the pricing was spot on. The place has a great atmosphere and is kept very clean. I’ll definitely be back!

the CC is a cosy and convenient place for community activities. It has clean facilities, friendly staff, and a welcoming atmosphere.
Great spot for classes, events, and neighbourhood gatherings.

I shouldn’t say this, but this is a good place to keep your health strong and away from the coronavirus! Free vaccination and booster shots at godly speed. The registering counter guys were extremely efficient and polite especially the younger boys.

Also, there’s a POSB/DBS withdrawal and depositing machines and ATMs for simple banking services.

Carpark is only for authorised personnels. So the nearest carpark would be the multi-storey HDB carpark right beside the centre.
Great place to play badminton or any sports related activities. Went to collect hand sanitizer and the staff were really efficient as well!
Nice and airy environment, clean facilities! Good place to come and take part in activities to keep healthy :)
I am sincerely thankful to the two ladies bringing foodstuffs to my home around 04.20 pm on 01/10/2025 at Woodland Rise, Block 785a. I will never forget this unique and touching experience. ❤️

One of the old malls that had been revamped.

We were here years ago and after coming back bout 15years later, the place looks really different!

Lets dive in!

Location:
Integrated with the bus interchange and mrt, this mall is considered a hub for residents living in woodlands. There are plenty of entrances and is easily accessible everywhere.

Amenities:
Other than a furniture shop, you can find almost everything here. We were in awe and the food selections that we can find here. Not forgetting the gadget shops that have competitive prices. They also have a prayer room, which we were glad to use.

Parking:
Despite the many accesible options around, this mall has lots of parking lots available., subjected to non-peak hours yeah😅. Alternatively, there are also other nearby areas to park actually.

All in all, if you are up for an exercise to walk, this is one place to head down to. After walking on all floors, treat yourself to shopping and a good meal too!

Highly recommended from us!

Cheers!

Explored Causeway Point in a day. Anchor tenants like Metro Store, Courts for electrical and furniture, Uniqlo Japanese fashion wear. Lots of F&B selections from Kopitiam Bagus halal food court, Food Republic food court, Chinese, Japanese, Korean & western cuisines including lots of fried chicken - Texas, KFC, Jollibee, 4Fingers , Chagee, and lots of BbTea shops. Bakeries & Patisserie like Swee Heng, Annabelle, rrooll. You can spend a whole day exploring beautiful home decor at Madera and kitchen needs. To pamper yourselves with a pair of new glasses or shades from the few optical stores as well as beauty salons for hair and nails. Nostalgic snacks store, automated machines for the SGFR viral snack, Popmart machines can be found too.  From Causeway Point basement it will lead you to Woods Square and Woodlands Civic Center. Highly recommended to visit.
Wow many eateries at the basement offering good food here. Also first floor have a lot of nice shops selling food for takeaway all in one shopping mall.

Causeway Point is one of the older heartland malls in Singapore. Located just outside Woodlands MRT station, which is now an interchange to TEL line, and a bus interchange, it is in a very strategic location which explains why it’s never not busy. Causeway Point is also connected to Woodlands Civic Center and Woods Square. Beside it is also a location for occasional temporary Pasar Malam.

So, if you don’t like people, this place can be hellish. On the other side, it’s also a very convenient place to get everything you need from grocery, stationery, household items, electronic, pet stuff, food, everything you need.

The mall has everything, from Cinema to Yakun, Popular bookstore to FairPrice supermarket. There are many food varieties to choose from including two food courts. There are Metro department store and Courts. There are pharmacies like Watson, Guardian, and Unity.

Pros: You can find almost everything you need.

Cons: People! So many people!

Love ❤️ Causeway Point.

It’s near my house 🏠 and it has a wide variety of stores under one roof. Satisfy all my needs.

My daughter and I often went there to eat.

Visited Chong Pang Hawker Centre recently and tried a few stalls… overall a really satisfying meal.

Started with Chuan Kee Boneless Braised Duck Rice. The duck was very tender and flavourful, with a nice herbal braised sauce that wasn’t too overpowering. I also liked that it was boneless, which made it easy to eat.

Then I had the char kway teow from Pin Xiang. Good wok hei, noodles were well-cooked and not overly greasy. Portion was decent and the flavours were balanced.

To finish, I got an ice blended watermelon juice from Fruit Palace. Very refreshing, especially in the heat… not too sweet and quite natural tasting.

Overall, a solid hawker experience. Would come back to try other stalls as well 👍

A wet market and hawker centre located in a local area under an HDB. People are very nice and welcoming. My go to every week for fresh fruit and vegetables. Hawker foods are amazing, from Chinese to Indian and every in between. Michelin Cendol here too and it is a must try!!
Good old hawker with a mox of variety of food choices. The famous Chew Kuah, Duck Rice, Fish soup and Porridge, delicious Nyonya Chendol are just a few to mention. Highly recommended.

Food lover can consider this places. But prepared to sweat if the weather turn hot! Today had tried this prawns noodle stall. First taste of the soup bring me back to my childhood life. Soup very authentic,  ingredients very fresh. And for the pork slice,  cooked to perfection tender.. I will come back for more . Alot of nice food too. Wish my stomach can eat from every stall. Hehehe. Only ＄4 for small bowl. But is more than enough for me.

Feb 25, this market is huge and plenty of food . Try the fish soup 01-147-‘s the Nonya Chendol.
Have the fish soup again, very good plus the chendol.
Try the Hainanese Chicken rice set at $5.80 plus a chicken wings - $8.10. Very good. Recommended to try.

Absolutely loved this! Well worth a visit, which took us most of the day. You could rush through it in half a day but you want to allow time to be able to spot all the birds and wait for them to come close. Such a wide variety of birds including from Australia, Asia and Africa, and loved the penguin exhibit where you can see above and below the water. Was very special to see Macaws and other rare birds so close up. Loved that you could walk through the netted areas where they were all just flying around freely. Lots of info and signage to learn about them. Accessible throughout and kid friendly. Bring your own bottle as there is water refill stations, plus a kids swimming area as well to cool off. Air conditioned rooms in between the areas was very welcomed as it's mostly an outside venue, although there's under covered areas in main entry and in some of the exhibits. Food options and places to eat on site as well including full meals or just light snacks and coffee.

Bird Paradise Singapore
​⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
​An Absolute Masterpiece of a Wildlife Park!
​As avid travelers who have visited countless sanctuaries and wildlife reserves across the globe, we can confidently say that Bird Paradise in Singapore is simply the most beautiful bird park in the world. It is a truly breathtaking experience from start to finish!
​What makes this park so spectacular is its incredibly thoughtful layout, which is cleverly organized by continents. Instead of traditional enclosures, you walk through massive, immersive aviaries that perfectly replicate the natural habitats of the regions they represent. The scale is unbelievable, and you feel completely transported as you move from one continent to the next.
​The variety of wildlife is astounding. We were particularly captivated by the magnificent, vibrant parrots, but every single aviary is teeming with incredibly healthy, active birds flying freely right around you. The design allows you to observe them up close in a way that feels completely natural and highly respectful to the animals.
​The lush landscaping, the giant walk-through environments, and the incredible avian encounters make this a five-star experience in every sense. We have traveled far and wide, but Bird Paradise has set an entirely new global standard. An absolute must-visit

A wonderful place to see a variety of bird life and very informative diagrams available for the little ones.

May get a little crowded, but you can plan ahead to get to the following shows.

Free team service is also available to both ends of the park if required.

Bonus points for having multiple nursing rooms around the park, and for keeping it sustainable and clean too!

Awesome park with an incredible variety of birds, from penguins to parrots. We were here for about 5 hours, the place is huge, so plan to spend a good bit of the day. There are nice air-conditioned areas between each park of the park, so you can cool down & read information about the birds. Definitely worth doing in Singapore & excellent if you're a photographer. Be aware that birds may land on you, I had two hopping on me for a while.

I recently visited the Bird Paradise Singapore and it was truly an amazing experience. The park is beautifully designed and extremely well managed, making it comfortable to explore even during long walks. I saw a huge variety of birds that I had never seen before in my life. There are so many types of parrots in all possible colors, from bright red and yellow to deep blue and green, and each section feels lively and vibrant.
One of the highlights of the visit was the large indoor waterfall area which creates a rainforest atmosphere and makes the entire environment feel natural and refreshing. The sound of flowing water along with birds flying freely around you makes the experience very immersive. I also loved watching the flamingos standing together in groups, adding a beautiful pink contrast to the greenery around them.
The walking trails are well planned and there are many interactive and educational zones where you can learn about different bird species and conservation efforts. Staff members are polite and helpful, and the overall cleanliness is excellent. This is a perfect place for families, nature lovers, and photographers.
It is recommended to try booking tickets in advance before going, as it helps save time and avoids long queues at the entrance. The park is spacious, well organized, and enjoyable for all age groups, and it easily takes three to four hours to explore comfortably without feeling rushed.

Depot for Thomson east coast line MRT, bus depot for future planning and a place of work for others.

Time flys for some people here, coming and leave with the flow.

But memories make will always be remember.

Happy birthday to you bro, with our very own mutabak birthday cake!
A lastly-open MRT Depot under SMRT. A wide area is occupied together with the Tower Transit Bus Depot.
Why sat Sunday no food, other days why closed 2pm
Mandai Depot is designated for the driverless MRT trains operating in Thomson EastCoast Line (STEL). It is also integrated with a Bus depot, which is 1st of its kind in Singapore.
A place that I will pass by when hiking to  Ulu sembawang park for the scenic sunrise when the sky is  clear.

Am I the only one feeling a bit hot in here? 🤔 Haven't come to this library and the cafe downstairs for a long time. I prefer here because many available seats. And I am someone who appreciates some little privacy so I can say it is better (than West Mall 😅) although we can't control the noise of some people. Hope they don't chit chat in the library...

Hope they can volume down a bit too. Even the Level 4 kids section was moderate. Convenient too, there is toilet. That's how I found out my menses comes rather sudden and unexpectedly 😭

Other libary blast the air con which is okay
This libary doesn’t bother to turn it on on the upper floors??? Booked S301 and when I came there was a pile of stuff there, told the librarian but she said cannot do anything.. the person never came back and I had to book another seat which this uncle was using my wall socket: asked staff again tell me don’t be so annoying then I put one of my power bank at the quickcharge station, staff told me cannot charge power banks?? It literally says you can charge power banks?

Convenient place to study. Relaxing and clean. The staff provided enough tables and chairs. But the only things is very limited socket to charge your laptop. It is better to fully charge your devices before come :)

Excellent place for self study at level 3. Level have cafe and sofa chair. And also charging station for phone powerbank and ipad etc. There is also workshop for enhancement on IT product. But might be different every month.

Don’t let the entrance deceive you. From the outside it may look a small and simple library. The lib actually do have 4 stories, and each story has a vast collection of books. Unlike other libraries, Woodland library may have the largest space all with comfy sofa or study desk. It also have a cafe if you need a quick coffee fix. Kids area upstairs.

Walking  distance to Yishun Town Garden Yishun Town Square hawker centre Sheng Siong Supermarket. MRT with beautiful views of Reservoir with breezy greenery away from busy cityscapes. Next to plenty of choices for local hawker delights after shopping banking grosseries postage dinning with hawker delights and Western Int'l delights supermarket amenities  and entertainments. One MRT stop to  Yishun

Walking  distance to Yishun Town Garden Yishun Town Square hawker centre Sheng Siong Supermarket. MRT with beautiful views of Reservoir with breezy greenery away from busy cityscapes. Next to plenty of choices for local hawker delights after shopping banking grosseries postage dinning with hawker delights and Western Int'l delights supermarket amenities  and entertainments. One MRT stop to  Yishun MRT bus interchange Northpoint City with  plenty of shopping banking dinning library postage groceries supermarkets services facilities and amenities  nearby. Friendly fast attentive cosy comfy welcoming ambience for romantic intimates groups students tourists Expats and families. Next to community clubs and residential committee.

The station layout one the first floor compared to the Yishun MRT station are similar. The FairPrice in the Yishun station is a GIANT in the Khatib station. The Yuu advertisements in the GIANT are good. The staff are friendly.

I like how there are areas that allow people to sit and wait (I know not if it was intentional). With a supermarket, a small eatery, a bakery, a few ATMs and an AXS machine, i think it has conveniently located facilities there as well. A particular cat can be seen there in the mornings as well (at the time of this review), and that's a big bonus to me.
Best way to get to Singapore Zoo, night safari, bird park and river wonders! The bus even has usb power points!!

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ An Absolute Must-Visit: A Magical Journey Through The World's Rivers!
​If you are visiting Singapore, River Wonders is an absolute must-see! This extraordinary park is brilliantly designed, taking you on an immersive journey inspired by the most iconic rivers of the world, from the Nile to the Yangtze. Every single zone is beautifully themed and educational.
​The absolute highlight of our trip was seeing the 15 magnificent manatees gliding gracefully through the massive freshwater aquarium. It is truly a mesmerizing sight to watch these gentle giants! Of course, we cannot forget the stars of the show — the two adorable giant pandas. Watching them munch on bamboo in their lush, climate-controlled enclosure was an unforgettable experience.
​Another thrilling part of our visit was the Amazon River Quest boat ride. Floating along the water while spotting amazing wildlife like jaguars and capybaras made us feel like we were on a real jungle expedition. There is just so much more to discover at every turn!
​Overall, this park is beautifully maintained, extremely engaging, and truly super beautiful. I highly recommend it for nature lovers and anyone looking for a unique adventure. Five out of five bright shining stars! Truly amazing!

River Wonders is divided into several zones themed around major rivers around the world, which makes the park feel more varied and easier to explore section by section rather than as one long continuous route.

Compared to the zoo, a significant portion of the park is air-conditioned and sheltered, making the experience noticeably more comfortable, especially in Singapore’s heat and humidity. Areas such as the giant panda enclosure and the aquarium sections housing the manatees offer a welcome break from the outdoors.

Some of the highlights include the giant pandas, capybaras, giant river otters and manatees, and my personal favourite - the red panda.

I found River Wonders more relaxing and easier to complete compared to the zoo while still offering enough variety to keep the visit interesting. It is also a good option for families with elderly visitors or for those who prefer a more comfortable pace.

River Wonders Singapore was a truly memorable and unique experience during our trip. One of the biggest highlights for us was seeing a panda for the very first time. It was such a special moment, and watching the panda up close felt amazing.

Another standout part of the visit was the giant freshwater fishes. Seeing such large and rare species in well-designed enclosures was both fascinating and educational. The exhibits are thoughtfully curated, giving you a chance to learn about different river ecosystems from around the world.

The entire place is clean, well-maintained, and easy to explore at a comfortable pace. The layout is well planned, and the atmosphere is calm and enjoyable, making it suitable for families and kids as well.

It’s a different kind of wildlife experience compared to a traditional zoo, which makes it even more interesting. You not only get to see animals but also understand their natural habitats better.

Overall, it was a wonderful experience, and definitely worth visiting. Seeing the panda and the giant fishes made it extra special and something we will always remember.

Visited on a weekday morning right after it opened for the day, so it was quite empty, save for occassional tourists.
Honestly, this place is quite underrated, there are many cool animals to see. I was also lucky enough to see both Kai Kai and Jia Jia 🙏
I would recommend spending the extra $5 for the amazon river quest as well, as you get to see more animals like flamingoes, capybaras and even a jaguar.
Overall, quite a decent attraction to visit. It is also mostly sheltered so the sun isnt shining down on you during your whole visit 🙏

PANDA 🐼 LOVERSSS, ASSEMBLE!!!

River Wonders Singapore felt like stepping into a magical jungle adventure full of cute animal friends 🌿🐼
Visited with my wife last June, and the absolute highlight was seeing giant pandas for the very first time ever. They looked like giant fluffy marshmallows just munching bamboo, taking cozy naps, and rolling around so adorably that we couldn’t stop smiling. 🐾

The whole park feels bright, fun, and super relaxing, almost like exploring a storybook rainforest. The river-themed zones, aquarium exhibits, and boat ride make every corner feel exciting and full of surprises. There are so many amazing animals to spot, and everything is clean, colorful, and easy to enjoy.

If you’re visiting Singapore, this place is a must-see — especially for panda lovers and little explorers. Just be prepared to leave with hundreds of panda photos, happy memories, and a big love for animals everywhere. ❤️

Pro-Tip: You can buy all inclusive pass for Mandai Wildlife reserve if you are planning to visit all 5 parks which will save you approximately 30%.

result.name: Gardens by the Bay East (Panoramic view)

reviews.text:
Great spot to go for a run, walk, bike tour. Quiet and away from the busy center. Beautiful skyline views BTW.
ATTENTION: i recommend to go in the morning, best chance for a shadow walk 😉

Gardens by the Bay East is one of the best scenic spots in Singapore. The panoramic view is amazing — perfect for photos with the Singapore Flyer, Marina Bay Sands, and the city skyline. It’s peaceful, spacious, and great for a relaxing walk or cycling. A must-visit if you enjoy beautiful views and quiet corners in the city!

Went here in the afternoon for a sunset bike ride and the view was stunning! You get the perfect panoramic skyline of Marina Bay while enjoying a more quiet and relaxed vibe compared to the main Gardens. Such a beautiful spot to catch the sunset and unwind.
Highly recommend visiting! You’ll get incredible photos here with a full panoramic view of Singapore’s skyline.

Bay East Garden offers a quieter, more tranquil side of Singapore’s waterfront beauty, unfolding beside the shimmering Marina Reservoir with sweeping views of the city skyline. Wide open lawns, scenic cycling paths, and breezy waterfront promenades create a peaceful escape from the urban rush. Unlike the dramatic spectacle of the Supertrees across the bay, Bay East Garden charms through simplicity, space, and calm natural elegance. At sunrise and sunset, the skyline glows across the water, transforming the landscape into a serene canvas of light and reflection. It is a place where nature, fresh air, and Singapore’s futuristic skyline exist in perfect harmony.
The gardens and the area are a nice walk and multiple places to purchase refreshments.  I walked a little over 5 miles without realizing it.
Go exercise and found this perfect spot to take photo of the MBS Bay back view. Met otters here, let me think what number should I choose for the next TOTO…
A nice place to have an enjoyable morning walk or stroll. Get to see nice sunrise, the sea and interesting birds like masked Lapwing and herons.

Nice cozy beautiful environment, enjoying family with kids cycling.
Breezy beach coast area, suitable for joggers too.
Highly recommended for family with kids and couples. evening view is picturesque

Breakwater@Marina East. Great place to unwind and have peace. Enjoy the tranquility, wind, breathtaking view and sound of waves. Sunrise is nice and night scene is amazing. If you are lucky (at night), some resting shore birds may keep you company.

If you want to explore St John, Lazarus and Kusu island, this is the ferry terminal that you want to come and take the boat.

2 different brands, Singapore Island Cruise and Marina South Ferry, with the former being more popular... tickets can be bought online with a simple Google search or via Klook which the latter can give you some discounts.

Nice southern island to get away from the concrete jungle of Singapore...

But best recommended to take public transport because parking lots are very very limited..
Interesting pier with some maritime exhibits going on now.  Parking is limited however, forced to park outside but ended up getting a ticket. Don't think this place is ready to handle exhibits.
Parking at MSP is always full. Be prepared to park on the street and be prepared for a fine. Just go by mrt and be worry free.

I definitely recommend a ferry ride to the southern islands of Singapore (Lazarus, St. John, Kuzu, Sisters) if you are in Singapore for more than a few days. Take a break from the city bustle and experience some peaceful beaches. There are a number of domestic ferry companies working out of Marina South Pier as well as international ferries to Batam and Bintan Islands in Indonesia.

The marina also features a viewing platform and an excellent and free maritime museum.

Be warned: There are no shops or eating places in the islands and wait times can be considerable. Bring your own food, snacks and drinks from the mainland.

Also, at times the sea can be pretty rough, so take that into account.

Pro Tip: I suggest travelling to the less visited Kuzu Island as opposed to the more crowded Lazarus (and St. John) islands. Or make a day of it and visit them all.

A Pier for domestic ferries to neighbouring SG islands. Decent facility but with limited car parking lots.

If driving, Suggest to park at Marina Bay Cruise Centre instead for better shade instead of letting car baked under the sun 😅 the down side is the 350m walk (sheltered) between MBCC and MSP

On 24 April 2025, at approximately 4:30 PM, a group of students in school uniforms were observed loitering and sitting on the floor at the entrance of the Cheers outlet located in Marine Terrace MRT station. While the students appeared to be taking a break or socializing, their presence created minor obstructions near the convenience store and along the public walkway. Such behavior may disrupt the flow of commuters and create an untidy impression of the station. It is recommended that the respective schools be informed of this incident, so appropriate reminders on public conduct and responsibility in uniform can be issued. Students should be encouraged to gather in more appropriate common areas or community spaces, and avoid sitting in commercial or high-traffic zones, especially in public transport facilities. Ensuring that students are guided with respect and accountability will maintain both their safety and the comfort of the broader public. MRT staff or school liaison officers may wish to follow up if such behavior persists during peak hours or in similar public settings.

Newly open TEL line that brings residents in the east and along the North-South Corridor closer to a train station and hence giving them faster access to the city.
Marine Parade MRT station is an underground Mass Rapid Transit station on the Thomson–East Coast line (TEL). The station is built underneath Marine Parade Road, serving nearby residential and commercial developments including Parkway Parade, Roxy Square and I12 Katong. Exit A next to McDonalds and Marine Parade Food Centre where you can find varieties of local delights.
Worth noting this station house Singapore's first underground bicycle parking facilities.
Direct link to Parkway Parade shopping mall have yet to be connected, once done it will be seamless connection the shopping mall.

New mrt station in the neighborhood. Well designed and build, comes with underground bicycle park and open air car park nearby. It’s so quiet that I didn’t realize a train had arrived! Able to reach Outram Part in 20min (instead of 23min using the Green Line). Environment was clean and very spacious!

This mrt station is super long in length, it takes a good 5 mins to walk from one end to the other. other than that, it's a standard mrt station with a cheers and bike park, nothing too interesting unlike marine parade which looks like the Xbox logo, but I'll give it 5 stars anyways since it does it's job well. It serves a lot of school students so yeah good job

Thomson East Coast Line (TEL) A very much needed addition to the est side , connecting easties to city and orchard .

Totally dig the huge ventilation fans for cool air circulation .

You can take the train to Marina Bay and switch to red line towards Orchard or continue on the TEL  to Orchard .

Parkway Parade has that rare old school Singapore mall charm that newer malls still cannot really replicate. It is not the fanciest mall around anymore, but that is honestly part of the appeal. The place feels comfortable, familiar, and genuinely lived in by East side families instead of trying too hard to become a luxury lifestyle destination.

You can pretty much do everything here in one trip. Good food options, decent retail variety, supermarkets, electronics, enrichment centres, cafes, and random nostalgic shops that somehow still survive after all these years. The recent refreshes and additions like MUJI and the revamped food options helped modernise the mall without completely removing its identity. Direct MRT connectivity also makes visiting way more convenient now.  ￼

What I still enjoy most is the atmosphere. Compared to some mega malls that feel stressful and overcrowded, Parkway Parade feels more relaxed and easy to navigate. There is a strong sense of nostalgia here especially for people who grew up in the East. Even online discussions always mention memories of the old arcades, bookstores, rooftop playgrounds, and family weekends spent here.  ￼

Some parts of the mall do feel slightly dated and there are noticeably more vacant units recently, but overall it still remains one of the most iconic and dependable suburban malls in Singapore. Perfect place for a chill weekend without the chaos of town.

Parkway Parade is one of the most popular mall in the east area.  This mall is very crowded during weekends. This mall is connected to the Parkway Parade MRT and hence very convenient for commuters. The Kopitiam Food Mall is located at B1.

A return to a place I frequented during my army days in the 80s. 再次重游，记忆犹新! It’s amazing to see how much has changed, yet how familiar it still feels. Plus, with the new Marine Parade MRT station open, visiting this iconic spot is now easier than ever.

Nu Skin cleansing foam leaves the skin feeling deeply cleansed, soft, and refreshed. Its gentle foaming formula removes dirt, oil, and makeup without drying the skin, while hydrating ingredients help maintain a smooth and healthy glow. Perfect for daily use for clean, fresh, and radiant skin.” best place to buy is Venus beauty at parkway   prade

Fabulous soursop juice was SO SO DELICIOUS. I had to buy 3 cups as they were so so so yummy.  Thank you so much for making such delicious drinks Samuel. Highly recommend to EVERYONE staying around here.

result.name: PlayFACTO School Katong | Student Care Katong

reviews.text:
To my surprise, this day care center offers wide range and comprehensive enrichment classes on weekday. My kid has more rest time over the weekend.

The creative math and robotic & coding are in-house designed.

Their classrooms are layout along window panels which come with good natural lights. Great day care environment for children.

Convenient sheltered drop off point and ample carpark lots available.

The student care centre has been a reliable and positive environment for my children. The teachers are patient, caring, and dedicated. My children enjoys going to the centre. Thank you for providing a safe and supportive space!

My child has been attending for 2 years and we’ve had positive experience so far. From Ms Jie Ning to Ms Lia, both teachers have been patient & encouraging to help nurture the wellbeing of my child. Appreciate thoughtful and constructive feedback that helps support my child’s growth—not just academically, but emotionally as well.

The centre itself is aesthetically pleasing & warm, creating comfortable environment for learning and winding down after school.

My son has been with them for a year and I think the environment has been welcoming. Aesthetically pleasing and easy on the eyes especially when kids are tired after a day at school, but the main thing for me is the staff. I appreciate the teaching staff in charge of my son’s class. He sings raises of Ms Han and enjoys being in her class. Work is completed and spelling is learnt.
Thank you, Ms Han!

Also I really comment on Miss Lia. She has been easy to approach and understanding in certain request, and I appreciate it because from a customer’s point of view, they care about flexibility and provide that to parents. Without going further into details, I cannot speak of higher process for her, creating a very welcoming approach to the parents and to the children. She is truly an asset.

My child is in Ms. Lia's class. We are thankful for Ms. Lia’s guidance. She is a strict but caring teacher who sets clear expectations while showing great patience and understanding toward her students.

East Coast Lagoon Food Village is one of the best hawker centres in Singapore if you enjoy local food with a relaxed seaside atmosphere. The location is excellent, especially in the evening when the place becomes lively and you can enjoy the sea breeze while having dinner. There are many good food options here, from satay and BBQ seafood to local favourites, making it a great place for families, friends and visitors who want to experience Singapore hawker food.

The overall vibe is casual, energetic and very local. It can get busy during peak hours, but that also shows how popular this place is. A great spot for good food, local culture and a nice East Coast dining experience. Highly recommended.

When we arrived here, it was dinnertime so getting a table for 5 took a little bit of time but not too long. Some of the food stalls have longer queues. Overall, the experience was great. Had some walk after dinner since the weather was good. There’s a skate park nearby and a bicycle rental that opens 24 hours.

Food here are great. Sugar cane juice is very sweet. Get the big one.

It’s right by the park so you can take a walk after food.

If you’re driving, parking was available even though the sign said full.

East Coast Lagoon Food Village located at 1220 ECP, Singapore 468960 . It have been so long we didn't visit East Coast Lagoon Food Village and very disappointed with the food i ordered. We ordered from a halal certified stalls and Nasi goreng kampung rice is really not nice, and very over priced for 2 plate of rice $15 ....if is nice we are okay but unfortunately it taste horrible.  Luckily, we ordered satay from another stall and taste was average. Only the sugar cane drink slightly lifted up the overall meal. Will not be back to the same food stall.

Stall 38. My favourite bbq seafood stall.

Family run stall. Friendly and nice owners.

Price is worth it for the portion i ordered.

The crab was the highlight of the meal. It was so loaded with roe! Fantastic taste!

Highly recommend. 👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

There are hawker centres, and then there are places that feel like a full experience. This is one of them.

Set right by the sea, East Coast Lagoon Food Village has a kind of atmosphere that is hard to replicate elsewhere. The salty breeze, the smell of charcoal grills in the air, the hum of conversation and the sight of tables filled with shared plates. It feels lively, slightly chaotic, and very Singaporean.

What makes this place special is the sheer variety. You are genuinely spoilt for choice. Walk a few steps and you will see everything from BBQ Stingray, satay, and charcoal grilled chicken wings to classics like Hokkien Mee, oyster omelette, char kway teow and bak kut teh  ￼. It is one of those rare places where you can satisfy every craving at once.

Some stalls have built almost cult followings over the years. The famous Lagoon Carrot Cake is known for its wok hei and slightly caramelised edges, while places like Haron Satay 55 serve smoky skewers with rich peanut sauce. Seafood stalls offering sambal stingray and grilled squid are also a must, especially when eaten hot off the grill with that unmistakable smoky aroma  ￼.

The variety is what really stands out. You can go for a full seafood spread one night, then come back for something as simple as satay and sugarcane juice the next. It is the kind of place where sharing is almost essential, ordering a mix of dishes and letting the table slowly fill up.

It is not polished, and that is part of the charm. It is open air, sometimes busy, sometimes a bit smoky, but always full of energy.

A seaside hawker centre where the food is diverse, the atmosphere is lively, and the experience is as memorable as the meal itself. One of those places that captures the spirit of Singapore’s food culture in one sitting.

Large playground with tall slides, rope climbing structures, swings and a smaller children play area.

Good place for children to mingle and have fun together!

There is a MacDonald’s and coffee bean nearby

Kids friendly. Lots of activities for kids.  spot to visit with families and kids.. activities to do: cycling(rent family/child/adult), swimming, playing with sand, kids playground. Coffee bean,Mc D and Mr biryani food choices to choose from

This place is amazing for those who want to give kid a place to have fun. I took the 196bus got here and went underpass then to the playground. I think its a best place for kids. It’s even with toilet for kids and nearby got mcdonalds

Wow. Best and biggest playground in Singapore maybe!  Great for kids. There are dedicated areas for toddlers as well as school age kids. But do keep me mind that big kids also go in the toddler section so need to watch out if you have a young toddler. rather crowded but still lots of space for everyone. Beach is just across so can play out in the sand too.  Washrooms with showers adjacent too. Bring water to keep hydrated and cool down, there is little breeze even though it is right next to ocean which I found surprising and was quite hot even though I visited in the late afternoon. McDonald's and other food places adjacent too for a quick snack.

Always a fun trip with family. Kids love Marine cove play area. With such a big playground, there's always something for everyone. The coffee shops and restaurants make it a complete deal however. Spend a few hours playing, walking or cycling in the area and come here to get some great food options. Coffee bean, briyani house, McDonald's and the East commune (new addition) take your pick 😄

My son joined the infant care group at Kinderland Marine Parade when we had just moved back to Singapore - he was just 9 months old. We credit the teachers and staff there with his smooth and happy transition to life in Singapore. Each of his class teachers shows interest and care for his wellbeing and communicates with us regularly about his progress or areas of concern.

It shows in the way he is excited to leave for school each morning - yelling the name of the front desk lady (one of his first words!) as he waves his shoes around to tell us that we need to leave now! Thank you!

Both my kids go to Kinderland at Marine Parade, and we've been pretty happy with it overall. The teachers are genuinely caring and patient — you can tell they're invested in the kids, not just going through the motions. My two have grown a lot in confidence and independence since starting there, and they actually enjoy going, which says a lot.

Overall, we’re happy with the experience with Kinderland MP

My sons enjoy going to Kinderland MP! The teachers are genuinely caring, nurturing and encouraging. The learning environment is great and I’ve seen so much growth and confidence in them since they’ve joined!

Kinderland Marine Parade has been my choice for my 2 kids since they were born.
