The teachers has always been putting in more than the extra mile needed, not only do they focus on academic it is the values that they incorporate to the children that is definitely worth mentioning. A special shout out to Fu laoshi, Ms Ying and Ms Gwen who has definitely frame up my child to be confident and independent. Thank you.

Highly recommended preschool! Principal is very caring and very interactive with the parents. Teachers are very engaging and willing to go above and beyond to help your child. CSO at the front desk is very welcoming and is always there when your child is in need of help! If you enrol your child at this centre, they’ll learn how to play the Yamaha Electone (as well as other instruments), learn how to perform confidently on stage, experience outdoor walks to East Coast Park and have the option of learning either Chinese OR Japanese.

Our eldest son has graduated from this preschool and we currently have two children attending the Marine Parade branch. Recently, we were involved in the Kinderland Sports Fiesta where the K1/K2 children participated in the ONLY Preschool Marching Band in Singapore. This is currently offered to only the Marine Parade and Yio Chu Kang branch. The Sports Fiesta involved parent-child sport activities as well as events like ‘Daddy’s Tug-of-War’ which was a highlight for all! It was a very good family bonding event where everyone came together. On top of the Sports Fiesta, the children have been involved in performing Lion Dances for Chinese New Year and performed a musical piece at a National Day event. End of year, they’ll be performing again on stage for their end of year concert, where they’ll be showcasing their musical talents. Bonus; your child will graduate learning how to play the Yamaha Electone as it is part of their curriculum!

Besides the Performing Arts, the children will have the opportunity to go on many excursions and have weekly outdoor walks around the neighbourhood (this includes East Coast Park and your local NTUC!). If you’d like to engage in more physical activities, the centre offers JACPA enrichment class in the afternoon. Not only do they offer JACPA but also Japanese language class as well!

Our children had no prior experience learning Chinese however they can now engage in Chinese conversation thanks to the dedicated teachers at Marine Parade. The centre also offers Japanese classes which has become very popular over the past few years. Some Japanese children graduate with the ability to speak English, Chinese AND Japanese as the main two form class teachers are English-speaking and Chinese-speaking.

Overall, highly recommended preschool as they offer so much for your child. Our eldest son was P1-ready and was able to transition with ease upon graduating from Marine Parade. Many of the students from this centre graduate and attend the neighbouring sought out Primary School. Thank you Kinderland for helping our children grow!

My P4 son has had the privilege to be taught English by Mr John Anthony, an exceptional teacher. From the very first lesson, our son shared that he never felt rushed nor discouraged when things were difficult, and that Mr Anthony always took the time to explain concepts clearly and at a comfortable pace. What truly set Mr Anthony apart was his ability to make every lesson genuinely fun and enjoyable; our son would come home excited to share what he had learned, which, as any parent knows, is no small feat. Beyond keeping lessons engaging, he had a remarkable talent for motivating our son to push past his comfort zone and strive to do better — not through pressure, but through encouragement and belief in his potential. The progress we have seen has been tremendous, and we have no doubt that it is a direct reflection of his dedication and passion for teaching. We are deeply grateful for everything he has done.

Teacher John Anthony is a very kind and helpful educator. He knows exactly how to guide children to make steady progress step by step. His classes are absolutely worth attending — his engaging and lively teaching style motivates students to learn in a joyful and positive environment. My daughter really likes him a lot!

We are incredibly thankful to Mr John Anthony from The Learning Lab for his dedication and passion in teaching English. He has been patient, encouraging, and genuinely invested in his students' growth. His engaging lessons and thoughtful guidance have not only helped build strong language skills but have also nurtured confidence and a love for learning. Thank you, Mr John, for your unwavering commitment and for making such a positive difference in your students' learning journey.

Ms Siu Yan is one of the best teachers in TLL. My child achieved an AL1 in the PSLE Science exam thanks to the teacher’s guidance. ms Siu Yan is extremely patient and meticulous, and the teaching methods are engaging and effective. My child loves the Science lessons very much.

Hi, "graduated" student here. Would definitely recommend this tuition centre, especially Mr Ben Tan of the English Department in TLL Marine Parade. Helped me score AL1 for my PSLE English, and has his own English resources for different components of the PSLE papers, especially compo. He expects discipline and civicness in his classes. Definitely he can be strict, but he can also crack jokes and laugh with you. He is definitely fair towards all his students whenever we used to talk during compo writing and wouldn't scold you if you did nothing wrong. With all his resources, he ensures you walk into the exam hall prepared.
Other teachers I'd surely recommend are Ms Cheong and Mr Marcus Tan of the Maths Department. Mr Marcus makes lessons engaging and fun while ensuring he doesn't go off topic of the curriculum. I miss his candy machine so much! Ms Cheong is also a very caring teacher, and is always ready to help her students. During PSLE, she also created a Whatsapp groupchat for us students to post questions that we have difficulties doing in the Whatsapp group and she will help us. She also doesn't focus on the answer, but rather the logic behind the method used to tackle the question. She also always printed out extra practices for us to do. miss the mixue she bought us during PPP! (psle preperatory course)
For Science, Ms Vanessa Ong is a competent and patient teacher who expects the best out of her students for them to reach their full potential. I still remember her helping me and holding me back after class to teach me mcqs with my friends.
I created genuinely good memories and made lasting friendships from my time in TLL! I still keep some of the resources like notes and books TLL gave us (especially Mr Ben's notes ;) and i genuinely wouldn't mind doing the whole PSLE thing again. if my teachers are seeing this, tysm for all ur guidance in psle!

Never thought I would write a review for a MRT Station. The Circle Line disruption beginning end-Jan 2026 was managed so well. At every corner, there was someone available to assist. On trains, on platforms. Great job to everyone involved! You are the Everyday Heroes getting us to our destination smoothly!

I am not going to lie, there was a disruption at Mountbatten MRT Station. They did do a good job with putting up signs for a shuttle service bus to take us to the other station. After the walking to the said place, the shuttle bus failed to arrive after numerous waiting

Love this Mountbatten MRT station location. Previously had to take a complicated bus route home from Kallang station but not anymore. It conveniently connects to town specifically Dhoby Ghaut MRT station. The staff are really professional and efficient too; there was once in recent years several of us commuters were rendering assistance to someone that lost balance and fell, and the staff were swift in activating all the right protocols too. Always in good hands at this station 🙏🏻

We travelled from Dhoby Ghaut MRT Station to Lorong Chuan MRT Station (Sat around 9pm). We were aware of the scheduled service disruption (due to some ongoing tunnelling works) and required to switch trains at Mountbatten and Paya Lebar Stations.
The crowd management measures at Mountbatten and Paya Lebar Stations were opined to be effective and organised. There were additional staffs and crowd marshals deployed to manage and direct crowds. There were also ample informative signages, barriers and dividers put up. Observed there were some ‘confused’ commuters but the staffs were there to offer assistance and direction and helped them navigate from one platform to another. The multi-age teams were very patient, alert and empathetic. They strived to ensure commuter safety and ensure smooth-flowing crowds (especially during boarding and disembarking from the trains and riding the escalators).

Mountbatten MRT is in a suburban area. Convenient buses on both old airport road and Mountbatten road.  Some that can take you down Mountbatten, fort road and to Tanjong rhu. Nice park just beside. Walking distance to Mountbatten square businesses.

Sept 25, 13. For 3 person it’s too much food, but all very nice. The WT noodle at the basement was very good especially the chilli. The Tau Foo was very good too. We like the fish head from OK stall. Will go again, highly recommend.
Standard has been consistent throughout the years. The noodles are tasty and the chilli is really good, not forgetting the pork lards. A comfort food for everyone

Ordered chicken satay here on 13/1/2026.
Satay not bad and service was quick.
Recommended!

Update 14/1/2026, breakfast for SGD 3.80.  That's a lot of breakfast for $3.80. Go for it! ( See last two photos)
Dunman Food Center is now under renovation.  They have a few poplar stalls such as Duck rice, Prawn noddle, Wanton Mee …. This is a small food center and parking is limited

Tbh I did not really meant the food was bad. Instead there are nothing to eat here. 80% of the shops are closed most of the time. I came 7am in the morning, 90% of the store are closed. I came 4pm, 80% of the store are closed. I can hardly eat or try anything.

However this place is very peaceful to enjoy a cup of coffee for many hours. Yes it's peaceful cos nobody want to come just for coffee at the food centre. The parking situation was very bad too. There are only 7 lots around here. 2 are taken up by some construction company. If u are here for the food u will be disappointed. If u are here to read a good book with a cup of coffee, u are in the right place. Oh the toilet here is very clean. Well renovated and it's free!  P. S. I am here on 7Nov 2025. There is no covid 19 lock down. Just incase u saw u see the photo and thought there is one. Lol

Our family including our toddler has been waiting eagerly for this MRT Station on the Thomson-East Coast Park Line (TEL), and I’m so glad we got to be part of the Public Preview today before it officially goes into service on 23 June 2024! 🚝✨🇸🇬 The station is spacious and quite deep underground. Everything is so brand new. The trains ran smoothly despite the higher volume of passengers today. The toilets are fantastic. There are lifts in place for those that need it. Our 2-year-old had such a wonderful time tonight here at Marine Parade, thank you! 🙏🏻 This will bring about a whole new level of accessibility and convenience to this neighbourhood, and is well connected underground to many parts of Marine Parade including Parkway Parade.

I had a very disappointing experience with an SMRT platform staff member. A toddler in our group was extremely hungry and distressed, yet we were told the child was not allowed to drink milk on the platform.

While rules are important, empathy and common sense matter—especially when it comes to young children. The staff member was rude in their approach and continued to glare at us on multiple occasions afterward, which felt uncomfortable and unnecessary. This made an already stressful situation worse for both the child and the parents.

I hope SMRT places more emphasis on compassion, discretion, and customer service when training frontline staff. A little empathy goes a long way in creating a more caring and humane public transport experience.
Its easy you can pay with your bank card ,and the map shows you the stops. Safe environment for everyone.

The long wait is over! Incredibly happy that the Thomson East Coast Line (TEL) has finally arrived (exact date: 23 June 2024).

Its design is simple. Love the dark green colour scheme of the underground platform.

It has a big sheltered parking space for bicycles - cyclists rejoice!! Have not gone to all the new stations yet. I saw sheltered bicycle parking space at the Marine Terrace station too.

The station is situated just beside the Hawker Centre & McDonald’s, and is a few minutes walk to Parkway Parade.

Going to the beach from this station would take you around 10 min. Head towards the NTUC FairPrice at Marine Drive, and the underpass to the beach is right behind.

i12 is close by too - a few min walk if you exit from the right exit.

I’m beyond happy that the TEL is finally here in this part of our island 🫶🫶🫶

This MRT is a game changer for Parkway Parade and the hawkers food market. It's so convenient located that once you come out of the exit, you see PP and next to it the food market. At least 40 stalls here for you to choose and all the shop beside it, 2 coffee shops all withing 5 min walking distance. You got banks here, like OCBC, POSB, UOB, DBS.....
It's more bustling and crowded too. Good and Bad. Anyway, always look at the bright side of life.

Man Chu cafe
Very impressed with all the staff from Man Chu cafe.  They are always smiling and patient with the members and guests.  Very well trained.  Special mention on Albany who recommended Nanyang Kopi to me and since then, have been there almost everyday for a cuppa.  He is another impressive guy! WELL DONE MAN CHU.  Keep the good service going!

I would like to express my sincere gratitude for the fantastic event held at the Chinese Swimming Club, Lattice Suite. A special shout-out goes to Edmund Lee and Andy Lam, whose outstanding assistance made the event a success. From the setup to accommodating special requests and providing excellent banquet support on the day itself, their hospitality was truly exceptional. The service was fast, efficient, and they went above and beyond to ensure everything ran smoothly. The food was delicious, with generous portions and a wide range of options to choose from. I highly recommend their services and look forward to working with them again!

Recently we decided to explore a membership with CSC and we spoke with Jack Ang. Jack is patient, friendly and very delighted to show us the facilities. He helped us with all the membership paperwork.
We just got our membership cards on 1st Nov 25. Till today, I am still asking Jack questions and he gladly replied to my queries promptly. We are so glad that CSC has such helpful and good service attitude staff member. Keep it up!
Best regards
KK & Alice Yong

Perfect hangout place for kids and adults to brunch there, tried their buffet there with the kids, there's something for everyone to eat! And not forgetting the swim there, big and small pool available, adults can also book the badminton court there for a game or two and bowling available. Everyone thoroughly enjoyed our half a day there at CSC 👍🏻

I am writing to formally raise a complaint regarding an incident involving a staff member, Zainul, at the club on the afternoon of 17 April.

At approximately 3:15 PM, I arrived at the club but had forgotten to bring my membership card. As such, my mother gave me her card to tap in, and we are under the same family account.

While attempting to tap in, Zainul approached me and forcibly wrestled the card out of my hand without any explanation. He then brought the card to the counter and kept insisting that I obtain a temporary card. I come here weekly and he recognised me as a frequent visitor; however, he was dismissive when I attempted to explain that I was a regular member who had simply forgotten my card on that particular day.

Later, he even escalated the situation further by informing the staff at Man Zhu Café to check my card as well. The situation left me feeling uncomfortable and singled out.

I fully understand and respect the club’s policies regarding access and identification. However, I believe the situation could and should have been handled more professionally, especially since I come here weekly. Forcibly trying to snatch a card out of my hands and getting fellow staff to single me out was so unprofessional and left me feeling unwelcome. This is extremely inappropriate for a club.

As someone who has visited CSC frequently over many years, this experience was deeply disappointing. I hope the management will take this feedback seriously and review the conduct expected of staff in handling such situations. I would appreciate reassurance that similar incidents will be addressed appropriately in the future.

Buona Vista MRT Station is one of the most convenient and well-designed stations in Singapore. It serves as an interchange for the East-West Line (EWL) and the Circle Line (CCL), making it an essential hub for commuters traveling across different parts of the island.

The station is clean, spacious, and efficiently managed. Clear signage and well-maintained facilities make transferring between lines smooth and hassle-free.

Its location is a big plus—Buona Vista is close to key areas like one-north, where many tech and research companies are based, and the Star Vista shopping mall, which offers great dining and retail options.

During peak hours, it can get a little crowded, but the station handles the traffic well. Overall, a reliable and strategically located MRT station that’s great for daily commuting and exploring the area!

Buona Vista MRT Station is a very clean and convenient interchange between the East–West Line and Circle Line. Transfers are easy, and the station has clear signs, lifts, and good accessibility. It’s also right next to The Star Vista and one-north, so there are plenty of food and shopping options nearby. A reliable and well-maintained station overall.

Great station - close to the Star Vista Mall where you can find food, drinks, Cold Storage. It serves Green & Yellow lines although it can be confusing to navigate from one to the other.
There are two restaurants, two tea places and convenience store at the station.
There are two bike stations (front and back).

There are no sheltered bike parking lots. Is all uncovered and exposed to the elements. If locking the bike at the walkway poles (outside of the mrt station mind you), the staff are so free that they have time to complain. It’s not even part of the mrt station. Perhaps y’all should rethink what are important matters in your job and what is just being a hindrance to people trying to live a healthy and eco friendly life.

A crowded station with 2 MRT lines - the circle line and east west line. My husband and I observed the circle line area and feel that there are insufficient seating area at the circle line platform.

The station can be enhanced by putting up more seats at the circle line platforms to better serve the needs of commuters waiting for the trains.

We are very happy with this kiddiwinkie! The teachers are caring, patient, and truly dedicated to the children’s growth and well-being. My son looks forward to school every day and has become more  independent, and curious about learning. The environment is safe, clean!

My daughter started at this school when she was 18 months old. I visited many schools, but I loved the environment here the most. The school is located in a quiet, standalone setting and offers plenty of outdoor activities every day, which I really appreciate.
Since joining, she has also started speaking Chinese, which has been wonderful to see. All the teachers are friendly, warm, and professional. I’m especially grateful to Teacher Zurah and Jia Xin Lao Shi for their care and dedication.
I truly feel thankful every day for this school and the positive impact it has had on my daughter.

Big thank you teacher Elsie and Lu Laoshi for their care and support of the children. The school is well organised with a lot of activities. My kid likes is happy to come to school and enjoys it very much. It s a safe and cosy school like a family ❤️ 👍

My 2-year-old daughter has been very well cared for by Teacher Shiho-san, Radiah, and Fang Ying Laoshi. Since she started preschool, we have noticed her growing interest in many things, her ability to learn new words, and her development into a brighter and more cheerful personality. We are truly grateful to the teachers for nurturing such wonderful growth in her.
In addition, Shiho-san’s Japanese class and the thoughtful daily communication have been very meaningful learning experiences for our daughter. We deeply appreciate all the dedication and support. Thank you very much!

We’ve had such a positive experience with KiddieWinkie Newton. From day one, the teachers and staff made my child feel safe, loved, and supported. What really stood out to me was how much they focused on my child’s overall development — not just academically, but emotionally and socially too.

The teachers are incredibly attentive and always provide feedback on my child’s progress. I’ve noticed huge improvements in communication skills, confidence, and independence. They use creative play, structured learning, and even introduce early literacy and numeracy in a way that’s fun and engaging.

I also love how they encourage curiosity and help the kids build healthy relationships with their peers. My child comes home excited to share what they learned every day — that speaks volumes.

Thank you to the KiddieWinkie team for being such an important part of our child’s early years. Highly recommend this centre to any parent looking for a nurturing and developmental-focused environment!

Clementi Public Library: A great resource, but often crowded with non-readers.

I was very surprised to find a large library inside such a modern shopping mall. The space itself is spacious and clean, with a wide variety of books available.

The Atmosphere:
I visited at 7 PM and noticed that almost all the chairs were taken by people using their phones. It felt more like a place for people to sit and rest after shopping rather than a quiet space for reading or study. If you are looking for a peaceful corner to actually read, it might be difficult to find a seat here.

The Verdict:
The library has an excellent collection, but the atmosphere can feel a bit like a busy extension of the mall. It’s better for a quick "grab-and-go" book pick-up rather than a long, quiet reading session.

One of the few libraries which bother to keep the patrons quiet. My family was very politely told off a while ago because my kids' flip flops were too loud. I think it is the right thing to do , but the libraries i went to when young. Gone are these days - u finds kids playing hide and seek or catching jn say woodlands library. So well done!!

The public library has became a place for social gathering and no longer a quiet place to read and enjoy tranquility. For others it's an air conditioned palce to rest.

Good thing is that books are still available for a wide variety of topics. Study tables are available for booking which is systematic and discourages place hogging.
A quiet, clean, and well-organised library with a good selection of books and study spaces. The staff are helpful, and the atmosphere is perfect for reading.
Its a resting place for old aunties and uncles hahaha no harm at all. They probably be busy with their C-Drama or candy crush. Or just shading their OAS 4D/Totos😂😂

HortPark has been around for more than a decade, and I’ve been coming here for many years — sometimes with family, sometimes for team-building activities, and sometimes just to enjoy a meal at one of the restaurants. It’s one of those places that quietly grows on you over time

What I love most is how versatile it is. The wide open lawns are perfect for kids to run around, the themed gardens are thoughtfully curated, and there are plenty of charming corners — from gazebos to shaded benches — where you can simply sit and relax. The greenery feels mature and well-established, giving the park a calm, lived-in character that newer parks sometimes lack

I’ve had meaningful team bonding sessions here, casual weekend strolls with family, and peaceful afternoons just soaking in the surroundings. It’s also conveniently connected to other green spaces, making it a great starting or ending point for a longer walk

The best time to visit is when the weather is kind — not too hot and not rainy. On breezy mornings or gentle late afternoons, HortPark truly shines. The combination of open lawns, beautiful landscaping, art features and dining options makes it a wonderful place to spend quality family time

Always an enjoyable stop for a Saturday morning outing with family. Young kids were fascinated with how the terrapins climbed out from the pond to get a bit of sunbathing. Good opportunity for being close to and learning about nature.

HortPark is a hidden gem that is perfectly connected to Kent Ridge Park and the Alexandra Arch, making it a great spot for a scenic walk.

The park is very well-equipped with a multi-purpose hall and several playgrounds that kids will love. For parents traveling with young children, there is a dedicated privacy room, which is a thoughtful and convenient touch.

If you are looking for a bite to eat, there are 2 or 3 restaurants on-site, including Canopy and Vineyard.

Things to note:
• The Best Part: They have water coolers throughout the park, and the water is always refreshingly cold—exactly what you need in the heat!
• Transport: There is a bus stop right outside the park entrance. nearest MRt is Labrador park MRT.
• Parking: On-site parking is available but is chargeable.

Highly recommend this park for families and hikers!

One of my favourite places to go for a walk when I need some quiet. Peaceful, green, and not too crowded. The gardens are beautiful and well maintained, and there are plenty of shaded paths to stroll along. It’s a nice place to slow down, clear your mind, and just enjoy being surrounded by nature. A little hidden gem in Singapore.

This Park is both a park and a horticultural test bed centre. It has much to offer to visitors and it connected to the sort of nearby famous Henderson Waves via the Alexandra Arch Bridge and Forest Walk in that order.

I recently attended a small group circuit training session at Trilogic Fitness in Novena and had a great experience.

The gym is spacious, fully air-conditioned, and conveniently located at the IRAS Building, just a short walk from Novena MRT station.

Eugene and his team were incredibly knowledgeable, supportive, and attentive throughout the session. They took the time to ensure everyone was performing the exercises with proper form and selected exercises that would help us build the conditioning needed for our upcoming challenge.

Their dedication, expertise, and encouragement made the session both effective and enjoyable. We are very happy with the outcome and would like to thank Eugene and the entire Trilogic Fitness team for their excellent support. Highly recommended!

My first time here today and was awed by the size and spaciousness of the gym. It has the best of both worlds; strength and cardio areas. Also hyrox stations if that’s your thing! Also there’s an area for physiotherapy and workout classes. It’s quite about everything you can ask for in a gym, and work out without feeling self-conscious. From the front desk to the boss himself showing us around, we felt so welcomed. There was a vending machine which intrigued me with plenty of other merchandise you can purchase on your own.

I believe it’s a place worth travelling for and they have PTs who can provide you attentive support for your fitness goals. Eugene explained the workouts and pointed out things I needed to work on to correct certain postures. He’s a great PT but if he’s not available I’m sure there are other great PTs for you to pick from.

Thank you Eugene for bringing us around and your hospitality too!

Trilogic is such an amazing gym: well rounded for all types of training needs, impeccably clean and well-maintained equipment and facilities, and always gives 100% positive energy whenever you step inside. All the trainers are super friendly and helpful without ever being intimidating or discouraging. I’ve been going to Trilogic for more than a year and it’s been consistent in all these areas and more. I’ve also been training with their Personal Trainer, Justin, and it’s been the most rewarding training experience. Justin helped give me the knowledge, guidance, and confidence I needed to be consistent with my fitness goals. He was able to modify and adjust anything I wanted to learn so that it worked for my body and goals specifically. And he always pays special attention to any pain or injuries I have. I never realized how super rewarding having a personal trainer could be! I’ve lost a lot of weight, I’ve gained strength and flexibility, and I’ve learned how to work with both my own strengths and weaknesses to get results and efficiency out of my exercise routine. I would not have been able to do this on my own so I’m really glad I decided to try personal training and even more so that it was in such a safe and welcoming environment, one that Trilogic has created with such ease, heart, and passion. With other gyms I’ve tried I could feel it was just a ‘business’, but at Trilogic I can really see their passion and that they truly care to make each and every member’s fitness health and wellness matter

Thank you eugene and ling for making my visit to the gym so pleasant. Eugene was knowledgeable and patient in correcting my form and giving me tips. Ling’s music is the best!!! Thank u trilogic fitness. Truely a 5/5 place. I recommend everyone to come here

Eugene & Ling are the bessssst!
Conned my friends (& sister) to join me for a friendly group challenge (we have all never trained together before)
And Eugene was so patient with us and our lack of knowledge about the challenge even 😂
He guided us through everything and showed us the best way to execute every station!
I would highly recommend anyone that needs help for anything to visit them!!
The gym is really spacious too! I love it!

The American Club Singapore consistently delivers a great experience across both the restaurant and the gym. The restaurant offers excellent food, attentive service, and a welcoming atmosphere that makes every visit enjoyable. The gym is equally impressive—clean, well-maintained, and equipped with everything needed for a strong workout in a comfortable and professional environment. Both areas reflect a very high standard of quality and service. Highly recommend.

Good food, good service, comfortable space, and one of our best investment so far.

I was a junior member growing up in the late 2000s and am glad to have joined again as an adult member when my family moved back to Singapore. It was a safe space for my husband and I to hang out with our friends back in high school & I’m looking forward to our kids doing the same as they grow up.

We’ve utilized the facilities as young adults, as working adults with no kids, and are using more of them now that our family has grown. Staff has always been friendly & some know us personally. Prices are fair and atmosphere very comfortable especially after the renovations.

Overall thankful to have this as our second home in town.

Visited as a guest last night and was genuinely impressed. The club is lavish yet welcoming, with an atmosphere that feels refined but never intimidating.
From the moment we arrived, the Front Office staff greeted us warmly and were friendly, attentive, and professional. It was clear they know their members, which speaks volumes about the level of service and relationship they maintain.
Had a meal at the Level 1 restaurant and the food was good and well-presented. What really stood out, though, was the consistency of the service.  Even after we were done and on our way out, the Front Office staff still made it a point to greet us again and thank us for coming.
Honestly, the service felt better than many hotels. Kudos to the team for setting such a high standard. 👏

Ordered the Chicken Caesar Burrito which was no doubt of a good size but it is lacklustre in terms of taste. The Wrap was thick and chewy and cold. Cutting it through took a couple of “sawing”. I could only find 3 slices of dry chicken breast. You will not miss out anything in not having this. The Double Chocolate cake was of a huge slice. The chocolate layer has a tinge of sea-salt infused into the mousse, hence the umami. It is still too sweet to my taste, not for the diabetic unless you live to eat.

Gammon ham - Excellent.
Roast turkey - Excellent. Not dry.
Log cake - Good.
Pecan pie - Good.
Pumpkin pie - Fine.

From an earlier order:
The beef was the best dish. The roasted turkey and the gammon ham were not bad. The shrimp cocktail was ok. The pecan pie needed improvement - tasted more like just nuts than a pie.

result.name: Agora Colearning (Preschool, Play School, Enrichment, Student Care)

reviews.text:
Our 14-month-old son absolutely loves his weekly Play and Bloom class and looks forward to it every week. He has thrived in their care, growing more confident and engaged through their perfectly tailored activities.

The teachers are truly wonderful—they are so patient, loving, and skilled at connecting with each child. A special highlight was the Christmas party. We were so impressed by the atmosphere; the entire place was genuinely filled with the joy and happiness of the children and their parents. It was a beautiful, festive event that everyone enjoyed.

Agora has created a warm, stimulating, and community-focused environment. We couldn't be happier!

I’ve had a really positive experience with the EP coordinators. They take the time to recommend classes that truly suit each child’s level, and I really appreciate how responsive they are—especially when it comes to arranging makeup classes quickly when needed.

I also feel reassured knowing that the vendors in Agora are carefully screened, so only reputable and trustworthy providers are included.

What stands out to me most is the regular check-ins with both parents and children. It really helps me understand my child’s progress and any concerns along the way. The coordinators often go beyond their roles, and I feel they genuinely care about providing holistic support and guidance for the kids.

One small suggestion from my side would be to consider incentives for longer-term or multiple EP sign-ups. It would also be really helpful if there were better lunch arrangements in Agora, especially for students with classes starting at 2:45pm, so they have enough time to eat and rest properly before lessons. A more substantial lunch option (beyond the usual student care catering) would make the experience even better.

My son has had an amazing journey with Agora Preschool right from his play school days to preschool. He made many wonderful friends, learned so many new things, and developed good manners and positive behavior along the way. He truly enjoyed coming to school every day and has grown beautifully under the care and guidance of his teachers.

We will always miss the school, his friends, and all the wonderful teachers who made his learning journey so special and memorable. Thank you for everything and for being such an important part of his early years.

Special thanks to Ms Claire for being super support and flexible throughout 🙏

My child had a hard P1 transition and had a rough Q1 with school + post school care before moving to Agora. Now she is thriving in P2 and attends 4 enrichment classes with Agora. During her stay, Ms Linda fr sch care was wonderful with helping an anxious and change resistant stranger fearing child adapt and integrate to make new friends at a managed pace. Yenn fr enrichment has been an awesome parent support. She arranges enrichment, make ups, checks up on kid, intervenes when my child/ kids arrives disregulated and in tears. She's always sitting there, always comforts her and is able partner enrichment teachers to ease my kid into classes on bad days (which was half of P1). Missed class refunds are arranged automatically, new interesting classes are sent for consideration. As a working parent, I can focus and I know my kid is well cared for at the center and spelling/ ting Xie is revised in Agora. I can reach out to Yenn anytime and the team is responsive and attentive to parents and kids needs. Thank you so much :)

My 2 kids are currently enrolled in Agora Colearning for their enrichment lessons.We love the experience and the interactions with the staff and teachers.I would like to specially compliment Ms Yen for her friendly, efficient and dedicated help thus far. She is always ready to attend to my queries promptly and make us feel so warm and welcoming when my kids have their lessons at the centre. The teachers, Ms Xing, Coach Charles and Tr Beth are wonderful teachers, always giving my kids guidance and encouragements.We are so grateful to have them to coach our kids through their education journey and looking forward to many more years with Agora!

The Fort Canning Tree Tunnel is definitely one of Singapore’s most iconic photo spots. The view looking up through the spiral staircase towards the lush greenery is beautiful and well worth seeing in person. It’s a unique spot that creates stunning photos, especially on a sunny day.

The only downside is the long queue, as many visitors are waiting for their turn to take photos. Be prepared to wait, especially during peak hours. That said, most people are patient and respectful, and the final result is worth it if you enjoy photography and sightseeing.

A must-visit spot in Singapore, just come early if you want to avoid the crowds!

Location and views are nice!
Bad experience with tourists however a lot of them are very rude and speak no English. Clearly signs that say no climbing over the edge to sit however they do this and line up for it so it causes lots of congestion especially at the bottom and on the actual stairs. Would recommend going very early to avoid crowds.

Lovely park to venture near City Hall. Visited the spice garden as well. The tunnel is very crowded with queues at ard 8am. Lots of local & foreign tourists there at the tunnel. Else where, there’s peace & quiet.

It's ok if you want to drop by.. But if purposely to come here far away to enjoy the view, it's not really worth it. Long queue during weekend. You still have to wait in queue for individual photography during weekdays. The park is not too small.

CROWDED - Probably the most we’ve waited in Singapore. It’s a beautiful spot but extremely difficult to take photos due to the crowds. We arrived here just gone 9am on a Saturday. Groups are sadly inconsiderate in that each person spends a number of minutes taking multiple photos stopping each time someone passes by the stairs. If you really wanted an image, earlier the better. Easy to find with public transport. We did not wait to take images on the steps as I can easily imagine this queue lasting into 45 mins+ and there are other things to see also

The school has a warm and inviting atmosphere with an expansive outdoor area - big plus point ! The teachers and staff in Etonhouse Newton are  nurturing, kind and approachable. That definitely shows in how the children are settled, always cheerful & engaged! Thank you for the wonderful experience

Our kid has been at this kindergarten for almost a year, and we’re so glad we chose it. At first there was a bit of separation anxiety, but now they’re always excited to go every morning. The teachers are super caring and patient, and the environment feels really warm. We can see our child becoming more confident, independent, and caring — it really feels like a second home for them.

We have had a truly wonderful experience at EtonHouse Newton. The school provides a nurturing and encouraging environment where children feel seen, heard, and loved.

We are deeply grateful to the dedicated teachers and community for the care, guidance, and warmth they show every day. Thank you for shaping happy little humans 💛

My experience with EtonHouse Newton has been nothing short of positive. The space felt calm, welcoming, and thoughtfully put together.

Nothing seemed without intention—which reflects the school’s commitment to the IB curriculum, not just in theory but in how it shapes daily interactions and learning experiences.

The classrooms were among my favourite parts of the school. Each had its own style, highlighting the individuality of both teachers and children—something I wish I had experienced more of growing up.

Altogether, it left me with the impression of a place that truly values not just how children learn, but how they feel while learning. I’d highly recommend dropping by, you’ll meet some of the kindest people I’ve ever come across.

As a family PA, I frequently communicated with EtonHouse Newton because my boss’s child attends the school.  I’ve witnessed the remarkable growth of my boss’s daughter into a sweet, loving, and respectful young woman under EtonHouse’s care.

The teachers at EtonHouse Newton are truly exceptional. They are caring, attentive, and nurturing, and their operations, parent liaison, and education are exceptionally impressive.  Every staff member at EtonHouse Newton, including the helpful admin personnel like Ms. Keisha, is prompt and professional in responding to my inquiries about school matters.

Thank you, EtonHouse, for shaping our little princess and her elder brother into the wonderful individuals they are today. Your hard work in preparing their foundation has undoubtedly played a crucial role in their future success.

Never seen a more beautiful MRT station that incorporates nature even above it. Newton MRT is very special and gives a luxurious vibe even from walking along the footpaths. Pretty expensive area in Singapore and truly different.

Passed by this station. It was, as usual, clean and immaculate throughout the MRT, which includes the toiletries. Many places you can go to from this MRT stop. Overall, a nice and comfortable atmosphere at this MRT station.

Another clean, efficient Singapore metro station.  This one is particularly convenient as it serves the blue and red lines, making it a popular interchange when travelling downtown.

These stations are so efficient and most people using them are very considerate.  They all make Singapore a great and easy place to travel around.
26 June 2025 I'm here at 00:12 for taking North-South line and the station have no air with heat, I guess air-con off early. Please understand for peoples waiting for next train.
I went here almost every day during my vacation in Singapore. Clean, structured and also pretty convenient (walking distance from the hotels and plaza)

This hawker center was the first place I visited right after I landed in Singapore. Personally, I cannot say that this was the best in Singapore since I have not yet tried other places at the time.

As a first time traveler, Newton Hawkers were so welcoming! I had a great time with the vibes by the uncles and aunties offering their products to me. Majority of the crowd are also local, which is a plus for me. This specific stall (in the picture) was the best (service-wise).

In the photo, I have made friends with Auntie Nico and Uncle John. I don’t know their relationship or if they own the store but they treated me so well and even offered to show me how they cook their food (I told them that I own a restaurant and was curious about how they cook their food).

In the end of the night, I asked them to take a picture with me and they did. Auntie Nico also used her phone to get a selfie with me lol. Fun times!

There’s always something beautiful about hard working people and everytime you get a chance to talk to them, do it!

WILL ALWAYS VISIT HERE EVERYTIME I GO TO SINGAPORE!

Newton Food Centre is a must-visit in Singapore. Lively, authentic, and packed with incredible food options, it’s everything you want from a classic hawker centre.

The variety is outstanding — from local favourites to late-night comfort dishes, there’s something for everyone. Prices are very reasonable, portions are generous, and the flavours are bold and authentic. It’s the kind of place where you can try multiple dishes in one visit and still want to come back again.

The atmosphere is busy and energetic, especially in the evenings, but that’s part of the charm. Despite the crowds, there’s plenty of seating and the space is well laid out. Being open 24 hours is a huge bonus, whether you’re after a late supper, early breakfast, or anything in between.

Great spot for travellers wanting a genuine local experience, groups with different tastes, or anyone keen to eat well without breaking the bank. Casual, vibrant, and full of character — exactly what a Singapore hawker centre should be.

🍜 Huge variety of food
🌶️ Authentic local flavours
💰 Affordable prices
⏰ Open 24 hours
🎉 Lively atmosphere

Highly recommended.

• Food
The food selection at this center is a reliable choice for local flavors. The chicken wings and satay are particular highlights, especially when paired with a cold beverage. Another notable mention is the Gong Gong, which is a dish that often prompts a visit to this location. The flavors are authentic and provide a satisfying taste of local seafood and grilled items.

• Service & Atmosphere
The atmosphere is lively and typical of a popular open-air food center in Singapore. It offers a vibrant setting that is suitable for casual gatherings. While the environment can get busy and crowded, it remains a practical and energetic spot for a late-night meal or a social drink. The service varies by stall but is generally efficient and functional.

Well known hawker centre that once upon a time catered mostly to tourists.

But locals are now crowding the place because of the good food and the variety and because the Singapore Tourism Board has successfully clamped down some seafood stalls touting and scamming patrons, especially tourists, with exorbitant prices.

This is one hawker centre where you will find people from all over the world savouring local street food of all races in Singapore which originated from China, India,  Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand,  etc and even the US.

Beer by the jugs are served here.

Most stalls are closed for lunch. But it is usually quite difficult to get a table during dinner time, especially on weekends. Sharing table is the norm.  So be willing to do so.

Queuing at some stalls to collect your own order is also the norm.

This hawker centre, including the free toilets, are very clean and airy. There are basins all over for patrons to wash their hands before and after meals
meals.

Pay when you order. In cash or via your phone transfer.

There is an adjoining carpark. A huge one. But it can still be hard to find a parking space during dinner time.

The Newton MRT is very near by.

A true foodie paradise, with flavors from across Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, and China all coming together in one place. You’ll find yourself eating alongside both locals and tourists, which adds to the energy and authenticity. Not too crowded on a Monday night, making it easy to explore. Highlights included the fresh sugar cane juice, black pepper crab, Singapore chili stingray, fried squid, and the classic Singapore “carrot cake.” A great way to experience the diversity of the region’s street food culture.

Christ oriented hotel which needs some TLC.

I did appreciate that this Hotel belongs to a Christian organization and that they value community and are God fearing. However, room upkeep needs more work. The rooms are not as clean as they could be, especially seats and carpet stained.

I watched the young boy change my sheets on the third day and he was putting our pillow cases on dirty surfaces.

The gym and pool have strange opening and closing times. These facilities should be freely available 24/7 to suit patrons. You also need to go to the counter to swap your room card each time you want to go.

The buffet breakfast is a real let down with really basic food choices that are the same every single day. I just think they could put a bit more love into it.

Overall, a decent location which is close to Orchard and 5 minute walk to Stevens MRT.

Metropolitan YMCA Singapore offers a pleasant and practical stay just a short distance from Orchard Road. The property is spacious, clean, and reasonably priced for an NGO-run hotel. It’s conveniently connected, with easy transport to Orchard and an MRT station within walking distance, and provides all the necessary amenities for a comfortable stay.

A highlight is its cozy café, which serves dishes made with fresh Hydroponics-grown vegetables. The hotel also reflects a strong social mission, showing inclusivity by employing people with special needs, and offers added conveniences like an automated e-mart.
Pleasantly surprised at how awesome this place was, enjoyed our room, location close to Orchard. We will definitely come back!! Thank you!

Just okay.  We were surprised how dirty our room was.  The "free" WiFi is so bad, we had to buy a separate date plan with the data app Saily.  The pool is nice.  The staff is a mix, some are really nice and helpful, but others are uninterested in helping with anything.

reviews.author_name: ひきこもり。高橋正臣。Masaomi Eric Takahashi.
reviews.text:
My son and his mum has been staying here and I joined my son occasionally. Except for the helpful and friendly Front staffs, Andrew and Karen, the rest of the other management team hiding inside their offices are the vilest and most hostile employees in this establishment. Unprofessional. No greetings from the woman who confronted me about bringing outside food into the hotel premises!? No greetings, No Hello. She just march right up to me then started chastising me and vehemently rebutted me. She complained to Karen about my presence and spared no expense screeching outside the lobby to let me hear her displeasure.
Subsequently, all the other management staffs were notified of my presence and one by one they came out of their office just to antagonise me! They sure made it very personal and it sure felt personal, disingenuous and contumelious!
I left and my son would never grace YMCA Stevens again, such shamelessly unprofessional and hostile management staffs!
Father and son definitely DO NOT RECOMMEND this place.

I was here today (17 DECEMBER 2025) arriving via the Downtown Line, to switch over to the North South Line. While this MRT Station is quite an old one, it is well maintained and the ambience therein is comfortable in that it is well ventilated. To me, this MRT Station or interchange allows me to go to some of my favourite places in Singapore on the North South Line - in particular, Novena, Toa Payoh, Orchard and Somerset.

Short transit betw Newton Downtown Line (Blue) and North South Line (Red). Hair cut place at Blue line! Very convenient. Visit them soon :))

The red line station can be warm sometimes due to crowding & aircon under service. Friendly SMRT staff.

Landmarks: Newton Food Centre (famous for BBQ seafood, prawn mee, duck noodles, Thai food, Nasi Padang etc.)

Prudential@Scotts, NEA, Sheraton Towers, Alliance France.

Thoroughly enjoy the Newton MRT station. It's probably one of the most important station connecting people from the West side of the country to the Central zones. The downtown line is probably one of the best addition to the rail system. Efficient, clean, and not a very long walk from the Blue to the Red lines, compared to some other interchanges.
Clean, well maintained and modern MRT station in the heart of Singapore.

After opening downtown line, the Newton circus area becomes very convenient. It’s easy to across the road toward each direction now.
(If you push the stroller or use wheelchair, you still need to check how to across safely)
But around MRT, there’s nothing special except the food court.

Orchard MRT station is one of the ‘classic’ older station in one of the earlier MRT lines in Singapore.

It's a shopping hub, a ‘must-see’ destination for tourists where all things happen. I think it still retains its status.

There are big chains of departments stores like Takashimaya, Tangs, Wisma Atria, and many hotels around here. If you want a leg cramping shopping experience, walk from here to City Hall.

It's tidier now compared to then, imho, although it caters much more to commercial. Back then, there were more bookstores and others, now it's mostly fashion and lifestyle.

It's always crowded and noisy, sometimes going to food court feels like battle royale. If you like people and shopping, you'd love Orchard.

Orchard MRT Station has been around for decades, since I was literally a small kid. Wonderful this station remains clean, and even more accessible than before with underground and ground level connections to so many places around downtown Orchard Road. This stations serves both the red North South Line and the brown Thomson East Coast Line, which makes it extra convenient. It may be a bit busy during peak hours so just watch your step. There are plenty of friendly staff to assist if needed too.

One of the more crowded train stations around the island. It's very conveniently located in the heart of the city, linking up neighboring shopping malls, and connected train lines, all in one single space. They're also very handicap friendly.

Just be mindful that the crowd can be quite a handful during peak hours.

Orchard MRT Station is an interchange station of North South Line and Thomson East Coast Line of Singapore MRT system. It offers seamless transfer between both lines as commuters just need to move among  platforms.

The station was clean and comfortable with good conditioning system. It has public toilet for commuters. Clear signages were stated at the station to help commuters to change between both lines or to look for the nearest exits or buildings or places. As this Orchard MRT station is located at the Singapore shopping heaven, Orchard Road, it has direct connections to shopping mall nearby.

I have come across that  in front the person zip the card at the entrance, slowly walk and talking the friend . My turn , my back that person push me,ask me to go quickly . But the door have not closed yet .how to I zip my card .cannot wait at all . If in the rush or need to zip the card also need time.
Only depends the front people .

ION Orchard delivers a high-end shopping experience right in the heart of Orchard Road. The architecture is striking, and the mall houses a wide range of luxury brands alongside stylish dining spots. It’s always lively and well-maintained, though it can feel overwhelming due to its size. Still, one of the most iconic retail destinations in Singapore.

It wasn't a goal on my list. I visited it only because through it you have access to ION Sky, an observation deck. What I liked is the absolutely unique architecture, very beautiful. Otherwise, it has nothing extra compared to other malls.

A huge, modern, expensive shrine to consumerism. If you have the money, and yoy need lots,  you can find anything here. We did however have the best lunch date at Twinings much to my surprise and delight. I would absolutely go there again.

ION Orchard is a premier shopping destination in Singapore, known for its futuristic design and luxury brands. Located on Orchard Road, it offers a mix of high-end boutiques, popular retail stores, and great dining options. The ION Sky observation deck provides beautiful city views, making it more than just a shopping mall—it’s an experience.
Full of high end stores. Can rest a little while inside when you’re a bit tired. The structure was amazing.

result.name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Punggol) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Singapore

reviews.text:
We are very happy with student care service provided by Edufirst Learning Centre. The teachers are kind, patient, and caring. My daughter feels safe and comfortable here.

Especially during P1, my daughter was still new and not familiar with school life.  She needed more guidance and time to adjust. Tr Huiling took very good care of her and patiently guided her through homework, school rules and gave her encouragement, which helped her to feel more confident.

The center is well arranged with many activities for the students. Even during the school holidays, there are excursions and activities planned so the students stay active, happy and motivated while learning.

We really appreciate the effort and care from the teachers. Thank you for creating a positive and supportive place for the children.

My child has joined the student care this year and I'm glad I had chosen Edufirst. The teachers here are diligent and responsible, especially teacher Huiling, who provides strong academic support and daily feedback. She monitors my child’s school homework closely and ensure that spelling revision is done thoroughly every week. Beyond the academics, you can tell the teachers truly care about the well-being of the students. It gives me great peace of mind knowing my child is in such caring hands while I’m at work.

Really glad to have enrolled my P1 kid into Edufirst as he’s happy and adapting well in the centre. Staff and teachers were caring towards him. Would like to thank all the staff and teachers for their patience and guidance, esp Tr Huiling, who’s in-charge of his class. She would ensure that the children complete their school work properly as it’s the centre’s top priority. After which, children may proceed with other learning activities or outdoor activities. Highly recommend this centre to any parent looking for student care in Punggol area!

We’re truly grateful to have found this student care for our daughter who is currently in P1 class. Teachers and staff are friendly and approachable. Thank you for creating such a supportive environment, guiding the children patiently with their homework, and always keeping us updated on their progress and school matters. Your commitment and initiative mean a lot to busy working parents like us and make a real difference in our child’s growth. Truly thankful for their care and dedication. Much appreciated! 💛

I’m very thankful to the student care for providing a caring and supportive environment for the children. I would also like to express my sincere appreciation to Teacher Hui Ling for her dedication and patience in guiding my son with his after-school studies. With her encouragement, I’ve seen an improvement in his learning attitude and confidence. She also provides timely feedback to parents and works closely with us to help him improve further.

Overall, I’m happy with the support provided by the student care and the teachers. There is still some room for improvement in certain areas, but I can see the effort and commitment from the team to continuously do better. Thank you for all your hard work in creating a positive and nurturing environment for the students!
NUS shuttle buses stop here. Many other SBS transit and SMRt buses stop here as well.
Excellent bus transit with clean toilet.  Next to NUS.

Very small sbs bus terminal. With shade and public toilet here

Bus 10 goes to vivo city, harbourfront and raffles place.

Bus 33 goes to redhill mrt

The KR and several of the NUS busses run here. It is located near NUS as well as three hotels, namely Ibis, Fragrance and Quay. The West Coast Park is nearby. Download the bus app to accurately plan your journey.
Bus 201 takes 16-20 minutes to come. Please fix this. U make people late for work alot.

As our triplets celebrate this important milestone, we want to take a moment to express our deepest gratitude to all the teachers who have guided, inspired, and nurtured them along the way.

Your patience, dedication, and kindness have shaped not only their minds but also their hearts. You have been more than educators — you’ve been mentors, cheerleaders, and role models. The lessons you’ve taught extend far beyond the classroom and will stay with them for life.

Thank you for believing in our children, for helping them grow with confidence, and for making learning such a joyful journey. This graduation is not just their achievement — it’s yours too.

I would like to express my heartfelt gratitude and appreciation to you and the entire team at Learning Vision at Changi Airport. Having been part of this school community for the past nine years, through four of my children’s graduations, I have witnessed firsthand the incredible care, dedication, and growth of this institution.
The teachers are not just educators, but genuine, compassionate individuals who truly care about the children. They have always gone above and beyond to create a nurturing and supportive environment. Over the years, I have felt like part of a family—a team that has always been there for both my children and me. I truly believe that the strong, positive relationships between the staff and families are one of the school’s greatest strengths.
Through the many phases of our journey together, from navigating the challenges of COVID-19 to embracing changes in daily routines and curriculum, I have seen the school continuously adapt and improve. The commitment to refining the curriculum and being open to feedback speaks volumes about the school’s dedication to providing the best possible education and experience for its students.
It is with immense pride and gratitude that I can say my child has graduated from Learning Vision at Changi Airport—and I am equally proud to have been part of this wonderful school community for so many years. Thank you for your unwavering support, your genuine care, and your continued efforts to ensure that each child thrives in a safe, enriching environment. You have made a lasting impact on our family, and we are truly grateful.

Love to all teachers - from the J’s and the K’s ❤️😊

We’ve had such a wonderful experience with Learning Vision @ Changi Airport! The teachers are incredibly caring and patience. always taking the time to understand each child’s needs and personality. The environment is very conducive for learning — bright, clean, and filled with engaging activities that make children excited to go to school every day.
I also really appreciate that the centre organizes many events involving both parents and teachers, which gives us a better understanding of what our children are learning and how they’re developing. It truly feels like a partnership in helping our little ones grow.
Highly recommended for parents looking for a nurturing and enriching preschool experience!

We heard so much about this school and how good it is so when our daughter was 8 months old we quickly registered her for Playgroup. Now 2 kids later, we never regretted our decision. We love their curriculum and inclusivity and will always recommend LV to our friends and family.

Learning Vision provides a nurturing environment where children can explore and grow. The educators are dedicated and create engaging activities that stimulate young minds. It's wonderful to see the children develop social skills and a love for learning in such a positive setting. The facilities are well-maintained and designed to ensure the safety and comfort of all the little ones. We are very pleased with the quality of care and education offered at this child care centre.

The only bus Interchange that is sandwiched between Hajjah Rahimabi Kebun Limau Mosque, Whampoa Park, and Whampoa River, this bus Interchange serves the residents of Whampoa linking it to Aljunied (loop at Sims Avenue), Bukit Merah, HarbourFront, Pasir Ris, Shenton Way, and Tampines North. The nature of the bus Interchange is that it is divided into two bus islands to cater more spaces for large buses parking at this tiny Interchange. There is also a public food court and small public parking space in front.

Quite small . But there is buses that go east side like 129 and 21 , South side like 186,124 and 131
The only problem with this interchange Not only is hard to find the bus 129 berth but also because if old people want some bus guides so they know where to go . They will need to go to the passengers service . I think it’s quite inconvenient tho

Kinda of confusing on bus 129 around the area. Bus stop serving same number but on different routes (1 to terminal, 1 outboard), cause me to be out of pocket. Is it deliberate to cause passenger new to the area to pay extra new trip?

MOST OF THE TIME YOU BLUFF ME.
SAY BUS 1 MIN BUT 10 MIN.
LUCKY TODAY EVE OF CHINESE NEW YEAR YOU NO LIE BUS REALLY COME 1 MIN. STEADY. I LOVE BUS 456 IS THE BEST EXPRESS BUS BUT PLEASE NO LIE ABOUT COME ON TIME.

Don't miss out on the coffeeshop here.
The western is not bad and generous.
The Aneka Selera Indonesian food is a gem.
Taste good and won't burn your wallet.
They operates early morning to afternoon only. Closed during evening.
Their Longtong rice is nice and soft not the bouncy factory type.
Longtong and Gado Gado both are nice. If you see tempeh ikan bilis stir fried in sambal. That's a must try dish.

they maintained this place quite well over the years, im happy to see that this rich culture and history is still nicely kept, with its creepy elements still creepy and its statues still worthwhile to visit. however it is quite hard to access but for those wanting to see something more unusual in sg it is a good place
Singapore yellow Colour Code Hawoar vila Mrt station no _ 25,,

Beautiful place for family and children. They gonna learn rich culture of our history.
#I love the old slow music.

Here is one MRT station that seniors can pass through safely. There is even ONE parking lot for personal mobility device just next to the entrance. Very considerate.

Mostly deserted except during peak hours. No queues at ATMs, topping up stations - and clean restrooms.

Conveniently located next to the historical Haw Par Villa.

Huge crowds used to throng it for its Chinese mythological figurines and depiction of "hell" punishment chambers. But alas, not anymore - even after reverting back to free admission.

Guess times have changed. Singapore have changed. Values have changed as well.
One of the tourist spots. Now not so popular but tourists should visit at least once.

My nieces and boy loves being with Teacher Wen Hee! Thankful for his patience and being able to impart his knowledge to my nieces and boy who have all shown great progress! WH manages to explains and communicate clearly and efffectively along with sharing great worksheets that the kids are able to learn from during the week. My boy certainly looks forward to his class weekly. thank you, Teacher Wen Hee for being a great and engaging teacher you are!

My boy attends P1 class @ Novena with Teacher Kyria. He thoroughly enjoys the curriculum and the fun online learning homework every time after the lesson. We’re pleased with his progression and thanks to Teacher Kyria’s positive bubbly energy, she truly encourages and brings out the potential of each student. Bravo! 👏 👏

Teacher Wen Hee is very good for my son and he shows concern even when my son does silly things to get attention. LBM’s teaching methods are systematic and I’m thoroughly impressed by their ability to break each topic down into segments which are easier to understand for my very messy boy.

I was from the old branch of Lil’ but mighty all the way until now. I really appreciate Teacher Elaine for teaching me to the best of her abilities and helping me. To everyone reading this review, I would definitely recommend teacher Elaine as your teacher. Today would be my last lesson and I truly appreciate Teacher Elaine. I have been here since Primary 3 and Teacher Elaine has not only taught me English but taught me Life advice. Thank you so much Teacher Elaine and I will never forget you!( Do continue to teach at Lil’ but Mighty and I promise I will return) :)

My child has been taught by Ms. Elaine since P3 and we are happy that Ms. Elaine has progressed with the kids through P6. We have nothing but praises for this caring and wonderful teacher who gives her best to all the students. What we like is also the small class size that allows more student teacher interactions.

Very nice place to either have a slow walk, trail walk, or trail run. There are challenging slopes & steps, but there are also friendly sections to catch your breath. Distance wise, one can choose to walk from a short 3 KM route or a longer 6KM+ route. With the terrain, it is a good place to burn some calories and at the same time, enjoy the greenery with some  sightings of animals in the wild. So far, I have encountered calugo, blue diamond coral snake, pangolin, wild boar, monitor lizards, drongo (bird) and of course, monkeys (plenty of them).

Went hiking at Bukit Timah and Rifle Range and it turned out to be a really refreshing experience. The trail was surrounded by tall trees and thick greenery, making the whole walk feel calm and peaceful. As I went down the wooden steps, I could hear the sounds of nature all around me—birds, insects, and the rustling of leaves.
Along the way, I noticed small details that made the hike even more special, like tiny plants growing out of old wood and patches of moss spreading across fallen logs. It reminded me how alive the forest is, even in the smallest places.
The hike wasn’t too easy, especially with the long paths and steps, but it felt satisfying. I managed to cover about 7.8 kilometers, and even though I was tired, it was a good kind of tired. Overall, it was a simple but meaningful experience—just being surrounded by nature, slowing down, and enjoying the moment.

The highest hill in Sg but there is no view at the summit. A little disappointing. Very hot and humid. The forest does not cover or cool down the trail unlike the one at Macritchi Reservoir. Pavillion are not powered with fans and have no vending machines. Prefer the treetop trail at Macritchi reservoir.

Bukit Timah Nature Reserve is a great spot to switch into hiking mode on the weekend.

There are lots of stairs, so be ready for a workout — definitely a sweat-it-out session 💦
Perfect if you’re looking to move your body and get a good sweat in nature.
Brisk walking during the early morning... able to feel the fresh air... a good place to have a short exercises as well.

result.name: PlayFACTO School @ Balestier | Student Care Balestier

reviews.text: We really liked this particular outlet because it is bright and spacious. The teachers are also wonderful. Ms Wei Ling and Ms Claudia were able to connect with the children - explaining and maintaining order. Homework was done in a good fashion, and kids had supplementary work in days without homework. Ms Cai Qi and Ms Vas were also caring even though they were from another class. There are also many awards to motivate the children and this leads to a good experience in general. Oh, and food is nutritious and delicious!

I would to express my appreciation to the team of teachers for their patience and love for my children.

Special shout-out to Miss Jash and Miss Wei Ling for the kindness shown to them - making after-school care enjoyable and fun-loving.

Highly recommended after-school care option as the teachers here have been
wonderful! They are caring, patient, and always go the extra mile to make sure the children feel happy and supported.

Daily updates would be sent to us parents on homework that’s been completed, 听写 and spelling that have been revised with the children. Definitely helpful for working parents like us.

We truly appreciate their dedication and the genuine care they show every day. Thank you to Ms Claudia, Ms Jash, Ms Wei Ling, Ms Priya, Ms Kausalya who are always so loving and kind. You ladies are amazing! Keep up the superb work!

My elder child has been with the center since end of 2023 and now my younger one will attend from 2026. Friendly and caring teachers here. Constant communication and updates from teachers. Play ground is among the favourite activities among the children here. No crossing of road or anything and it’s 80% sheltered from rain. See for yourself!

My kid enjoys being at PF Balestier and asks to be picked up later in the day, rather than earlier! She enjoys being taught by Ms Wei Ling and has made many good friends in the centre. The staff, including Ms Claudia, Ms Kausalya, Ms Jas, and the support staff, are caring and take good care of the children.
Homework and spelling are completed in the centre. Holiday programmes are also fun, with interesting excursions.
Staff are responsive to queries and messages, giving parents a peace of mind.
Will recommend.

I was here today (17 DECEMBER 2025) arriving from Newton MRT Station, after alighting thereat via the Downtown Line from Hillview Station. I live in the Hillview area and this MRT Station is a very convenient one for me to alight at, to go to my two adjacent to each other favourite shopping malls of Velocity @ Novena Square and Square 2 @ Novena. And while this MRT Station is quite an old one, it is well maintained and the ambience therein is comfortable in that it is well ventilated.

We needed a 4 day visitor pass and they dont sell at this stop. Have to go to Orchard station, problem is the MRT no longer sells tickets and you have to pay to get to Orchard station. Finally a super friendly guy helped us. Note to visitors DO NOT buy visitor pass at Changi airport.  A four day pass is $37. At Changi they wanted  $52.
Went to Sanouk halal Thai for lunch. Absolutely value for buck, great service. Clean and spacious seating.

I stayed at the Oasia Hotel.  The hotel basement is connected to the station which makes commuting less of a hassle.

It’s a busy station that's connected to a mall with some  F&B eateries. Also it has  easy access  to places at Novena like square 2, Royal medical centre, novena medical centre, Mount Elizabeth Hospital.

🟢 Novena MRT station (NS20) is an underground train station. 🔴It is part of the North South Line.

⬛️ from Novena Station, the train goes 2 ways.
- All the way to Jurong East ( West region)
- down south to Marina South Pier.

🟦 Novena Station is located at Thomson Road in Novena district. 🟡 It is the nearest MRT station to hospitals like Tan Tock Seng Hospital & Mount Elizabeth Novena Hospital.

🟪 Malls like Novena Square, Velocity mall, Royal Square and United Square malls are located in close proximity to Novena Station.

🟡 The Inland Revenue dept ( IRAS) is just sited opposite to this station.

🟢 Novena station was built on a former Jewish cemetery, and the station was completed in 1988. I know because I live around this area when I was a child.

My go to whenever I fly through T4 to get a quick bite. Quick, no frills, okay staff, and it has minced ginger, something that many chicken rice stalls have forgo, which is a shame.

The food is decent, price is okay for airport foodcourt. A basic roasted chicken rice is $5.5 while additional like braised egg is $1. Juat like other stall, ,there are special prices for staff.

There are other sets to choose from.

Novena Square has went through many improvements over the decades. Now it connencted to other shopping malls like United Square, Velocity, and Square2.

It's quite nice to shop there during the weekend morning because this area is mostly populated by working crowd during working days.

There are many sport shops, because Velocity is intended to be some kind of sport mall. There is a wall climbing on the top floor of Novena Square. Besides that, F&B, pharmacies, Cold Storage, bakeries, banks, toy shops, courses can be found here.

Went to Tcm here and the standards of cleanliness is still v high. V impressive. Keep up the good work guys! Air con was fresh and crisp and u could feel the cooling sensation. Attached are how clean looking the place is and how clean the lifts are

My wife and I were here today (17 DECEMBER 2025) for lunch and for shopping. We often come to this shopping mall via the North South Line alighting at the Novena MRT Station that is beneath this shopping mall and which is conveniently connected thereto via escalators or a flight of staircase. My wife and I love coming to this shopping mall for its very good eating places and retail stores therein. We also find this shopping mall to be well maintained and to afford a pleasant dining and shopping ambience therein with its wide enough aisles and cool air-conditioning.
buzzling mall. I like the shopscand food outlets here. very nicely furnished and inviting.
Good mall in Novena. Has most outlets for food and shopping. Decathlon, Uniqlo, Saladstop, Liv Activ, Mr Coconut. Connected to Novena MRT station and bus top.

Teacher Natalie is one of those teachers that students genuinely enjoy learning from. Ally always looks forward to her lessons and feels encouraged to do better after every class.

What we appreciate most is not just the academic guidance, but the positive and motivating classroom environment she creates. Even when the mock papers were challenging, the students stayed engaged and motivated because of her teaching style and encouragement.

We were especially happy to hear that Ally’s effort in reviewing her mistakes helped her achieve the highest score in class. Thank you, Teacher Natalie and The Learning Lab, for making learning both meaningful and enjoyable for the student

My son has been in TLL since primary 1 and since then, I never regretted signing up for TLL. TLL teaches topics in advance and teaches techniques to hone your answering skills, especially in Primary 5 and 6 where the workload and demand can get pretty overwhelming. In P6, all worksheets provided by TLL were helpful and benefited my son’s learning.Each worksheet had a section it focused so if my son wants to study a specific section, he can easily study it with the TLL worksheets. In PSLE, he achieved an AL6. TLL gave him so many techniques to use in exams and it was without a doubt the key reason why he achieved this score.

Very disappointing experience. The teaching quality was poor, and instead of providing proper support and a clear learning plan, the teachers kept complaining about the student’s level without offering real solutions or structured guidance.

We enrolled expecting a professional support centre, but most of the responsibility was pushed back onto the parents, with constant requests for support from home — as if the student was in a regular school, not a centre specialised in learning support.

Communication and follow-up were also very poor. Replies were slow, updates were limited, and there was little genuine care or attention towards the student’s progress.

Unfortunately, the overall service was far below expectations.

Mr Welson Lee taught my daughter science from Primary 4 to primary 6. He has high efficiency, is very patient and is very kind. Whenever science questions that my daughter does not know how to solve are sent to him online, he always replies in time. With Mr Lee's help, my daughter's science has improved a lot, getting AL1 for science in PSLE.

Would like to drop a note of appreciation to Teacher Deborah for guiding my boy to AL1 for English. Really appreciate Teacher Deborah's dedication, patience & the way she helped him grow more confident in the subject.
