We are grateful that she always updates us, and clearly points out his strengths and areas to improve. Even online, she makes sure every child gets equal attention. Very thankful for her hard work.

Several good eateries, bustling at the weekend. Convenient location across the road from the dragon playground. We enjoyed the family fun day.
Quite nice nursing room.
Very well maintained. It has Food (Mcdonald, Yakun, Restautant) , Sports (Swimming pool, Squash court, Multi Sports halll) , Tuition centre (Berries, Ballet, Physics), Korean Haircut shop...
Bowling was fun. Helpful staff that helped us with setting up the lanes for us. Restaurant on-site had a wide range of finger foods to make our little celebration a cheerful event.

Very big and spacious safra with many facilities. 1 Olympic sized pool with 2 baby pools. On a weekday, the pool is relatively empty as there are not many jobless people swimming on a weekday afternoon. They have MacDonalds and Yakun as well. We went to play the darts which is located inside the KTV room on lvl 2. There is a billiard room beside it and a bowling room on top.

I liked ❤️ to compliment Vini at the counter upon my visit on 16 June 2026 on weekdays at about 530pm as she served me with a great passion she helpfully check my almost soon to be expiry membership well and I will love to sign ✍️ up to renew in future as the space and surroundings was pleasant everything change here good idea for a meeting and events set up ⬆️ free wifi internet 🛜 worth visiting here!

This MRT station largely mirrors the standard efficiency and cleanliness expected of Singapore's rail network. An overhead bridge, complete with escalators, provides seamless access to the opposite side of Bukit Timah main road, and the short walk to the nearby bus stop is also comfortably sheltered. My only significant reservation lies with the current state of the surrounding area, which is unfortunately quite messy due to ongoing, seemingly endless road works.
I LOVE THE DIM EMO VIBES, very soothing vibes wheb I just got off the train. Really relaxing, please don't brigthen it like other stations ❤️❤️🙏🙏🙏

🟦 King Albert Park MRT station is an underground train station on the Downtown line (DTL) in Bukit Timah, Singapore.

🟩 Located at the western end of Bukit Timah Road at the junction of Blackmore Drive, this station serves the upscale residential estates along the Bukit Timah corridor.

🟦 The station has 2 Exits ( A & B). 🟨 Exit B is towards Sime Darby Centre ( building) at 896 Dunearn Road, Singapore 589472.
Like most DTL stations, very clean and aircon is strong

Just like a normal mrt station, copy and paste. Looks like woodlands north design. Good thing it's not crowded, there is a mall opposite the station linked by an overhead bridge. Rain no issue getting there.

Paragon has been a familiar hangout spot for me for many years, and it’s still one of Orchard Road’s most dependable malls. It has a classy, spacious feel and remains a great place to spend time whether you’re shopping, meeting friends, or just taking a slow walk indoors

There’s a strong mix of retail stores, good eateries, and practical conveniences all under one roof. I also like that it has Cold Storage Gold / Fresh for quality groceries, which makes it more than just a shopping mall. The presence of medical clinics adds to the convenience too

This is a modern upscale 6 level shopping mall right in the heart of Orchard Road. Most people who shop here knows what they want and they just go straight to the shop. Others come here for their lunch or simply have tea here for nice chit chats. Now’s the time for Chinese New Year shopping so the mall is beautifully decorated this year !! I love the beautiful lights !!! And can feel the Chinese New Year vibes and mood !!! I came here to buy my CNY clothes at Metro Paragon on level two. It’s been a wonderful and fruitful trip !! Bought 6 pieces 👍👍👍💗💗💗

A wonderful and good shopping experience having good brands like Prada and Gucci in them. It also has amazing food and restaurant options on the basement level. The best food you could find is the fish and Coast company which serves authentic fish to perfection and also the area has some good Italian food joints, and some nice Asian and local cuisine as well. It also has a nice supermarket to buy daily essentials and fresh fruits.

Stepping into Paragon feels like entering a high-end gallery. The layout is spacious and intuitive, featuring polished marble floors and a glass-fronted facade that has become a landmark on Orchard Road.

​Key Highlights
-​Luxury Fashion: The mall is synonymous with high fashion, housing iconic multi-story flagships for Gucci, Prada, and Givenchy, alongside luxury heavyweights like Saint Laurent, Balenciaga, and Loewe.

-​The "Junior" Floor: Level 5 is a dedicated sanctuary for families, featuring high-end children’s labels like Armani Junior and Petit Bateau, complemented by a free indoor playground and top-tier enrichment centers.

-​Exquisite Dining: From the Michelin-starred Crystal Jade Golden Palace to the legendary Imperial Treasure Super Peking Duck, the culinary offerings lean heavily toward quality. For something more casual yet chic, PS.Cafe and the basement's Din Tai Fung are perennial favorites.

-​Medical Wellness: Uniquely, the mall is connected to Paragon Medical, a premium tower housing world-class specialists and aesthetic clinics, allowing visitors to combine wellness appointments with luxury retail.

Paragon Shopping Centre is one of my favourite malls along Orchard Road when I feel like doing some upscale shopping. I enjoy browsing the luxury brands here—it’s elegant but still comfortable to walk around. The basement has plenty of nice food choices too, whether it’s grabbing a quick bite or sitting down for a proper meal. I also like that the mall has a supermarket and even a medical wing with specialist clinics, so it’s really convenient for almost everything.

It had been years since I last stepped foot in this library, as I hardly visit Toa Payoh.
For those who enjoy a touch of history and nostalgia, don’t miss the old photos along the staircase. They showcase the history of Toa Payoh town, Toa Payoh Town Park, and the library itself. It’s really interesting, and I think all libraries should have something like this so residents can learn more about the history of their own town. Thumbs up 👍

I’d describe Toa Payoh Library as one of those “just nice” neighborhood libraries — not massive and overwhelming, but definitely not cramped either. It’s an easy few-minute walk from the MRT, which already makes it super convenient, and the location is a big plus since Toa Payoh Mall is right nearby. If you ever feel like grabbing a snack, bubble tea, or a proper meal before or after studying, you’re honestly spoiled for choice.

One detail I really liked was the stairwell — instead of being plain and boring, it’s illustrated with little bits of information about Toa Payoh and libraries, which adds a nice local touch and makes the walk between floors more interesting. The layout is also very straightforward: the first floor is dedicated to children, the second floor is for adults, and the third floor is for teens and the programming or activity zones.

Both the adult and teen sections have a good amount of seating, and what’s even better is that the seats are usually available. I didn’t need to make any reservations, and it didn’t feel stressful trying to hunt for a spot. It’s a comfortable, practical place to read, study, or just spend a quiet afternoon without feeling rushed or crowded.

Toa Payoh Library is more than just a place for books; it’s a time capsule. Growing up here, the library and the iconic Dragon Playground nearby were the backdrops of my childhood. I am so glad that as the library has modernized, the effort to preserve its essence—and the heritage of the surrounding area—hasn't been lost. It’s a rare piece of 'Old Singapore' that still feels vibrant and relevant today.

The place is convenient for everyone one with every age to read or borrow the book for library member. There is give chance to exchange book as well which everyone could donate books been read and take others book donate by others. There is 3 floors building and providing books from children to adults with various topics.

A 3-storey building comprising various collections at each level to suit every age group’s needs. A captivating local Singapore collection which hosts a multitude of authors and genres is something to check to out.

There is a “just returned” shelf the moment one enters so browsing through that for a quick borrow is effective. Displays based off of the theme for a particular length of time are seen at the staging area and book recommendations which follow the theme are also recommended. Very creative!

There is a lack of own ownership when it comes to the librarians checking on patrons to see if they’re following the rules. This has been observed quite a few times!

There was a bunch of noisy children in the Teen section nonetheless who were running around and creating a lot of commotion. There is a dedicated space for young readers at the 1st floor and some noise is to be expected there but the 2nd and 3rd floors are generally quiet. Not one librarian checked on them…

Also, there was a man lounging on the seat at a 180 degree angle, almost seeming like he was back home! A certain decorum is to be maintained when it comes to human behaviour but this individual had no social awareness and yet again, not one librarian checked up on him.

There was another NLB branch that’s currently undergoing revamping that was very strict with its policies. Librarians would chide patrons should they sit on the floor, even if their bags were kept on the seats. Should this not be the expected role of a librarian, among many others?

Last point to note is that if one is to use the browsing computer to search for a particular title, the shelf number may be inaccurate.

Edit: There are constant incidents of uninitiated noise and the worst bit is it’s the librarians themselves who are making it. Also, at this time, a lot of technicians move in and out yet completely forgetting library rules and etiquette. Do not visit if you’re looking for a quiet, peaceful read.

We joined Odyssey for 2 year now, as parent, very happy to see the development of our kid, the curriculum is great, inclusive, not just focus on academics, but also in social, culture diversity and creativity. Attach some photos in today’s little chef event.

Incredible campus with big outdoor field! Teachers are all passionate, patient & caring for the kids! Early childhood development is crucial for our kids and Odyssey Wilkinson is the top choice, second to none.

Very nurturing teachers and great environment to grow the little ones. We love the outdoor space and also the many curated activities and events the school put together. Thank you Odyssey Wilkinson team, awesome work you are doing!

The school organises a Chinese Revival Festival every year. The children performed traditional Chinese drums and Kong Fu fans through which they were immersed in Chinese culture and given a great chance to feel the power of team work.

Odyssey Wilkinson has been such a positive experience for us - the teachers are nurturing, campus is beautiful and we align greatly with the learn through play curriulum approach while encouraging independence.
The teachers have greatly helped our 3 year in developing and bringing out his own personality!

This is my most-visited out of all the Fitness First branches. It is usually not as crowded as other branches and the spacing is good. The equipments and locker/shower areas are well-maintained by the cleaning staff, and there is enough space for stretching. Also, they closes at 10pm on the weekdays so you got plenty of time to come after work.

I had a great experience at Fitness First Orchard. The facilities are clean, well-maintained, and the overall atmosphere is very comfortable for working out.

Special thanks to Harvino at the front desk — he is extremely friendly, kind, and welcoming. From the moment I arrived, he made me feel comfortable and well assisted. His positive attitude and professionalism really stand out and add a great first impression to the club.

Highly recommended gym in Orchard, both for the facilities and the amazing staff!
Great gym in Orchard - lots of equipment including good free weights, running machines and resistance work - would recommend! Sauna and steam room also available as well as cycling studio

Friendly & professional
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Shoutout to Lany from Paragon Fitness! She’s always welcoming, super attentive, and goes out of her way to make sure members feel comfortable. Whether it’s helping with equipment, sharing tips on form, or just checking in with a smile, she makes every gym session better. Paragon’s lucky to have her!
I’ve been coming to Paragon Fitness for a few months now, and Lany is hands down one of the best staff members. She remembers names, keeps the gym floor spotless, and is quick to help anyone who looks lost with the machines. Her positive attitude is honestly contagious. If you’re new here, ask for Lany — she’ll make you feel right at home.
Best customer services and working out experiences in Fitness First Paragon. Creating memories with my friends 💕❤️

result.name: BlueTree Education (Orchard) | PSLE & O Level (Science and Math) Tuition Centre

reviews.text:
Teacher Jolene has been an amazing Science teacher for Noah. Even though the lessons are conducted online via Zoom, she is able to keep the students highly engaged through her energetic teaching style, interactive lessons, and clear explanations.

Noah genuinely looks forward to her classes every week and often shares the interesting concepts and scientific discoveries he has learned. Teacher Jolene has a wonderful ability to break down complex topics into simple, easy-to-understand concepts, making Science both accessible and enjoyable for young learners.

What impresses us most is her patience, dedication, and genuine passion for teaching. She encourages students to ask questions, think critically, and develop a curiosity about the world around them. Her lessons are well-structured, engaging, and effective, even in an online setting.

Thank you, Teacher Jolene, for inspiring a love for Science in your students and for making learning such a positive experience. We truly appreciate the effort and care you put into every lesson. Highly recommended! 🔬🌟📚😊

We have had a very positive experience with BlueTree Education. Teacher Faith, Teacher Jolene and Teacher Hui Lin are all very dedicated, patient and caring teachers who genuinely want their students to do well.

What I appreciate most is that they do not just rush through the syllabus. They take the time to make sure students understand the topics clearly, and very often go beyond the scheduled lesson time just to ensure everyone is able to follow. This really shows their heart for teaching and their commitment to helping each student improve.

The lessons are engaging, well-explained and thoughtfully conducted. The teachers are also approachable and supportive, which gives both students and parents a lot of confidence. Thank you Teacher Faith, Teacher Jolene and Teacher Hui Lin for your dedication and care. Highly recommended.

Teacher Hui Lin is a dedicated and caring teacher with a true passion for Science. I greatly appreciate that she provides weekly updates to parents on what has been covered in class, which really helps us stay informed and support our child’s learning.

She is also very thoughtful — when my son couldn’t make it for his usual lesson or during her absence, I will check with her availability on her other lessons. She remembers I have a preference for that and there's once, prior to her absence, she asked the admin to arrange a replaced session under her for him. Though I remember eventually we also can't make it for the replaced session, that level of care really stood out.

Since transferring to SSC in P5, my son has made excellent progress under her guidance, achieving great results in Science. We are very grateful for her support and highly recommend her to any parent looking for a dedicated Science teacher.

My boy takes online lessons with Bluetree. He went with some skepticism, but said with pleasant surprise that it worked out very well, and the teachers were very approachable, knowledgeable and teach very clearly and he found the lessons very helpful.

As parents, we like the structured routine approach of the online set-up of Google classroom to submit the assigned homework before the lesson and it is marked almost immediately. The structured timeline means the son has consistent contact with Science and is used to a disciplined study schedule.

And the lesson have delivered. We started when he was in Primary 5, after WA2. Now, at the WA2 mark in P6, Science is now his strongest P6 subject when it was previously his weakest, pulling down his overall AL grades in P5.

I will strongly recommend Bluetree.

My child was enrolled in the PSLE Preparatory Course (PPC) with Teacher Jo, a very exprienced teacher. She has very fun and creative methods to help students remember science concepts and answering techniques. Teacher Jo also consistently highlighted crucial traps and important things to look out for in the PSLE paper, which really built up my child's confidence. Teacher Jo goes beyond just teaching to ensure the students enjoy science and are well prepared for PSLE. Thank you Teacher Jo!

Private members only club it is one of Singapore 's oldest and most prestigious clubs steeped in history and heritage. Incredible location in the heart of the city with fine dining , sports and recreation facilities and even accommodation for members and their guests.

Super impressive, super classy, super historical club in the heart of the city. Very impressive and and I’m grateful my friend Jacob brought me as a guest. We were surrounded by the movers and shakers of 🇸🇬— as well as their families.
WoW! I was the invited guest of a member family and this was one of the highlights of my trip. Had so much fun at the tavern and enjoyed the food. Highly reccomend!
Be the 1st visitor at Flower Dome n enjoy stunning sunrise views

Update December 2025:
Still a great roastbeef experience, accompanied by a very reasonably priced excellent wine.

- - -

One of our "must-do's" when in Singapore is a roastbeef dinner with friends at The Tanglin Club's "Tavern" restaurant. This members-only club has served them for well over 100 years, and they are a perfect treat for beef aficionados. Make sure you order the "Gentleman's Cut" 😋

Pros: Excellent location and a nice atmosphere for studying.
Cons: The aircon doesn't circulate well so it gets very stuffy, and some of the books are quite outdated.
Overall it's still very good.

All the memories here at the SMU Library, is extremely heartfelt. Closing time, that every day song. Level 5, that corner where you'd always hang out. Those international school kid corners where you find cliques. People hogging quiet corners early in the mornings. Really, good times and bad times spent here, that will always hold a space deep in my heart.

The library is old and outdated, with many broken chairs and tables. The lights often don't work, forcing students to study in dimly lit areas.

The students here have no manners at all. They don't understand the importance of keeping quiet in the library. They talk and play with abandon, and laughter fills the air.

The only positive thing is the receptionist at the door. She always greets students with a smile.
This Library Is Very Good All The Library Staff Are Very Friendly ! Best !
Quiet. Conducive environment for studying. The building is cool during the day also. With lots of book to refer if you need additional materials not taught in class.

This shopping mall is highly convenient and offers an impressive variety of stores, making it a place where you can find almost anything you need. The directory includes a well-stocked supermarket, numerous fashion boutiques, electronics retailers, and a wide selection of dining options ranging from quick bites to sit-down restaurants. The layout is generally logical, though it can take some time to navigate due to the sheer size of the complex. It serves as an excellent one-stop destination for both daily necessities and specialty items. Facilities like restrooms and parking are well-maintained and accessible. Given the extensive range of shops and services available under one roof, it is a highly practical choice for weekend errands or casual browsing. However, because of its comprehensive nature, it tends to attract large crowds, especially during peak hours and weekends. Overall, it provides a highly efficient and reliable shopping experience for visitors looking to complete multiple tasks in a single trip.

A nice, bustling shopping mall set over multiple levels. Lots of restaurants and takeaways across multiple levels, plus a large Kopitiam food court on Level 6. Besides the eateries and 'regular shops' there is a cinema, games arcades and various niche stores, plus access to the Dhoby Ghaut MRT station. Notably clean restrooms.

Plaza Singapura is very crowded but extremely useful. I had multiple skin and hair treatments here and got excellent results was 99.99.9% effective. You can trust the professionals here; they deliver exactly what they promise.😎

This is a long established shopping mall very popular with the locals especially when Yaohan, a Japanese brand first opened up it’s flagship super supermarket here and it was always crowded with families visiting, shopping and dining. It’s sitting immediately on the right when you face the ISTANA.

Sleek and modern mall with immense verticality, spanning up towards the 7th storey. Each floor especially the upper ones are not that large hence it's quite easy to navigate, it is very cool and air-conditioned. Aside from that, the toilets arerelativelyv clean and there is an assortment of food, entertainment, and shopping as well scattered throughout the mall. It is also conveniently linked to the large Dhoby Ghaut MRT Interchange which makes this mall very accessible.

Came here and forgot my bus card. Thank god that singapore has amazing people. The bus terminal person there understood my situation and let me go to bugis for free. When I got to bugis message already passed through. Incredible people,cooperation and kindness. Also 2 people helped me convince the person.
I add already but can’t see in google when will updates
Spaceship looking station that just on the main road of singgapore poly. There a 7 eleven store that not open 24hours

Bad service at  28A block. Lift spoil, had to climb 28 stairs down for work that morning,
not a nice way to start Monday. 😖
Leads to Singapore Polytechnic.. my institute 😀

Had a great experience at Anytime Fitness Orchard 313. The staff member who assisted me, Warren, was exceptionally friendly and welcoming, which made a real difference to my visit. Great customer service goes a long way, and he definitely helped create a positive atmosphere.

The gym is clean and well-maintained. One area for improvement would be to add more HYROX-focused equipment and training tools, as HYROX has become increasingly popular and it would be great to see the facility cater more to that growing community.

Overall, a positive experience and I look forward to visiting again.
One of my fave outlets, the air conditioning is exactly what you need. Aesthetic is great too. Equipment maintained well.

Amazing gym, love how it's nice and cold enough for me to put on a hoodie and workout more comfortably. Is super well equipped with incredibly friendly, helpful staff, one of whom I personally believe plays excellent music as well. My only gripe is the 40kg and 42.5kg dumbbells are frequently missing (taken to the personal training studio and not returned after use), making certain workouts less optimal/ more bothersome.

The location is quite convenient especially if you're those who like to workout during the weekend and go shopping/eating out at town after. The staff is friendly. Plenty of machine varieties suitable for bodybuilding or powerlifting. The free weights area might be a bit cramped, but thank God the squat racks/lifting platforms are not. The changing rooms are well-equipped with toiletries.

AF Orchard is conveniently located near public transport and surrounded by plenty of restaurants, making it easy to fit workouts into a busy schedule. The gym is well-equipped with properly maintained machines and clean, modern facilities including toilets and lockers. Experienced trainers are always on hand to offer guidance and support. Equipment is handled with care, and the overall environment is safe and welcoming. The support staffs are welcoming and approachable.
Amazing vegetarian am eat curries cooked to perfection with absolute perfect blend of spices and flavour ...most authentic and with passion and love...this is a real  family restaurant.

Excellently located MRT Station on the Northsouth Line (red colour line), conveniently connecting TripleOne Somerset and 313 Somerset, with covered walkways to either sides which helps during rainy periods. The only issue which has improved over the years but is not fully resolved is the illegal smoking that still takes place on the narrow sheltered walkway between the MRT Station exit (side facing 313 Somerset) and TripleOne Somerset, with passers-by including families with young children sometimes sadly breathing in second hand smoke from inconsiderate smokers who flaunt the clear rules and multiple signs all over the place. This is not a dedicated smoke point. Photos here of the smokers taken 11 April 2024 at 2108-2109hrs.

this is one of the mrt stations we go to the most! although it is crowded throughout the day, it is very clean. it is also directly connected to 313 somerset, orchard central and orchard gateway, and is also close to two bus stops, allowing it to be an extremely convenient mrt station! there are also convenience stores and other stores right next to the gantry. this is definitely a good mrt stop!

Somerset MRT station is a very interesting set up, being one station away from Orchard, and Dhoby Ghaut. It's home to the many malls that have flourished around here, all strung up by the underground walk ways.

Station has been around for a long time, yet it's still clean and very well maintained. The station also connects to the many Bus Stations that you'd likely need to transit at.

Personally I like to leverage Somerset as a slightly strategic place to transit, given the many sheltered walkways they have established, so whether rain or shine, you'll have protection getting from place to place.

I lost my card yesterday, and today I went to the SimplyGo counter at Somerset MRT. The staff, Juliana, was really fast and polite. She helped me block the lost card and even made a new one for me. Great service!"

A lifesaver in the heat of Chinatown! Located right above the MRT station, this mall is the perfect place to cool down. I highly recommend visiting the NTUC FairPrice supermarket in the basement to buy souvenirs (chocolates, coffee) at local prices instead of tourist prices outside. Plenty of good dining options too. Clean restrooms and very accessible.

I really enjoy walking around here, the streets are very walkable, clean. There are so many food spots, cafe, and small shops, so it never feels boring. Easy to access by MRT and a great place to just stroll, eat, and enjoy the atmosphere, especially in the evening.

Chinatown has the old side that has not changed and the new side with full of contemporary restaurants. In the center is a beautiful temple. Along the streets are full of shops selling souvenirs, food, fruits, etc. At night, it is totally different when it is lights come on. The famous street food place called Hawkers market is also located in Chinatown. Had a wonderful time there.

Chinatown is one of the most lively and colorful places in Singapore. It beautifully blends traditional culture with modern city life. The streets are full of energy, with souvenir shops, local markets, and amazing food options everywhere.

The area is very clean and well maintained, making it easy and comfortable to explore on foot. You can visit famous landmarks like the Sri Mariamman Temple and Buddha Tooth Relic Temple, which add a strong cultural and spiritual charm to the place.

Chinatown is also a paradise for food lovers. From street food to authentic Chinese restaurants, every corner offers something delicious. It’s a perfect place for shopping, photography, and experiencing Singapore’s heritage. A must-visit destination for anyone traveling to Singapore.

This is the newest and best shopping mall in Chinatown precinct with Fairprice supermarket and many F&B shops without the quaint massage parlors or antique shops. There is even a library that offers rich heritage resources and comfortable resting place for both locals and tourists.

A community center in an old district to serve the citizen living in that vicinity.
Collection of the hard copy CDC voucher

I would like to sincerely commend Tiong Bahru Community Club for hosting a fantastic Christmas event on 6 and 7 December 2025 at the CC and Kim Pong Park.

As a proud member of this neighbourhood, I look forward to this annual event — and this year’s edition truly stood out. The programme was wonderfully engaging for all ages, from the entertaining magician who delighted the children, to the creative booth games with fun tidbit rewards that appealed to both young and old. The soothing carols and lively band performances added to the festive atmosphere.

The vibrant scene of prata stalls, karaoke on one side, and the children’s funfair with exciting rides on the other was heart-warming to experience. It was evident that the event was thoughtfully planned and beautifully executed.

All the staff and volunteers were friendly and helpful, contributing to a joyful and memorable weekend spent with family and friends.

Thank you to the committee, management, and staff for your hard work and dedication. You did a fantastic job this year!

Went to Tiong Bahru Market Hawker Centre to try Loo's Hainanese Curry Rice 02-67. Very very crowded and long que as well as very hot today 1st April 2026. Had pork chop, kong bak, cabbage,eggs and tofu for $8.30. Ok, good but not the best. 4/5. On the other the Jian Bo Shui Kueh  02-05 is still the best, 5/5.

Nice and quiet and clean!!! Clean toilets and vending machines near the court. The floor is nice and the nets seem like they are regularly maintained, though when I was there in December it was a little torn. Excellent food all around. Classic Singapore architecture and clean roads! Lovely!!
Sufficient activities to keep the community engage. there is a learning center within the compound. I really enjoy the older building structure and glad is part of the heritage trail.

It’s great working out at Fitness First One Raffles Quay club. The front desk staff are always friendly, welcoming, and helpful, which makes every visit a pleasant one.

My personal trainer, Joe is very knowledgeable and fun to train with. There is never a boring or scary session with him. I’m always learning new things from him and he help pushes me to my limit yet keeping it manageable. He has been incredibly supportive and played a big role in helping me achieve my fitness and weight loss goals, which I truly appreciate. Highly recommend him if anyone is looking for a PT.

The equipments and machines are clean and well-maintained, creating a comfortable environment to work out in. You can also enjoy the amazing marina bay view while running on the treadmill.

You can also enjoy other free facilities like the body analysis to keep track of your body goals and also the sauna and steam room.

Bravo to ORQ gym outlet for creating a motivating and welcoming experience. GX instructors are outstanding -knowledgeable, energetic and genuinely attentive to every participant.
Front desk crews deserve special praise too: friendly, proactive and remarkably personal. It's impressive that they remember members name, clothing sizes and preferences; that thoughtful touch makes very visit feel like returning to a community, not jus a facility. A heartfelt thanks to the cleaning aunties and uncles who work diligently to keep space spotless throughout the day; their consistency sets the tone for hygiene and comfort across the gym.
One small suggestion to enhance an already excellent environment: the music volume at the resting area can be a bit loud at times. Slightly lowering it would make conversations, recovery and post workout cool down more comfortable while keeping the upbeat vibes on the gym floor.
Overall this is a well run gym that blends professionalism with warmth. Keep up the fantastic work. Thanks for caring, listening and consistently elevating our experience everyday.

I really enjoyed this Halloween BodyCombat class by Ming Fei and Kenny! This theme, combined with the entertaining instructors, really made this workout a lot more enjoyable and engaging! However, I hope the class can also be extended for a longer period, as the 60 mins felt too short for a Halloween BC! I hope these classes can continue for the years to come :)

Del was the one who call me immediately when I request trial out at website. Was replying to Ella AI on WA n receive call from him. Super fast response. Did heads up me need 1 day min for trial pass but he managed to squeeze me in for yoga flow at 1650 r 1700. Really miss going for yoga classes much needed after emotional drain that I went thru today n this class perks me up n make me wanna go back yoga.
Han who Del passed to was really cheerful n patient to talk n go thru with me the promo for new sign up.

One of the nice fitness clubs to go. Location is strategic. Equipments are complete. A balance split among free weight, equipment and resting areas. Main studio is equipped with full length mirror which means dance class can be held there. There are spacious steam room and dry sauna room. Staff are friendly and helpful.  Hopefully this club can maintain here.

This entrance to Chinatown is clean and efficient. It has good toilet facilities which are tricky to find outside the station. Like a number of MRT stations it has internal decoration reflecting the notable characteristics of its location. I think it’s always fun to look out for these.

Chinatown MRT Station (DT19/NE4) is a key interchange on the Downtown and North-East Lines, making it incredibly convenient for both locals and tourists. The station is clean, modern, and well-maintained, with clear signage and easy navigation.

Its location is perfect for exploring the vibrant Chinatown area, including nearby attractions like the Buddha Tooth Relic Temple, Chinatown Heritage Centre, and Maxwell Food Centre. The station also provides direct access to Chinatown Point Mall, making it a great spot for shopping and dining.

Highly recommend using this station for seamless travel and access to Singapore’s rich cultural district!

I like this station exit A , accessible with escalators from the basement station. The moment you come out, the street is mostly covered, with lots of souvenirs shops and restaurants. The prices are pretty reasonable and good value for money. You can find various options for souvenirs, be it for the tourists or for Singaporeans who intend to buy some souvenirs for your overseas friends. If you are taking train to this station and looking for food or shopping for souvenirs, try come out out from this exit. Enjoy.
Recently I went to Chinatown using another exit C, this place is also very vibrant, full of activities, a lot of events, hawker Centres are within 1 min walk. Lots of food, shopping, drinks, fruits, and money changers. If you do not know how to go, you can always go back to the basement MRT stations and look for the exit guide A,B,C,D.

The MRT is the best way to get around the city—efficient, affordable, and well-connected. Several stations are conveniently located near major malls like Chinatown Point, OG, and People’s Park Complex, making it easy to combine shopping and sightseeing

about 33 minutes ride from the airport.

a bit of a maze going out to this exit. had to ask from train personnel and follow the arrow closely.

good thing I went to the correct exit..

Many stalls are closed or not rented out.. those that stays open have pretty good standard and choice available.
The hawker is airy and face a park, it’s windy and very serene in the morning and evening.
Some stalls with long que is the western grille and fish soup.  Can come for a beer or coffee to chillax.

Special mention to the gourmet mixed rice at level 1! Couple running the stall is very generous and nice, food is perfect for my palate (not too oily, spicy or salty)! Feels quite healthy. The most affordable and yummy caifan ever~ I always patron their stall when I’m around the area, and would go out of the way for it too :) I’ve been raving about their food a lot to my friends too, looove it so much!

$3.30 for this amount of food is insane (in a good way) in this economy…

Sad place. Every other stall is up for rent. Some of the stalls with great reviews didn’t survive. At 7.30pm on a Friday night, the place is empty except 1 or 2 stalls with good reviews and still surviving, seen queue.  Rest of stalls, it’s just depressing.
Not worth the effort.

Huge spacious and clean. Pretty airy too.
Overall quite nice feel and vibes to it.
Saw quite afew stalls which looks good however tummy couldnt allow them all.

Not in my hood but will be back to try this the next time i'm near.

Go to the 2nd floor of this hawker center to find this Ramen gem. I ordered the $9.90 Sakura Ebi Premium Ramen and thoroughly enjoyed the rich umami flavour of the prawn stock soup. The noodles are firm and tasty, it comes with generous clams, scallops, charsiew, smoked duck and meatball. It's a serendipitous find!

result.name: PlayFACTO School @ Pasir Ris Vidacity | Student Care Pasir Ris

Pasir Ris Mall — The New Heart of the East

Standing beside Pasir Ris MRT and Bus Interchange, Pasir Ris Mall is the newest lifestyle destination in Singapore’s eastern coast — a modern community hub where urban living meets coastal charm.

Opened in 2025, the mall is part of the larger Pasir Ris 8 integrated development, developed by Allgreen Properties and Kerry Properties. Designed as a seamless link between home, transport, and recreation, it offers residents and visitors everything within reach — from fashion and dining to wellness, groceries, and family entertainment.

Architecturally, Pasir Ris Mall draws inspiration from the nearby park and sea. Its design blends natural light, open terraces, and lush greenery, creating a calm and airy environment that mirrors the laid-back rhythm of the East. Within minutes, visitors can stroll to Pasir Ris Park or the beach — making it a rare retail space that connects urban convenience with nature’s serenity.

More than just a shopping centre, Pasir Ris Mall is envisioned as a social heart — where people gather, families unwind, and community life thrives. It reflects Singapore’s new urban design philosophy: integrated, sustainable, and people-centred.

Pasir Ris Town Park, which serves as a wonderful recreational hub for the community. The park centers around a large marine fishing pond, making it an ideal destination for anglers and fishing enthusiasts to practice their skills.

In addition to its appeal for fishing, the park is excellent for residents looking to stay physically active, as it features a dedicated Fitness Corner and a paved, flat jogging track. Visitors can also enjoy a variety of dining options within the park itself, including a hawker centre, various eateries, and a bistro, providing a great setting for an evening meal by the water.

Accessibility to the park is very convenient; it is well-connected by two bridges crossing Sungei Tampines and via the Park Connector. Furthermore, Pasir Ris MRT Station is located just a few minutes away, making it easily accessible for everyone.

It truly is a fantastic space for residents and visitors alike.

My son has been at Kinderland since his infant care days, and now he's moving to N1 next year. There is a special Childcare Centre - Loyang Point Kinderland Child Care Centre. ​From the outside, you might not see it, but inside this centre is a place bursting with life, love, and learning. It’s a Centre of dedication, led by the incredibly humble and respectful Principal, Ms. Mei Fong. ​I am consistently impressed by the entire team, from the lady who takes temperatures each morning to the teachers and Principal—they all know every child by name. This center turns every small moment into a learning opportunity, from encouraging kids to put on their own shoes to teaching them to carry their haversacks and personal hygiene. This hands-on guidance truly helps our children build skills and values every single day.

​It's clear that the teachers here genuinely love the children. As a parent whose child was once an infant here, I saw firsthand the level of care given. They pay close attention to every detail, from a child's appetite and health to teaching them new social and developmental skills through fun activities. My baby returns home every workday with clean diapers, well fed , changed clothings and happy.

​A special shout-out and deepest appreciation to the teachers who have had a profound impact on my child's journey: Principal Mei Fong, Teacher Nira, Teacher Florence, Teacher Fizah, and Ms. Wang. My First Impressions of Tampines Eco Green Park: A Mini Forest Park in Tampines Town

Experience nature just a stone's throw away from the bustling urban environment of Tampines.

This tranquil park is the ideal destination for anyone looking to connect with nature.

*What is Tampines Eco Green Park? - Mini Forest Park!**

Tampines Eco Green is a 36.5-hectare park, offering a rare glimpse of nearly untouched nature in this urban area. Unlike Singapore’s more manicured parks, Tampines Eco Green features marshlands, secondary forests (mini forest park) and freshwater ponds. Opened in 2011, it has become a favoured spot for bird-watchers, nature enthusiasts, and anyone seeking a peaceful retreat without venturing far from home. Residents in the surrounding neighborhoods can truly appreciate this natural oasis.

**Where is mini forest park?**
This triangular park is situated between Tampines Avenue 9 and 12, bordered by the Tampines Expressway and the Sungei Tampines canal. With entrances located along Avenue 12 and via Sun Plaza Park, it is easily accessible, and it connects seamlessly to the broader park connector network for those traveling on foot or by bicycle.

This mini forest features three walking trails and boasts a rich biodiversity. Nestled at the edge of Tampines town, this green space offers joggers and walkers a rustic escape just moments away from the urban hustle.

It’s truly a hidden gem, featuring well-maintained pathways that provide a peaceful passage into nature. The early morning tranquility, before the day's activities commence, is particularly enchanting. Whether you are a nature lover or simply in search of a refreshing start to your day, this park merits a visit. Residents of nearby HDB blocks or those just across from the park will greatly appreciate this natural retreat.

Come and immerse yourself in the beauty of nature!

Chris
30 May 2026

A decent spot for a quiet stroll, but nothing particularly special. It's quite small, just ~3km of  basic grass trails that felt repetitive. It's okay for a quick 40-minute loop, but don't expect  amenities like paved paths, cafes, playgrounds or carparks, since it is designed as a minimum intervention sanctuary for wildlife.
I love the rustic feel during the walk. It's a small but good place to escape the concrete jungle.

Tampines Eco Park: A Brilliant Morning Awakening with Nature's Wonders (Sunscreen & Mozzie Spray Essential!)

My early morning walk through Tampines Eco Park was an absolute delight, a vibrant start to the day under the brilliant Singaporean sun. The ground, still soft and yielding from the previous night's rain, released a lovely, earthy fragrance into the fresh air.

The park was already teeming with life. Numerous roosters and hens busily foraged, embodying the early bird adage. The pond was equally active, with sleek monitor lizards gracefully swimming and others basking lazily on the banks, their scales shimmering in the sunlight. It was a captivating glimpse into the park's diverse wildlife.

To fully enjoy this natural haven, a couple of tips are essential: don't forget your sunscreen, as even the early morning sun can be strong in Singapore. Additionally, a good lemongrass-based mosquito repellent is highly recommended to keep those pesky insects at bay, allowing you to fully immerse yourself in the park's tranquility.

Tampines Eco Park is a true gem, offering well-maintained paths for a peaceful escape into nature. The early morning serenity, before the daytime bustle, is particularly special. Whether you're a nature lover or simply seeking a refreshing start to your day, this park is well worth a visit.

Highly Recommended for Tampines Residents

🌳🌿Tampines Eco Green, unlike other typical heartland Park, is a ecological Park set in a totally natural setting with unique landscape which include marshland, freshwater ponds, open grassland and forested area.
🐢🐠🐙The Park's three natural trails, Diversity Trail, Marsh Trail, Forest Trail, wound around the entire Eco park, leading visitors to diverse natural setting, including various strategically located bird hides and freshwater ponds.
🌿🐦🦅The Park is best enjoyed for bird watching and being close to nature.
🧺🚽🧹Interestingly, the only toilet at Tampines Eco Green is a eco-toilet that has no flushing, but uses biodegrading to turn human waste to compost.
🎄The ecological Park is a welcome deviation from the usual Park.
🍂🌿Very tranquil and idyllic park, except for the occasional MRT trains.

​The park's main activities are conveniently clustered around McDonald's, which serves as a great central spot for dining, socializing, and recreation. Aside from active play, it is a fantastic place for gatherings, featuring camping grounds, barbecue pits, large open lawns for picnics, kite flying and jogging.

West Coast Park is a long but relatively quiet park that stretches along Singapore’s western coastline. It’s a wonderful place for a relaxed outdoor day, especially for families with kids

One of the highlights is the large playground, which is one of the better ones in Singapore. The climbing structures, slides, and play areas keep children entertained for a long time, and there’s plenty of open space nearby for running around

The park is also great for cycling and casual walks. You can easily rent bicycles or family pedal carts near the entrance and ride along the park connectors while enjoying the sea breeze

For those who enjoy spending more time outdoors, the park has BBQ pits and camping areas, making it perfect for weekend gatherings with friends and family. There are also scenic spots along the waterfront where people fish or simply relax and watch the ships in the distance

I do like to come to this nature park by the sea - to take in the salty air and the cool sea breeze, both of which relaxes and then invigorates me. I like to hear the waves crashing against the shore and to be among the greenery in this nature park - in particular, the bamboo fringed walkway as regards the latter. The amenities at this nature park is adequate - with enough car parking spaces and public toilets. And overall, this nature park is well-maintained.
Well maintained park and a good place to spend time alone or with family and friends. Suitable for all ages, whether to exercise in the mornings or catch a lovely sunset in the evenings.

West Coast Park is considerably smaller than East Coast Park (ECP), but it remains a highly viable zone for R&R. I have scouted the perimeter and identified several optimal coordinates with good sightlines to hold position and decompress.

Civilian population density in this grid is minimal. The decreased foot traffic results in a secure perimeter and a highly tranquil atmosphere—ideal for lowering your guard and recovering stamina.

Be advised on two minor variables:

First, visuals on the maritime border are compromised and do not match the aesthetics of ECP.

Second, heavy transport vessels are active in the adjacent waters. My MJOLNIR armor’s environmental sensors occasionally detected industrial exhaust drifting into the area. It is non-lethal, but noticeable without a helmet filtration system.

Overall assessment: A solid, quiet sector for downtime. Proceed to coordinates at your discretion.

The first shopping centre at Pasir Ris .It still holds that familiar charm with many of the old  tenants still there. As it is just next to the mrt station, it remains an important food location for those coming back from school or work.

I used to enjoy eating at White Sands Basement 1, especially the fishball noodle stall beside Twyst Pasta. Unfortunately, the experience has become very unpleasant because of the extremely strong and irritating fumes coming from the pasta stall whenever they cook.

The smoke and chilli smell are so strong that many people walking past start coughing immediately. Even customers sitting nearby, neighbouring stall workers, and people across the dining area are visibly affected. I have personally seen elderly customers coughing repeatedly because of the fumes.

What disappointed me most was the staff attitude. While people around were coughing from the strong fumes, one staff member appeared to mock the coughing and laugh about it instead of showing concern. It felt very unprofessional and inconsiderate towards customers sharing the public dining area.

I understand cooking produces smells, but this situation has been ongoing for a long time and seriously affects the overall dining environment. Sadly, because of this experience, I no longer feel comfortable coming here to eat.

result.name: MindChamps MindSpace @ Pasir Ris (Pasir Ris Mall) | After School Student Care for P1-P5 | MindChamps Enrichment for P1-P5

reviews.text:
My son recently joined the centre, and I am so glad to see how much he enjoys his time there!

Initially, he was very resistant to the idea of attending a student care centre and preferred to stay home alone. However, the warmth and dedication of the staff and teachers quickly helped him feel welcomed and comfortable. The science experiment activities conducted during the school holiday programme were a particular highlight for him — he came home excitedly sharing what he had learned and was genuinely looking forward to returning the next day.

I would also like to express my appreciation to Lydia, who was incredibly patient, responsive, and supportive throughout the enquiry and enrolment process. She took the time to address my many questions thoughtfully and professionally. What I value most is that Lydia and her team place students' growth, well-being, and psychological safety at the heart of everything they do.

Thank you, Lydia and team, for creating such a nurturing and engaging environment for the children. We are truly grateful for the positive impact you have made on my son's experience.

highly recommend MindSpace at Pasir Ris! The learning environment is wonderful—bright, clean, spacious, and highly conducive to focusing. What truly sets this center apart, though, is the exceptional support and guidance. The team goes above and beyond to provide dedicated help whenever it's needed, ensuring no student is left behind. The trainers are incredibly professional, engaging, and patient, making complex concepts easy to understand. We feel incredibly grateful and reassured having such a reliable and nurturing team supporting our learning journey!

Many parents, including myself, may initially be skeptical about the value MindSpace can provide given its high school fees. I too had reservations, especially considering that the fee is significantly higher compared to general after-school care. However, after attending a sharing session from the center, I decided to give it a chance.

My children's academic results were not satisfactory, and we lacked the time to help them with their studies in the evenings, especially considering how tired they were after a long day. Having enrichment classes during their time at after-school care has allowed them to use their time more purposefully than simply playing around without a structured program. This also frees up our weekends, allowing us to engage in interactive activities with our children instead of shuttling them from one enrichment class to another.

After being at MindSpace for over three months, we have already noticed improvements in their academic performance. We are still monitoring their progress and hope to see continuous improvement, ultimately leading to better grades by the end of the year.

In my opinion, it's worthwhile to give MindSpace a try if you are able to manage the fees.

The centre is clean and welcoming, and the students are friendly. It provides a conducive environment for students to thrive. The principal, Ms. Lydia, was warm, patient, and took the time to answer all my questions thoroughly.

I chose Mindspace as we wanted a reliable student after school care for my child. Mindspace ticks all the boxes for educational needs, mindfulness care and strategically located in close proximity to my home!
