It was my first time going to the Pasir Ris library, but I was quite surprised at how many topics they had. Art, culture, fiction, non-fiction, thriller, mystery, horror, travel, and more. There were two levels. The second level had the teen section. The first level had a quiet reading lounge. The location is a bit far. Depending on where you are, of course. But if you stay close to Pasir Ris, the location is really good because you just have to go out of exit A, and the mall in which the library is right in front of the MRT station. They probably have what you are interested in since there is such a big selection of books. The Pasir Ris library is a library I would go to with a group of friends and just sit down and read. I would recommend this library for people that maybe want to learn something or just read something. Studying or working on your computer is also an option in the quiet reading lounge. The location at the same time is kind of nice when you're actually at the library because if you want to eat a snack or a meal, you just have to go downstairs because it's a mall. Overall, the library does have a good selection and a good location once you're actually at the library.
Not a great selection of books to begin with. Very stuffy. The air-conditioning in the mall is so much more comfortable. Left within 20 minutes as I was close to perspiring.

Pasir Panjang Mrt
Rating 4.8

Today Sylvia Ang known  as Ang Lay Kheng took the Pasir Panjang Mrt
Circle line to Serangoon North MRT.

The control station was well maintained. The staff are polite & answered passengers inquiry promptly.

Kudos & thanks to the management, the station is clean & well maintained.

It takes 30 mins
Was a smooth ride
The train is not so busy. So be able to find a seat.
Very convenient to go walking up Canopy walk, Hot Park and Reflection at Chandu and surrounding nature.

Nice station which is close to a mall making it convinient for people to go to buy food or just shop around. Mrt station is close to a hawker centre making it convinces for user of the mrt to get food easily
Excellent infrastructure i like that MRT

To me this is one the most crucial and strategic station. It provide access to PSA the shipping berth or terminal for Singapore trade and the Pasir Panjang Food Centre for its legendary eateries or hawkers.

The station have brought easier access to these 2 location. Servicing the seamen as well as workers in PSA and the general public to Pasir Panjang Food Centre.

result.name: Munchi Pancakes @ Pasir Ris Hawker Centre

reviews.text:
23 Oct 2025, 4.20pm. 3 stars.

Well, I can't understand the hype or the 5 star reviews given for this brand.

Their min jiang kueh is definitely not the best I've had. I ordered the original red bean $1.60 and there was no queue, no wait. Female staff was quite efficient & responsive to questions.

The hardened edges were like eating fortune cookies, which I quite like, but the baked surfaces of the pastry was a bit too hard & chewy. Inner texture was a little bit softer. Overall can be lighter / crispier. Red bean paste was fragrant, a good amount, but could be less sweet. It got a bit jelak as I was finishing the pancake.

Noticed that the portion size was a bit smaller for the green tea skins compared to the original & charcoal skins.

CDC vouchers accepted for payment.

Will only buy if in the vicinity & have a craving.
This place is pretty airy and the environment is great. The food is hot and tastes good with plentiful of peanuts.
The filling is overflowing and they can heat up for you! Best ! Value for money

Great options of pancakes and fillings. Good service from staff. Crowd was managed efficiently during busy periods with a great smile especially by the lady staff who I believe is the boss as she was the only one not in uniform. Overall, one of my favorite Munchi branches.

i would like to praise one of the worker name yusrah. she is very outgoing and patient when i was taking order from her. she even suggested which one is nicer and her taste is definitely good. will come back here agan !

Perfect plan to build last station of green line MRT. All the sign boards are placed at proper entry and exit points. In both directions expected time to reach mentioned very clearly in the signboard. Wheel chair accessibility is very well managed with well maintained ramps lift as well path is properly designed to keep   In mind visually impaired as well. Amazing station staff service is available. Top up machine to top up MRT cards are very fast in service.

Some construction work is on going at pasir ris mrt at this moment and alot of construction material are been stored at a temporary area. Many construction workers can be seen sleeping at the temporary storage area early in the morning . This is very unsafe and post a hazard to the public and environment.

Pasir Ris MRT Station is clean, convenient, and well-connected. Located next to Pasir Ris Mall and White Sands, it offers easy access to shopping, buses, and recreational areas like Pasir Ris Park. The station is spacious with good ventilation, lifts, and clear signage. Trains run frequently, and recent upgrades have improved travel efficiency. A great starting point for both daily commutes and weekend outings.
Lanlan highly recommended masseur. Her hands are like magic which can make you fall asleep during her massage.

The terminal station was quite quiet when we arrived at 3.45pm this. The place was very clean and well laid out, with clear signage and escalator for elderly and the physically impaired. We get out of the station efficiently. It is also well connected to the White Sand Mall & bus interchange with seamless connectivity, which really helps because we were in a rush to visit a friend in Pasir Ris by bus. Love it.

First time trying A Hot Hideout at Kinex, and I can see why there’s always a long queue whenever I walk past. Finally gave it a try and it didn’t disappoint. Ordering was quite straightforward with clear step-by-step instructions, so it was easy even for first-timers.

We ordered the dry mala and tomato broth. The mala was nicely flavoured and not too overpowering, which I liked. The tomato soup was rich and flavourful, not the bland kind.

Adding toppings like scrambled egg really made a difference and enhanced both dishes.
The green Thai milk tea was also not very sweet, which was a plus and paired well with the meal. The highlight for me was the fried lotus root—super crispy and went really well with the dry mala and scrambled egg combo.

Overall, a very satisfying meal and a good experience. Will definitely come back again.

Mala xiang guo restaurant located in 1st floor of kinex mall.

Food: Delicious. Had not had a delicious tasting mala xiang guo in a along time. The ingredients don't taste wet and boiled like the other stalls you find in hawker center and food court. It's dry and mala flavoured well mixed in. Topped with creamy scrambled egg, the texture of the well cooked instant noodle and chewy potato noodle makes me not want to stop eating.

The lotus root and potato were sliced and fried. Its a good combo to dip the fried item in their secret powder.

Service: Staff was very prompt to help with the ordering process which is not complicated. Food was also served quickly.

Atmosphere: Air conditioned with lots of seating. Place was also clean and tidy.

Only feedback I have is for the lotus root and potato to be sliced to small portions. They are huge for a 1 person portion.

Food was great. Had the mala collagen soup (medium spicy) it was “ma” 麻 and “la” 辣, not jus the overpowering spiciness.
Iced tea was also very good.
The cost depends on the amount of ingredients you pick.
Service given 4⭐️ because everything was self-serve and collect. But the staff was helpful for me, a first timer.

Best and comfy atmosphere. The space is spacious. The staff is always ready to help even with self check out. My most preferred dine in place.

Food has good wok hey.... and free flow of special sauce, peanut, spring onion, coriander and fragrant powder. The service stands out with good cleanliness.

I would like to start on complimenting the staffs! They are very nice and accommodating! They give the service that you need over and beyond. And the place is so clean, organise and tidy! FOOD WAS HEAVEN TO ME! I tried the collagen soup and it’s very comforting! I don’t usually get to taste this kind of soup in other places here in SG. Definitely this is now my go to now !  The mantou was perfectly cooked . The crunch and softness even it’s not hot anymore was still there! . HOPE THEY GET TO THE SINGAPORE BEST FOOD.  Also the price is not so bad ! It’s good and it won’t break your bank!

Located beside Simei MRT station on the EW line, it is a convenient location. Not too far from Changi General Hospital too. Mall has variety of shops - clinic, fashion, Daiso, phone accessory, TCM, hobby art etc. Food wise is not an issue available in basement, 2nd and 3rd floor. NTUC is on the 5th level. POSB on the 1st level.
Parking is not a problem nearby and in the building. Outside the mall, you can many other shops and food. MacDonald is nearby

✔️ Review of Hawkers' Street @ Eastpoint Mall Wok Hei Hor Fun (Michelin Bib Gourmand)

Location:
Hawkers Street, Stall no. 8
3 Simei Street 6, Level 3, Singapore 528833

A popular Singaporean dish featuring flat rice noodles stir-fried over high heat to create a smoky, charred flavour called wok hei. Usually served with beef or fish in a savory, prawns, egg-based gravy, known for tender meat and smooth noodles.

My first visit to this stall at Hawker Street located at East Point Mall was last week, on Saturday, February 28, 2026. The taste was okay but lacked the signature wok hei flavour; it felt like regular hor fun, similar to what I usually have for takeaway or eat at hawkers.

The original, famous stall is at Redhill Hawker Centre, which has been awarded the Michelin Bib Gourmand for their smoky hor fun with prawns.

Quote
“Wok Hei Hor Fun (Redhill Original
Stall)
85 Redhill Ln, #01-94 Redhill
Food Centre, Singapore
150085
Located in: Redhill Food Centre
Unquote

The wok hei flavour is strong and lingering, providing a smoky aroma, and the sauce is flavourful and comforting.

I was recommended the Assorted Hor Fun ($6.90), which includes sliced fish (soft and fresh), tender pork, and plump prawns (not the freshest). The noodles (Kuey Teow) are uneven, lacking enough wok hei because they didn't use sufficient heat for a smoky, charred flavour. This could be improved.

I will try another dish with wok hei again on my next trip. I would like to thank the cook, Xue Lu, and his colleague Joanne for their effort in preparing the meal. There is room for improvement. There may still be some variation in the quality of dishes among different locations as they strive to attract more customers.

Nevertheless, Keep improving the quality of the dish. Customers will return for the best Wok Hey Hor Fun in the east!

Chris
2 Mar 2026
Super-close to Simei LRT station, just one stop away from Tampines Central. Around it there are hawkers and food options 😍

I like this mall. It is not huge but it has stores/shops to my liking like KOI, QI JI, Sanook, NTUC and POSB.

The money changer at B1 is my go-to whenever I have to buy foreign currencies for my travelling needs. It offers good rate. Often, esp during peak travelling periods, there would be a queue which says something about its rates.

Parking is not difficult and there's easy access for persons on wheelchair from the car park.

A very small Heartland mall. A very basic mall where you can get your groceries from NTUC, a small foodcourt on the Level 3 and then small eateries located at the basement. Has a big household store and other smaller ones on the higher levels. Caters to your basic needs mostly.

If you’re visiting for sports, casual games, or community activities, this is a solid local spot with decent amenities. The facilities are functional and serve their purpose well, though it’s not something to compare with premium or modern sports complexes. The place has a community oriented feel and a generally lively atmosphere, especially during busy hours. Overall, the experience is positive to neutral many regulars seem to enjoy coming here, while others may find it just okay if expectations are high.

Big thanks to Hope Village @ Kaki Bukit Recreation Centre for letting us use the Multi-Purpose Hall for free! The stage, chairs, lights, fans, and clean toilets made everything super convenient. The place was spacious, well-prepared, and perfect for our workers’ gathering. Your staff were really helpful and made sure everything ran smoothly. We truly enjoyed the event and appreciate your support. Looking forward to coming back for future gatherings!
Xenophobic staff at the main reception. Front desk staff demanded to know in a gangster attitude if I was local or a foreigner, would not recommend to come.

Various dishes to choose from..way better than before and food are alll affordable! There's a small convenient store located beside it and are selling cheap veggies and some fruits...the only downside than im not very please with is the floor of this foodcourt but other than that a very good place to grab a lunch...did i.mention that that cereal prawn set (with 5 prawns) is only 4 dollars??!

This place I went 4 times for open my posb bank account and enquires of my acc details.good experience.always so many peoples come there for bank acc related works.so crowd place also.longtime standing for posb bank go inside.that is my experience there.thank u guys.

PLQ Mall – The Standout Mall of the Paya Lebar Area
10 Paya Lebar Road, Singapore 409057
Having spent considerable time in the Paya Lebar area across multiple Singapore visits, PLQ Mall has consistently proven itself to be the best shopping and dining destination the neighbourhood has to offer — and a personal firm favourite.
The food options are a particular highlight, with an impressive selection of dining choices spread across the upper levels, building anticipation as you ride the escalators upward. The top floor food court is especially noteworthy — well stocked with a wide variety of cuisines, clean, and comfortably laid out, making it an easy choice for any meal of the day.
Beyond dining, the mall's connectivity is a genuine strength — conveniently linked to nearby hotels and within easy reach of the MRT station, making it an effortlessly practical base for visitors and locals alike.
For anyone staying in or passing through the Paya Lebar area, PLQ Mall is an easy and enthusiastic first recommendation.
A mall that provides wide variety of food choices. Big  outdoor space between buildings can be use for event, temporary pop up for different seasons. A very lively mall.

PLQ Mall is a vibrant and convenient shopping destination designed by DP Architects. The mall offers a great mix of shops, restaurants, and cafes, with modern, well-thought-out spaces. Its location by Paya Lebar MRT makes it super accessible. Highly recommended for families and urban explorers!

I’m a little confused, are they connected or I do not where I was? Followed my Queen as she brought me here to explore a corner of Singapore I have never seen before. We had Onkei Tonkatsu and Ramen Keisuke here

Four floors n two basements of shopping pleasure! Great restaurants, children-related stores, kopitiam and NTUC supermarket plus many more varieties.  It's a wonderful,  friendly environment. Third floor has a number of great restaurants like Tung Lok and Haidilao.

Came here for my father's birthday. The food here was decent, but the price was definitely at the higher end. I had the ribeye steak (220g). The steak was tender, but was definitely too salty for my liking. Might be because of the chimichuri, so im not sure. Fries were nicely seasoned and thickly cut. The duck was nice, went well with the risotto. I also tried the ribs, which were nicely seasoned. The cauliflower were really good. Calamari rings were nice, but wasnt really very crispy. Cheesecake was really good, with a hint of saltiness and sweetness.

Whisk & Paddle is a fantastic spot with a great atmosphere and both indoor and outdoor seating. The view of the river and surrounding nature made for a truly relaxing dinner. The food was good, but the seabass was definitely the highlight and the best dish we ordered. It’s a bit on the pricier side, but the experience was worth the trip!

Nice restaurant with option for indoor vs outdoor seating. This us my 5th  time here and was not disappointed with the breakfast!!  Food was delicious and service was prompt & staff friendly.
Usually you have to wait for a table but we were lucky it was not crowded probably due to the June holidays.
A great place to hang out with friends over food & drinks.
Not very easily accessible if you don’t drive.

Wonderfully executed duck confit on risotto, brussels sprouts could be more caramelised or cooked. Mac and cheese tasty but a bit too wet, could be drier and cheesier. Wagyu beef burger was alright, fries not as fresh as they looked. Iced matcha latte was watered done after ice melted. Iced coffee was a small cup. Vanilla milkshake was delectable and sizeable, the best drink at the table. Prompt and adequate service. Complimentary water served. Place is huge, with lots of natural light. There's a small sheltered play area outdoors for kids. A little overpriced but a nice place to chill for an afternoon with its wide selection of drinks, mains, and desserts.

Visited Whisk & Paddle for the first time and was pleasantly surprised. The food was reasonably good—flavorful and satisfying without being over the top. Staff were warm and welcoming, which made the whole experience feel relaxed and easy.

It’s a great spot for families too. There’s a dedicated area for kids to play, which keeps them entertained while the adults enjoy their meal. The outdoor seating is a nice touch, especially if you enjoy dining with a view or a bit of fresh air.

Overall, a comfortable and friendly place worth checking out—especially if you're looking for a casual meal with good vibes.
Just next to a bus station, LRT and Waterway Point. Very useful clean and efficient way of travelling for all Punggo residents.
Super station. In and out very fast. Connected to the LRT and also the bus station in Punggol. Plus next to Waterway Point. Superb.

On Friday the 13th in September 2019, I took C751A 7007/7008 to the station to use exit C to the bus interchange. I noticed the poster of Express 43e which runs between Punggol and Marine Parade Road operated by Go-Ahead Singapore from Loyang Bus Depot (LYDEP) using Mercedes-Benz O530 Citaros and MAN NL323F A22s. The bus that I took on Express 43e was SBS6454K, a Mercedes-Benz O530 Citaro Batch 2.
Super rude chinese uncle working 1st March afternoon shift. I am asking you question because I dont know right? Responded like I owed him money
Relatively new neighborhood, with plenty of greenery and waterways to walk/run/bike

Safra punngol has a huge ramge of facilities for families but their most famous one is the 3 story swimming pool. It is big and has slides both big and small to accomodate young children and adults to have fun using the play equipment. There is a also a bike rental and emart at is basement. Good for the residents of punngol to spend some time here for recreation

We had an amazing family day out at SAFRA Punggol, especially at the KidzAmaze waterpark! This place is a paradise for children with its creatively designed water play areas and slides that are both safe and exciting. The facilities are well-maintained, and the staff are incredibly friendly and attentive, ensuring that all kids have a fun and safe experience. It's the perfect spot to cool down and allow the kids to burn off some energy in a fun-filled environment. Highly recommend for families looking for a great way to spend the day!

One stop for some food and entertainment. Indoor water playground to bowling and coffee shop to McDonald’s it’s a good way to spend some quality time with kids. Sometimes carnival for kids in ground floor as well

We had a great time with my little one ( less than 1 year old ) . This place is great for 1-2 hrs of splashing entertainment. Very clean and hygienic pool and slides . The bathrooms and storage space was really well kept ( hot water - soap - quick availability) really made our time very smooth. We had a minor issue with being unable to book our tickets online ( for some reason the payment link was not visible and the rate cards online are not very accurate ) they have separate costs for members and non members ( 22$ for non members) makes sense to buy the membership card to save up. The kids love it. On Friday we had manageable crowd around. Quick bite at McDonald’s which is next door is a good option for closeby eats. Highly recommend ( carry your own water / toiletries )  for an easy experience.
A considerable amount of restaurants choices, but more fit to go to when you're looking for a place to enjoy sports and similar activities.

result.name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Waterway Point)

reviews.text: Mammia Mia Trattoria E Caffe is truly a hidden gem for my family! Last time we did not even bother looking at this restaurant but because we decided to try this today, we will definitely come back to eat again. The food came at an acceptable amount of time and it was worth the wait! The pasta was moist and flavourful and the eggplant parmigiana was extremely cheesy, not to mention the tiramisu was absolutely phenomenal. The atmosphere was energetic with loud booming music and the lighting was truly a vibe. Staff was kind and friendly and attentive to our orders and was quick to act. Overall a good place to have dinner, 5/5 stars!

Wow! Pleasantly surprised by the taste and quality of the food here. I ordered the Minestrone Soup,0Milanese Chicken with Creamy Spiced Pasta, Chilli Crab Risotto and Cheese, Pep Mafalda, Eggplant Parmigiana and Tiramisu. Waiting to try the Tiramisu as I write this.  All the food is very tasty and well cooked and presented. Happy to try this place out!

Staff here were really kind to my kids which made the dining experience so much better. The food portion was generous, service was efficient. They gave my kids biscuits after they were done with their food as a little distraction while the adults finish their foods.

We were kindly served by Sulaiman, who was very friendly and prompt in his services. Truly appreciated the welcoming and cosy atmosphere created by the team at the Waterway Point branch as all them wished us goodbyes as we left. More importantly, the food blew all of us away and we ended up ordering more and more even though we were stuffed because the dishes are all just so well-made. We had the crunchy, well-seasoned calamari, the pan-seared chicken with THE BEST MASH POTATOES, sea bass, squid ink pasta, beef bolognese pasta and the fries. We ended the meal with a plate of tiramisu. Each order was a complete hit with our family. It was such a lovely experience dining out here with the whole family.

Enjoyed our meal at Mamma Mia Trattoria e Caffè at Waterway Point. The truffle mushroom risotto was rich and comforting, and the carbonara was creamy with a generous topping of crispy bacon. Loved the bruschetta trio — especially the tuna and egg salad toppings. The fried chicken wings were nicely seasoned and crispy too. Great ambience, friendly service, and well-presented dishes. A lovely spot for casual Italian dining.

This mall has everything you need for a relaxing weekend. There is a kids’ play area, as well as a jogging track with a beautiful lake view.

It offers plenty of dining options, including Din Tai Fung, Song Fa Bak Kut Teh, and many others. For tea lovers, there are popular choices such as CHICHA San Chen and CHAGEE. If you’re looking for dessert, Henri Charpentier and Mister Donut are great options. Coffee lovers can also grab a drink at Starbucks.

The mall also has a supermarket, making it a convenient one-stop destination for shopping, dining, and leisure. It’s a perfect place to spend the weekend, especially if you prefer not to travel to the city centre.

Waterway Point is a large and spacious mall with a good mix of shops, products, services and plenty of food options. It covers most day-to-day needs and works well as a one-stop destination for residents in the area. That said, it gets very crowded on weekends, so be prepared for heavy foot traffic. On the plus side, there’s ample parking available, which helps. Overall, a solid and well-planned mall — just better enjoyed during off-peak hours.

Waterway point shopping mall shopping I would say clothing shops like Uniqlo n playdress.there is a popular bookstore n a toys r us.there is a miniso ...Japan home n value shop I would not really put it in a shopping category... More of home maybe ... waterway point for shopping not exactly a place .... For food I would not say that I'm so satisfied with waterway point's food options .... There r foods like fast foods like McDonalds, Jollibee,mos burger, n there r foods like kaya toast n munching snacks like old chang kee n fried banana.there is a foodcourt within the mall but NOT HAPPY WITH IT.there r foods like fish soup n yong Tau foo but there is no Indian food or Muslim food.there is a western stall in the foodcourt but I don't find much difference in eating fast foods n eating a western meal.There r also a Thai food stall which sells fried noodles n fried rice ... This foodcourt mainly Chinese foods ... For a Indian girl like me food options r very limited not only in this food court but I m talking about the whole waterway point mall ... I mean seriously the mall is so big n spacious but there is no any Indian food in it or at least a Muslim food on it ... Very disappointed ☹️ whenever I go waterway point the only options I get to eat is either fast food or Milo toast ...there r restaurants like buddy hoggies n han's ... But seriously if I want to enjoy a nice meal I have to spend so much money??? Can't I get to enjoy a nice meal for a cheaper price at a normal foodcourt???  There r take away shops like stuff'd .... Kebab, burrito, burrito bowl used to cost much cheaper than the price is costing now ... Just increased the price for kebab, burrito n burrito bowls ... N no dine in options from last time till now ... I felt not worth buying from this shop ... But I always c a long queue in this shop ... ☹️ There is a shop which similar like stuff'd...
Guzman y gormez which also sells kebab n burritos ... it has dine in options... BUT VERY EXPENSIVE... have tried there before ... Did not went back to that shop again 🤫 ... Oh n I just remembered... Waterway point has waffles.... For any1 who is a sweet tooth ... 😁 Many flavored drinks like luckin coffee ... N boost ... There r many flavored drinks shops in waterway point but not healthy... Too much sugar... I don't really encourage people to drink all this flavored drinks...
Whatever age anybody is or whether people with diabetes or not too much sugar intake n too much fast foods intake NOT HEALTHY...
Another thing which I want to tell everyone is that when eating at McDonalds please do not give your hp to any1 in the McDonald's... I have this prob with McDonalds whenever I m eating McDonalds... Since I eat at waterway McDonalds today n this prob happened again I wish to tell everyone... The person who is wearing the yellow shirt in the McDonald's I guess she is the manager's assistant she asked for my phone to do McDonalds feedback...I did not want to be rude so gave hery HP ... Customers r supposed to do the feedback not any1 else ... The McDonald's was so dirty ... I had to wipe the table before I eat ... Fly was flying around... I want to feedback but cannot bec she do feedback... The feedback she did from my phone was submitted under my name but I did do the feedback... Just bec they want to increase their outlet rating they do feedback from customers phone ... Everything they put 5 star ... Taking a customer's phone n doing feedback is very wrong ... As a customer I can make a complaint as this has happened many times ... But I just decided to just leave it ... Next time I go McDonalds when again ask for my phone I m strictly going to say as a customer I will do feedback ... I guess I have to be rude at times ... M being too nice ... Peo taking advantage... I don't want the same thing happening to any1 else so m telling every1...

Very convenient mall with many food and shopping options all in one place. Easy access because it’s connected directly to Punggol MRT. For anyone looking for the musollah, it’s located at B2 near NTUC and hidden beside the lift door.

The last time I visited this mall was a few years back. I went for a fund raising walk when centre first opened . So much has changed since then and the place has grown with so many new interesting food outlets. New residents that move into this estate are blessed with such a modern hangout place with everything under one roof. Great food, sports facility, shopping variety covering personal care , home improvement, pet grooming and cinema. Two wings East n West are loaded with 3 floors of shops to pamper oneself and with 2 podium hosting events . One is so pampered, no more need to go downtown as everything is at your doorstep. A great place for families to spend time.
Punggol Bus Interchange is great serving many bus services and the nearby MRT & LRT station. 1 thing was buses kept parking at a place that said “DO NOT PARK”. Still ok because it doesn’t affect me.

I went to Punggol by MRT and it is very spacious bus interchange with modern facilities.If you need to go to the City area, then of course, you need to take MRT.

It is very clean bus and MRT interchange including the facilities like Toilet.
Very useful and efficient bus station. Can be slightly unsafe at the opening, with some bad accidents involving deaths to pedestrians. Be very careful crossing the roads here.
Bus don’t stop even after bell was press

Spacious bus interchange that you can reach easily from the MRT. Connects you to the rest of Punggol area. Take bus 84 to go to Coney Island. Make sure you take Exit B from the MRT to reach this place.

It is a great place to spend time with family and friends. It is new, spacious, and has many facilities, including McDonald's, Punggol library, and a food court at Level 2. The library offers various activities and has a wide selection of books. There are also running and cycling paths, fitness areas, and community events for people of all ages.

One Punggol is a new building that is huge, well located, has a nice food court, a spacious multipurpose hall, a wide ground floor for viewing shows on a big screen, and has an excellent car park. Just beside Punggol Waterway and a short walk from other places. Love there’s a Blood Bank here too. Thank you 🙏🏻
Good airy, spacious hawker centre with many variety of food.

Came here on 22-10-25 evening for dinner.  Most store were closed, went round and decided to try this HKM which recommended by Google map. I ordered the $6 which came with 3 prawns, 2 small ring of sotong and few very small dice of pork belly. This is somewhat the dry type. Nothing special not worth the price but cab eat lah.

An office here where one can get help for government services. It's especially good for old folks to get help. My favourite part of this place is the Punggol Regional Library. It has a vast no number of books and seating areas for browsing books. Lots of computers to access the internet as well. Many students go to the study area to study. A very condusive area for students. However, the food sold at the food court needs improvement. Lots of car park in the basement.

This is a seaside dining and lifestyle destination located at the northern tip of Punggol. The two-story building features a variety of restaurants with waterfront views:
Seafood Specialties: Famous for classic Singaporean seafood at House of Seafood and Ponggol Seafood.
Cuisines: Options include Thai food at Siam Square Mookata, Japanese at Izakaya 95, and local favorites like the White Restaurant (known for White Bee Hoon).
Bars & Cafes: Georges by the Bay provides a relaxed pub atmosphere.
This Korean BBQ has plenty of performances to keep you entertained while waiting for the meat to be bbq by the staff. Service is quick and good.

Just had siblings reunion here and the atmosphere was good. Large crowds maybe it was on Saturday.

Food was tasty only it was not served together as in it was served 1 by 1 so we never get to eat it with the rice altogether.
In the small island of Singapore, there are not many "village style" place for recreation, F&B and back to nature. This place goes to my "hang out with friends list"

This seafood restaurant is away from the city in Ponggol. But if you drive don’t give it amiss. The food is sumptuous and in good proportion. It was a treat for us but I suppose it is reasonably priced. The service is good and the the ambience is good if it was not for the small irritating flies. Then, it went off. If you a seafood lover, please visit this restaurant. 😊

Hainanese curry rice: Fragrant curry, good portion (especially with the rice)
Mixed veg rice: Often a long queue in the evening. They have good fried rice, about $5+ for 1 veg+1 meat+fried rice. Ingredients are nice
Herbal soup: Average portion, nice lotus root soup (though, it tastes about the same in most herbal soup stalls), goes well with pumpkin rice.
Korean: Both Korean stalls are fine, Jin Kimchi in particular is a chain stall but the quality is good and the packaging is well sealed.
Butter and Cream: really good cakes and tarts, but I recommend eating fresh. Especially the egg tarts, they taste much better fresh than kept overnight in the fridge.
Carrot cake: white carrot cake is pretty good, but can be quite salty / oily. They forgot to add / remove chili based on request a few times...but overall the food is good and I will buy again
Drink stall: Teh peng is ok. Avocado drink is a scam, the gula melaka is very sweet and tastes good but I am fairly certain "avocado juice" should be blended...not have the whole avocado....

There's nothing in particular that stands out a lot, but it's relatively affordable in general and food is not bad! Just won't recommend the avocado "juice" 😹

Big, convenient hawker centre near Sengkang MRT & Compass One offering a wide variety of local & international food at reasonable prices. Seating is spacious & breezy after renovations.

10% off when paying via the FairPrice app 👍

This 1 star is given to a Vietnamese Bánh mì stall.
It cost me SG$8 and the piece of chicken inside can't even been seen outside the bread. It's smaller than 旺旺米饼. When the lady pass the packet of Bánh mì to me, its feels like I am holding a empty bag. Don't waste your money on this stall. For SG$8, I can have a big piece of fried chicken for Economic Rice with another meat and vegi, a big burger, a 2 pieces fried chicken from western food stall, a whole chicken from Cold Storage with $0.10 less.
Photo shows that I have eaten half of the bread and the chicken is haven't appear yet.

Dear Owner of Kopitiam(Fairprice Group), may a humble common peasant like me ask, have your company staffs trained to handle the drinks stall with the standard operation procedure through out all the kopitiam outlet. If so, how do you justify the amount of drinks that is served to customer. $2.70 for this cup of milo, a cup full of ice (to the brim) and poured in with cold (ready made) milo, compared to outlets that serve the same thing, a cup full of ice and poured in with hot(freshly made) milo. And also given the recent inflation, does Fairprice Group find this price ($2.70) reasonable? Thank you.
The stall that sells curry rice v generous. Can choose veggie ☺️. Prev tried the chicken chop. Today tried the fish fillet
An unforgettable family sports day where the children learned to be brave, work together, and participate actively. There were a wide variety of activities, and the kids had a great time.

MindChamps Preschool has exceeded our expectations. The school places strong emphasis on confidence-building and critical thinking, which has really helped my child become more expressive and independent. The teachers are attentive and provide regular updates, so parents feel involved in the learning journey. The environment is safe, positive, and structured, yet fun. I appreciate how the school focuses not just on academics, but also on character development and emotional growth

Recently enrolled to MindChamps Punggol. It's a great school with good facilities, nice and engaging teachers, condusive environment for learning.

They also frequently conduct workshops to let parents know what they are learning at school and how we are able to support them at home.

Update - December 2025:

Attended a Year-End Musical and Graduation concert for the first time. I was genuinely surprised by how GRAND it was! I was quite astonished to see the little Champs able to memorize such long lines, complex dance sequences, and choreography. It was easy to forget just how young they are!

The theme, "A Small Island - Little Hands, Big Dreams," was especially close to our hearts. It was truly heartwarming to watch my daughter, Evelyn, learn about Singapore and sing 'Home' so beautifully. She genuinely loves to perform, sing, and dance. I could clearly see her growth and increased confidence throughout this entire process.

A huge thank you to the entire team for teaching, organizing this incredible performance, and, of course, for taking such wonderful care of Evelyn and nurturing her growth ❤️

Great preschool, My child feels comfortable and supported, and always comes home with excitement to share what they’ve learned. The school also places strong emphasis on values, discipline, and teamwork, which helps shape the children not just as students but as individuals.

A strong foundation in language begins early in a child’s life, and as an educator, I firmly believe that parent workshops are a powerful way to nurture meaningful home-school learning connections. This is my third MRW workshop at MindChamps, and I am truly heartened by the genuine interest our parents show in their Champs' learning journey. Their enthusiasm and eagerness to be involved are not only encouraging but also play a vital role in building confident, capable and joyful learners.
This  big and modern library located just 100 meter from the Punggol MRT station. 5 story building which cater all ages of readers. A nice place to study...

Punggol Regional Library: A Visual Masterpiece in the North-East

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

As a photographer, I usually look for subjects behind the lens rather than books on a shelf, but the **Punggol Regional Library** at One Punggol is a rare exception where the architecture is as much a draw as the collection.

The Architecture & Light
From a design perspective, this isn't just a building; it’s a study in geometric lines and natural lighting. The massive glass facade allows for incredible "Golden Hour" shots if you’re positioned on the upper levels. The way the light spills across the reading tiers creates a beautiful interplay of highlights and shadows—perfect for those who enjoy **minimalist or architectural photography**.

Key Spots for Enthusiasts:
The Lens of Literature: This is the library’s "hero shot." The circular, tiered seating area provides a fantastic perspective for wide-angle frames, capturing the sheer scale of the 5-storey atrium.

The World and Us (Level 5): The curation here is top-notch, but the real prize is the outdoor terrace. You get a sweeping view of the Punggol skyline and the lush greenery surrounding the area—ideal for some urban landscape captures.
ExperienceIT & MakeIT (Level 4): For those who love macro or tech-focused shots, the 3D printers and robotics labs offer some great industrial-chic textures.

Inclusivity & Tech
Beyond the visuals, the library is a marvel of inclusive design. The **Calm Pods** and the **Borrow-n-Go** accessible walkways are thoughtfully integrated into the flow of the building. Even the way the shelves are spaced feels intentional, providing clear leading lines that make the space feel infinite.

Pro-Tips for Visitors:
Gear: Bring a wide-angle lens (16mm to 35mm equivalent) to truly capture the interior volume.
Timing: Try a weekday morning for "clean" shots without the crowds.
Post-Visit: You’re right next to the One Punggol Hawker Centre for a quick meal, and a short walk from the Waterway, which offers plenty of additional landscape opportunities along the river.

Whether you're there to research your next project or to capture the soul of modern Singaporean architecture, this place is a must-visit. 📸📚

Overall, the library is pretty clean with many interesting facilities. What really made this a 4 star review rather than a 5 star review, is that the children's section is not to right place to read. On level 2 (children's section), i could hear kids screaming and stomping on the floor every second, so i advise to never study on level 2.

Punggol Library is an amazing space for readers and learners alike! With its fully automated system, it’s incredibly convenient to borrow and return books without the need for staff assistance. The library offers a wide range of books and digital resources, catering to various interests and age groups.

The environment is quiet, clean, and modern, making it perfect for studying, reading, or simply relaxing with a good book. The dedicated children’s section and interactive areas add a fun touch for families.

Its seamless self-service technology and thoughtfully designed spaces truly make it a library of the future. Highly recommended for anyone in Singapore looking for a productive or peaceful spot!

This library is big, it is clean & comfortable it comes with 4 levels. The is a few pod for students or worker to have discussion. There is many spaces for families. Over here, sometime they will organise some workshop for ppl to join.

I had my vaccination here in the week of 15 June, it was a fast and smooth experience. Within 5mins of my arrival, I had my jab and just had to wait 30mins to observe for side effects. Staff were all friendly and clear with instructions, and I even left with a box of masks and bottle of sanitizer!
Go get your vaccines here if you live nearby! 💉⛑️
Very Comprehensive offering with PMEs and Skills Craft centers at its basement. A great level 3 to house study areas and children's corner.
One of the CC with the best service! The staff here is professional and polite. Facilities are clean and well maintained. Friendly grassroots leaders organising interesting and intriguing events!
The new concept community center that has everything...came here for the 3rd booster jab. Just next to punggol plaza. Pretty efficient for the 3rd jab..thank you all
Very nice arrangement for COVID vaccination.. Next to it is McDonalds and plaza is just 2 mins walking distance

Well built! The station is linked to the mall, too! I’ve took the newest train to Harbourfront, and it was AMAZING! The trains were silent! There were even screens displaying the next train and other safety rules as well. The onboard TV up the train will also play some interesting videos. Good!

Just want to share so next time you won't be confused. Exit A is going to the Waterway Point Mall, Exit B is the opposite of Bus Interchange, the road is along the Mall as well, and Exit C is going to bus interchange. I would like to rate their toilet also, even though there are a lot of people, they maintained it clean. Good Job.
LRT is spacious but crowded during most of the times during the day. West loop trains are bit small in size ( 1 compartment ) compared to most east loop trains (2 compartments)

MRT Singapore is efficient vastly superior to what I experience back home very clean and the PA very clear and precise 4 language yes 4 once u get down just in front is a lift u don't have to walk long to get one . One thing I find puzzling is where is the dustbin and of course no public clock . If u have trash figure it out . Punggol MRT Station is clean, spacious, and really well-organised. The interchange setup with the LRT makes transfers easy, and the signs are super clear so you won’t get lost even during busy hours. Trains come frequently, and the station staff are usually helpful when you need directions. The surrounding area is convenient too — Waterway Point is directly connected, so grabbing food or doing errands after your commute is very easy. Overall, a reliable and comfortable station to travel from.

• Free entry & hours: Compass One is a public shopping mall, so entry is free. The mall’s main contact page lists opening hours as 10:00am–10:00pm daily, while individual stores may open earlier or close later depending on the tenant.

• What & why go: Compass One is a major Sengkang town-centre mall with 180+ brands and a wide mix of shopping, dining and everyday services. It is described by the mall as one of the area’s most extensive and fully integrated malls.

• Top features to see: You’ll find a mix of fashion, food, beauty, electronics and family-friendly outlets, plus anchors like Cold Storage, McDonald’s, Starbucks, POPULAR and the Sengkang Public Library inside the mall.

• Getting there: The mall is at 1 Sengkang Square, Singapore 545078. The official “Getting Here” page says to take the North East Line and alight at Sengkang Station (NE16); buses also serve the mall.

• Parking & practical access: Compass One has on-site parking with published hours for cars and motorcycles. The parking page lists operating hours as 7:00am–11:00pm daily.

• Best for quick visits & family stops: With groceries, food, library access and many daily-need stores, it works well for a short stop, errands, or a family meal after Sengkang Station.

---

⚠️ What to keep in mind

• Store hours vary: Not every shop follows the mall’s main opening hours; some open much earlier for breakfast or close sooner on holidays. Check the tenant page if you are going for a specific store.
• Holiday changes: The mall publishes festive operating hours and some stores may close or shift timings during major holidays.
• Parking charges apply: Parking is paid and has no grace period, so short visits can still incur a charge.
• Facilities are useful but simple: The mall offers customer service on Level 3 and free Wi-Fi with membership perks, but it is still mainly a neighbourhood mall rather than a luxury destination.

Compass 1 shopping mall linked directly to mrt station n Lrt station... Lots to shop like miniso, clothing shops,Japan home,value shop ... Lots to eat ... Kopitiam with wide range of food options from halal to non halal ... Food for everyone to eat ... Fast food options such as McDonalds,kfc, burger king,mos burger,popoyes ... All at compass 1 ...
Bun shops,cakes,old change kee,Mr bean also at compass 1 ... There is a ananas food shop at just outside the entrance to compass 1 which has dine in options... Also a Muslim shop right beside it which is a takeaway place ... Boost,fruit juice shop,pizza,stuff'd and lots more food at compass 1 ... Weekdays r usually less crowded compared to weekends...

**Compass One** is a well-planned neighbourhood mall directly linked to Sengkang MRT and bus interchange. Designed by **DCA Architects**, the mall features a clean, contemporary layout with good natural light and clear circulation. Practical mix of shops, dining, and community spaces makes it convenient and comfortable for daily use.
Good place good vibes, not much halal options for food.

First time today at this Compass One mall in Sengkang area and quite enjoyed it! Love that it is not as crowded as some of the other heartland malls so we were able to get a carpark without much waiting. Restaurants are not overcrowded too so it is easy to walk with children. Bencoolen MRT Station is near to V Hotel, Mi Hotel, Bencoolen mosque, NAFA academy. This station is very clean. Just like all MRT Station in Singapore. Singapore's comfortable  mass transportation system will take us to places at  Singapore comfortably and in relatively cheap fare. Having STP and/or Ezlink will be very helpful for keeping our tour more economical.

At a jaw-dropping 43 meters down (AA good as 13 storeys!), the journey traveling down to the train platform is a long journey into the city's subterranean. Like its counterparts, Bencoolen stands as a testament to Singapore's commitment to excellence: clean, modern, and impeccably maintained.

Despite feedback done couple of months ago , issue still remains the same . Passengers rushing to the lift without giving priority to wheelchair passengers while staffs are seen chatting with each other / resting in the station control .

Bencoolen MRT Station (DT21) on the Downtown Line is a clean, modern, and well-maintained station. Its strategic location near cultural and educational spots like the National Museum and SMU makes it a convenient stop for students, tourists, and office workers. The station is known for its deep underground structure, one of the deepest in Singapore, but escalators and lifts ensure accessibility. With clear signage and a generally smooth passenger flow, commuting here is hassle-free. However, exits are limited compared to larger interchange stations.

#BencoolenMRT #SingaporeTransport #DowntownLine #MRTSG #CommutingLife #SGPublicTransport #ExploreSingapore

ok mrt station. very deep.
usually air con is quite powerful

A compact but lively local football ground with a real community feel. The Lion City Sailors ultras bring great energy and atmosphere, making the match experience feel personal and passionate. The stadium itself is on the smaller side, and the selection of food, jerseys, and activities is pretty limited, but it still delivers a solid, authentic local football vibe that’s easy to enjoy.
Played here before in the 80s during my club's local cup match. Everything change, now. Good field, spectacular event matches held here. Historical landmark Jalan Besar Stadium. 👍👍👍

A must visit for soccer die-hard fans. The place has changed a fair bit over the years -- but it still retains its old world appeal. Love it! Easy enough to find a car park with the next door multi-storey carpark facility. Loads of good food all around the stadium too!

Watched the historic match between Lion City Sailors and Hiroshima Sanfrecce here. The seats were close to the action which made for a great viewing experience.

However, it seems like the stadium/staff weren’t prepared for the large crowd (4000+ people). The toilets ran out of water so we couldn’t wash our hands, and the water coolers also ran out of water during halftime. Plus they did not allow people to bring water bottles in, forcing us to leave the water bottles on a table outside, but the table was not large enough to accommodate all bottles, by the end of the event the bottles were all placed on the floor instead. This led to a very long queue for drinks, which we managed to get prematch, but abandoned our halftime queue for second cups due to the super long wait. I get it that LCS isn’t used to attracting such large crowds, but they need to up their game for the upcoming semifinals, which is bound to attract an even larger crowd. The toilets need to have water, and there needs to be more drink vendors (otherwise, simply let people bring their own water if you can’t sell them water). Looking forward a better semifinals experience!

Easy to navigate your way here.
Nice atmosphere here for games.

Late lunch after a “Reunion Senior Friendly Tour” at National Museum of Singapore (NMS) on Sat 10 Jan 2026 morning. Remembered there was a “Y Cafe” at the 4th floor of the YMCA building next to NMS.

A quiet place with great window view of the sky, The Cathay and the School of the Arts (SOTA). A good place to enjoy an afternoon tea/coffee/drinks and catch up with friends.

Prices are reasonable. Taste of my fish & chips was mediocre. I don’t visit Orchard area often so I am not sure if we can get better tasting food at similar prices nearby. The view is great though.

Friendly and patient service staff who had his own late lunch after serving me.

A family of 4, two groups of friends in 2s or 3s and a solo visitor when I was at the cafe.

Some 12 table set up that can accommodate groups of 2-4 pax and a counter area with high seats that can sit 3-4 pax.

The YMCA of Singapore is so much more than just a building; it’s a heartbeat for social good in the city. I’m consistently impressed by their dedication to youth development and their programs for the special needs community (like the Y-Stars). It’s a professional, inclusive, and transparent nonprofit that truly puts people first. Whether you’re looking to volunteer or support a great cause, this is the place!

Strategic location. Very clean rooms. Don’t have much to say except it’s a decent value for what you’re paying. Nothing fancy, but the internet is fast, the water is hot, sheets are clean, aircon is cold. There is a sizeable fitness center and the staff are helpful.  Would recommend.

We stayed at the YMCA orchard road
They are the most amazing staff you will come across. The room was clean and spacious and the be was comfortable.
Public transport is on your doorstep for busses and  two train stations a couple of minutes walk away.
It's an a amazing central location to everything and don't miss satay street or you will regret it. Amazing food.
To get there walk out of arrivals hip on bus 36 and it will drop you at the door. When returning to the airport hop on the same bus for $3-6 and you go back to the airport hassle free.
My wife and I can't recommend this place enough it's amazing.
After a day of sightseeing come back to a beautiful air-conditioned room relax and you are ready to go again.
A big thank you to all the staff you are amazing people
Bob and Judy

I had a nice and pleasant stay at the YMCA. The staff was very friendly, offering me an early check-in and providing a room with a window as requested. The cleanliness, room quality, and location were excellent! For a 3-star hotel, the YMCA is truly a great deal.

As an artist working in the Singapore Sax Symposium 2026 (SSS 2026) at NAFA this June - it was such a smooth, well-run event by all the employees/student workers there!! The event (started originally by lecturer Dr Zechariah Goh) has also brought much value and recognition to NAFA within the local/regional saxophone scene for its contributions to the community over the years. I hope NAFA continues to acknowledge and hopefully increase its support of this event and the artists/workers involved (e.g. funding, manpower support) in the coming years.

Special shout out to Chew Kai-Xin (NAFA employee) who organised this event and led her team to success. In all my years of attending this event, this has been the smoothest and best experience that I’ve had. Speaking from someone who has first hand experience handling administrative/organisational roles, Kai-Xin and her team were professional, helpful, and totally competent in creating the best experience for artists and attendees alike. Will 10/10 recommend her to continue running this symposium and/or other projects at NAFA. She and her team deserve a raise!!!! 🎷😎👍

I just attended a short course in "Making a Complete Picture Book with Procreate" with NAFA this year (2026).

It has been a great privilege to learn from a very experienced and professional Procreate Storybook Illustrator, Mr. Josef Lee. In this short span of 7 weeks (one lesson per week for 3 hours), he generously shared with us his storybook creation processes - his idea thumbnail sketches, design developments and thought process. It was truly an amazing experience as we are learning from a very committed storybook designer whose storybooks creations are actually published and can be found in many bookstores, national libraries and even a hotel in Singapore!

Mr. Josef Lee helped us to understand the rigour, the detailed planning, the workflow and the putting together of all the research and ideas to come up with a really enticing read for children and even adults. He was also very helpful in encouraging us to ask questions and answering them all. He respects our privacy and shy disposition as well and gave us one-to-one constructive feedback in our weekly beginner story-creating attempts.

In an overview, we had learnt how to come up with:
-  a good storyline and story structure
-  storyboarding (in Procreate)
-  characters design methods (in Procreate)
-  picture composition and layout (in Procreate)
-  colour palettes (in Procreate)
-  digital drawing, colouring and animating (in Procreate)
-  exporting our digital artworks into an eBook, and more...

He has also shared with us successful examples by other internationally renowned storybook creators. The takeaways that he shared from his journeys of constant storybook creations and readings to children are very meaningful...  such as - The lesser you draw, the harder your text will have to work to provide the information for your story.

The class size is intentionally kept small (max of 10) to ensure the high quality of students' learning. Thank you, for this decision is greatly appreciated and valued! I have also made many nice friends and laughed in class. Highly recommending this course 😊

Reignited a long-forgotten passion through an 8-week oil painting course with NAFA CLE. It was a genuinely rewarding experience—guided by a wonderful trainer, Nhawfal Juma’at and shared with an enthusiastic group of classmates from all walks of life, all taking time to reconnect with their creative side. Thank you for creating such a warm and inspiring learning environment.

PS: Love the Spotify song list too! ")

Completed Beginner, Intermediate and Advanced Watercolour courses and it has been a truly rewarding and fulfilling learning journey. I only wish each level had more sessions because the time always felt too short!

One of the highlights was meeting people who share the same interest in watercolour. My classmates were incredibly supportive — we cheered each other on, shared experiences and often continued conversations over lunch or coffee after lessons. It made the learning experience even more enjoyable and motivating.

Our instructor Edwin Ho, is patient and supportive of our learning. He encourages us, while giving us challenges that push us out of our comfort zones, which really helped us grow and improve.

With the National Silver Academy (NSA) subsidy and the use of SkillsFuture credits, the courses are even more affordable for those aged 50 and above, which is a wonderful initiative.

Overall, a wonderful experience. We definitely need more watercolour sessions!

I attended the Nagomi pastel art classes, and our facilitator, Zu, was very encouraging and friendly. The classes were enjoyable, and her instructions were clear and easy to follow.
I’m giving one star less because the administrative support from NAFA could be improved. We were only informed of the materials list less than two weeks before the class start date, and as it was during the CNY period, many of us had to rush to get the items. The response time from the administration office was also rather slow and they could do better with support for their staff lah.

Farrer Park MRT Station (NE8) is a key stop along the North East Line, conveniently serving the vibrant Little India area. It is strategically located near residential buildings, hotels, shopping malls, and cultural landmarks, making it a frequently used station by both locals and tourists.

The station is clean, well-maintained, and designed with accessibility in mind, featuring lifts, escalators, and clear signages to assist commuters of all ages. Its direct connection to City Square Mall is a huge plus, providing easy access to shopping, dining, and other amenities without stepping outside, which is especially convenient during rainy weather.

However, despite its convenience and upkeep, the station’s platforms and walkways feel relatively narrow, particularly during peak hours. With the growing population and increasing foot traffic in the area, the station often feels cramped, causing congestion and slowing down the movement of commuters. This can be uncomfortable, especially for those in a hurry.

Expanding the station’s width would greatly enhance the overall commuting experience, allowing smoother crowd flow and ensuring the station can accommodate the rising number of daily passengers comfortably.

Farrer Park MRT Station remains a vital transportation hub, but improvements to its capacity and space would better serve the needs of today’s fast-paced and busy commuters.
A convenient stop by Rangoon and Serangoon Road to get to the medical centers, hotels nearby and Mustafa in Little India.

Farrer Park MRT Station (NE8) is an underground station along the North East Line (NEL), located within the Farrer Park and adjacent to the Kampong Java and Lavender planning subzones. The station sits underneath the Connexion building, an integrated hospital and hotel complex.

Farrer Park was named after John Farrer, who was President of the Municipal Commissioners from 1919 to 1931. The area was renamed to Farrer Park in 1935 after the old Singapore Turf Club moved to Bukit Timah, leaving behind an old racecourse where Farrer Park Primary School now stands.

The station is located in the vicinity of residential apartments near Pek Kio and Tessenohn Road, shophouses and commercial / residential mixed developments near Owen Road and Rangoon Road, as well as condominiums and shopping centres near Kitchener Road.
Loved the MRT system on our trip to Singapore. Easy to navigate with a familiar tap and pay to use. Cheap as well.

Quite a few entrances which gives easy and sheltered accessibility to the surrounding areas.
Cool and not usually crowded.
This serves only one line though, NE or purple line.
There are no amenities there so one has to go all the way to City Square to go to the restroom or buy a drink.
Also , the taxi stand is quite far and a very quiet taxi stand which hardly anyone uses , except maybe on Sundays. So it is hard to get a taxi leaving from this station

reviews.text:
Despite having dedicated teachers for each class, the teachers work together as a collective, creating a kampong spirit within the centre that we truly love. We hope our child will embrace this same spirit as she grows.

We’re especially grateful to the teachers of Patience 1, He Laoshi, Teacher Aliyah and Teacher Ann, for their care and guidance towards our little one. We can see that our child feels comfortable and safe with her teachers. She has built a close bond with them, often talking about them and even imitating them at home.

Since she started school, we have witnessed tremendous growth in her confidence, independence and ability to express herself. It has been heartening to see how much she has grown under the care of her teachers.

As parents, we are kept closely updated on our child’s development. The process feels very collaborative, with the teachers sharing thoughtful advice and answering our questions with clarity and patience. At times, they feel more like friends walking this journey with us, rather than just teachers.

A special shoutout to He Laoshi, who has been with our child for almost a year now. They share a very special bond. Even on difficult mornings, He Laoshi has a way of comforting and reassuring her, helping her settle in with warmth and ease. We are truly thankful for the love, patience and consistency she has shown our child.

I am truly grateful to have such wonderful teachers at My First Skool.

They are friendly, caring, and take such good care of my daughter, allowing me to go to work without worry.

My girl is always bringing home something new that she has learned. She is learning well and has adapted wonderfully to her environment, friends, and teachers.

He Laoshi, Teacher Anne, and Teacher Aliyah are all friendly, helpful, and dedicated. I am truly appreciative of their efforts and the love and care they show to the children every day.

Thank you for making a positive difference in my daughter's growth and development. We are very happy and grateful for all that you do. ❤️

We would like to express our heartfelt appreciation to Complement, especially HeShu Laoshi, Teacher Aliyah, Teacher Anne, Ms Zakiah, Ms WeiMei and Ms PeckQi for taking such wonderful care of our son, Joaquin.

Their patience, dedication, and genuine love for the children have made a positive difference in Joaquin’s growth and learning journey. We are grateful for the nurturing environment they provide and the attention they give to each child’s needs.

Thank you for always going the extra mile and making Joaquin feel safe, happy, and cared for. We truly appreciate all your hard work and commitment. Highly recommended!

Amazing school with a fantastic team of educators! MFS offers balanced, clean, and safe space for all children. The teachers are professional, loving, and always go the extra mile for both my son and daughter.

I'm very thankful for Lin laoshi for being compassionate and amazingly patient. She has been caring for my son for 2 years and genuinely concerned about his well being.

Many thanks to my daughter's teachers in K2 especially Feng laoshi and Teacher Christine and Teacher Priya for being extremely responsible, patient, and caring.

We feel very fortunate to have all the teachers as part of both my
son and daughter's educational journey. Highly recommend this school to all parents.

My kid has been in this school since 18 months old. The teachers in the school are very caring and responsible. The teachers are always doing their best to teach and care for the kids and through the years I can see the kids are really enjoying the the class. My kid was taught well and love the teachers so much. I am really very glad that I have placed my kid in this childcare center as I have no worries on him since day 1. Thank you to all the staff for taking care of my kid growth! Thank you Teacher Jo and Zhang Laoshi taking great care of him on his last year!

I lost my Oppo phone today at 930pm and called in to seletar bus depot to assist me in finding my phone. Customer officer only told me to follow the procedure to wait but I took the matter into my hands and gone down to the bus terminal at 11pm+.

Kwan voon kai, Assistant supervisor and Hiew min soon, OO, took 2 rounds to find my phone as I saw my OPPO phone GPS located at the bus terminal, in one of the bus 24.

They were in sweat and still continue to find about 1 hour for me, in every possible bus 24. I really appreciate it!!! Finally they managed to find it 12.15am in bus SG1815Y.

Thank you bus supervisors for going beyond and above to find my missing phone!!

Conveniently located at neighborhood heartland of Lentor and Yio Chu Kang with scenic views of Seletar Simpang Kiri River leading towards Seletar Reservoir breezy greenery  away from busy cityscapes,  this bus depot occupied by SBS Transit as anchor operator and SMRT buses as tenant. It is a main bus depot with offices and maintenance facilities servicing most of the buses in Singapore. It relieved the pressure off Ang Mo Kio Bus Depot.  Noted for environmental friendly, the green roof reduces energy consumption. The bus washing system recycles 80% of the water used. Amenities include free wifi modern gym and handphone  charging stations. Bus stop with buses serving Yishun to Yishun MRT bus interchange Yishun Town Gardens Yishun Town Square Northpoint City with plenty of dinning shopping banking supermarkets library cinemas entertainments groceries sundries postage services community centre and amenities nearby. Direct bus 853 to Kallang and Geylang. Clean tidy orderly relaxing friendly fast attentive cosy comfy welcoming ambience.

A bit secluded. Hear say there’s air con and that it is ok. Not sure if I would recommend coming here since it’s quite a distance from the nearest bus stop and facilities.

It is big, spacious and clean. The latest bus depot for a while.
Do not try to trespass! Unless you are a staff member or bus captain!
Nice and clean working environment... Canteen here is good and cheap...

One of SBS Transit’s latest bus depots. Went their for a depot tour. SBS Transit has really stepped up their improvement of their bus services and maintenance of their buses and trains. Hope SBS can continue to provide wonderful service for the buses and MRT.

This is a great hawker! Well-situated with a lot of spacious seating and access to open air by SIT. The food options are plentiful and they have a lot of unique and delicious food options. Overall, I was impressed with the variety and the quality and if it were closer to home, I’d come back more frequently.

Definitely recommend stopping by and enjoying! A bonus if you use FairPrice app, can save 10% on the meal!

**Punggol Coast Hawker Centre**
84 Punggol Way, Singapore 829911

Punggol Hawker Centre offers a wide selection of mouth-watering dishes. Here, you can enjoy a variety of famous Singaporean local dishes.

On our last visit to this hawker centre, I found the delicious fried Char Kway Teow Whampoa Traditional at Punggol Hawker Centre, and I decided to order it as a takeaway.

Today, we went back for lunch and ordered the same meal again for $6 per serving.
**Rating: 7/10**

On my next visit, I plan to try other dishes from this hawker centre and recommend signature dishes from various stalls that also offer delicious options at affordable prices.

"Delicious food shared with friends."
Bon appetite!

MUST TRY!!! the roasted meat and char siew rice and it was quite cheap and delicious. The plate came with a good variety roasted pork and char siew all served over fragrant rice with sauce drizzled on top.

Had a wonderful experience at Munchi Pancakes Sengkang! A big thank you to Eileen and Chau for their excellent service. They were both so friendly, welcoming, and attentive, making the whole experience even more enjoyable. The pancakes were freshly made, delicious, and prepared with care. It’s always nice to be served by staff who genuinely make customers feel valued. Highly recommend this outlet, and I’ll definitely be coming back again. Thank you, Eileen and Chau!

I'm a huge fan of their pancake! Staff is friendly with a smile. Fillings - they're generous and consistently good. I even saw the staff carefully weighing out the ingredients. Plus, if you want your pancake warmed up, all you have to do is ask. It's those little touches that show they really care ❤️❤️❤️❤️.

This stall surprised me with very delicious and rich selection of pancakes n munchi, the stall owner offers heating up the pancakes to make it even nicer. Price friendly and suggest using fair price apps for payment to enjoy additional 10% discount 👍

Got 3 flavours, original black sesame, original hazelnut and charcoal peanut. Fillings were generous and rich, pancake was spongy and dense. Recommend eating it asap though.

Friendly and cheerful service is always the cherry on top.

Two thumbs up!
Stall owner was a very bubbly and polite person! Heard good things from my colleagues on the munchi pancake and definitely was up to standard. Will buy again!

Sengkang MRT/LRT is super convenient — everything you need is connected in one spot. Whether I’m heading to work, grabbing groceries at Compass One, or just getting around the neighborhood, this station makes it easy.

The transfers between MRT and LRT are seamless, and it’s well-integrated with the mall, bus interchange, and even food options nearby. The place is generally clean, organized, and easy to navigate, even during peak hours.

I pass through here almost daily, and I rarely have issues. Trains come on time, announcements are clear, and the whole station feels safe and efficient. One of the better hubs in the northeast, for sure.

Best place for crowds. Weekday and weekend peak hour full of crowd. This place is the place to be if you like to squeeze, enjoy the body contact and stress.  Sometimes breast to breast. Next stop to look out after here is Serangoon. Same situation, but with interchange to CC line and so on.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 5-Star Review for Wildseed @ The Summerhouse

Wah, come to Wildseed Cafe at The Summerhouse and kena first-class service sia! Staff got eagle eyes one — see us trying to 自拍 (take photo) until hand stretch like yoga pose, immediately volunteer to help take nice photos. End result so good, can use for profile picture already!

Our order taking a bit longer, but the staff never disappear like ninja. They proactively checked with the kitchen and kept us updated. Service level really power lah.

Vegetarian choices got plenty plenty, not the usual “salad only” situation. The Truffled Brie Pizza was the superstar of the day. So fragrant and cheesy that every slice disappeared faster than salary after payday. The truffle aroma attack the nose first, then the creamy brie come in for the finishing move.

Good food, beautiful surroundings, and service that makes you feel welcomed. Come hungry, leave happy, and maybe with a few new photos for social media too! 😆🍕🌿📸

Lovely staff who were friendly and patient even on a hot afternoon. The first pizza came slightly burnt but the staff offered to redo it which we were very thankful for. Wings were quite tasty too! Not recommended to go there on a hot afternoon though as it is an open-air cafe.

The food was quite standard, nothing particularly special or memorable. Considering the price, I personally don’t think it offers great value. The environment and dining experience were also fairly ordinary.

However, if you would like to experience the unique black-and-white house surroundings and atmosphere, it may still be worth visiting once.

Called prior to arrival to request for indoor seating due to hot weather. Given a nice table within the air-conditioned place but it was not enough to cool us down. Felt a little stuffy and our group felt warm throughout the meal.

Came on a weekday afternoon and place was almost empty.

We were presented with the lunch set menu just 2 min before promotion was over (2.30pm). We tried to order the set online at 2.35pm and was only then informed it wasn't available! We were disappointed since we thought having been presented the menu by the staff, we would be able to order the set, which is a good deal (1 drink + 1 side+ 1 main for 29.90 while usually a normal main dish is already ~32). Tried reasoning with the staff to no avail.

After we started placing our normal orders and submitting the orders, someone came and told us the manager agreed to let us order the set menu. Poor management on this part, in my opinion.

Food came nicely presented and taste legit. Mushroom soup was thick. Steak was nicely done to our request. Coffee of good size. Desserts need wait but was served hot.

Overall ambience a nice place to hang out for a couple of drinks and tea.

Beautiful restaurant, with all the warm light bulbs and live band, great atmosphere to dine in! But food was just disappointing.🥲

Soft shell crab linguine: rate 3/5 stars
- linguine not al dente.
- smallest soft shell crab i have ever eaten
- not sure what is the purpose of putting a whole chili intact on the pasta instead of chopping it. We just took it out and place it aside. The pasta wasn't even spicy at all, so i guess they expect customer to bite on that chili directly while having the pasta?
- significantly overpriced. $29 for that little bowl of pasta.

Sweet potato fries $14 (rate 4/5 stars)
- love it, but it isnt crispy at all. Its texture is rather soft n moist to me.

Sunset fizz mocktail $10 (rate 1/5 stars)
- nothing special. It tastes like the sunkist drink that u have on a plane.

Overall experience: Not worth the price. Not worth the visit.

Very small but efficient airport. Firefly is the only one airline flying scheduled passenger flight to this airport. Transport to the city can be a concern as there isn’t much option and the taxi fare obviously higher than Changi. But still, flying to this airport is easier and less hassle than Changi.
Very impressive! The distance from plane to immigration n take luggage is so near. All can settle in 5 mins (excluding waiting luggage time). Not a big airport, but it's clean n nice.

I took a flight from Subang Airport and was thoroughly impressed. Despite being a small-scale airport, it is executed in a truly world-class manner. From drop-off to Grab pick-up, the passenger flow is thoughtfully designed and seamless.

The immigration e-gates are more than sufficient for the passenger volume, with officers on standby to assist when needed. Overall, Seletar Airport is well positioned to scale up and effectively serve underserved regional routes.

If you’re in the area and flying to KL / Subang, this is the best choice. Autogates, no queue, no crowds, water dispensers and clean toilets. Can come just a half an hour before departure time. The cons are food/drink outlets limited, no shopping, and no airport lounge. As far as I know Batik, Firefly, MAS, Scoot have flights here. But they tend to be the smaller propeller planes.

10 steps from entrance to check in, another 10 steps to pass security and another 10 steps to the gate 😂 exaggerated but thats how small the airport is. I like that theres no rush, no longer lines and no echo but the only downsides are the limited choices in F&B and the longer wait times for grab.

I really love shopping at FairPrice Finest Waterway Point. The self check out is super convenient and fast, and they always have cool collab promos. I got KitKat with a free tumbler and Milo with a free duffel bag hehe. Prices are affordable too, and it’s open 24 hours so I can even go at night anytime.
