Dishonest Behaviour at Customer Service Counter!

I visited the customer service counter today (28 April 2026) at around 3pm to top up my Frasers Points and had to wait quite a while as the customer in front of me was resolving a billing issue. After she finished, that customer kindly offered to let me have her points since she is not a Frasers member.

I showed my app to the staff member for scanning and asked her to add in those points. However, she only credited the points from my own receipt and did not include the additional points from the other customer. I specifically asked her if the points had been added, and she replied, “done already.” As I trusted her, I did not check on the spot, although I had some doubts based on her expression. That’s why I checked my account after I left the counter and realized the extra points were not added.

I understand there may be policies about transferring points between customers, and if this was not allowed, it would have been better to clearly explain that upfront. Instead, confirming it was “done” when it was not is misleading and unprofessional. I hope the staff can provide clearer and more honest communication in such situations in the future.

The leading supermarket chain in Singapore and their premium supermarket brand.

At this outlet, you can find a more premium range of products which is not commonly found in other normal Fairprice outlets. They have a good range of fresh produces including Organic and Japanese products. Pricing is cheaper compare to another premium supermarket chain.

The place is always crowded. Suggest to come during the opening time. As all the items especially the fresh produce had just stock up.

I'm a tourist from Taipei. On April 12th, I lost my iPhone at this store. I immediately asked the front desk for assistance. The staff told me that they checked the security camera footage and found someone had taken it, but due to privacy concerns, they couldn't investigate further and asked me to file a police report. Since the phone could not be traced, it must have been switched off. I immediately went to Sengkang to file a police report. Throughout this time, I kept contacting the police station, only to be told that the investigating officer was on leave. They finally contacted me on April 30th after I returned to Taipei, informing me that the investigation revealed a cleaning staff member had taken it and thrown it away. This was completely different from what the staff had told me. Where did things go wrong? I'm so disappointed with Singapore. This whole ordeal, with all the back-and-forth contacting the police station, has shown me that Singapore is not the efficient country I imagined.

Had a pleasant experience at One Punggol Hawker Centre. The space is one of the more modern and comfortable hawker centres in Singapore, with high ceilings, plenty of seating and a bright, airy layout that makes dining here enjoyable even during peak hours. With over 30 stalls offering a good mix of local favourites and international options, there is usually something for everyone.

The biggest strength is the overall environment. It feels clean, spacious and family friendly, and being part of the larger One Punggol community hub means you can easily combine a meal with a visit to the library or nearby facilities.

Overall, a comfortable and modern hawker centre with a good variety of food choices. Not every stall is a hit, but the pleasant dining environment and wide selection make it worth visiting if you’re in the Punggol area.

How come this simple plate of vegetables, fish, and rice I bought from this stall costs $8.60? At a hawker center some more 😅 Got a big surprise when I was informed upon payment. I double-checked with the store owner regarding the price, and they mentioned that the fish itself is already $6!!! I hope they would advise the price for high-priced items before even plating it. Do you think it’s worth the money?? I think 菜饭店 should also indicate price tags for openness and fair pricing. The food is not fantastic, neither bad — the fish is quite dry though. 2 stars from me.

Reach there almost 8pm on a Wednesday night for dinner for end up more than half of the stores closed ( not closing or cleaning up).. Diners this timing balance about 70% of table unoccupied..  Compare to when I went to Punggol coast mall side hawker centre, fernvale hawker centre and Buangkok hawker centre even 8.10pm seat at least over 80% taken and the stores almost 90% opened or about to prepare to close during this timing.. on weekday.

Or maybe the good business store closed early as run out of serving.. balance a few stores opening.. the last store at 9pm still able to place order is Botak Cantonese Porridge..

Come again early next time to try out maybe other food choices..
The Thail food is horrible! Green curry become green soup! White rice was super cold rather hot, not even warm! Don’t waste your money on this stall. Tried twice! Gave up! 🤮😤😠😡🤬

A very big shopping mall with 2 wings. There are a lot of shops for eating, snacks, shopping and entertainment. You can spend quite a fair bit of time here since the mall is quite big. And you can also lose your way too since the area is quite huge.

The mall can be quite crowded too since it is also popular with the residents around the area.

This mall is massive and packed with shops on every floor. Great variety — international brands, local boutiques, and plenty of F&B choices. Admiralty MRT (NS10) is an elevated station on Singapore's North-South Line (NSL) in the heart of Woodlands, known for its seamless integration with Kampung Admiralty, an innovative senior-focused development featuring housing, a medical centre, and a hawker centre. It serves as a vital transport hub connecting residents via MRT and bus to the city and other parts of Singapore, offering amenities like shops and food options within its premises and nearby Causeway Point, reflecting the area's transition from a former naval base to a modern, family-friendly hub.
Key Features:
Line: North-South Line (NSL).
Operator: SMRT Corporation.
Structure: Elevated, with two tracks and two platforms.
Connectivity: Excellent links to Woodlands, Yishun, and the Central area; near expressways (SLE, BKE).
Accessibility: Wheelchair accessible with bicycle facilities.
Notable Integrations:
Kampung Admiralty: Directly linked, this unique vertical village combines public housing for seniors with a medical centre, childcare, and a hawker centre.
Causeway Point: A major shopping mall adjacent to the station, offering extensive retail and dining.

One of the older stations in NSL. Simple, straightforward, elevator and escalator accesses, and connect directly to Admiralty Place. Not exactly a mall, but a consolidation of many facilities like wet market, supermarkets, eateries, coffee places, pharmacies, doctors, money changer, fast food.

However, while there are many atm machines here, there is no bank here. There used to be an UOB Bank but it has been replaced.

Admiralty mrt is now a very convenient place with lots of amenities and food. Just in front of the mrt station is the  Admiralty Place mall and Kampong Admiralty hawker centre. Within the vicinity, you can find 3 supermarkets - 24hr Fairprice, Prime and Giant and also a wet market.
You can also find a Cantine food court, KFC, Subway, A&W and a large 2 storey McDonald's here. Overall, this makes Admiralty a much more pleasant and convenient place for the residents living here.

Exciting neighborhood Heartland MRT station and bus stations with great connections. Free wifi. Plenty of local delights and Western food. Freshly prepared healthy ingridients with tender loving caring skillful cooking and professional warm services.  Shopping includes shops in the overhead bridge POSB banking Kumpang Admiralty Kampung  Admiralty hawker centre Admiralty place Giant supermarkets entertainment and amenities including wet market and NTUC foodfair and fair price. Friendly fast attentive cosy comfy ambience for romantic intimates groups students tourists Expats locals visitors and families.

Located just outside Sembawang MRT, Sun Plaza is a heartland mall that has plenty of things that one might need. Sometimes I am amazed about how many shops it can fit inside.

There is a food court, multiple shops including a shop with eateries inside (Scarlett), big supermarket and multiple pharmacies in the basement, and there is some kind of balcony where we watched the Dragon Dance (it’s CNY when we visited). There are ATMs as well, sport shop like Decathlon (not as big as Decathlon experience but slightly bigger than their other small branches). There is money changer, multiple coffee shops, household necessity, quick hair-cut, pet shop (with surprisingly good range of products despite the size), stationery/book shop (which has become a rarity here), even computer and handphone accessories.

I would say it’s better to come here instead of Causeway Point (Woodland MRT) because it’s less crowded, cuter (I think the mall has its charm) and there are a very good range of shops and merchandizes.

A mall invested with Big House Flies. From when you're dinning (Wang Cafe) to literally just standing outside Hugabo while waiting for your family in the toilet, the pesty houseflies just keep hovering. With so many F&B establishments I do not understand how this can be hygienic. Thankfully this ain't my neighbourhood mall

Sun Plaza holds a lot of good memories for me, as I used to visit this mall very frequently between 2017 and 2019. It has always been a fantastic and highly convenient community hub, perfectly situated right next to the Sembawang MRT station. Even back then, I loved how it offered a great mix of retail and dining—from the famous White Restaurant to the always-reliable Haidilao. It’s a well-organized and compact mall that is very easy to navigate, whether you're there for a quick grocery run or a long meal with friends. Seeing how it has remained a vibrant and essential part of the North for so many years is wonderful. It truly caters to the daily needs of the community with its library, supermarket, and diverse shops. A solid 5-star rating for a place that has consistently provided great experiences over the years!
As I live in the vicinity, I get to visit it very often. A relatively small mall, but fully self-sufficient. Has the usual spread of tenant shops like NTUC, ToastBox,  StarBucks, KouFu etc.

Mall with lots of food and amenities.

Down side the lift are limited and you need to queue. Carpark space are kinda tight and also limited during peak hours.

Good part: many food options available and layout of mall it’s quite compact on the lower levels

It is a park located in sembangwang. It is near the coast and used to be a navy base. So there are things inspired by the naval heritage like the compass and the battleship playground here. It is only accesible by bus 882 and it come in 15 min frequency. Not much people here but can catch a glismp of malaysia.

Nice park located at the coast off Sembawang. Quiet enough for a good walk and chat with a friend. It has a nice and clean toilet in the middle of the park. There's also an activity area for the kids and young people. There's a jetty for casual fishing. You can also see parts of Malaysia in the distance..

Nice quiet park and great for taking slow walk. BBQ pits available for booking. Nice shelter and a beautiful playground. There is a Western and seafood restaurant. Dog lovers will be happy to bring their pet dogs here for a run... Plenty of plants and trees. Even passed by a place where plants lovers could rent a small plot of "land" to start farming 😅

There is bus serving this area too!

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ 5-Star Review – Sembawang Beach

If you are looking for a peaceful and nostalgic seaside experience in Singapore, Sembawang Beach is truly a hidden gem. 🌅🏝️

The moment you arrive, you are welcomed by the calming sound of waves, soft golden sand, and the refreshing sea breeze. Unlike the more crowded beaches in Singapore, this place feels wonderfully relaxed and natural. It’s the kind of beach where you can slow down and enjoy the simple beauty of sun, sand, and sea. ☀️🌊

One of the most heartwarming sights here is families and friends gathering along the shoreline with small nets and buckets. Children laugh excitedly while trying to catch tiny crabs and small fish in the shallow water. Watching everyone work together—some holding nets, others spotting crabs hiding under rocks—creates a joyful and nostalgic kampung-style atmosphere. 🦀🐟

The beach is also perfect for:
•	Picnics with family and friends under the trees 🌳
•	Relaxing sunset walks along the quiet coastline 🚶‍♂️
•	Fishing or crabbing during low tide 🎣
•	Enjoying a peaceful break away from the busy city life

The nearby park area is clean, well maintained, and has plenty of space for children to run around safely. When the sun begins to set, the sky turns beautiful shades of orange and pink, making the whole beach feel magical. 🌇

Overall, Sembawang Beach offers something increasingly rare in Singapore — a simple, authentic seaside experience where nature, family, and friendship come together. A perfect place to relax, reconnect, and create happy memories. ❤️
Near Malaysia, sunset view is nice. Some can fishing. Famous for camping and BBQ pit. Suitable for families, pet 🐕 etc. cool environment

Bus interchange within walking distance covered pathway to Sembawang MRT Sun Plaza with plenty of dinning shopping banking supermarkets library cinemas entertainments groceries sundries postage services Canberra Community Club and amenities nearby. Besides buses serving Woodlands Sembawang Yishun Canberra Admiralty Montreal link Senoko Loop Heavy Vehicle Park there are direct buses to Bukit Merah Geylang Punggol and Shenton Way. What kind of service is this. There is too many entitled expats working and can’t even provide proper customer service. Very unacceptable!

Attached are their photos below. I’m hoping the respective bus companies will take action against them.
Your good old regular bus interchange. Very clean and has less crowd.

Great interchange with connections to Sembawang Park via 882, the city area via 980, to Yishun by 859 and to Punggol via 117. Very conveniently located next to Sembawang MRT station on the North-South Line and the Sun Plaza mall beside it.

A large park not far from the Republic Polytechnic and the upcoming new RTS terminus of Woodlands North. It's also the gateway to Woodlands Waterfront Jetty and its seafood restaurant. The premise encompasses the large playground with multiple slides, some as high as the ridge surrounding the playground itself, and the mangrove forest.

An interesting fact I found during my visit - the playground has the rare swing for wheelchair users, although this was inoperable in time being.

Came here 7 years ago when it was new, and the place was nice and new. Kids had a lot of fun there. 7 years later, the place is so fun down, and most of the slides are closed for repairs. There's also green moss on every surface. Facilities were no better, the toilet is not very clean and everything was just not well maintained. Really night and day. Hope the repairs bring it back to its former glory.

A decent-sized park in the north, around Woodland neighborhood. What I like about this park is the feel. Going inside into it, it takes me out of the urban space where I can usually enjoy the nature without feeling that I am in the middle of the city.

There are multiple entrances into this park. For those who wants to take bus, there is a direct bus from Woodlands interchange where one can alight near Woodland waterfront, if I remember correctly. We used to come here quite often for macro shots, taking pictures of insects, hunching our backs over some shrubs.

For those who enjoy walking, this can be a decent walking/jogging route. Some parts are quite flat, while near the end where the mangrove is more hilly with some ups and downs and even the ramp type road on a spot (picture).

When I came here again (March 2026) it still feels nice, but some maintenance is needed. Missing and rusty signages, stagnant water, some trash (this has to be attributed to irresponsible visitors), but other than that, it still looks like what I remember, except for some ongoing maintenances and constructions around the park.

There was a sign of Bridge C closing (near the mangrove), but it was small and located in the middle of a shelter that I didn’t stop by because I didn’t need to take a rest. As a result, I missed the announcement and walked all the way to the Mangrove Forest from the wrong trail. I ended up in front of the hoarded up area and had to circle back through another trail until I found the mangrove and the other side of the hoarding.

I saw a bunch of monkeys, a bunch of otters, a big monitor lizards, and since my main purpose is to walk, I didn’t go for macro hunt. There is a big playground in the park, a water lily pond, mangrove forest, and nice natural trails. Overall, I like this park because it still feels natural and it’s not that crowded or popular so I can still enjoy a nice walks. During weekdays, there are older folks. The monkeys are respectful, leave them alone and they won’t bother you. Not sure if this are different groups or the ones we usually saw back then.

There are playgrounds, ampitheatre, mangrove inside here.

Beautiful nature and a great route for jogging! Wish the park could’ve been more preserved as you can tell that it’s been awhile since someone came to
do any cleaning. Citizen needs to clear their own trash as it can be seen everywhere which will definitely affect the wildlife there.

Would be great if we can install lightings for night walk/run as it can get pitch black once the sun set.

This park offers a wonderful setting for a family outing. Children can delight in the available slides and swings. There is also ample space provided for enjoyable cycling and jogging. It is a truly pleasant environment for outdoor activities.

Modern and open space hawker centre with high ceilings and big as fan (sorry might get banned for spelling the real name). The food here is affordable but taste wise, very plain and basic, catered to the old people. The Bak Kut teh that food king reviewed is over hyped (see my review for this). Pleasantly surprised by the ah Ma meesua when I gave it a second chance. It tastes better when dining in than take away. Avoid egg tarts at all costs as it tastes like rock. Friend say it’s usually good but when he bought at 8pm, the taste is bad.

One downside is the seats. Very hard to find proper seats especially during peak hours as the seats are all weirdly shaped and built for 8-10 people. The designer of this place doesn’t know that loners with no family or friends exist in the society. Even for family of 2, also need to share table. I guess, if you like to make friends with strangers sitting at the same table, do give here a visit.

This hawker centre offers a good variety of traditional local food at reasonable prices, making it a pleasant spot for everyday meals.
For vegans and vegetarians, there are several welcoming options. Gong Fu Yuan Vegetarian serves a range of Chinese vegetarian dishes with many vegan-friendly choices, while Xi De Li offers classic fried vegan snacks that are both nostalgic and satisfying.

Even non-vegetarian stalls provide meatless options. Kopi Kiosk includes vegetarian selections within its Signature Breakfast Sets, Hao Cheng Kee Carrot Cake offers a meat-free carrot cake that’s flavourful and comforting, and Saamsudeen Indian Muslim Stall has various Prata options suitable for those avoiding meat.

Overall, it’s a convenient and enjoyable place to find tasty local food, with plenty of choices for plant-based diners.

It’s only my third visit here after so many years, and I’m surprised by how popular the Muslim food stalls are at Kampung Admiralty Hawker Centre—even on a weekday dinner hour. The queues were so long I almost fainted just looking at them!

I ended up choosing Soon Kee Roasted Delights. The roast pork skin was crispy and flavourful—loved the crunch. The lower layer had quite a bit of thick, soft fat, which fans of that texture would definitely enjoy. I even added some chicken rice chilli to my plain white rice (I know, unorthodox), and it turned out surprisingly tasty. 🤤

Although the space is large and not too crowded, I still had a hard time finding a seat. 😅

But hey, taste and service are always subjective. ☺️

Eatbook article was in 2017, so when we finally checked this place out, some stores were no longer around.

We often visit Causeway Point and even Bukit Canberra HC and Marsiling HC than this place.

I often read that Kampung Admiralty is an elderly care housing estate.

This HC is run by Kopitiam but they don't run the 10% discount for Fairprice app and Merdeka users. The price is somewhat kept low.

I suppose we won't find it as appealing if we do stay around this precinct.

As a local Singaporean, I was pleasantly surprised to find numerous food stalls offering delicious meals for just $3! Contrary to the belief that affordable dining is hard to come by in our city, these budget-friendly options are not only tasty but also showcase our vibrant food culture.

For anyone looking to enjoy great meals without spending a fortune, I highly recommend exploring these $3 stalls. It's an excellent way to experience the flavors of Singapore!

There are also charging ports at the study desks if you need to charge your phone/laptops. It is very noisy at night when the library is about to close as a lot of kids do come here. First time visiting this Sembawang public library. Very cosy venue on first impression, lighting not overly bright but not too dim to make users to fall asleep. I noticed this venue got ramp, i think is for baby pram easy access. Even got a few decks for students to use and stretch the legs, thats absolute lovely. Love the overall design. Check out the interior pictures too. Worth to visit for library hopping. :p

I understand that library should be quiet and conducive for everyone. Hence seeing all the negative comments saying the children are loud in the library i understand why librarian keep reminding to lower the volume.

However since it is a children section, children are expected to be children. My child grabbed a book, librarian immediately said "are you borrowing it?" for every book my child took. This scared her that she didn't want to browse any books anymore. Then when we were reading in the children section, they just kept coming and say lower our volume, when we weren't even loud, my child wasn't making any noise. 3 librarians just kept hovering over us the entire time there that make it so unenjoyable so we decided to leave.

The built of the children section had this grassy patch them children can sit on. But it's hollow so whenever someone walks over it, its make a "thump" sound, even for a child who is much lighter. Hence i gets why it because noisy for others. I think instead of blaming children for being noisy the library can think of ways to make it less noisy or just enclose up the children section. It is literally a children section while children can be children.

While we leaving we witness another set of parents asked by the librarian to ask theor child not to grab a book. I guess we are not the only one experiencing it. Would rather go other libraries with children section if this continues. It really takes the curiosity of learning and reading out of children when they can't even get a book out of the shelves.

I switched from Yishun Library and have been visiting Sembawang Library for the past few months as it is closer to my home.

On a few occasions, I searched for a book using the app and located the shelf, but was unable to find it. When I approached staff for assistance, they were also unable to locate the book and mentioned it was likely misplaced. This happened more than once.

However, on another visit, I met a staff member, Ms Ong Cheng Luan Agnes, who was very proactive. She took down the book details, followed up, and eventually managed to locate it. I really appreciate her initiative and helpfulness.

In the meantime, I had already made a reservation and incurred the reservation fee. Thankfully, I was still able to get the book in the end.

Feedback: It would be great if other staff could be more proactive and supportive in assisting patrons.

Shoutout: A big thank you to *Ms Agnes* for going the extra mile to help!

My email to SMRT today..

Dear Customer Relations Team,

I am writing to raise a concern regarding an incident reported to us by my brother that occurred on 21 December at approximately 2.10–2.15 pm at Sembawang MRT station.

According to my brother, he observed an elderly man using his mobile phone to zoom in on and take photographs of two females who were standing near the station map area. The conduct appeared targeted rather than incidental.

My brother informed one of the station staff members at the baggage check area on what happened, and suggested that they look into the matter. However, he was told that because the incident occurred in a public area, it was acceptable, unless the individual was pointing the phone at a specific person. My brother again clarified that the man had indeed zoomed in and directed his phone towards the two females.

I am extremely concerned both about the incident itself and the response given by the staff member. While photography in public spaces may not always be unlawful, targeted or intrusive photography particularly when zooming in on individuals warrant intervention or at least monitoring.

I would appreciate it if the station could:

1. Review the incident, including any available CCTV footage from the relevant time and location; and

2. Reinforce guidance to frontline staff on how to respond appropriately when members of the public report potentially intrusive or concerning behavior.

The intention of this email is to ensure commuter/public safety and to prevent similar or more serious incidents from occurring in the future.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your response.

(Maxwell Chicken rice.) The food was absolutely delicious. Service was great. The soup for some reason tastes like cucumber. Ordering Kiosks give discounts(why?), but there were a lot of people. Brunch with the Wife here today. So good food to choose from. Local as well as Middle Eastern and Western.  Michelin accredited stalls as well and relatively inexpensive.  Had the Teochew fishball noodles today as its handmade , originally from Kim Keat in the 70s. Oh so good 👍.  Give it a try the noodles and the place.

Superb hawker centre. It serves some of the best local cuisine to Thai Food one can ask for.

Li Xin’s fish cake to Pink’s mango sticky rice to Pork Boat Noodle. The XLB and Dumplings are a must try too. So is the Muah Chee.

The hawker is clean, spacious, and well ventilated. Superb place. Strongly recommend this place to the foodie.
A well designed Hawker Centre with numbers to identify the different blocks and wayfinding. I tried the fried carrot cake, it was nice. The price considered reasonable in 2026, with the portion ok.

20 mins away from Sembawang MRT, can take bus if doesn't want to walk.

Went here for a wedding invitation. The hall is just suitable for holding a moderate wedding ocassion - not too small and not too large. What's important is the air conditioning is set at the most convenient temperature where you don't feel the temperature loss even though there is a continuous flow of guests coming through the opened door.

I booked a badminton court recently and had a lot of fun here. The place is clean and quiet especially during the night . One of the few cc I know that had badminton courts late at night. Would definitely come here again

Was there to check the CDC voucher 2023. Encountered very unfriendly staff was told that the registration has already closed but government stated validity till 31 December 2023. The sembawang CC staff is totally not helpful and doesn’t follow the government datelines and guidelines.

result.name: Star Learners @ Sembawang Way | Preschool & Childcare

reviews.text:
I would highly recommend Star Learners Sembawang Way to other parents. The teachers are friendly, caring and approachable, which gives me peace of mind. Since joining the school, I have seen positive changes in my child. He has become more independent and is now able to eat on his own. His speech has also improved, and he is using many more words to express himself.

The school provides a variety of engaging indoor activities that support the children's learning and development. Beyond the classroom, they also organise regular excursions that allow the children to explore new environments and gain meaningful experiences. What makes these excursions even more special is that one parent is allowed to join, creating precious opportunities for parent-child bonding while sharing in the child's learning journey.

Another highlight is the additional enrichment programmes, such as Zumbini, which make learning fun and engaging through music, movement and interaction.

I am grateful for the support and dedication of the teachers and would definitely recommend Star Learners to other parents.

We are extremely happy with our son’s experience at Star Learners @ Sembawang Way. Principal Ms Su provides excellent leadership and ensures a warm, welcoming, and well-organised environment for both children and parents.

The teachers are caring, patient, and dedicated. A special thank you to the N1 teachers for their support and commitment. My son looks forward to attending school each day, which speaks volumes about the positive environment.

We have seen great progress in our son, not only academically but also in his confidence and social skills.

The centre management is also very efficient, and communication with parents is always clear and timely. We truly appreciate the effort and commitment of the entire team and would highly recommend this centre to other parents!

My son has been with Star Learners since the beginning of this year.

I have heard good things about Star Learners but I fully accept the fact that their school and their faculty is amazing.

Ever since my son enrolled with them, he has gained confidence, improved in his academics and is able to express himself even better.

The teachers are extremely friendly, caring and most importantly, patient with him. They ensure they maintain a good rapport with the parents and also ensure the parents feel safe and secure for sending their children to school.

I am glad that my son is enrolled here and I hope he grows even more confident, happy and pleased in the coming years.

Special thanks to the Principal of this Center, Ms Su and Huang Laoshi whom are very engaging with the kids, my son and for being very supportive.

My son starts N1 in early Jan 2026 and distance is my first priority so i chosen this branch.
My son took bout 1 mth before dropping all the crying drama and detour from the centre.
The teachers know how to handle ithe cry baby  and i dont have to worry once he enter the centre.
The curiculum part wise is vy good and my son show  alot of skills like vy agile in handling artworks and glue (before that scare of glue cox will dirty his hand). He also will sing many songs tht teacher taught. He also start feeding himself independently.
Language improves tremendously too.
And kudos to the mgt staff too who help me in the subsidy resubmission which i thought i is going couldn't get it.
I love every staff there as they really remember every child name, comforting & talk to them.
. They make sure every child go into each class & fully attended.
My son class teacher, Teacher Rina and Teacher Huang will try update  me as much possible if i see them be it whether he sleep or drink milk, etc. After 1 mth, my son already adapted to the routine which is important. Now, whenever i go and pick my son he is reluctant to go back as he want to play with friends there.
Lately they hold a workshop with parenta on kids emotion control and is helpful to help parent to understand that each child brain is still developing on emotion control.
Safety and hygiene is also strictly implemented. each child is checked thoroughly before they arr allowed to enter.

One of the best preschools in Sembawang! You will never regret enrolling your child here. The support staff and teachers are consistently warm, friendly, and genuinely caring. Every morning during health checks, I notice how they greet each child with smiles and encouragement — such a simple gesture, but it shows how much they value each little one.

The Centre Principal, Ms Su, leads with so much heart and passion. She is always approachable, full of positivity, and quick to respond to our queries. Whenever feedback is shared, she receives it graciously and takes immediate steps to address it, which gives us great confidence as parents.

The learning environment is not only nurturing but also engaging — you can see how the children are happy, growing, and thriving. As parents, we feel reassured knowing that our child is in a place where teachers care deeply, leadership is strong, and communication is open.

All in all, we are truly thankful to have come across this preschool.

result.name: Munchi Pancakes @ Anchorvale Village Hawker Centre

reviews.text:
This is the Mumchi stall at Anchorvale Hawker Centre. I just bought 2 coconut munchi pancakes last week and today the stall called out to me again. This time round it is the red bean flavour and yes, 2 pieces.

A friendly staff who saw my glassware containing my dinner and he put it in a plastic bag automatically without asking as he was concerned that if it slipped, I may be injured or the glass splinters would injure those around me.

I appreciate his gesture as he is sincere about his customers and his friendly demeanor will always bring a smile to my face. While customer can be taught, sincerity is demonstrated. Looking forward to my next purchase.

Great service. Staff is always friendly. She also advise us accordingly on the available flavours and waiting time. Always happy going to this outlet. The pancakes are also very tasty even the kids being picky eaters love them.

Absolutely love their pancakes here! The pancakes were soft, fluffy, and packed with tasty flavours. What made the experience even better was the owner was super friendly, welcoming, and genuinely enjoys interacting with customers. You can really feel the passion and positive vibes while waiting for your food. Highly recommended if you’re craving a delicious sweet treat with great service!

Friendly staff helped to heat up without asking. But the kueh seems a little undercook, and the starch taste is a little strong. The fillings all fall off when the staff pack it for me. Not as nice as last time when they just had it at Yishun

I’ve been visiting munchi for quite awhile but first time seeing Uncle here this evening (28 May 2026). Uncle today was very enthusiastic and welcoming! Thanks for his recommendations too! Very happy buying munchi from him 😄

result.name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Sengkang 2) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Singapore

reviews.text: My child attended EduFirst from Primary 1 to 6, and we’re truly elated to have found a student care centre with such high service standards. The team at Anchorvale is thoughtful, attentive, and always on top of things. We really appreciate the effort they put into planning engaging and meaningful holiday programmes for the children’s enrichment too. Thank you for making life so much easier for working parents like us — keep up the excellent work!

I am so grateful for Edufirst Learning Centre (Anchorvale) All three of my kids have attended, and our experience has been nothing short of wonderful. The teachers are incredibly dedicated, and we have built a strong relationship with them.

A special thank you to Teacher Joeyi, who has been a constant support for our family over the years. We truly trust that our children are in great hands with her. Highly recommend this center!

I’ve been attending Edufirst Learning Centre for the past few months, and it has made a big difference in my understanding of the subjects, especially Math and Science. The teachers are very patient, knowledgeable, and always willing to explain things clearly until we fully understand.
The lessons are well-organized and focused, and the materials provided are very helpful for both school exams and revision. I also appreciate that the class sizes are small, which means we get more individual attention.
Thanks to the support from the tutors, I feel more confident and motivated in my studies. The learning environment is also very comfortable and encouraging.
I highly recommend this tuition centre to any student who wants to improve their grades and enjoy learning at the same time!

We have been extremely happy with the care and support our child has received at Edufirst Learning Centre. The teachers and staff are warm, attentive, and genuinely care about the students’ well-being and development.
Since enrolling, we’ve noticed positive changes in our child’s attitude towards learning and schoolwork. The daily routine is well-balanced with time for homework, enrichment activities, and play, which keeps the children engaged and motivated.
Communication with parents is excellent—they provide regular updates and are always approachable if we have questions or concerns. The environment is clean, safe, and welcoming, which gives us peace of mind as working parents.
We truly appreciate the dedication and effort put in by the team and would highly recommend this centre to other families looking for a nurturing and supportive after-school care option.
Thank you to the team for making such a positive difference in our child’s life!

Both my children have been very happy at this student care. We have tried other centres in the past, but what really stands out here is the dedication of the teachers. They genuinely care for the children and put a lot of thought into planning meaningful activities and outings. You can tell that every programme is planned with heart, not just as a list of places for parents to pay for, like what we experienced elsewhere. My kids have no complaints at all and always come home happy — and that, to me, says everything about the quality of care here.
Kudos to Teacher Joei and team !

Overall the staff are professional but I think they are not precise. More details below

Called Passion Card hotline to get refund on lost passion card on behalf and is told to go cc. So I called the cc to confirm before I went down. After filling the form, I left and I call back to told them that I have tick the wrong bank. It was then that they told me the card lost is silver passion card and i had to go down to hougang before tearing my form.

I went home and realize the card is not a silver card. I called them and told them that they gave the wrong info and had already tear my form. So they ask to me come down again.

They are professional throughout but seems like they are not clear of the process and not able to say no. I had told them that if it is possible to get the refund in my case and they use professional terms like they can't guarantee I can get back the money, which is not helpful. I had to use the right terms like is it administrative possible to process such case and I understand money refund is not guarantee. I even had to mention that it is okay if they are not sure as I can escalate it with the passion hotline. They still insist I come down and confirm it is possible. To be fair to them, I mention again if they not sure is okay but if I had to come down and it is administrative not possible to process I will escalate.

So I made another trip down and they mention that I can't sign on behalf and have to bring back the form.

I gave up and do it at Rivervale cc. You may refer to my review there. The experience is better and I even got an acknowledgement slip.

Lousy service. Well I already called to inform and apologise that I am late to collect my HPB tracker on behalf of my family member and this person said can come now and take at singpost level 3. And so I rushed here. And in the end it's all closed.

Please know your stuff well to serve the public better and if I could suggest, please stop all this collection with certain timing.. Just do self service collect parcel since it's 24hrs. You only burden people like this. Please report this to MP or whoever in power to improve this.

Thank you and I look forward for this change.

Sengkang Community Club, more commonly know as Sengkang CC is located fairly close to the Sengkang General Hospital and even closer to Compass One(previously known as Compass Point).

Foreigners or people new to the place may not have a clue to the purpose of the building as it does not host the common restaurants or businesses.  The signages  there do not stand out either. However, locals know it to be a building of necessary amenities.

There is no ColdStorage, nor Fairprice or even a bubble tea shop here. I have only ever seen just one standard shop or restaurant by the name of Uncle Toby's Tuckshop. It serves food, beverages and patrons there have a simple  outdoor seating area to enjoy their snacks or meals. The only period of time where there will be a lack of customers outside on the seats is in the afternoons, where the sun heats the seats up to a rear-end scorching temperature.

The Sengkang CC building hosts  the Anti-Narcotics Association office, a neighbourhood police centre, a SingHealth Polyclinic, a women's clinic,  Singapore Post postal service and a People's Association CC. There are of course others such as the gym, tuition centres, a multipurpose hall and a basketball court situated outside.

Just attended CNY 2024 dinner here. The food is nice. Multi entertainment. Really enjoyed. Looking forward to 2025. Good Job for the volunteers too. Thanks for the coordination for us to enjoyed the program. Plus lucky draw too

ADLINA is the best! Thank you for your exceptional customer service. First time to visit this clinic as I am suffering from extreme lower back pain & I need an urgent medication. I am so lucky that she's the one who assisted me in my inquiries and she even advise me to go to TCM and my back pain was resolved through therapy/massage instantly. If it's not for her recommendation, I maybe sick until now. Thank you so much 🙏🏻

I want to express my gratitude to Skool4kidz Sengkang Riverside Park for providing a secure and safe environment for our child Nova Zehraa. Thank you to all PG teachers for educating, comforting, loving and nurturing Nova Zehraa. She enter there, she started in PG class. It’s Nova first time being away from us, so im so glad that Nova can really adjust to the place. Now Nova has promoted to N1D. As a mother, my top priority has always been my child’s happiness, health, and security, and finding this at your preschool is reassuring. Due to all the consistent guidance at school, Nova is now quite an independent child and is able to do some things on her own. She knows what to pack in her bag when going to school. Earlier was hard to settled Nova every morning when entering the school because of separation anxiety with us, but the teachers really know how to win Nova heart and comfort her till she is adjusted to it. Going home time, Nova will always cry upon us fetching her, but now Nova will show us happy face n smile, hug her teachers n wave goodbye. Nova often tells me that she enjoys the school, in a way of her sings and dance at home whenever I ask her how school is. Although she can’t talk much yet, but her movements and the way she try to explain to us, makes me think that she really loves the school curriculum. Teacher Lestly & Han Laoshi invested time and effort in planning engaging lesson activities and meticulously documented their observations for our child: These portfolios kept us well-informed about their progress in school. I was happy reading them. I know Nova had a very great, fun & loving teachers at school. Thank you Teacher Lestly & Han Laoshi.
HUGS! ❤️❤️❤️❤️

Had a really pleasant experience visiting the school today. Sharon, who helped us with the registration, was extremely detailed and patient in explaining everything to us.

Principal Reina also gave us a full tour of the school and explained the setup and design of the facilities very thoughtfully. We really appreciated how she shared the reasons behind the school’s environment and learning spaces, as well as the strong emphasis placed on maintaining quality through continuous teacher and principal training every year.

The whole visit felt very professional, warm, and reassuring. Thank you Sharon and Principal Reina for taking the time to make us feel so welcomed and informed!

My wife and I recently visited the preschool for a school tour with our 2-year-old daughter, and we were genuinely very impressed with the overall experience from start to finish.

One of the biggest highlights of the school is definitely its unique and independent location within Sengkang Riverside Park. Unlike many preschools located at the void decks of HDB blocks, the school offers a much greater sense of privacy, security, and exclusivity. The surrounding greenery, peaceful atmosphere, and fresh air create a very pleasant and healthy environment for children to grow, learn, and play in.

The school environment itself felt extremely welcoming and conducive for early childhood education. The classrooms and play areas were clean, well-organised, and thoughtfully planned out for young children. We were also very happy to learn that the school prepares meals through its own in-house kitchen instead of relying on external catering services, which gave us additional confidence and assurance as parents.

Another aspect that stood out strongly was the professionalism and friendliness of everyone we met during the visit. From the teachers and administrative staff to even the security guard at the guard house, every single person greeted us warmly and made us feel comfortable and welcomed throughout the tour.

We would especially like to express our sincere appreciation to Senior Principal Reina, who personally conducted the tour for us. She was incredibly patient, detailed, and knowledgeable throughout the session. As first-time parents preparing to send our daughter to school for the first time, we naturally had many worries and concerns regarding separation anxiety, safety, routines, and how our child would adapt to the new environment. Reina showed great empathy and understanding towards our concerns and took the time to address every question carefully. She explained the school’s daily routines, programmes, learning approach, outdoor activities, and safety measures very clearly and comprehensively. Her genuine care and passion for early childhood education were very evident, and she made us feel much more reassured and confident after the tour.

We would also like to specially thank Customer Service Officer Reena for her professionalism and patience in explaining all the administrative and financial matters. She provided a very clear breakdown of the school fees, subsidies available, enrolment procedures, first-day requirements, as well as the terms and conditions. Everything was explained thoroughly and transparently, which we greatly appreciated.

Overall, the school left us with an extremely positive impression. The combination of a beautiful green environment, caring and professional staff, quality facilities, and a strong sense of warmth and safety truly makes this preschool stand out. We are very thankful for the wonderful hospitality and informative experience throughout the tour.

We really enjoyed our visit to the school. Principal Reina was very warm and welcoming, and took the time to personally show us around the classrooms from Nursery to K2. It gave us a clear picture of the environment and how the children learn and interact each day.

Throughout the tour, everyone we met — from the security staff to the teachers and admin team (especially Sharon)— was friendly and approachable, which made us feel very comfortable.

Overall, the school left us with a strong impression of being a safe, caring, and nurturing place for young children. As parents, that sense of warmth and reassurance means a lot to us. We truly appreciate the time and effort the team put into hosting us.

Didn’t take long for me to register my child in this school after the tour :)

We had a wonderful experience with Skool4kidz at Sengkang Riverside Park, and I highly recommend it for infants and toddlers.
The teachers are exceptionally responsible, dedicated, and knowledgeable. What truly stood out to us was their attentiveness to the children’s well-being. On several occasions, they were able to pick up early signs that our son might be unwell—even before symptoms like wheezing cough or fever became apparent. Their proactive communication allowed us to take timely action, and we are very grateful for that level of care and vigilance.
The teachers and staff are also consistently warm, friendly, and welcoming. Every drop-off and pick-up was a pleasant experience, which gave us great reassurance as parents.
We also appreciate the school environment. Being located next to Sengkang Riverside Park provides a pleasant and refreshing setting for the children.
Our son spent 17 months in infant care and 1 month in playgroup here before we had to transfer him to another school closer to our workplace. Even during the transition, the school was very supportive—they kindly loaned us a playgroup uniform at no cost, which we truly appreciated.
A special thank you to Teacher Siti, Teacher Evi, and Admin Sharon for their care and dedication. Your efforts have made a meaningful difference in our child’s early years.

You can always find some hidden gems there! Basically, if we see a stall with a long queue… we just try it 😂

🍗 Hainanese Chicken Rice — We love chicken rice! The chicken was okay, some parts were a bit dry, but still satisfying as our first chicken rice in Singapore this year.

🍜 Mixed Hor Fun — Loved it with a mix of meats and seafood! Not a big fan of the burnt parts, but overall still enjoyable.

🥤 Sugar Cane Juice — So refreshing, especially in the hot weat

Visit there on a Saturday morning about 9am. Most of the stalls are not operating yet. However we found 2 stalls that gives real value for money. The wanton mee stall ( facing the blocks behind) served a sumptuous bowl of noodles with 4 wantons and a generous helping of char siew at $4. Have eaten at other hawker centres I have not seen such portions. The prawn noodles is even better. Their normal sized prawns were so big it's like a big prawns and it's only $4.50. Worth another view.

0 star for the char kway tiao and hokkien mee(#01-13)! They are terrible! No fragrance just full of salty burnt taste. Yucks.

The dumplings were decent.

Stopped by Ding Sheng, unit #01-78. Great service by this 'uncle'. Ordered Mui Fan, $5.50. Average, nothing exceptional. Tried Aunty Jo's dessert, ice jelly with sea coconut, $2.80. This place gets a little crowded from 7pm onwards. Also try Hane Chiang Thai food , #01-21. Tom Yum soup is ok.

The Thunder Tea Story. Came to AV food centre for lunch and am glad to find a healthy food option. Affordable at $5.50 (option for white or brown rice) and extra charge for any add-on option . I added Chai Por Neng (Egg with Radish) for some protein at extra $1.50.

result.name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Sengkang 1 Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Singapore

reviews.text:
My son enjoy going to the student care very much. The teachers here are very patient and caring and genuinely care for the children’s well being.
The daily schedule is well-balanced — children have dedicated homework time, enrichment activities, and opportunities for rest and play. The environment is clean, safe, and welcoming. My child especially enjoys the holiday programmes and themed activities, which are both educational and fun.

My daughter was having challenge in some subjects at school, we explored few tuition options and decided to choose and start with EduFirst Learning Centre Fernvale in July. The teachers have been very patient and helpful in guiding her, within short period of time, she rebuilt her confidence in these subjects and I saw the improvement in her. I recommend this centre as the environment is conducive and teachers are engaging and committed.

I'm thoroughly impressed with this learning centre! The staff are knowledgeable, supportive, and genuinely care about their students' success. The environment is welcoming and conducive to learning. I've seen significant improvement in my understanding and grades since joining. I highly recommend this centre to anyone looking for a nurturing and effective learning experience.

My son is attending EduFirst@fernvale for the student care service and just started on English tuition. The teachers are attentive and would highlight to us as and when they find that our child is not feeling well. The school holiday programme arranged are engaging and our child looks forward to go for the activities everyday during school holiday period. The teachers also guided him to do past year papers and assessment books during the after school hours even before we started him on English tuition there and we see progress specifically in his English and Chinese. We can see that although the teachers allocated free time for the kids to play, they are also strict in ensuring that they do not misbehave.

At Primary 6, I noticed that my daughter struggled with mathematics as she had problem answering various problem sums and she had just passed for the mid year exam paper, which is exceptionally lower than her expectations. She wanted help in pulling her grades back up for Prelims and Psle, thus she approached me, requesting me to help her find a tuition centre where she can improve on her maths. After searching through multiple tuition centres, I collated multiple options for her to choose, which ultimately, she decided to choose EduFirst Learning Centre (Fernvale). This was a decision she did not regret whatsoever. When she first joined, the lessons were hosted on zoom due to COVID-19. Although she was a newly-joined student, the current students and teachers welcomed her with open arms. Despite facing multiple challenges regarding online learning, she persevered though and she also found the lessons with the teacher engaging, fun and interactive. After a few weeks, the COVID-19 safety measurements were lightened and students could return to their respective tuition centres. That was her first time meeting face-to-face with her teacher. She felt a little nervous at first, but it did not take her long to adapt to the new environment. So not long after, it was time for Prelims examinations. With all the help and support she received from her teachers and friends, she managed to meet her target for the exams. She was super elated and that gave her motivation to strive and work harder for Psle to score even higher marks. By psle, she had achieved her goal, and even got 1 grade higher than the desired goal! She was super happy and she was grateful for all the help and support she received. Overall, all the teachers at the centre are very professional and engaging. They are also friendly towards their students and the best thing is about the own time own pace method they use where the students can work on their work themselves and if they have any questions, they can simply ask the teacher so that they can guide the students.

We enrolled our daughter to MindChamps Buangkok earlier this year and it has been a great decision for us.

The teachers are caring and take care of our daughter well. Kudos to outgoing principal Ms Shannon Lim, who has really done so much for the school and laid out what works. We are sure the foundations that you’ve laid will help the next principal to take the role like a duck to water, and build on the good work you’ve done.

The year end concert was so well thought through, from the theme itself, to the art exhibitions that the children have done and extending to the actual year end performance. Such wonderful attention to detail!

We had a wonderful time at the family carnival! The teachers were very friendly and supportive, which made the event a great success. The kids especially enjoyed the activities, with the bouncy castle being a big highlight.

We are so grateful to have found MindChamps Preschool @ Buangkok! From the very first day, the warmth, professionalism, and dedication of the team have been outstanding. The teachers are nurturing and genuinely committed to the children’s growth, creating a safe and stimulating environment where every child feels valued and encouraged to explore.

A special mention goes to Principal Shannon, whose leadership truly shines through in the centre. She is approachable, attentive, and deeply invested in both the children’s well-being and the parents’ partnership with the school. Shannon always takes the time to listen, provide thoughtful guidance, and ensure that the centre maintains the highest standards of care and education. Her passion and positivity are evident in every interaction, and it sets the tone for the entire school community.

We’ve seen such wonderful progress in our child’s confidence, language, and love of learning since joining. MindChamps @ Buangkok feels like a second home, and we couldn’t be happier with our experience here. Highly recommend this centre to any parent looking for a nurturing and inspiring preschool journey!

The teachers, supporting staff, and principal at MindChamps Buangkok are very attentive to my son’s development. They provide constructive feedback and guidance, which has helped him improve and grow tremendously.
We also appreciate the well-rounded curriculum that balances academics with play, creativity, and social skills. One of the key highlights for us is the bilingual emphasis, especially the English phonics program and the Chinese curriculum, which have both been engaging and effective in building a strong foundation.
We are truly grateful for the dedication and care shown by Ms Shannon and her team, and would highly recommend MindChamps Buangkok any time!

We are grateful for the wonderful care and support my girl received during her time at MindChamps.

The teachers created a nurturing environment that helped her grow in confidence and independence, while learning important values like kindness and teamwork.

We also truly enjoyed celebrating Chinese New Year together with her and the school — it was a very special memory for our family.

Thank you for being such a meaningful part of her early years ❤️

Please check the photos carefully. It might be helpful.
The authority is updating the MRT lines. We need to concern about the route and movement.
Whose bright idea was it to have one-direction escalators that stay empty while the foot traffic is clearly flowing the other way? The efficiency is non-existent.

The passenger  services are lst class in their services.

The passenger readily to give up their seats. To senior.

The MRT vicinity is

Purple line to Kovan to Buangkok MRT is
Convenient

Serangoon MRT is closer to my heart. Is a transit point. I always have a sense of gratitude.

Serangoon MRT
Passenger Service
Staff
