Sylvia Ang known as Ang Lay Kheng
Visited Serangoon.mrt
Passenger Service staff.

Felicitations to Serangoon MRT, passenger.service
Staff.

You have given me unyielding support when I asked for directions to places.

Located in the middle of Serangoon, it is highly accessible and connects both the NEL and Circle line within a stop.

The area in front of the gantries is spacious and more than sufficient for crowds to pass through despite groups of people with low situation awareness blocking key passageways, especially during peak hours.

There are a few convenient shops underground, and were lifesavers during the pandemic, selling single pieces of masks at non-cutthroat prices for those who were forgetful enough to leave house without one.

The many ATMs here make this area a great dealing spot for Carousell users, but make sure the person you are dealing with is on the same side of the gantry as you, for obvious reasons.

Beware of the overzealous staff standing slightly behind the gantry, who would conduct random bag checks the moment you make eye contact. Just walk and move on with life.
Easily accessible by public transport. Toilets, tracks and seating areas are clean! I train there often for my intervals. It may be quite crowded on the tracks in the evenings. Mornings are better!
Serangoon Sport Centre, formerly known as Serangoon Sport and Recreation Centre, operates in a mature estate, serving the basic needs of the community.
Running track was in good condition, but the toilets are pretty dated. Plenty of parking spaces. A suitable place to do track running in the heartlands

Frequently come here for my runs together with my friend.

There’s a fitness corner, running track, a soccer field and a swimming pool. There’s also a place to play basketball.

Come in the morning on weekdays if you like the whole stadium to yourself. Usually quite crowded on weekends and Friday nights.

Also, avoid coming to this stadium when it’s raining or after a rain. The running track would be semi-flooded due to poor uneven track flooring. This stadium is relatively old after all.
Decent place for a run and kids can kick around at the side of the field. Swimming pool is nearby. Still bustling even though toilet is abit run down

Too good ...well maintained area in Singapore
What a people they are ...we are shocked after seeing so disciplined people with good manners and cleanliness
Barrier close early like 3 mins later when I want to go in I cannot already because the barrier is closed and the guys are very impatient. Worst station ever

It's at Serangoon Mrt     It's in Nex mall and Serangoon bus interchange. This is a busy MRT. *CC Circle from Serangoon going to ,Bishan. Transit to Junction 8. One can get shopping mall, food, resturant. One can transfer DLT or blue line from  macPherson. It's very safe ,conveniençe. Comfortable. I am in gratitude as younger gave their seat for a. Senior like me. Blessinng. purple  NE goes douby Ghuat to plaza Singapura for mall & Food.

Control passenger service go the extra mile.  give it a 5.5
served with a smile. I usually thanks then for showing the direction.
it has always be a great experience. I Sylvia Ang known as Lay Kheng Ang show gratitude

Service from ticket office lady was one word.
Poor, impatient, rude, and offered no help.
It is fine if she couldn't help.
But no need the attitude.
I said thank you, rolled her eyes at me.
Walked home which took more than half an hour.

A very large and busy mrt train station intersecting the Circle line and North East line.  Very convenient with many escalators to cater for the large crowd. There is also a large shopping mall NEX near the train station.

I had a great experience at Bendemeer Market & Food Centre! 🤩 The nasi lemak was truly delicious and satisfying. 😋 The service was efficient, though not exceptional. The atmosphere was adequate, with typical hawker center seating. 🥢 It’s a decent place for a quick meal. 🚶‍♀️

Ambience is hawker centre so don’t expect aircond etc. But when we are here the weather is quite cooling so it’s okay.
Food is good, drink is good.
Queued about 30min for the prawn noodle, yes it’s good.
The soy milk is good too.
Curry puff also good.
Will come again to also try the dimsum stall which is closed today.

Stumbled upon this gem specialising in beef noodle and soup while exploring this food centre after a long hiatus. The beef tendon soup and grilled beef ribs were impressive - to me, they were pure culinary delight. I am sure I will head back for more.

The Bendemeer hawker centre has a great variety of food, this review is for a particular stall - Yong Tau Fu.
Selected 5 pieces and add noodles for laksa should be total of 80 cents x 5, add  60 cents, totalling $4.60.
When I asked how much, owner was sneaky and eventually answered $5.40. When eating, I realised he has slipped another piece of brinjal in. Who can finish 2 pcs of chunky brinjal in a bowl?!
To me dishonesty kills it.  Don’t think I’m coming back.

Downtown hawker fare next to Boon Keng MRT station.

Bendemeer hawker fare is diverse, affordable, featuring iconic dishes like Chicken Rice, Laksa, Char Kway Teow, Bak Kut Teh, and Kaya Toast, costing just a few dollars per dish and now payable by Grab & Shopee scan & pay functions.

It's a vibrant culinary scene during lunch hours with a hoard of office workers descending onto this hawker center. The queues never lie & those are the stalls offering a taste of authentic popular dishes or they are just economic for daily consumption.

result.name: Wordsmith Learning Hub Pte. Ltd. (Main Centre)

reviews.text:
Hi Team,
Greeting!
Today session was very helpful in improving my understanding of marketing and the tools used to promote it effectively. It was my first time learning how to use these tools, and I gained valuable knowledge on how they can be applied in a practical and effective way.

The second half of the session was especially valuable, as it provided deeper insights into the current challenges faced by the preschool sector. It helped me better understand the industry and its requirements in a timely manner.

The Q&A session was also very beneficial, as it clarified many of my doubts and provided a clear understanding of the future direction and opportunities in this field.

After attending the complete session, I feel much more confident about pursuing a career in teaching. The session has helped remove many of my fears and uncertainties, and I now have a positive outlook towards this profession.

Thank you to the organizers and the entire team for conducting such a useful, informative, and well organized session.                             Thank You!
Parimala Devi

Thank you for the programme. I found it highly insightful and gained valuable knowledge in marketing strategies. The curriculum was engaging and well-structured, with meaningful sharing sessions that allowed us to learn from one another and broaden our perspectives looking  forward for other  programs

Two major sessions were conducted — Marketing and Curriculum. In the Marketing session, I learned various marketing strategies through Facebook and Instagram. I also gained experience in creating logos, posters, videos, and reels using Canva. Canva is a new tool that I learned, and I found it extremely useful. These marketing strategies are not only helpful for home tutoring but can also be applied across different sectors.

I also learned how to create Google Forms and link responses directly to Google Sheets, which are very useful for student inquiries, feedback collection, and enrolment processes.

In the Curriculum session, I learned different teaching methodologies to address the varying abilities of students. This included assessing students in areas such as comprehension, oral skills, and written work, while also focusing on improving their weaker areas. Through this training, I have gained the confidence to teach P1 to P6 students. I also registered for level 2 (Intern). I hope it will increase my confidence level to teach Secondary students.

Wordsmith Tamil tutor training session was well-structured and informative. The trainers explained teaching strategies clearly.
It helped us understand how to engage students effectively.
The session improved my confidence in handling different learning abilities. Useful resources and techniques were shared for better lesson planning. Overall, it was a valuable and enriching learning experience. Thank you Wordsmith.

The training was very well coordinated. The marketing tools shared were very useful and all the information shared was very helpful. The end shared by Mr Praveen was indeed inspiring and he is very good with his marketing skills!
Kudos to mindchamps @serangoon for organising such a fun family event! My girl had so much fun here. Thank you!

We’re very grateful to Ms J and Lee Lao Shi for their patience and dedication. They make sure our child is well cared for, from encouraging him to eat well (including his vegetables and meat!) to making sure he feels supported when he’s tired. They also guide him with appropriate discipline, helping him learn good habits and behaviour in a nurturing way.
-ETCZ’s parents

Overall, I’m happy with Cailey’s preschool. The teachers are patient, caring, and supportive—especially important for a child who takes time to warm up. Communication with parents is clear, and while minor incidents happen (as expected at this age), the school is responsive and handles them appropriately. A safe and nurturing environment for her to grow.

MindChamps has been a great environment for my son’s development. The teachers are nurturing and attentive, and the curriculum focuses not just on academics but also confidence, independence, and character building. Communication with parents is consistent, which gives peace of mind. Overall, a solid choice for parents looking for a well-rounded preschool experience.

Serangoon MC is the 4th branch I’ve experienced, due to closure of the 1st and 2nd branches etc. And I must say this is by far THE BEST Mindchamp branch (a HQ branch) we have experienced. Very organised, structured, responsive and responsible management! Teachers are awesome too! And we are glad we finally found a really good one and highly recommend to parents who are searching for preschool! Double thumbs up

Facilities:
The Olympic-sized swimming pool is a highlight, with plenty of space for both casual swimmers and classes. The water playground is perfect for young kids to splash around, while parents can relax in the jacuzzi. Do note that some lanes are reserved for lessons, usually in the mornings or late afternoons, but it’s a small trade-off for such excellent facilities. Swimming classes are reasonably priced at ~$100/month (4 lessons), though kids need to pass a water confidence test to join.

Kid-Friendly Features:
The kiddie playroom is a lifesaver! Stocked with Fisher-Price toys, it’s a cozy spot where kids can play while parents enjoy a meal at the nearby alfresco area. The glass walls allow for easy supervision, and it’s wonderful to see kids mingling with new friends. A small but important note: kids over 1.2m aren’t allowed in the playroom, so it’s ideal for younger children.

Dining:
Food options cater to all tastes and occasions. The Atrium Cafe offers casual dining with a mix of Western and local dishes, making it perfect for a quick bite. For something more upscale, the Garden Grill on the second floor is a must-try. With a changing monthly menu inspired by different countries, it keeps things exciting. While not every dish is a hit, the freshly baked bread rolls and butter are consistently amazing. The colonial-style setting adds a touch of charm, though note the dress code.

Fitness & Activities:
Fitness enthusiasts will love the gym overlooking the pool, recently renovated for a fresh and modern feel. The club also boasts tennis courts, a bowling alley, and squash courts, so there’s plenty to keep everyone active. The adjacent studio hosts classes like yoga and Zumba, making it convenient for parents to work out while the kids are engaged in their own activities.

Convenience:
Parking is a breeze, and its location in Serangoon Gardens means you’re just minutes away from other eateries and amenities.

Overall, Serangoon Country Club is a well-rounded spot for families, combining excellent facilities with thoughtful touches for parents and kids alike.

Service staff Lelia stationed at the bowling alley was very rude, condescending and was not willing to listen to our explanation after an accident occurred. She was speaking in a way that made it seem like I had willingly and willfully caused that accident on purpose. After she had repeatedly cut me off and choose not to listen to me as I tried to explain the situation, she began speaking to me and my friends in a very condescending and unacceptable tone. I accept responsibility for the accident but I still believe that her tone and the was she carried herself was very unacceptable and could be better. However the same cannot be said about the rest of the club and it's staff as they were very welcoming and accommodating.

This is a Very established family club with many amenities and facilities. On the heliconia Wing there is a huge function room on the 2nd level. The first level features a Bowling alley and a Chinese restaurant. In the main wing there are several food and beverages options. A causual dining on the first floor, formal dining on the 2nd floor, a night lounge with live band, poolside cafe serving ice cream and other finger food and bbq area.
Attended wedding reception of my dear friends daughter. Beautiful hall. Well lit and loved the deco. Food was simply delicious

I like the newly renovated gym, which comes with 2 storey, the upper one overlooking the swimming pool. The lounge with live band is a very good place to chill, and has darts corner. Will be better if they put a new pool table and make it like previous sports bar. The atrium food standard has dropped over the years, and noticed a lot of the staff has changed too. Overall a nice place to hang out with family friends or clients.
Samudera lrt station has 2 shopping NP1 & 2. I love the Kopitiam operated by Ntuc. So many yummy Food  & budget meal for the small eater too . Beautiful view and is windy too.

As a new resident in the North shore District Samudera LRT Station is a boon for commuting to and from the city with a short connection to Punggol MRT from either of it's platforms.
Connected to it by a walkway,on abnd above ground is Northshore Plaza One and conveniences like a 24hr supermarket, GIANT and gym.Decatholon has a very large 2 level outlet and Coffee Bean operates all night.Haidilao recently also opened it's outlet here that serves it's hot pot fares until the wee hours of the morning and closes at 4am.
Catch beautiful sunsets here at Northshore from the end of Northshore Plaza 2, a leisurely extension of Plaza 1 while having local fare at Cantine or Dancing Crab on the ground floor that is at the seafront of the Johor Straits...
This LRT Station lives up to its name Samudera...
Good connection and nice eating place. Coffee shop should be bigger to cater for more food varieties and has sea view.

Opened on 31 March 2017 as part of the Punggol LRT line west loop operated by SBS Transit, this LRT station is located at Punggol Way near the junction with Northshore Walk. The station brings direct rail connectivity for residents of Whiteshores, White Cove, Northshore Bungalows and Punggol Neighbourhood 4 (Northshore Residences I & II, Northshore Edge and Waterfront I & II @ Northshore) bringing them along to Punggol Park Connector, Punggol Tu Di Gong Temple, Marina Country Club, Punggol Town Centre and Northshore Plaza I & II. The bus services serving Samudera LRT station are Go-Ahead Singapore bus services 84G and 84W which ply between Punggol Bus Interchange and New Punggol Road and Go-Ahead Singapore feeder bus service 384 which plies between Punggol Bus Interchange and Northshore Drive. The station takes its name from its geographical location near the sea, with ‘Samudera’ meaning ‘sea’ in Tamil, which is similarly used in Malay.

I commute at Samudera LRT every weekday. Reach this station before 5pm. Its poorly lit and can i say dark. The stairways.. after tap-out area where the convenient store is.. and along the two exits, A and B. Make it bright pls. Even if it is not yet 7pm, its feels dark. Samudera is located near forested area.. so its eerie and unfriendly.
Provide fans too if possible. Vey stuffy since the station is covered up.

This has been one of my highlights in Singapore and I saw a lot. The extensive gardens are incredibly beautiful and all the trails are well maintained. I recommend coming early in the morning to avoid the crowds and the heat. There are plenty of cafes and water filling stations along the way to have a drink or simply refill your bottle.
Aiyooo..some of da Southern Ridges routes ard Forest Walk & Earth Trail r currently closed for maintenance & slope repair until 31Jul 😯😯😯
A great walking path. You were not in Singapore if you didn't go there.

I wish they repaired the skywalk soon. It has been closed for a year now and there are only few green areas in central Singapore so this one is really needed. I took the route on the ground and it was hard to find the way even if there were some temporary signs for the detour. Anyway, was nice to hike.
27 jul 2025 Crossing Vivo city to hiking up to southern ridge. Walking path is easy and well-prepared.

The building interior appears to be quite old, and the space does not seem to be effectively utilised. It feels like it could be better planned, but the SAFRA membership is very affordable.

One good thing is that there is a McDonald’s here. It is convenient for drive-through orders, and the interior of the McDonald’s outlet is quite nice and up to date too.

There is also an EnergyOne gym (need to sign up for membership / per entry) on Level 2, above the swimming pool area, which is convenient for people living nearby. The gym is decent and well-equipped, and it comes with shower facilities, a sauna, and a jacuzzi (located at the Level 1 swimming pool, but accessible only through the gym).

Many activities at Safra Yishun for family and a swimming facility with 5 pools including kids and jacuzzi 😘🤩

Female toilet at swimming pool needs to upkeep as flushing is always difficult and the tap is spoilt and has a musty smell.

Recently we planned bbq, it is good location as it is near to housing estate. The chair and table were a bit worn out so you need to do some cleaning first. I see that there is a proper charcoal bbq pit, washing bay, power point for each pit. I thought it is a good place to gather!!

Recently they upgraded their bbq pit and it has new chairs and tables!!
Tried HaveFun KTV—overall a fun experience. Rooms were comfortable and song choices were good, though it can get a bit crowded during peak hours. Still a solid choice for group hangouts.

Visited HaveFun KTV recently and it did not disappoint. The ambience was lively, service was prompt, and the audio quality made singing way more enjoyable. Huge song library too—easy to spend hours there without getting bored.

This interchange is purely a functional hub for taking buses. It lacks the modern amenities of other stations and feels quite dated and disorganized. However, the convenience of the Bukit Merah Town Centre just across the bridge makes up for it, offering plenty of dining and shopping options. If you're coming here, it’s likely just to catch your next bus rather than to spend any significant time.
TV display purely for aesthetic purpose, bus says 1:15 but when the clock turns 1:15 the timing change to ten minutes later. Tested and proven for 2 different buses.

Everyone here is complaining!! The buses here are never on time!!!

Another update, see attached photo with date and time on. Scheduled 13:09, no bus at the bay, many people waiting, till 13:11, still waiting, and scheduled to 13:22...

This is an update. Still about 272, and still disappointed. Nothing has improved. Arrive at the berth at 8.38, already 2 old ladies waiting. Display show next bus at 8.40, now 8.48 never come, and departure time changed to 8.52...

Many buses are not punctual especially 272. Showing 1448 depart but driver only on board after 50, and arrive at berth 1452. Not once but often times like this!  What's the point of showing departure time when you cannot make it? Disappointed!
bus never comes on time bro. if they say its coming in 1 minute, you better pray its 5 minutes instead of 10 lma.
Great station to roam at different locations across Singapore suburbs

Station very good location but a bit dirty with empty bottles strewn everywhere. Also many beggars sitting near station selling tissues etc but untidy look. Need to do some enforcement. Embrassment to Singapore government.

When you are at Tan Quee Lan Street, the stark juxtaposition of modern hi-rise architecture and traditional pre-war shophouses is very clear for all to see.

On one side, many restaurants have sprouted in the row of pre-war shophouses making it a very lively place to be in the evenings and weekends.

On the other side, construction is underway for Guocoland’s Midtown Modern and nearby Midtown Bay. These new buildings are next to Shaw Towers and are looking quite impressive before it’s even completed.

In the middle of these two sides is the Bugis MRT which is very conveniently located that once the construction is completed, you can expect that this MRT Station will bring air of foot traffic to this area for live-work-play.

If you travel along Nicoll Highway, we will see the construction going full steam ahead … even Shaw Towers jumped on the bandwagon and got itself rejuvenated so as not to pale in comparison with the sparkling Midtown buildings when it’s ready.

This security guard was being very rude and started scolding people like he owns the place. I was just waiting at a corner for a friend to pass something and he started yelling saying this is not allowed. Bugis MRT station staff please advise!

The toilet door lock need to fix.

I went in and while I squat down suddenly the door open on its own.

Shameful experience.

#westlinebugis

I stayed near to Bugis MRT when I was in Singapore. It was just 400 meters away from my hotel. Such a nice staff who behaves very politely and give detailed answer to our all queries. The best metro service I have seen in my entrie life and Bugis Metro station is one among the best metro station in the world.

Thank you Singapore

Clarke Quay Central is a great mall with a fantastic view of the Boat Quay riverside 🌊🚤✨. It has a wide variety of shops to explore, and you can even find some discounted items at the first-floor lobby—perfect for a little bargain shopping 🛍️💸.
What I love most is its convenient location 📍. After shopping, you can easily walk over to Boat Quay, Fort Canning Park, the National Museum of Singapore, and other nearby attractions. It’s a nice spot to relax, shop, and enjoy the scenic surroundings all in one place 🌿🏙️.
Overall, a convenient and enjoyable place to visit if you’re in the area 👍😊

It has been a while since I was last here - at this, in the Chinatown area, shopping mall that is patronised by locals and tourists alike. While I have always found the layout and connectivity inside the building to be at times, somewhat "confusing", I can see that it is still fairly popular - helped by a Don Don Donki and its food stalls at its basement level one. In fact, I was here this morning to check out its recently (?) opened food court - Sinfoodie, and I was impressed by the choice of food stalls thereat and the dining area; although admittedly, I was there well before the peak lunch hour and I did wonder what it would look like then. Anyway, I see myself returning to this shopping mall for Sinfoodie.

​Clarke Quay Central is undeniably awkwardly built, featuring a quirky layout that defies standard mall logic. The placement of shops and odd corners often cuts off the natural flow of foot traffic, making spontaneous discovery quite a challenge. It isn't the best place for a casual stroll if you’re looking to explore new stores.

Clarke Quay Central is a vibrant mall popular with both tourists and locals, thanks to its prime location next to the nightlife scene along the Singapore River.
The mall offers a good mix of cafes and restaurants, with some dining spots featuring scenic river views.

In the basement, you’ll also find the bustling Japanese supermarket Don Don Donki, which is a draw on its own.
Conveniently situated near several hotels and directly above Clarke Quay MRT station, it’s especially lively and atmospheric from the evening onwards, when the riverside lights come alive.

Went down last week to Taiyo Bread Shop at level 3 for a afternoon tea break. The cake is sweet & delicious. Price reasonable for this area. Environment is quiet for chill & relax for solo ranger cafe hopper like me 😃

Clarke Quay is one of the MRT station of North East Line. It is located just next to the southern part of Singapore River and Clarke Quay, underneath The Central building. It is also within walking distance to Chinatown. This station is also serves by bus stops which may bring passengers to different parts of Singapore.

It was located at underground level with lifts and escalators leading down from the ground level. Toilets were available for commuters. Signages were numerous and useful to assist travellers. to the right path for boarding the train or even exiting the station. This station was clean, comfortable and had well air circulation.

Superb MRT station...
Neat & clean...
Every 2 minutes have metro train timing...
Simply grocery item's are available here...
Like 7 eleven etc....
Very nice experience...

Singapore's MRT is the standard by which the rest of the world needs to catch up. Incredibly well designed, efficient, timely, well maintained and clean. So easy to use it's a tourists dream for getting around Singapore. You don't need a special ticket or card to use it, a credit card or cash card is all you need to tap on and tap off and the cost is cheaper than anything else we've seen; certainly much cheaper than Australia or Europe.

So full of lighted lanes and structures at night. In a restaurant called wings bar, happy hour is from 5pm-8pm and all drinks are 50% off during that time. Saw a lot of bars and restaurants with the same. Not much crowded, maybe due to Covid. Could eat decently for around 100SGD for 2 people

From Clarke Quay MRT (Exit 5)
You can walk to Chainatown Point Taxi Stand that have Chinatown MRT Station below

Also from Chinatown MRT Station Exit (3)
Near OG Building you can actually walk to
Outram Park MRT Exit (3)

Save your money for three stops by walking 👌🏼

A very good way to visit a number of attractions in one go. They have two stops and allow customers to take off. Full ride takes around 49 minutes. Encourage to take the ride early evening to maximize the enjoyment and capture the beauty of Singapore lights.

The 40 minute cruise met my expectations. The boat tour provided an excellent overview of the city's landmarks, complete with fascinating historical narration. The traditional bumboats, beautifully refurbished for modern sightseeing, were a highlight themselves. It's a fantastic way to get a feel of the city's cultural transformation to the impressive buoyant, thriving city it has become. If possible do this before exploring each landmark individually. 🚢🚤

as a singaporean, can use sg culture pass! not a bad experience to do with kids and older folks. the best part was waving to all the people walking and sitting along the river hehehe felt so youthful.

40 minutes of calm chill bumboat riding experience. people will snatch the seats at the back cause there has no shelter and is the nicest to take photos. we did the 7.10pm to 7.45pm timing for the sunset (but cannot see lol) so it was nice to see the day turn into dusk.

id say the counter service can be more clear on the ride haha. thought the ride has fixed timings but there arent. the boats come about every 15 minutes and you just queue up. also didnt know that you can drop off at mbs!!! why never tell us!

inside the tour, the audio recorded tour guide is only in english and not loud enough for those seated at the back.

A must do when in Singapore. Our group did it while waiting for our hotel rooms to be ready. We had the boat to ourselves. Many were pleasantly surprised when we went out into the marina (we had come from Merlion). This cruise provides a very nice view from a different side. And a nice audio history of the area. Let the little dude take your picture, he plays some nice camera tricks creating interesting pics. If I ever make it back, will do this at night.

That's a great experience that allows visitors different views of the most gorgeus parts of Singapore. The cruise lasts about 44 minutes and they have a more expensive time slot that coincides with the light show at the Marina Bay. Ideally, starting the cruise just before sunset allows you to experience different views of the skyline. It is a no frills boat, with an automated narration,but it feels safe and well managed. Definitely a must do experience in Singapore.

There are two exits here. Air-conditioning very strong. Super clean MRT but couldn't easily find the toilet signage.

The attached photo is at exit A.

Fort Channing MRT station has two exits, each leading to different areas, however close. It was relatively empty when I arrived there, and it was quite extensive inside.

In terms of cleanliness in the toilets, it is the usual clean standards, which you don't have to worry about. If you are going to the Fort Canning Park, exit via so that you can reach there faster. Overall, quite a nice MRT station.
Woah the mrt staff is so nice once my phone died and I didn’t bring my EZ-Link and he let me ride the MRT for Free!!Love this staff
clean but security only does checking of Indian individual bags eventhough so many other people are carrying big bag packs…just seems so wrong leh

Fast MRT. Cheap. Discount for kids. Got lifts. Very fast service. Wheelchair assessing. Helpful staff. Goes a lot of places. Useful maps. Pet friendly. Can hold lot of commuters. Will take you lot of stops. Quicker than car because no traffic. Near places visited a lot.

Clarke Quay is one of the most energetic and colorful areas in Singapore, especially in the evening. The riverside setting, combined with the bright neon lights and lively atmosphere, makes it a fantastic place to walk around and enjoy the city at night.
There are plenty of restaurants, bars, and entertainment options, ranging from casual dining to upscale spots with beautiful waterfront views. The colorful canopy overhead adds a unique architectural touch and helps keep the area comfortable even in warmer weather.
Whether you're looking for a relaxed dinner by the river, live music, or just a scenic stroll along the Singapore River, Clarke Quay delivers. It’s clean, well organized, and feels safe — perfect for couples, friends, or even families earlier in the evening.
Highly recommended if you want to experience Singapore’s nightlife in a vibrant and picturesque setting.

I visited Clarke Quay during the day and found it to be a really charming place. It offers a wide variety of restaurants, making it a great spot to explore different dining options all in one area. The colorful buildings and riverside setting give it a very cute and vibrant atmosphere.

Although I went during the daytime, I can imagine it becomes even more lively and visually appealing at night, when all the venues are open and the lights reflect beautifully along the river. Definitely a place worth visiting, especially in the evening for the full experience.

Clarke Quay is a vibrant riverside destination that’s very popular with tourists, offering a huge selection of bars and restaurants for nightlife and dining. The atmosphere really picks up in the evening with the music and light. It is a great spot whether you're looking for light bites or a full night out.

As a local, I appreciate that it’s well maintained and clean. It is a good place for an evening stroll or leisurely walk. While it feels touristy and the prices are definitely on the higher side, it’s still a solid spot to soak in the city’s energy and enjoy a wide variety of food and drink in one location.

Clarke Quay is one of those places in Singapore that truly comes alive after sunset. Set along the river, the area buzzes with neon lights, upbeat music, and a constant flow of people soaking in the atmosphere. Lined with a wide mix of bars, pubs, clubs, and restaurants, it offers something for everyone—whether you’re in the mood for a relaxed drink by the water or a high-energy night out with loud beats and dancing crowds.

The riverside setting adds a charming touch, making evening walks especially enjoyable. You can stroll along the promenade, watch boats glide past, and take in the colorful reflections dancing on the water. There’s always something happening—live music spilling out of venues, street performers adding flair, and groups of friends laughing their way from one spot to another.

Yes, it does feel a little touristy, with busy crowds and popular chains, but that’s part of its appeal. The energy is infectious, the vibe is festive, and the options are endless. If you’re looking for nightlife, people-watching, and a fun place to unwind after dark, Clarke Quay delivers the experience with style and spirit

On day time, this place is quiet, only a few people walking around. After sunset, this quay becomes one of the most lively location in Singapore. Colorful light, buzzing bars are all around. There are shopping malls and ferry pier for a ride along the Singapore river. Would recommend to go, but remember don't go on daytime.

So happy to come across this affordable makan place, just next to Clarke Quay!

The Minced Meat Mushroom noodle was really good! The noodle was nicely cooked, not soggy nor hard, and the sauces mixed really well with it. The mushroom has good vinegar taste. Meat was nicely cooked and tender. Soup was tasty too.
(Pic only shows half of what is left. Too excited to eat!)

The one I most appreciated was the staff at the drink stall. Very polite and patient. So rare to have someone like him as almost all drink stalls staff are usually quite rude and always rushing the customers.

Visited: 1 April 2025 around 9.30pm

Parked at Clarke Quay CP by Canning Lane, in the early morning I wasn't sure where I could get some simple breakfast or should I walk over to The Central mall. But I walked along the canning lane road and turned right, facing Fort Canning Park, found SG Hawker a 24 hours opened place just right at the corner. The toast set was only $4.50! The toast was crispy and the butter was thinly melted. A great find for me.

Located near the river front they offer a wide variety of curry rice options.
Ambience - place is pretty hygienic and they offer outdoor sitting and it is situated near the river front .
Food - Quality and quantity of food they offer is great, we got there fried prawns curry rice and fried chicken curry rice both tasted really awesome.

SG Hawker is a place which has many local dishes like Fried Hokkien Mee, Hainanese Curry Rice, Laksa, Fried Kway Teow, etc

It's located near Fort Canning and Clarke Quay Mrt.

Though it's not air conditioned, there's many fans around to keep us cool.

Interior design is cool and trendy. There's also different types of chairs and tables. There are cushioned, metal and wooden chairs. There's indoor and outdoor seats which is along the road.

I bought the 85 Redhill mini wok. It's very nice and affordable.

#ILoveSGHawker
We had our morning food here and the place is beside Clark Quay area near the road. The place is good and is open not that crowded. The food sold were also nice and good.

Very happy with the courts upgrading though Court 1 has some issues but it's minor enough to ignore for casual players lile myself.

Played for 6 months here and always see the same faces all the time for other courts (which is good!)

Super hard to book but still bookable with some effort (not botting), very happy to know that the SAFRA staffs does court booking checks from time to time to allow genuine users/members to enjoy the facilities and yes! The booking App has improved to deter bots as well.

One of the better maintained badminton courts for SAFRA.
Came here for my son's bowling competition. Top bowling alley. Place is clean and peaceful. Love the cold brew from kopifellas. I enjoyed my time at this place.

My 1st time experience at Mount Faber Safra is not good . Walk passed the pool toilet toward to pool. I can smell dog in the premises while walking towards the pool. Nearly puke after having a heavy lunch on Sunday Mother’s Day .it was so smelly . Thought it suppose to be no animal allowed ? Didn’t see any dog around but I can smell them . My husband also smelled it . We quickly went back to the mall area.
Safra needs to look into this matter . The condition of the toilet needs to upgrade too . What the reason behind it to have this smell when no animal is allowed .

Everything you need is definitely in here.

From bowling, gym, darts, soccer and more! There is at least something for someone here.

Lets dive in!

Carpark:
There are lots of Carpark spaces here under the main building. Charges apply as well ya. But do note that you may need to walk abit, to come over if in Safra, the lots are all taken up.

Amenities:
There are plenty to do here. From swimming to soccer, cycling..etc. Food can be found easily. We recommend coming here to see what you wana do🥳🥳😁😁

Location:
Nestled among residential areas, this is a great place to chill and unwind.

We particularly enjoy this place since it has all the amenities that one can use. And it also felt homely and true to its tagline, 'Building Bonds, Creating Memories'.

Recommended to all to pop over if you have yet to see what they have here.

Cheers!

I like to express my deep concern and disappointment regarding the persistent poor condition of the swimming pool water at SAFRA Mount Faber.

Having abstained from using the pool for the past few months due to the visibly dirty water, I recently returned on Sunday and Tuesday with the hope that the situation had improved. Unfortunately, it was immediately apparent that there has been no meaningful progress in addressing the issue.

The water remains cloudy and murky (See video), raising serious hygiene and safety concerns. As a regular pool user both in Singapore and overseas, I must say that this is one of the worst-maintained pools I have encountered. It is unacceptable for a facility of this nature to be in such a state, and frankly, it is embarrassing that this is in Singapore.

During my recent visit, I attempted a few laps but found it difficult and unsafe, as visibility in the water was reduced to barely two meters ahead. This not only poses a risk to swimmers but also reflects poorly on the standards expected of a SAFRA club.

I urge SAFRA Mount Faber to take immediate and decisive action to improve the maintenance of the pool. Regular and proper cleaning must be enforced without delay to ensure the facility meets basic hygiene and safety standards.

A pretty small mall located at Alexandra area of Singapore, there isn't much here other than some usual chain stores like McDonalds, Starbucks, Cold Storage etc.. but the one thing I have to compliment are the toilets of this mall. I was surprised by how nice the design of the toilets were and it was pretty clean too. 👍✨ 🫧

The Steamed Fish Delights stall really stands out in the food court with its bright setup, and the uncle running the stall is warm and welcoming, always greeting customers with a pleasant smile.
We ordered a set each of the steamed seabass, which comes with a bowl of rice and a choice of veggies or steamed egg. The uncle was very flexible — one set we swapped the rice for extra kailan, and the other we opted for bean sprouts instead. This thoughtful accommodation was much appreciated.
The fish was freshly prepared, tender, and satisfying, with no lingering aftertaste of thirst — a sign of clean and well-balanced cooking.
Overall, it’s a highly recommended spot for a comforting and wholesome meal in a clean, cool, and pleasant environment.

I am an occasional visitor to this "satellite town" shopping mall for many years and I have seen how it has evolved and refreshed itself from time to time over the years. That said, some of its tenants have remained in place since day one attesting to their continued popularity with customers. However, it is quite sad to me that the food court located at its basement level one seems to perpetually struggle - I only wished it was more well patronised and all of the stalls in the food court are taken up.

Hui Shan one of the waiters was so observant and friendly the entire time knowing exactly when we needed a refill, when we needed an extra utensil and joking with us. It made our experience and dinner so enjoyable. We had a similar experience in Bugis Ma maison with another waiter there. Ma Maison is a great place for a family meal, it has great food and soup that brings us back for more all the time!!

The recent revamp from last year is great! This 2 level mall maybe compact in size but it has everything one needs: supermarket, fast food, bubble tea, cafe, coffee joints, hair and nails salons, dry cleaner and etc! Some restaurants have been here since it opened in 1997 like nostalgic Jack’s Place. The new Comic Library by NLB is a wonderful addition to this mall!
Visit here to check on the huge Merlion statue..! Lovely place with  great walkway. One can go up the statue of the Merlion with a nominal entry fee.

It's a park and an Island. It's big....,  so can get lost 😉! You need to be prepared well for a long walk.  Though there are escalators, who knows you might miss a spot...!  Get a map from the entrance, nd make up your mind on the path you would like to take. There r mono rails nd busses inside which is free. There r lot of activities inside for which additional tickets need to be purchased.  You will need at least 5hrs to cover the island in a rush... More time, the more you enjoy...!
Well managed parking lot for pick up of tourists...class apart cleanliness across the island...
Lovely and wonderful place for sight seeing and enjoyment of living in the world
Must know place for all travellers. Though it's confusing but their enough and more signages around to take you there ...

HarbourFront MRT Station is one of Singapore's most convenient and well-connected transport hubs. It provides easy access to VivoCity, Sentosa, and many nearby attractions, making it a popular station for both locals and tourists.
The station is clean, spacious, and well-maintained, with clear signage that makes navigation simple. Despite being busy during weekends and holidays, the crowd flow is generally well managed.
Its direct connection to VivoCity and Sentosa Express makes traveling hassle-free and convenient.
Highly recommended for shopping, sightseeing, and seamless transportation across Singapore.

I’d rate HarbourFront Centre an 8 out of 10. It’s a convenient shopping and transit hub, especially for those heading to Sentosa or taking a ferry. The mall has a decent variety of stores, including fashion, electronics, and daily essentials. Food options are plentiful, with both local and international choices. It’s generally clean and well-maintained, though it can get crowded during peak hours. The layout is straightforward, and it’s directly connected to VivoCity and the MRT. Overall, a practical and enjoyable place for shopping, dining, or travel connections.

Good and interesting place for people to enjoy time with friends and family. Here food court and many other gifts shops are available and apart from that there is availability of ships for easy travel to near islands
Neatly maintained station with connections to all parts of the Singapore from Sentosa island. Shopping mall is also available around the station and it’s a gateway towards Sentosa island.

HarbourFront MRT Station in Singapore is a bustling and well-connected transportation hub that serves as the gateway to Sentosa Island and the HarbourFront Centre. It’s located within the VivoCity shopping mall, making it convenient for travelers to shop or grab a meal before continuing their journey. The station is clean, well-maintained, and efficiently managed. Signage is clear, and the layout makes it easy to navigate between train lines. With its seamless connection to buses, ferries, and the Sentosa Express, it’s an essential and highly functional stop for both tourists and locals.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Had a wonderful time at Ola Beach Club in Sentosa! The beachfront and poolside atmosphere makes it a perfect place to relax, unwind, and enjoy the island vibes.

A special thank you to Chef Vishnu for his warm hospitality and kind hosting. He made our visit even more enjoyable with his attentive service and welcoming attitude.

The food and drinks were great too and we especially enjoyed the pizza, refreshing juices, and delicious ice cream. Everything was fresh, tasty, and perfect for a sunny day by the beach.

Highly recommended for anyone looking for a relaxing beachside dining experience with good food, great service, and beautiful views. Looking forward to visiting again! 🌴☀️🍕🍦🥤

A nice beachside spot with a great view of the sea. The place is family-friendly and even has a pool for kids, which is a nice touch. The DJ plays good music and creates a fun atmosphere. Cocktails are okay, though they could be a bit better. Overall, a good place to relax by the beach.

Really good food and drinks. They have different plates…as share main or share starter. Drinks are really good. Also, amazing beach spot. They have a wee swimming pool if you don’t want to be on the beach. Finally, really good customer service!

My family and I have visited Ola Beach Club several times, and each visit has been truly wonderful and memorable. The food and drinks are consistently excellent, and the ambiance strikes the perfect balance between relaxing and fun.

It’s a fantastic place for both the young and the young at heart to unwind, bond, and spend quality time together. Friends can also enjoy meaningful catch-ups in a vibrant yet laid-back setting.

For the kids, the swimming pool, sand play area, and banana boat ride are definite highlights, making the experience even more enjoyable for the whole family. Totally recommend this place!
Food were pretty good, we enjoyed our food! The pacing of the dishes could be better, my children have almost finished their food before ours were served. We enjoyed the atmosphere and the service!

VivoCity is a mega mall in the south end of Singapore. With a rooftop deck (public access), it offers spectacular views of Sentosa, HarbourFront Centre, including the ferry terminal. You also get to view the PSA Brani Terminal, with the large cranes looking like jurassic monsters. It goes get very crowded over the weekends, so just be patient, move around casually, and have a chilled out time.
Just going up to rooftop, you can find a good view point! And also food plaza in there

A great shopping mall 2 stations. 1 connecting to sentosa and the other with harbor front. This is the entry point to sentosa and there is a boardwalk across the island. You could also take the sentosa express.
Very busy malls, lots of dining options and shops
Rw8 now accept visa / ezlink cards only , 1 dollar per person  , bus will make a round turn route before entering the island and align at rws drop off point entrance ( need to walk zera crossing )

At the southern end of the MRT North-South Line, this is the station that connects you to the Marina South Pier for ferries to the southern islands of Singapore and Bintan and Batam Islands in Indonesia. It is also the connecting station for the seaside promenade where you can catch spectacular views of the bustling Singapore port or where you can charter a fishing boat.

The station itself is beautifully decorated with a very interesting installation of tiles engraved with pictures and artwork.

Sinay says:

The station has only very limited facilities so if you need food, drinks and snacks items, please head to the South Pier.

Like everywhere in Singapore, the MRT Station is spotlessly clean.

Love how they added mirrors. Really meaningful initiative for dancers to use, really wish they can add these Mirror Moves to other MRT stations in Singapore especially some TEL stations, where space is a plenty

the best way access to the city affordable budget friendly for seafarers.This railway station has a mission that automatically dispenses juice for two dollars This is a good place for sailors to relax after shopping and heading back to the ship.
Female toilet so dirty!! So so so many cockroaches. Really unacceptable given it is a MRT and right near cruise centre.

This is the southern-most terminal station of the North-Sputh line. Its exits lead to the Marina South Pier ( exit B) and the Marina Cruise Centre (exit A), making it very convenient for visitors to the southern islands and the regional Cruise Centre. There are sheltered walkways linking the exits to either the pier or the cruise centre.

I alighted at this bus interchange (the newer one as opposed to the older "Temporary" one) getting off Bus number 961 from Hillview where I live. I arrived here to visit Causeway Point that is just "above and next to" this bus interchange. Unlike the older "Temporary" bus interchange, this one is new and nicer in terms of comfort as it is air-conditioned and access to Causeway Point is by a convenient set of escalators.

The bus driver seemed half asleep; he was driving incredibly slowly. A bus is meant to get people to their destination quickly, not to make them late! This is terrible! Does he not know how to drive a bus, or is he just sleepy?

so much space, which is good. my only complaint is the main passenger alighting area .. where it can be quite a distance to walk all the way towards the direction of the MRT Station.

there's also this food stall simply named "Nasi Padang" (01-24) which serves surprisingly good tasting malay food at decent prices.

Bus interchange walking distance to Woodlands MRT Causeway Point Civic Centre with plenty of dinning shopping banking supermarkets library cinemas entertainments groceries sundries postage services community centre and amenities nearby. Bus services including Direct express buses to Sengkang Tempines in addition to neighbourhood heartland of Woodlands Marsiling Admiralty Kranji Sembawang Yishun Khatib even Ang Mo Kio and Orchard Road. Tidy orderly relaxing atmosphere cosy comfy welcoming ambience for romantic intimates groups students expats locals visitors and families.

Take bus 950 at woodlands temporary bus interchange from Singapore to reach Malaysia customs directly. Conveniently located beside woodlands MRT. As it is the first bus stop, ensures you'll get on the bus rather than potentially miss it when it's crowded subsequently.

Kranji MRT station is a convenient and well-maintained transit point that serves commuters traveling between northern Singapore and the rest of Singapore. The station is especially valued for its practical layout, clean surroundings, and smooth passenger flow, making daily travel comfortable and efficient for both residents and visitors.
One of the station’s strongest advantages is its accessibility. It is strategically located near important facilities, checkpoints, and industrial areas, making it a key stop for workers, students, and travelers. Signage inside the station is clear and easy to follow, which helps first-time users navigate without confusion. Ticketing machines, gantries, and platforms are arranged logically, so even during peak hours movement remains relatively smooth and organized.
Passengers often appreciate the station’s cleanliness and orderly environment. The platforms are spacious and well-ventilated, and safety markings are clearly visible. Staff presence and surveillance systems contribute to a secure atmosphere, giving commuters peace of mind while waiting for trains. The surrounding area is also straightforward to navigate, with bus connections and taxi points located nearby for easy transfers.
Another positive aspect is the calm and less crowded feel compared to major interchange stations. Many commuters find this refreshing, especially during morning and evening travel, as it allows for a more relaxed commuting experience. The station’s simple design and functional layout reflect efficiency rather than complexity, which many users prefer.
Overall, Kranji MRT station is a reliable, commuter-friendly transit stop that offers convenience, cleanliness, and accessibility. While it may not be the busiest or most glamorous station, its practicality and smooth operations make it an important and appreciated part of Singapore’s public transport network.

I was lost at Kranji mrt stn.
Assistant Station Manager Syahirah assisted me with finding my direction.  She showed an exemplary customer service attitude.  SMRT is lucky to have her.  Great job Syahirah..!!!  👍🏾👍🏾👍🏾
On 6 November 2025 I spotted nice sunrise kranji mrt kranji area good place Sunrise

A Convenient place for friends to gather and catch a bus to JB. Both  SBS and CW services are available, pay only $2+  This is also the station for start of the Rail Corridor North trail. There’s a supermart and food stalls for refreshments.

If you intend to alight at Woodlands Train Checkpoint, your journey will not be suspended like 160, 170 at Woodlands Checkpoint. Boarding any SBS Transit or SMRT bus at Woodlands Checkpoint will become a transfer or New Ride to those bus services at checkpoint.

Internet, chatgbt, google, Ai and the pervasive mobile devices may have changed the way we seek answers. But a library is where serious learnings and discoveries are found.

They are depository of knowledge, curated and preserved. The Central Library is that - and more.

There are librarians dedicated to the preservation of knowledge and their proliferation. Here, they are not stuffy and grumpy matrons. They are professionals and helpful in a way we boomers never knew.

Tell them a hypothesis and they can help you turn an idea into blueprint. Need to fill a gap in citations, they can find you a landfill.

History is our heritage. Lessons learned to inform us. Latest technology is flashy but libraries tell us how they came about.

The Central Library is more than a meeting place for varsity students. Here is where a thousand nuggets of wisdom and knowledge exists - waiting for those with inquiring minds to be transformed into “professors.”

lots of study spots! most of the library is for nus students/staff only as there are gantries at the entrance which mainly open with student/staff passes

most study spots have charging spots, each level has a bathroom and water dispensers. there's also food options at level 1 and a bookstore with essentials

only complaint is that the wifi can be quite slow/spotty at times. also level 6 study area can get quite warm sometimes when its busy, like the ac isnt strong enough :")))

I spend a lot of time in this library when I did my undergrad and post grad studies. It has many books which were not available in other libraries. I did join as a corporate member for a few years too.
Beautiful and modern library with a very rich collection of books and ample seating space.

I entered as an alumnus. I needed an NUS alumnus lifelong email address and also had to download and install the UNivUS app, which generates the QR code for me to enter the library. There was mobile data signal at the registration counter therefore I needed to log into NUG Guest wifi. But the NUS Guest wifi needed an OTP which required mobile signal! Therefore I needed to go back and forth between the Forum (mobile signal to get OTP) and library registration desk (for wifi). Altogether, it took me perhaps half an hour to sort it a out and finally enter the library. Future trips should be straightforward I hope!!! General stacks on 5th floor. Photocopier is on 5th floor but in the Chinese SEA Collection. Use your EZ-link card. $0.03 per A4 - great value!  :-)  The facilities are lovely -wow... compared to 1990s! Love the place. The Coop had lots of cute merch too. Ah... to be a Gen Z or Gen Alpha... 😄😄😄
Usually go there for table tennis. Only Table 2, 3 and 4 are good for match play. Other tables have smaller spacing and suitable only for training.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Jurong West Swimming Complex — Hidden Gem Alert

Best Sunday experience, hands down. Not crazy crowded, which is a rare win in Singapore. The lap pool is bliss—can actually swim freely without dodging elbows. Bonus: it’s shaded from the sun, so no need to fry while doing laps.

Even under CAT1 lightning alerts, you can still swim (huge plus). The kids’ pool is a comfortable 0.9m depth, perfect for little ones. And yes—warm showers in the children’s facilities. Game changer.

There’s a decent water park setup for kids and a surprisingly long water slide that’s way more fun than expected. Honestly, this place is the most underrated swimming complex in Singapore. Total hidden gem. Don’t sleep on it.

The Jurong West ActiveSG was very Beautiful specially when it is Night time... It is very helpful for people of all ages specially the Older & Kids to do various kinds of Workout & Physical Activities...

As a visitor I got the Chance to visit it both Night & in the Morning... the Experience was very good

A multi-story complex consisting of a stadium, swimming pool with water feature like slides and lazy river. The. Complex also houses Gymnasium, child care, tuition center, dance hall, cafe.....etc.
A complex cater to all age group. You can literally spent a whole day here. Car park is at complex ground level so no worries even if it rain.
As of 23 April 2022, the children play pool is still closed due to some maintenance works

Since NEA says there’s nothing wrong with smoking when it’s not a covered space, I can only resort to Google rating here. Can’t put zero. Skip this swimming pool if you bring your kids or also treasure your own life and respiratory condition, cuz while you wait on them in swim class, you can definitely I hale their and hand smoke upwards .

Nice and serene place. Place is rather dated but well maintained, though it does show its age. There is a simple variety of activities that can be done here, though it is a common place for functions and meetings.

Civil Service Club Bukit Batok swimming pool.

A variety of fun and relaxing water activities, but access is limited to members and their guests.

The pool area includes a large children's pool, and a wave pool that starts at 10am and runs for 5 minutes every hour—check the website for the schedule. For $3 in cash, you can rent a float, which is required for the towel slide.

The lazy river provides a nice spot to lounge, while kids can enjoy the water playground and a simple water slide on the second level. Swimmers also have access to a lap pool located upstairs.

Hot water showers, hair dryer, and locker available.
Service was good, portion was really big. Ordered 2 small noodles, small veg, small sweet and sour pork and small tofu for 4 people and we can't finish half the amount of food.

I love this club as it’s a really huge place for families gathering. There are a few swimming pool cater for kids and adults. The awesome wave pool is well liked by the members.

The bathroom is also well designed and provides much comfort for members.

The whole premise is clean and greenery!
vc

Great environment, hillside club with surrounding nature scenery.
Pool facilities and amenities are club standard.
Slide pool is down for maintenance indefinitely.
Wave pool starts from 10am every 1hr intervals or at such times where the personnel deem appropriate.

Lifeguard stationed at wave pool has a working fan, but subjected to availability of sunlight on solar panel or has restricted power source?

Guest pay $2 entrance. Changing room locker $1.

🌿Choa Chu Kang Park is situated opposite Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange along Choa Chu Kang Drive.
🚶‍➡️🏃‍➡️It's a community garden within the Choa Chu Kang Estate where residents gather for morning workout, walk, jog and do some gardening.
🌿This green oasis offers a relief to the hectic city pace.
🛝🐕‍🦺🍆Like many community gardens, CCK Park too have its fair share of Playground, Fitness Corners, Dog Run, Allotment Garden, Open Lawn and Amphitheatre.
🛼🛹🌶️Besides these regular features, CCK Park have a Skate Plaza and a Community Garden.
🍂Also, somewhat hidden from view, is an Old Tomb Trail.
🚍How to get here:
Exit from Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange to Choa Chu Kang Drive.
Cross over the road.
Park is next to Shell Station.
Some parts under remodeling.  Over all still good for visiting. Quite, shady with Macedonia just outside SAFRA, and walking distance to  Lot 1 and CCK MRT.

The park is clean, well-maintained, and very peaceful. It has lush green lawns, shaded walking paths, and plenty of seating areas. The atmosphere is refreshing and ideal for morning walks, yoga, or spending relaxed time with family and children. Overall, it’s a great place for fitness, leisure, and connecting with nature.

Can an urban park be a space for relaxation, education and creativity? Can it become a unique attraction of the region? Or maybe you are looking for a business idea and want to create an outdoor eductation center? Bet on an interactive park of experiences!

Families are increasingly looking for unusual forms of recreation. When traveling on a trip, they want to not only visit the beautiful old town or museum, but also to provide their children interesting experiences, combined with education. Experience Park is a place where children will not be bored!

The open-air museum will provide great entertainment and learning through play. You can touch each exhibit, run it and learn about the laws of nature through the individual experimentation.

Until now, city parks were equipped with benches, fountains, and nature monuments. Recently, you can also work out in the outdoor gym. It is worth to equip this space with educational devices and in addition to muscle training, practice the mind as well! We suggest creating an educational path, which may consist of several thematic zones, located in different parts of the park.

Small park with lots of ants, wouldnt recommend to come for a stroll either day/night. Most people come here for Mr Teh Tarik Cartel that's located inside the park itself (this would definitely recommend).

Hillion Mall is a lively suburban mall with a very wide variety of shops, dining options, and everyday services. One of its biggest strengths is the excellent connectivity - it is located right beside Bukit Panjang MRT, LRT, and the bus interchange, making it extremely convenient to visit even during rainy weather

The mall itself has quite a good mix of retail stores, cafes, bubble tea shops, supermarkets, and family-friendly spaces. I especially enjoyed exploring the Kopitiam Food Hall, which has a surprisingly wide range of food stalls and a lively atmosphere during meal times. There are also entertainment and arcade areas that make the mall feel more vibrant for families and students

The only downside is that the layout can feel a little confusing at times, especially for first-time visitors, as some sections and escalators are not very intuitive to navigate
My first time visiting this mall. Many shops and eateries in this mall. Will come again to try other food in this mall

From the looks of it, the area seems pretty accessible. There's a paved pathway leading to the seating area under the red archway, and it looks like there's enough space to maneuver a wheelchair around the tables. Are you planning to visit with someone who uses a wheelchair, or were you just curious about accessibility?

This mall is super convenient because it’s an integrated hub with the MRT, LRT, and bus interchange all in one spot. The NTUC here is a major highlight because it’s huge and stays open late. The layout is a bit vertical, so you might spend a bit of time on escalators, but it’s a very functional place for a quick meal or grocery run.

Updating my review after visiting Hillion Mall again today. Unfortunately, the toilet condition has become even worse instead of improving.

One of the cubicles I entered had poop stains and splashes all over the toilet bowl and seat, making it extremely unpleasant and unhygienic to use. The basin area was also dirty and poorly maintained. Overall cleanliness was honestly quite disgusting.

I understand some users may behave irresponsibly, but proper cleaning and maintenance by the mall management is equally important. For a shopping mall, basic toilet hygiene standards should be maintained consistently. Hope management will seriously address this ongoing issue soon.

————————————————————————————-

Unacceptable hygiene standards at Hillion Mall. The toilets are consistently dirty, and overall cleanliness is clearly not being properly maintained. This appears to be an ongoing issue rather than a one-off incident. For a shopping mall, basic hygiene should be a minimum standard. Management needs to address this seriously and improve maintenance immediately.

Outstanding library. Convenient location (OPP Science Centre) and many floors or materials. Their program room is great for kid activities.

Surprisingly, toilets are badly maintained :(
Nice, quiet and with lots of books. Highly recommended library of you love reading!

Amazing! Just no words to say. It was my first time to the Jurong regional library and the very scale of it took me in awe. So many collections of different books and so many different levels to go and explore! They have a community space for events and workshops , then there is a cafe and bakery inside with good food options. There is so many different things to explore! I couldn’t even finish going through all the sections! I will definitely go there again soon. The place is also located very close to the MRT  station and the Jurong east bus terminus . It has a sheltered walkway so we could easily reach the place even though it was raining. Many people from all different age groups were there. There is study tables with charging points. For some of the study tables you need to book in advance but for the rest you can always find and empty space. There are DVDs and other stuff as well. If you have to borrow books you need to register , but I just went for reading so I didn’t have to register anything. The library closes at 9pm and is open all days of the week. They will announce over the mike before the closing time so you have time to pack your stuff. I especially liked the photography section and farming section . There’s a separate magazine section and newspaper section as well. Different languages from Tamil to Malay to Korean you will find in the library .

This library was visited few times in the span of 15 years. Still impressed by its cleanliness, calm and  peaceful atmosphere in addition to the silent air, the display of new arrivals also catch my eyes. Always full of study-loaded students and other book-lovers, you will still find a seat for enjoying some books or surfing internet on the provided PC's as the library is very spacious. Remarkably, there is a vast collection of books.

Spacious JE Centre Library with many seats availability. Cool Air-Cond Environment, Front Welcoming Display, Information,  Photo Wall Taking, Green Wall Digital Huge Screen & Clean well maintained Toilet. Great Place for Reading, Desktop PC Computer Internet access & comfy sofas. Wonderful!👏🇸🇬

The aircon is always not on. Only the aircon at the guard area is on. The only public area which is cooling is the big fan which is turned on at lobby area. The rest of the area is too hot especially during the hot weather season. Can the management do something about it? Started sweating immediately when stepped into the building.

I stay opposite Yishun SAFRA for most of my life so it’s my first time stepping into Tampines SAFRA. It does look a lil old but the stores inside are 10 times better than Yishun’s. The two stores (KEK and Taira Pastry) I’ve visited accept HTNS membership for 15% too. Main reason why I visited was the Pork Liver in KEK, it was really spot on

Great place for family activities,  food and of course to maximize my NSman benefits. Decent variety of food outlets. I usually send my kids for classes and get my prata fix at Springleaf Prata. Will miss this place when they move to Bayshore next time.

Pretty run down and the directional signs are not clear enough to point you to the badminton halls. The door of the badminton hall is also slightly faulty. Hope the management can upgrade the place soon.
KEK restaurant serves up tasty Chinese meals at trafobanle priced.

Tampines MRT Station (EW2/DT32) is a major, highly integrated transit hub that serves as the primary gateway to Singapore’s bustling East. Combining an elevated station on the East West Line with an underground station on the Downtown Line, this massive interchange anchors the entire regional center and seamlessly processes hundreds of thousands of commuters daily.
The station is uniquely split into two distinct structures due to the timeline of its development. While the East West Line sits high above the ground with its iconic, curved white barrel roof, the modern Downtown Line section is fully subterranean. The two sectors are connected via a dedicated, sheltered public walkway that runs alongside the bustling town center, making transfers straightforward and highly integrated with the surrounding streetscape.
Epicenter of Retail & Lifestyle: The station exits open up directly into the heart of Tampines Central, offering immediate, un-sheltered steps to three massive malls—Tampines Mall, Century Square, and Tampines 1—as well as the nearby Tampines Bus Interchange.
Civic Access: Provides a quick, direct pathway to Our Tampines Hub, the nation’s largest integrated community and sports complex.
Strategic Regional Gateway: Offers effortless transit lines for residents traveling downtown, workers heading to Changi Business Park, or adventurers journeying out toward the coastal bus connections at Tampines East and Changi Village.

Nice and clean station, very busy all the time. Use for daily commute to work. Also an interchange to connect to other train line and bus services.
Took the train early this morning, train service every 4/5 minutes and fairly crowded.
To me, can't live without the train, it gets you to where you want to go no time, unless theres a breakdown...keep up the good work..MRT/SMRT...

This station is the ultimate example of urban connectivity. It's a big intersection where the Downtown Line (DTL/Blue) and the East-West Line (EWL/Green) meet up, making it the best way to get around the "Heartland" part of Singapore.

📍 It's a ​Retail Paradise! You are literally dropped into the middle of three major malls: Tampines Mall, Century Square, and Tampines 1. If you need tech, fashion, or a quick Uniqlo run without Orchard Road crowds, this is the spot.

​A 5-minute walk brings me to Our Tampines Hub, Singapore’s largest integrated community and sports center. It’s great for seeing local life, catching a free movie screening, or visiting the stunning regional library.
​And it's only a few stops from Changi Airport via the East-West Line.

Pro-Tips : ​Be aware that switching between the Blue and Green lines requires exiting the fare gates and walking through a sheltered outdoor walkway (Tampines Mall side). Don't worry about extra costs—as long as you tap back in within 15 minutes, it counts as a single journey!

For ​Foodie, head to the nearby coffee shops or the basement food courts in the malls for authentic local fare like Laksa or Hokkien Mee.

It’s busy, it’s bustling, and it’s the perfect place to experience the "real" Eastern Singapore lifestyle outside the tourist bubble of Marina Bay!

I would like to lodge a complaint regarding an unpleasant incident that happened recently.

I was simply sitting at a corner of the stairwell to take a short rest while waiting for someone. Due to my asthma, I often feel tired after walking and needed a brief moment to recover. The stairwell was not in use at that time, and I was not obstructing anyone from passing through.

However, a worker came up to me and told me that I was not allowed to sit there. I explained that I was only resting for a while, but he insisted and asked me to leave. In fact, he told me twice to move away, which made me feel very uncomfortable and disrespected.

I hope the management can look into this matter, as I believe I was not causing any inconvenience or safety concern. It is important that staff members show understanding and empathy, especially towards individuals with health conditions.

Thank you for your attention. I look forward to your reply.

The staff is rude and disrespectful.

I was rushing to work , and realised my card didn’t have enough balance to pass the gantry , when I went to the cash top up machine , this staff was tending to it as cash jammed the machine. I waited for a while, and politely asked roughly how long it would take to fix the issue . She snapped at me and said just wait, there’s money stuck in the machine, then after turning back to attend to the machine, she turned around again to tell me that I could go to the counter to top up if I was in a rush, seconds after I left the to go to the counter, he fixed the machine and left. So she knowingly wasted my time and asked me to go queue elsewhere when she was already about the fix the machine. The staff was an Indian adult female .

Tampines MRT is more than just a transit stop; it’s a powerhouse of connectivity that serves as the primary gateway for residents and visitors in the East. As an interchange for both the East-West Line (EW2) and the Downtown Line (DT32), its convenience is hard to beat.

Why it’s a Top-Tier Station:

• Unbeatable Connectivity: It serves thousands of residents daily, providing a seamless link between the residential heartlands of Tampines and the rest of Singapore. Whether you're heading to the city for work or Changi Airport for a flight, it's the central nervous system of the town.

• Direct Mall Access: This is a shopper’s dream. The station offers direct, sheltered access to three major shopping malls: Tampines 1, Tampines Mall, and Century Square. You can transition from the train platform to your favorite retail stores or restaurants in minutes without ever worrying about the rain.

• In-Station Conveniences: Within the station itself, there are quite a number of shop options. From a quick grab-and-go snack or a baker's treat, to ATMs and essential service kiosks, you can settle your errands.

• Walking Distance to Bus Interchange: The station is just a short, sheltered walking distance (approx. 2-4 minutes) to the Tampines Bus Interchange. This makes transferring between the rail network and the extensive bus feeder services incredibly efficient.

💡 Pro Tip for Commuters:

If you are transferring between the East-West and Downtown lines, do note that it is an "out-of-station" transfer. You’ll need to tap out and walk through a sheltered public walkway to reach the other line—don't worry, as long as you tap back in within 15 minutes, you won't be charged a new boarding fee!

✔️👍 Review: Tampines North MRT Station (CR6) is an upcoming underground station on the Cross Island Line (CRL).

Located diagonally across from the Sunray building, the future Tampines North MRT Station (CR6) is currently under construction and is projected to open in 2030, in four years. Residents of Tampines North, especially those at Tampines GreenView. GreenGlades and Green Crest, will benefit from convenient travel options to various parts of Singapore such as:

A. CONNECTING YOU ACROSS THE ISLAND

B. Facilitating quicker crossings from Bright Hill to Punggol and Changi!

C. Connected to four major interchanges, allowing easy travel to Bright Hill, Ang Mo Kio, Hougang, and Pasir Ris.

Phase 1, scheduled to open in 2030, will be situated near Tampines Avenue 11 and Tampines Avenue 6. It will include an Integrated Transport Hub (ITH) combining rail, bus services, and amenities like a hawker centre and community club.

Looking forward to this opening soon!

See the attached photos and video for your information.

Have a pleasant day!

Chris
31 Jan 2026

The malls are within walking distance but you have to walk a bit for interchange and downtown line (it’s not that bad but i would like it if there were to link the 2 stations via an underpass underground)
I've been living here for 15 years now. The only diff from other mrt stations is the mailbox. Very good when I'm sending letters overseas to my relatives. Not to mention the stuff to do nearby
