result.name: Munchi Pancakes @ Our Tampines Hub Hawker Centre

reviews.text:
Pancake quality: Freshly made at the right texture, not too dry nor hard.
Price: Reasonable.
Store Cleanliness: Good.
Staff: Helpful, patience and attentive.

Tried & Recommend:
- Red Bean filling with Green Tea pancake (note: regular red bean not Azuki from Japan)
- Peanuts filling with Green Tea pancake
- Black Sesame filling with Charcoal pancake
- Hazelnut filling with Charcoal pancake
- Mini munchies all cheese flavours e.g. strawberry, blueberry, Oreo, Belgian chocolate and many more.

My first time buying from this outlet. I go for Munchi Red Bean instead of the traditional one called Ming Jiang Kueh.  This round piece of Munchi is generously filled with red bean paste and it's still soft and jiggly even after I ate it like 3 hours later!!  Many kinds of flavours. Belgian chocolate filling is highly recommended. I like to chill this chocolate ones🩷

I visited this stall today and bought some min jiang kueh. The display food items looked yummy and tasty. The staffs were friendly and nice.

Overall, a great experience.

If you have a craving for traditional Min Jiang Kueh with a modern twist, Munchi Pancakes at the Our Tampines Hub (OTH) Hawker Centre is a must-visit. I dropped by around noon, and the experience was smooth and efficient.

The Queue & Service

Visiting at the peak of lunch hour, I encountered a slight queue of about 5 people in front of me. Despite the crowd, the service was brisk, and I didn't have to wait long at all to place my order.

The Food & Variety

The first thing you'll notice is the incredible variety of choices. They've truly elevated the humble pancake with a mix of traditional and adventurous fillings.

• The Classic Favorite: I absolutely love the peanut ones. The filling is fragrant, crunchy, and generous, providing that perfect nostalgic bite.

• The Crust Options: A unique feature here is the choice of skins. They offer charcoal pancakes (and even matcha ones) for an extra 20 cents per piece. The charcoal skin gives it a sleek look and a slightly different texture that pairs beautifully with the sweeter fillings.

• Unique Flavors: Beyond the usuals, they have other unique flavors like Belgian Chocolate, Thai Milk Tea, and Biscoff, making it easy to try something new every time you visit.

Verdict: A fantastic spot for a quick snack or dessert. The ability to customize your pancake skin and the wide array of flavors make it stand out from your typical hawker stall.

Camy was super friendly and patient while we were deciding which Munchi Pancake flavours to get. It was our first time here and there were so many flavours to choose from! 😊

The pancakes were freshly made and tasted really good. Great service and we’ll definitely be back to try the other flavours!

result.name: Our Tampines Hub ActiveSG Community Auditorium

reviews.text: Our Tampines Hub community auditorium is one of those spaces that really shows how well-designed public facilities can be. It’s a modern, air-conditioned, state-of-the-art setup that works brilliantly as both a sports venue and an event space. Most days, it functions as a multipurpose sports hall with plenty of courts for badminton and table tennis, and the layout feels spacious, clean, and thoughtfully planned. What I really like is how easily it transforms into a full-fledged auditorium for community events — the convertible seating is smart, practical, and even feels like a sustainable design choice rather than a compromise. Whether you’re there to play a game, watch an event, or attend a community program, the space adapts effortlessly. The atmosphere is lively and welcoming, which makes it feel like a true community hub rather than just another sports complex. The only real downside is the popularity — getting a court can be quite challenging because so many people want to use the facilities. That said, it’s a good problem to have and speaks volumes about how well-loved and useful this place is.
First time visited OTH Tampines gym. The “security guy” Steven and gate keeper were super unfriendly towards gym goers. Nice facility but poor customer service.

The Community Auditorium is located on the 2nd floor of the OTH. Spacious, air-conditioned, a really nice place to play badminton and table tennis. The only downside is the difficulty in securing the slots during peak hours.

Sport activities, swimming, Gym, Stadium,  Football, Hockey, Badminton,  Table Tennis, indoor jogging track. Food court, Fast Food, Restaurant. Groceries, NTUC. National Library for Books. Starbucks. Plenty of parking lots. Everything is conveniently there. Tampines Residents are so blessed & fortunate to have these all in one facility hub! I'm from Serangoon Central.

Now it's probably less popular because most MRTs has modern-styled mall now, while Toa Pa Yoh is still the open air shopping area with small shops that come with all the mess, crowds under the mercy of Singapore's humid weather.

It's still nostalgic to visit, with its prominent yellow colors. All the shops and facilities mentioned above are just some walking distance away, including various buses to go anywhere in the country.

Toa Payoh is a vibrant, all-in-one hub that perfectly blends convenience with local charm. Everything is within reach, from the seamless MRT connectivity to the bustling bus interchange and diverse shops. Its legendary food scene and abundant amenities make it a truly amazing, self-contained jewel.

It is one of the crowded and busiest places in Singapore although it is weekdays. Many shops and restaurants are located along the premises    selling at the cheapest price you never get it from anywhere else.

🟨 I live in Toa Payoh Estate, and Toa Payoh MRT train station is just 2 bus stops away from my block.

🟨 Toa Payoh Station consists of 2 levels
***************************************
🟦( ✅️gantry gates & ✅️ ticketing concourse, B1 level)

🔴 B2 level = Train platform
***********************************

🔴 Toa Payoh MRT Station sits on the North South Line ( aka NS line). ✌️The station was established in 1988. I know because I live here.

🔴 The train from here goes up north to Woodlands side or south to Marina Bay side.

If you’re curious about what shopping in Singapore might have felt like before all the shiny, towering megamalls took over, Toa Payoh Mall is honestly a pretty fun little time capsule to walk through. It’s laid out more like a long sheltered walkway, with rows of small shops lining both sides, similar to the kind you’d find under older apartment blocks. The whole place feels refreshingly down-to-earth and very local, which gives it a charm that’s hard to find in newer malls.

There are roughly 50 shops packed into this stretch, and they sell a surprisingly wide range of things. You’ll find bags, clothes, electronic accessories, little gadget shops, salons, massage centres, and even a few hawker-style food stalls tucked in between. It’s the kind of place where you can wander without a strict shopping list and still walk out with a couple of random, useful finds.

One of the biggest highlights here is the pricing. Most items are noticeably cheaper than what you’d pay in more modern malls, though many of the brands are lesser-known or unbranded. Still, if you’re not too picky about labels and just want good deals and a nostalgic shopping experience, this place really delivers.

Overall, Toa Payoh Mall is a charming throwback that offers affordable finds, local character, and a refreshing break from Singapore’s usual mall scene.

result.name: MindChamps MindSpace @ Toa Payoh Central | After School Student Care for P1-P5 | MindChamps Enrichment for P1-P5

reviews.text:
We are extremely disappointed and frustrated with our experience at Mindspace TPY.

Firstly, the way basic needs are handled is unacceptable. The children’s water intake appears to be deliberately controlled to reduce toilet trips, and they are asked to wait until someone is available to bring them to the toilet, instead of being allowed to go when needed. This is not only unreasonable but inhumane for young children.

Safety is also a serious concern. The toilet is located outside the centre, yet supervision practices are questionable. We have personally observed a male trainer bringing a young female student to the toilet, which is highly concerning. In other instances, a female trainer simply waits outside instead of accompanying the children and ensuring the toilet environment is safe. For young kids, this is completely inadequate — there is no assurance of safety, and any parent would be worried about potential risks.

The teaching environment is equally disappointing. Staff have been heard shouting at children simply for being excited and eager to answer questions. This is not discipline — it is discouraging and creates a negative learning atmosphere.

There is also a lack of basic respect for parents’ time. Despite informing the centre in advance of a 5:30pm pick-up, children are still held back for homework or spelling during dismissal time, leaving caregivers waiting outside unnecessarily.

Communication is poor and unprofessional. Feedback from parents is acknowledged but never followed up on, with no updates or resolution provided.

The centre also fails to deliver on what was promised. We were told they would teach the “art of learning,” something they claim to be known for, yet there has been no evidence of this being implemented throughout Term 1.

On top of that, despite paying for learning materials, we are still asked to provide our own practice books for class use. We were also told that additional worksheets would be given, but we have neither seen them nor has our child received any. This raises serious questions about transparency.

Given the high monthly fees we are paying, the level of service, professionalism, and quality provided is nowhere near acceptable. It simply does not justify the cost.

Overall, this has been a very disappointing experience, and the centre needs to seriously review its practices, safety standards, and accountability.
We had a very insightful and structured orientation. This gave us confidence in how our child will be supported when school starts. We greatly appreciate all the preparation work by the centre!

I recently attended the childcare orientation and found it extremely informative, well-structured, and reassuring. The staff introduced the center’s philosophy, daily routines, safety procedures, and communication practices in a clear and organized way.

Overall, the orientation gave me confidence that my child will be in a nurturing, safe, and engaging environment. The staff came across as warm, knowledgeable, and genuinely invested in the children’s development. I left feeling comfortable and well-informed about the program.

We had a great experience sending our child to Mindchamps and so this was a natural choice. When we went for the open house we were very impressed at how well it was run!

The center is great and well furnished, the staff is friendly, helpful and informative when we asked about the program and schedule. We like how structured everything is and it feels like it I would be a worthy investment.

We are excited for her to be part of this school and her learning journey!

Great experience enrolling my kid to the center, everything is done in a structured manner with lots of support from center personnel, questions were answered along the way. Looking forward for my kid to have happy and fulfilling journey with Mindspace.

To the bus 238 driver at 1.39pm on 15 Feb. You saw me and stopped for 1 sec before driving off..leaving me stranded outside the interchange. I tried all the doors and wasn't able to return inside. So heartless and cruel of you.
Cleanliness of the bus interchange was good. Bus interchange is near to alot of eateries and HDB hub. Good location and very convenient.

Standard bus interchange, however air-conditioned. Well-connected to the HDB Hub which offers plenty of stores. Directions can be a bi confusing for first-timers to this interchange but this interchange is well connected to many parts of Singapore.

Not the first time I had to wait for a bus for more than 30 minutes when it showed about 15 minutes waiting time on the tv display. Please ensure the bus drivers are well aware that they are supposed to be reaching at their designated timing and not make passengers who are going to work or school wait this long. The do not have anything to rush for since they are already at work driving buses but we are rushing for time so that we can be on time for work/school.
The food court located beneath the bus interchange provides a multitude of reasonably priced food options. You can find a diverse range of local delicacies and Asian favorites to choose from.

Everything is fine n good just that not sure why your staff and old uncle like to stand near or below the stairs when we are walking down. And times he is look up. There's alot of student n working adults wearing short skirt in the morning. Appreciate if u can look into it.

Braddell MRT station, one of MRT stations which located in Toa Payoh area, the station is organised. Several facilities like ATM machines, mini market event pet shops on this MRT station. Exit from MRT have several shops, mini market, restaurant including hawkers centre also.

Underground neighbourhood MRT station that’s part of the North-South (Red) Line. Has a 7-11 underground near ticketing exit, as well as ATMs and an AXS machine. You can also top up your EasyLink value in the automated machines here. This station is relatively quiet and is well air-conditioned.
The MRT station is convenient location and wheelchair friendly is also nearby to senior care, market, it is also near to SPH media

I was here today on my way to Toa Payoh Lorong 4 - my MRT journey began at Hillview MRT Station and I changed to the North East Line at Newton MRT Station. It was good to see upgrading works at Braddell MRT Station - with the installation of escalators at it various exits. For me, this MRT Station is a convenient public transport point for the nearby Toa Payoh Lorong 4 area where there are a lot good food choices.

Very very informative and engaging session! Trainer was so helpful and patient with all the questions asked by the participants. She has a passion and it’s quite contagious as it makes me so excited to teach my children using Jolly Phonics!

The Jolly Phonics and Grammer course organized by September 21 exceeded my expectations. Khushbu is very experienced and passionate about Jolly Phonics. She shared invaluable experiences during the training and provided ample hands on activities so it’s easy to understand what Jolly Phonics is about. The course is very organized and the training materials are informative. Great discounts on related products offered to participants too. I would highly recommend this course to anyone who wish to gain a better insight to Jolly Phonics and support your child’s development at home. And not forgetting, September 21 offers a great range of educational products and courses too!

I've recently attended the Jolly Phonic's Literacy Garden by Victoria Carlton. Her explanations were very concise, clear and easy to understand. She gave many examples and ideas for us to teach children on Jolly Phonics and Grammar. We also had break out group sessions where we had a chance to be hands-on on different activities. Chew Yeh and Andrew were also very helpful during the entire session. Overall, the workshop was very extensive, enjoyable, and I am so glad to have attended it. I can't wait to try out all the activities with my students. Thumbs up!

This course has helped me to understand better the methodology of Jolly Phonics. It was quite an eye opening experience for me. The training was fun & engaging. Khushbu is a passionate & inspiring Teacher Trainer of Jolly Phonics.  May she continue to be authentic & inspiring in all the courses that she conducts in the future. I wish her success!

Lesson is very interesting and understandable. The downside is that they took almost 1/2 of the session to promote their material. Honestly some of materials are good for lesson plans and teaching the kids. The rest might not be that necessary. Overall, was a good beginner course.

result.name: Toa Payoh West Market & Food Centre

reviews.text: Love the food there. Old school taste. But due to the increase of cost, some sellers have to reduce the portion, which we fully understand. The table arrangement are close therefore may consider to have more space for those disabled person on wheelchair.

Come here for breakfast frequently before going to work. No problem getting a table. There are enough options for breakfast. Most stalls would be closed on Mondays. I particularly like Ching Ching Coffee Stall. Cofee and service are good.

A heartland classic. With a wet market below & hawker stalls upstairs, it still feels very much like old-school Singapore. Best visited in the morning for breakfast or lunch, this simple, no-frills place holds plenty of neighbourhood charm & memories.

Recently renovated Market & Food Centre … So everything looks nice! Came Monday but only 4 stores open. Came today (unfinished business) to try this Curry Chee Cheong Fun. I like it but different ppl different taste. 3 of us seniors finished our bowl. Hope you like it too.

convenient amenities of for food o 2nd level and wet market on ground level. after the wet marketing, we could take a breather upstairs at the food court for our brunch. lots of fantastic traditional food sold by first generation hawkers. one of the tofu, chicken rice, Teochew kueh tao huey bean curd.

Just have our dinner here at Shampoo Food Centre. Here you have a wide varieties of food to suit everybody...just like any food court. We have fried hokkien noodles from this Singapore Fried Hokkien Noodles. Here you must prepare to queue for it cos it is always long queue. However, guarantee you will love it hence dunt mind to wait in the queue for it. Whenever we come here is alway must eat this fried hokkien noodles without fail. ( See attached Photo) In addition, it is also a must have this egg oyster dish from this Just Heng Fried Oyster stall. Previously, was run by a old uncle who passed down his trade to his daughter in law who has learnt the trade from him. She is equally good as her father in law who of course is the best. (Photo attached) There is also this Nadi Lemak stall here who sell a good NasiLemak with wide range of dishes to go with it besides it's normal ikan billis with a good taste of it's chilli. Of course you can end your dinner with some cold desserts such as ice kachang, red beans soup, green beans soup, and of course there is also a Chendol store here that sell solely Chendol. You guys must come here to try the good, scrumtious and mouth watering food here. You sure will enjoy it....

This is a stall selling Fish Soup Noddle. Each time I visit, there is always more than 10 pax in the queue. Kindly note that the stall opens 0900-1400hrs or sold out which ever comes first. Also, it is closed on Monday, Wednesday and Friday.

Today, I queued for 30 minutes, sat down to enjoy the delicious soup noodles and the stall shouted out Sold Out at 1230hrs.

This place gave a retro atmosphere. Sitting down for a meal bring black memories of childhood days.

The food centre is not huge yet it compasses a large variety of food selection.

Hokkein fried mee, fish slice soup / porridge, mix pork organs soup, chicken rice, duck rice,laksa, lor mee, sugar cane drink, chendol and etc.

Another over-rated food centre. Contrary to many reviews posted here, I have yet to have something decent at this food centre. The only reason I come here is because the wanton mee that I use to eat while growing up has relocated here. And this wanton mee is not spectacular. It is an acquired taste. The chicken rice here is underwhelming. I have tried 2 stalls here and I can say that I will never ever eat chicken rice here again. Parking here is also a pain. Lots in front of the food centre is never available. It might be best to park at the back around the ntuc area and make your own way here.

This market is a treasure trove of authentic local fare, brimming with vibrant, down-to-earth charm. Two standout gems are Xin Ji Congee and its adjacent soup stall, which have become my go-to for hearty, flavorful meals. I’ve visited the market twice specifically to savor Xin Ji’s congee, which boasts an exceptional wok hei that elevates its taste to new heights. The pork stomach soup is a steal at S$5, generously portioned with fresh ingredients, and comes with the added perk of free soup refills, courtesy of the friendly owner. Completing the culinary experience is the nearby chendol shop—with a concise 4-item menu that delivers nothing but perfectly executed, satisfying treats.

Very well connected and sheltered bus stop, that brings you to the neighboring spaces, malls, and train station. It's a no brainer orchard road bus stop, that's right in front of Tangs. If you're passing through this bus stop, there's nothing much to worry about, whether you need accessibility features or otherwise.
Beautiful and old but historical shopping mall. Have many luxury brands. Clean and spacious.

Bus stop nearest to Orchard MRT with plenty of direct buses to varieties of locations including Dhoby Ghaut Shenton Way Harbourfront Raffles Blvd Jurong East Bukit Merah Promenade Marina Bay and Suntec City. Convenient alternative to MRT. Walking distance to Luxurious shopping restaurants hotels resorts residents service Apartments cinemas banking restaurants supermarkets entertainments and convention fasciitis in Orchard Road and Scotts Road including Shaw House Tang Plza Paragon Shopping Centre Ion Orchard Wisma Atria Wheelock Place Takashimaya and Mandarin Hotel. Friendly cosy comfy welcoming ambience for romantic intimates groups students expats locals visitors and families.
Easy access
My favorite place to visit on the Christmas Eve.

I have headed here in singapore to head to IMM mall. Metro ride was good. Im thrilled to see everyone using metro besides personal transport. Most of the people are very friendly giving directions when needed. Security team and staff are so helpful in showing the directions. I depended so much on them as I visited and took train for the first time. There was some confusion when taking the circle change in the station.

I love in the West. I've been using the JE MRst exchange since 1991. The ceiling plan hasn't changed at all since then.

Until recently, this roof wasn't washed much at all and even now it's hard to get rid ofnth grime and bird waste accumulating here. I understand that this is very difficult given the business of the interchange. But something has to be done.

This MRT Station is strategically located in Jurong East with two popular shopping malls in the area directly connected to it and with the IMM shopping mall and the Ng Teng Fong Community Hospital just a bit farther away from the aforementioned two malls. Therefore this MRT Station sees a heavy footfall. As I live in Hillview, I can easily get to Jurong East by taking bus number 184 followed by a one stop MRT ride from Clementi MRT Station to Jurong East MRT Station.

I cannot believe my eyes! Totally different from 20 years ago.

I have not been to Jurong East MRT station for 20 years. When I stepped out of the train nowadays, I was shocked. It has changed so much!

20 years ago, this was just a normal station. Now, it looks like a whole new modern city. It is very interesting and well-developed. Here is why I was so impressed:

If you have not been here in a long time, you must visit. It is amazing to see how much Singapore has built and improved this area. It is a true transformation!

Jurong East MRT Station is a very convenient interchange station connecting both the North-South Line and East-West Line. It is well organized and makes travelling across Singapore easier, especially for people working or living in the west area.

The station is directly connected to nearby shopping malls like JEM and Westgate, which is very useful for shopping and dining after travel. Clean environment and clear signboards make transfers smooth even during busy hours. Highly recommended interchange station in Singapore.

A beautiful, relaxing and vibrant place for walks, for taking a step back from the hectic pace.
Spending 3 hours walking and appreciating the fresh air, the manicured gardens, the lake and wonderful architecture, helped to destress and reset.
If you've not been, well worth some time out.

Definitely one of the nicest and must go to parks in Singapore if you are alone, couples, families group of friends wanting to escape from the concrete jungle of the city. Jurong used to be perceived as a very faraway venue to commute to but with so many new MRT train lines it’s so much easier and faster to reach. Do take note of opening hours. Free entry and easily accessible with many entrances by Chinese Garden MRT or Lakeside MRT and buses. Great for all activities, walking , strolling, jogging, cycling, picnics and there is even a skate park and dog run. What makes this park stand out from the rest is the sheer size, the large lake with canoeing; stunning replicas of historical Chinese and Japanese architecture and pagodas, bonsai garden and wildlife. And especially the stunning Lily Flower Pond at the Japanese Garden and the Lotus Flower Pond at the Chinese Garden. Definitely worth to make a trip down to enjoy this green oasis escapade rich in flora and fauna and water. It’s very tranquil and peaceful from Mondays to Saturdays. Ample toilets and drinking water dispenser and also a beverage shop selling nice cold beverages.

NParks really did a good job in revamping this park.
There's plenty of interesting plants and wildlife to spot.
Took me and my friends 3 hours to casually explore the entire area.
It's definitely a nice place to run in the mornings/evenings.

I visit Jurong Lake Gardens quite frequently since I am staying nearby. The walking tracks are well-maintained, and the view around the lake at anytime is just impressive. It’s a wonderful place to clear your mind and enjoy the beautiful scenery during the walks.

I highly recommend to visit this gardens, as there are a lot of attractions to see and they are all free! It’s a beautiful area to dive into culture and nature, just 40 min from Singapore with the bus. Very relaxing and a moment to take a pause from the overtourism and consumistic view of marina bay and the Singapore center.
Not as big as the botanic garden, but I’d say it’s better to view this than that.

New Revamped Jurong East Swimming Complex with Lazy and waterplay areas for the kids. Hv a good wave generation pool which operates every hour.A bit sad that the water slides has been replaced with a new children water play area for toddlers and young children. Not enough shaded canopies for users of the facility to keep their belongings the heat on hot sunny days. No food vending machines for snacks, only hv a drinks vending machine.
Fun time with kiddos. The toilets are great with large cubicles and compartments to hang your dry clothes. Definitely a comeback for my family.

Although I must say that I missed the huge slides that my generation grew up with. But it is a nice transitional change to me being a Dad now, bringing my little kiddos in what is now a kids play area.

Also the toilets have had a face-lift. Kids changing room is nice and clean. Adults changing room is equally good.

They had a baby pool, kids playground, kids splash area, lazy river and a wave pool. Wave pool had waves every hour lasting about 15mins each time. Lazy river is where we spent most of our time at, easy going and fun. Rates are affordable with activesg. Minus one star because I'd prefer having the slides.

Went with family on a Saturday. Place was busy but still have space for a good swim. Kids enjoyed the new water play area and wave pool (though wave was mild and very far apart). The lazy river is still there.

Jurong Point has really evolved into one of the largest and most comprehensive suburban malls in Singapore over the years. The mall keeps expanding and now offers an impressive variety of restaurants, cafés, retail shops, supermarkets, and leisure options all under one roof. There are also at least two sizeable food courts with plenty of local and international food choices, making it easy to find something for everyone

One of the biggest advantages is the excellent convenience - it has always been directly connected to both the MRT station and the bus interchange, making it extremely accessible rain or shine. Despite being a heartland mall, it feels vibrant and lively with many different sections to explore, from Japanese-themed food streets to family-friendly spaces and lifestyle shops

Jurong Point is one of the more convenient suburban malls in Singapore with a huge variety of shops, supermarkets, restaurants and daily necessities all under one roof. The mall is directly connected to Boon Lay MRT, so accessibility is definitely a big plus.

What I like most is the wide food selection ranging from local hawker-style eateries to proper restaurants and cafes. It’s also spacious enough for long walks without feeling too cramped on weekdays.

However, the mall can get extremely crowded during weekends and peak dining hours, especially around the food areas. Some sections also feel slightly dated compared to newer malls in Singapore, and navigating between the different wings can be a bit confusing initially.

Overall, still a very practical and convenient mall for shopping, dining and everyday errands in the west side of Singapore.

Didn’t expect tiramisu to hit this good, but here we are at Tirato Tiramisu location at B1 near boast.

Caved and tried two flavours — pistachio and yam — and honestly? No regrets. Just me wondering why I didn’t get more

Pistachio was rich, nutty, and somehow still light? Super creamy, but not the kind that makes you feel jelak. The subtle crunch inside? Chef’s kiss. Definitely feels a little luxe.

Yam caught me off guard — it’s soft, naturally sweet, and has that nostalgic vibe, like something you grew up eating but upgraded. Super comforting and lowkey addictive.

What made it even better:
✨ No pork, no lard, no alcohol — totally kid-friendly (my little cousin loved it)
✨ Freshly made daily — no preservatives, no colouring, nothing artificial
✨ So many flavours to choose from — I was stuck at the counter for 5 mins trying to pick. Spoilt for choice fr.

They’re only $6.80 each, which feels like a steal for the quality.
Very fun shopping at Jurong point. Lots of things to see for Chinese new year from ornaments to cookies in jars and gift boxes. Got god of fortune everywhere too. Pineapple tarts and more ~

Jurong point is huge! Discover their Japanese street, Mongkok street for restaurants, bakeries and bubble teas. Donki, Scarlett, Thai Supermarket for international selection of snacks and products. One whole day shopping, lunch, tea breaks and dinner to explore about. Definitely a must visit in SG

*Dapur 3A Power Kacang Pool @Ayer Rajah Food Centre*

It's been a while that I feel compelled to give a review but after returning for a second helping I knew I wanted to. I'm truly not only impressed by the delish Middle Eastern classics Dapur 3A Power Kacang Pool serves but that they really give deep meaning to a degustation delight to make both your happy mouth and stomach dance with joy. And mind you being of mixed heritage my palate is rather well exposed.

First off, there is no denying, like their namesake, their signature kacang pool is an absolute sublime delight and winner. The texture of the fava beans or foul medames base is beautifully textured. Not too pureed and with enough light ruffage. Knowing how those who love the soulful pleasurable dish would want either a little more or less they've got a double egg version and add-ons one can easily customise. Now their dish that bowls me over is their shabsuka. I've had and sampled plenty, and theirs is a wonderfully balanced one that everyone in our home who had a bite agree is sensational. It's sweet but from the chunky tomatoes used so it glides well. And the spices used elevates its nuanced flavour. And today with my choice of beef adding a rich flavour threading through its already lovely base it's a flavour bomb from the first taste.

Now the one thing that to me helps literally mop and absorb the delicious flavours has to be their bread. Expecting a lightly toasted French baguette or local loaf version I was not prepared but was pleasantly delighted by their softer version. I was so glad I ordered extra for it's such a clever and brilliant pairing to the dish selections they offer.

I'll make it simpler. It is hard not to grab something from here when we swing over to this rather nondescript heartland food centre, and so should you. Also, the kind smiling lady who made and served the food is just so sweet and friendly.

A good and spacious Food Centre that offers delicious food at a reasonable price.
For vegans and vegetarians, there’s a stall serving delicious local Chinese vegan food (V4 Vegetarian) ~ made without MSG, eggs, milk and alliums. It’s 100% vegan and Pay-as-you-wish (one can decide how much  to pay). I sincerely hope more people can come and support this stall, as it’s truly inspiring to see a business put so much heart, love and compassion into their dishes.
There are also stalls that serve good Teh Halia (Ginger Milk Tea) and Teh Pandan (Pandan Tea) at truly reasonable prices.

@Ayer Rajah Food Centre

If you’re craving authentic Indian-style Mee Siam, the Mee Siam Mama with rusuk at Ayer Rajah Food Centre is seriously one of the hidden gems in the west. The gravy is rich, spicy, tangy and full of depth — very different from the usual Malay-style mee siam. The bee hoon soaks up the flavorful kuah perfectly, while the sambal gives it an addictive kick without overpowering the dish.

What really steals the show is the rusuk (ribs). Tender, juicy and packed with flavor, it pairs amazingly well with the spicy gravy. Add the crispy tofu cubes, lime, boiled egg and fresh spring onions, and every spoonful becomes comfort food at its best.

This is proper old-school “Mee Siam Mama” done right — hearty, messy, spicy and deeply satisfying. You can tell this stall has perfected the recipe over the years. One of the few Indian stalls in Singapore still serving this unique version of Mee Siam, and definitely worth travelling for if you love authentic hawker food. Highly recommended for anyone who enjoys bold flavors and traditional Indian-Muslim hawker dishes.
horrible kunafa sold by shawarma man stall. ordered pistachio kunafa and it was very oily, little to no cheese, and a huge load of pistachio sauce.  pay $12 to get diabetes and oil

This food stall is great — the food is delicious, affordable, and generous in portion. It’s run by an elderly couple, and every time I come, they always serve a lot. Sometimes, they're even worried you might not have enough to eat, so they’ll give you an extra bowl of soup. Truly a stall run by a kind-hearted couple — highly recommended!

7 Feb 2026 2pm Unexpected detour that we met this halal gem. Delicious food n good vibes. Chicken salad is tender n seasoned well. Mash potato on point. Pasta with right amount of sauce n flavours....yummy....quench with lemongrass tea....refreshingly awesome! Staff is friendly n fast service. Friendly lady boss too. Oh btw we came via walk in n thank god there is a table for us yeay! Almost full house!
Just hope they change their system to order n pay at the same time upon order via QR code as business in 2026 hard to stay a float in Singapore. Would be sad to have this kind of local halal gem lost.
Recommended!
Sorry to missed out the good portion of chicken salad...my MIL already dive right in after the sight of the mouth watering chicken hehe....
They are at the entrance at Active SG Gym

I was very happy with my experience here. I was shocked to see so much reviews for this restaurant,but when the food arrived, I understood why the review was so high. The food was in a reasonable price, came in a very generous amount, and tasted great. I ordered the carbonara, tom yam pasta (extra spicy for the kick), grilled chicken and the burger. For dessert, I got the choco-lava cake and cookie milkshake. For people who are craving something sweet, I recommend trying the cookie milkshake. The place felt cozy and inviting. I truly recommend trying the food here!

The generous portions here really stood out to me. Even the House Salad came with a really huge grilled chicken thigh. Tried the Thai Green Curry Seafood Pasta which was packed with ingredients along with their Fried Chicken & Waffles and Thai crispy fish with Thai chilli sauce too.

The giant milkshakes were one of the highlights and the molten lava cake made a nice ending to the meal. Overall a nice casual spot to gather with friends plus it’s halal certified and opens till 5am daily too.

Decided to treat my friend for her birthday at a place which i believe both of us could enjoy. Heard of this place since 2022 i believe but never had a chance to come previously. When i step in, i could feel good vibes with a good choice of song list i could say, clean and cozy with nice decor. We've been greeted and attended by Azrul upon our arrival and  been ushered to out seat. While seating i found the atmosphere was very nice and calm/chill which very comforting. I find their menu is very interesting since they offered Thai and Western food (some even a fusion of both). After ordering our food, i approached their counter to ask about the cakes that they offered and been attended by Sheryln, since i wanted to surprise my friend and Sheryln guide the how to. Overall we were stuffed with good foods served by both Azrul and Sheryln which is quite friendly and professional. Its definitely a good choice on my side (self praised, lol).

First time here. Called to check if reservation is needed, straightaway reserved seats for my family. Wonderful!

The mains here are all flavourful & the portions are HUGE for 2 persons... See the pics for yourself. Come here for the chill vibes, definitely NO REGRETS!!!

I went up to the 6th floor on a Friday night to check out the observatory. For a small fee, you get to use a powerful telescope that gives you really clear views of the moon and stars. It is one of those "hidden" spots that not many people know about, and it makes for a very unique and affordable date night in the North.
There is a digital class here every Tuesdays and Thursdays 10am-1pm at level 2. But honesty this place is quite humid.. Only if there's air conditioning for the whole building
Visited the place yesterday for my community Sharing session and everyone was welcoming and friendly!

Some good stuffs:
CC has been repainted with some cool new designs.
A Subway store would also be opening soon.
Hall is big, air conditioned can book it for events. If no events you can book the hall to play badminton. (For a fee)
At the highest floor, the CC has an observatory on top (just pay a small entrance fee for it)
Recently renovated the Karaoke 🎤 room can go and try the new facilities.
Anytime fitness is there also at level 5.
Police station just beside it.

Good Interest Group eg. Boardgames

Bad stuffs:
Seats at level 1 no longer available.
No place to park cars.
Toilets quite dirty.

Great place for workout! There is so much greenery and space.

There is a big food court with many varieties of local delights to choose from.

888 Plaza was a genuine surprise for me. On the surface it looks like a quiet neighbourhood mall, but step inside and it quietly flexes. You have everything here. DIY shops, fast food, proper hawker fare, and those in-between stalls that you did not know you needed until you saw them.
It is the kind of place where you can buy household essentials, grab a quick kopi, settle a full meal, and still wander around without feeling rushed or overwhelmed. Nothing flashy, nothing pretentious, just practical variety done right.
What I liked most is how functional it is for daily life. Families, seniors, students, everyone seems to have a reason to be there. Food options are solid, prices are reasonable, and it feels very lived-in in the best possible way.
Not a destination mall, but absolutely a dependable one. Sometimes that is exactly what you want.

I have been coming here to eat for years at all times of the day including bright and early and late at night and it has never failed to please! There's a huge variety of different food to satisfy everybody's craving. The sitting area it's usually available but at worst a small weight and is kept clean by the staff

The management ALLOWS walkway to park bicycles all over the place. Every time I walk past always see such views…. What a NICE scenery…

Updated:
Pass by on 21 March 2025 11am
Same best scenery again…

Updated:
10.10.2025
Same scenario again…
Nice vibrant place with a wide variety of things which can be purchased here.24 hours NTUC supermarket, 24 hours coffeeshop, pharmacy,  etc. .Its like a one-stop neighbourhood place. 👍

I patronized woodlands plaza almost every day... I really want to say this lady is extremely rude to all the customers at this drink stall. I will never buy drinks from this stall when she's around instead I rather tabao food here and tabao drinks from the other side of the coffee shop with a few friendly and more professional staff members.

The system to book is so damn bad - it got stuck at the payment system. Called up the cc and the staff also laughed, saying that they are facing the same issue. Please get the website working properly! Supposedly able to get the slots I wanted but nope… :(

The location is very hard to get to if you're from other parts of Singapore, but if you're from the area, you'd probably say it's convenient given how close it is to Causeway Point.

We came here only for our Moderna booster jab, and rarely make our way here. I must say, I'm very impressed by the Raffles Medical team that facilitated the vaccination exercise. Queues were fast, people were nice, and we were done in a super quick time.

We had the 730pm appointment, took 3 minutes to get our queue number, jab, and sit at the waiting area. Another 15 minutes of instituted waiting time, and off you go, you're done. It's literally like a solid factory line, which is a little stressful but efficient.

Thanks team! I'm rarely at Woodlands but you made my evening, and my rare road trip up North, a little better.

Community club with comprehensive fasciitis and amenities. Famous for great CC Indian Classical Dance Group. Walking distance to Woodlands MRT station and bus interchange. Plenty of shopping banking supermarkets local Int'l delights groceries  sundries postage services cinemas library and entertainments. Beautiful views of greenery. Free wifi. Friendly fast attentive cosy comfy welcoming ambience for romantic intimates groups students tourists Expats locals visitors and families.
This is amazing experience just 2mins process to get singpass account and they are very polite to communicate with me
Great food available here students and working class take note! Awesome food  Available! Will definitely come back again worth the trip!

This MRT Station sees a tremendous amount of passengers as it's the closest MRT Station to the Causeway linking Singapore and Malaysia via Johor Bahru. It's also just next to the very popular and very crowded Causeway Point shopping mall that sees a very heavy footfall. One can only imagine how much busier and crowded it will get the RTS or rail system linking Singapore and Johor Bahru opens in the very near future.

This is the station I come to whenever I want to go to Marine Parade from CCK.
It's takes a bit longer but I love and enjoy the journey. And there is always seat. The walk from Woodland to the TEL stations is a nice and enjoyable walk. It's spotless clean. Good job to all the people that keeps it in top condition 👏 👍

Woodlands MRT Station, located in Singapore’s northern region, is a major transport hub that became even more accessible with the opening of the Thomson-East Coast Line. Visiting the station shortly after the line’s launch offered a fresh and modern experience, showcasing Singapore’s commitment to enhancing connectivity and convenience.

The station’s expanded facilities, sleek design, and improved accessibility impressed me. The integration of the Thomson-East Coast Line with the North-South Line has significantly reduced travel times and made commuting to and from Woodlands much easier. The signage and wayfinding systems were intuitive, reflecting the high standards of Singapore’s MRT network.

Beyond its functional aspects, the station connects seamlessly to nearby shopping malls, residential areas, and the Woodlands Waterfront, providing plenty of options for dining, shopping, and leisure. The new Thomson-East Coast Line platforms stood out for their modern architecture and spacious layout, making the commute comfortable even during peak hours.

Visiting Woodlands MRT Station during the early days of the Thomson-East Coast Line’s operation was an exciting experience, offering a glimpse into Singapore’s ever-evolving public transport landscape. A must-visit for transport enthusiasts and a welcome improvement for commuters in the area.
Nicely designed station link to NSL .

I really love woodlands MRT and how convenient it is for me to use the transport to travel but I am putting this review 1 star because you guys need to fix the aircon for the shops inside the MRT (north south line)!!! The workers are sweating like crazy. Please fix the aircon, it’s so hot.

It’s not just that air con that is down in one shop, it’s multiple shops. Miniso, Popeyes, Rotikek, POSB and etc

Please fix the aircon!! I can literally feel the heat of the shop whenever I walk pass them. Also the workers have to install multiple fans for themselves and customers. If ticket service and passenger service have aircon, surely the shops can have AC too right?😢 I have high hopes for SMET and believe in you guys so please do something about it

It was my first time in th Woodlands MRT station straight from the Woodlands North Singapore immigration. The station is very accessible by the bus coming from the Woodlands North and easy to get to. I also love that there's Jollibee there 😍.

Woodlands MRT Station 🚉 always have a lot of passengers but it is always orderly, clean and neat.

Love ❤️ that it has many eating establishments so provide a lot of convenience to passengers who just got out of the station and can easily grab dinner on their way home 🏠.

Woodlands MRT has become a bigger hub now that it connects to TEL Mrt Line that goes into the ciry and hopefully not too far in the future, to the Changi airport.

It's also a big bus interchange, with the aircon indoor one, and the old bus interchange now serves as ‘temporary’ bus interchange although it has been temporary for years.

The MRT is also connected to a heartland mall Causeway Point, which connects to Woodlands Civic center and Wood Square. Daily necessities, supermarket, F&B, fashion, electronic, book store, library, coffee, cinema. It practically has almost everything necessary.

There are several F&B options in the MRT station itself, but to be honest, most of them are not good options, but they are still okay for quick bite.

During certain timing of the year a pasar malam would be opened on the field next to the Causeway Point.

Since it's a hub, except this place to be very busy, full of people and traffic. The flow can be confusing, but there are many directional signs available. The food option for early morning crowd is very limited though, even on the weekend, but that is normal everywhere.

Clean, spacious and trains run regularly. Only issue is a common one across stations across the world: clear signage. Sometimes too small, contradictory or unclear. It's a little confusing for any new visitors.

Really like how spacious the interior is, and how the red and brown line links underground to its multiple exit points.

Ventilation is good and facilities are new. Tel line takes an average of 5~7 mins to gome for every interval. But between stops is really fast. I take 30 mins to reach Orchard via TEL line, whereas red takes me 45 mins or so.

Down side is that TEL line closes quite early.

Marsiling Park is a beautiful and well-rounded park that offers something for everyone. The scenic lake creates a calm and relaxing atmosphere, while the Chinese pavilion shelters add a lovely traditional touch. There are also badminton courts, stages, exercise instruments, and even a gym pod, so it’s a great place for both leisure and fitness. Whether you want to unwind, exercise, or spend time with family and friends, Marsiling Park is definitely worth a visit, especially if you are trying to run 1.8 km!

Located very near where my Queen stays, we walked here for our morning walk as we decided to start a new healthier routine and hoping it will turn into a habit and lifestyle. All starting on 21st September 2025 10am :) it’s a soothing sunny morning, we see Malay Pakcik practicing Silat, very similar to Qi Gong, making use of sudden burst energy, spotted families playing swings, boys at the playground, beloved maids having picnics, saw pavilions, found a Gym Pod and many more. We tried out their fitness corners, and played at their interactive playground spots. Spotted an observatory here too, but didn’t climb up cos we wanted to save some leg power for the walk to breakfast. Overall, a very funny park, hope to visit more!

A Walk That Changed My View: Marsiling Park

I used to think of parks in Singapore as predictable—neatly landscaped, well-paved, and mostly meant for joggers or families with children. That was until I took a quiet walk one weekend morning through Marsiling Park.

Tucked away in the north, Marsiling Park doesn’t have the same fame as East Coast or Botanic Gardens, but perhaps that’s what makes it so special. As I wandered along its shaded paths, past the tranquil pond and over the wooden boardwalks, something shifted in me. The calm rustle of leaves, the subtle ripples in the water, the calls of kingfishers and mynahs—it all felt surprisingly immersive. The park wasn’t just a recreational space; it was a gentle, breathing reminder that nature still has its place in our urban rhythm.

What struck me most was how seamlessly Marsiling Park blended both serenity and surprise. Every turn offered a different mood: a sculpture quietly nestled among trees, a small bridge leading to reflections of sky and trees mirrored in the pond. It invited me not to rush, but to slow down and observe.

That walk transformed my perception of Singapore’s green spaces. It reminded me that our parks aren’t just backdrops for activity—they are living sanctuaries, places to reflect, recharge, and reconnect. Since then, I’ve made it a personal weekend ritual to explore different parks and trails across the island. It’s not about the steps or the distance anymore—it’s about feeling grounded, soaking in the textures of nature, and remembering that peace can often be found just a short walk away.

Walk at the park and enjoyed the moments of feeling grounded over the weekends before you kickstart another busy work week ahead! 🫶🏻💪

Went here for a morning walk, it was quiet and nice.
There is a pond in the middle, small outdoor amphitheater and quite a number of benches around.

Admission: Free!

Marsiling Park in Singapore is a peaceful green space ideal for outdoor activities such as walking and cycling. It features lush greenery, a pond, and playgrounds, making it perfect for families and nature lovers. The park offers upgraded facilities, including fitness stations and seating areas, creating a relaxing atmosphere for visitors to enjoy. Overall, it's a great destination for leisure and exercise in an urban setting.

Visited with my wife today and we were both thoroughly impressed. By far the biggest and nicest mall in JB that I’ve seen! The atmosphere is modern and upscale, making it feel more like a premium destination than just a shopping center.

There is a huge variety of international and luxury brands (SOGO, Uniqlo, MUJI) and the dining options are endless—from casual bites to high-quality restaurants. The layout is spacious and clean, which makes walking around very comfortable even when it's busy. If you only have time for one mall in JB, this is definitely the one to visit. Highly recommended!

​If you are looking for the ultimate shopping and dining destination in JB, The Mall, Mid Valley Southkey is hands-down the place to be. It feels like a slice of Kuala Lumpur’s retail luxury brought right to the South.

​Why It’s a Must-Visit:
-​Retail Therapy Galore: The mall is massive and incredibly well-curated. You’ll find everything from the high-end SOGO Department Store to flagship-sized favorites like Uniqlo, ZARA, and Cotton On. Whether you’re hunting for luxury goods or street style, you’re covered.

-​The Food Scene is Elite: This is a literal paradise for foodies. You can grab a quick fix at FUIYOH! It’s Uncle Roger for that signature fried rice, indulge in a brunch at The Founders Cafe, or brave the legendary queues for the famous egg tarts and Nasi Lemak at Oriental Kopi.

-​Modern & Spacious: Unlike some older malls, the layout here is bright, wide, and very easy to navigate. The air conditioning is a dream on a hot day, and the overall vibe is chic and upscale.

-​Great for Families: With the GSC Cinema, Kiddytopia, and an indoor theme park, there’s plenty to keep the kids entertained while you shop. Plus, the Village Grocer is perfect for picking up high-quality international snacks before heading home.

Mid Valley Southkey is one of the best shopping destinations in Johor Bahru. The mall is huge, modern, and very clean, with a great mix of international and local brands. There’s something for everyone — from fashion and electronics to cafés, restaurants, and entertainment options. The layout is spacious and easy to navigate, and parking is well-organized. I also liked that there are plenty of food choices, from casual bites to premium dining. A perfect spot to spend the day shopping, dining, or just relaxing.

🌿Yishun Neighbourhood Park Is located up the hillock at the junction of Yishun Ave 2 and Yishun Ave 7.
🌳The Park is a former rubber plantation.

🛝The Park features a Children Playground, a multi-generation Fitness Corner, and an Allotment Garden which were all located at the hilltop.
👣A slightly sloped Foot Reflexology Path lies on the slope of the hillock near the summit.
🏋️There is also a Gym Pod just across the Fitness Corner.
🌹The entire Park is clean and peaceful with plenty of greenery and beautiful flowering plants.
🧘Residents living nearby visit the park in the morning for their morning exercises.
🍀Yishun Neighbourhood Park is among the several town parks around Yishun area.
♦️Fun note:
Yishun is the chinese hanyu pinyin for Née Soon, derived from Lim Née Soon, a prominent businessman and philanthropist, who had large area of his rubber and pineapple plantations located here in Yishun area.

Khatib MRT is one of the busiest MRT stations in Singapore, offering excellent connectivity and accessibility. Located near Yishun MRT and Yio Chu Kang MRT, it serves as a key transit point for both daily commuters and visitors. Compared to other MRT stations, Khatib is one of the older yet well-developed stations, making it a significant landmark in the area.

One of the unique aspects of Khatib MRT is its connection to the HDB residential area. To reach the HDB section, commuters need to cross a passenger flyover, which provides a clear view of the surrounding area, including a frequently used lift.

Additionally, Khatib MRT is a major access point to Singapore Zoo, with a dedicated shuttle service running between the station and the zoo. This makes the station particularly crowded, especially on weekends and holidays, as families and tourists use it to reach the zoo conveniently.

Within the same building as Khatib MRT, there is a Giant supermarket, offering easy access to groceries and daily essentials—an added convenience for residents and travelers. The area also features several food shops, serving delicious local cuisine, making it a great spot to grab a meal.

Right in front of the station, there is a main bus stop with direct routes to Changi Airport and Tampines City, using the expressway for a fast and smooth journey. The same stop also provides highway bus services, making it easy to reach key locations like IKEA, the airport, and Tampines efficiently.

Overall, Khatib MRT is a well-connected and strategically located station, offering easy access to essential services, transport links, and recreational destinations. Whether you’re commuting, shopping, or heading to the zoo, this station provides convenience at every step.
Surrounded by many basketball courts, open Multi purpose court. Many coffeeshops nearby. Giant at MRT station. Mac donald nearby.

Yishun MRT Station, located on Singapore’s North-South Line (Red Line), is one of the most convenient and well-connected MRT stations in the northern region. Whether you’re commuting daily or just visiting the area, Yishun MRT offers excellent accessibility and a smooth travel experience.

One of the standout features of Yishun MRT is its strategic location. Just next to the station is the large and popular Northpoint City Mall, which makes this area a vibrant hub of shopping, dining, and services. The mall has a wide variety of retail outlets, supermarkets, food courts, restaurants, electronics shops, fashion stores, and essential services like banks, clinics, and salons—all within walking distance from the station.

Right outside the MRT station is the Yishun Bus Interchange, which connects to many bus routes across Singapore. This makes it extremely convenient for commuters to transfer between MRT and bus services with ease. Whether you’re heading to other parts of the North, or travelling toward the city, Yishun serves as a great starting point or transfer hub.

The station itself is clean, well-maintained, and safe, with helpful signage and barrier-free access for elderly and wheelchair users. During peak hours, it gets quite busy, but the crowd is managed well.

In terms of convenience, everything you need is nearby—groceries, food, retail shopping, public transport, and even community facilities. It’s an ideal location for residents and visitors alike.

Overall, Yishun MRT Station is not just a transport node—it’s a central hub for lifestyle and daily needs in the northern part of Singapore. If you’re looking for easy commuting with all amenities nearby, this is one of the best-connected and most practical MRT stations in the city.

Yishun MRT Station in Singapore is a busy yet well-connected transport hub on the North-South Line that links seamlessly with the Yishun Bus Interchange and Northpoint City mall, offering commuters easy access to shopping, residential, and community areas, but despite its convenience, accessibility, and integration, it faces recurring challenges such as heavy crowding during peak hours, occasional escalator breakdowns, cleanliness issues around bicycle parking zones, and periodic service disruptions due to ageing infrastructure—making it both a vital and sometimes frustrating centerpiece of daily life in northern Singapore.

Yishun MRT is a simple station that connects North Yishun residents to the rest of the country via the Mass Rapid Transit (MRT). There's nothing special about the station itself. However it is linked to the main mall in Yishun (North point City) as well as the Yishun Bus Inter-change.
The common area charging point for commuters was great. Except that max only 2 users can charge at any one time. Perhaps a few more charging stations can be provided by SMRT.

I had a truly wonderful time at Springleaf Park! 🏞️ The atmosphere was so refreshing, and it was the perfect spot for a delightful outing. The serene surroundings and beautiful greenery made for such a pleasant experience. I'm definitely looking forward to my next visit! 😊🌳✨
Lovely park connector — peaceful, clean and great for a relaxing walk or cycling. Lots of greenery and well-maintained paths. A nice escape from the busy city!

🍀Springleaf Park Connector connect Upper Thomson Road to Lentor Avenue.
🍀The entire Park Connector runs side by side to Sungei Seletar.
🚦Née Soon Road cuts through the Springleaf Park Connector, so you need to cross the road to continue on.
🍀As you stroll through this lush Park Connector, you can hear the constant bird calls, testimony to its rich biodiversity.
🐾It's a jogging track for the nearby residents.
Nice short PCN connecting Lentor Avenue to Upper Thomson Road. Good for a short morning walk before breakfast. Within 5 minutes walk from Springleaf MRT Station.

Since the opening of the Springleaf PCN between 2014 to 2015 , it has been one of my cycling and walking routes to cross over Lentor Avenue to Upper Thomson Road & vice versa. For bird watchers, it's one of the spots in SG. One thing for sure, I will never failed to see the water lilies in the Seletar River.

result.name: Runningkids Yishun Student Care & Learning Hub

reviews.text: Very good student care. Especially they promote Chinese learning and reading, my girl really enjoys RK programs. Now she takes higher Chinese in P3 and she can read Chinese books independently. RK also conduct English lessons by MoE teachers. I never need to worry about her academic performance, sometimes she brings surprise tome. RK teachers are very supportive and give me peace of mind. Highly recommend!

Ms. Zhou and Mr. Ho are truly professional and passionate about teaching. They put in great effort to highlight the most important topics, concepts and applications, ensuring students build a strong foundation. The classes are interactive and engaging, which makes learning enjoyable and effective. My child is always motivated to improve and has shown steady progress under the teachers’ guidance. Highly recommended for both Chinese and Science tuition!

From Primary 1 through now his PSLE year, Running Kids has been more than just student care - it's been a second family for our son. The warm, nurturing environment and dedicated teachers created the perfect place for him to grow both academically and personally.

As an English-speaking household, we were especially amazed by Zhou Lao Shi's remarkable impact. Through her patient and engaging Chinese lessons (P5-P6 currently), she transformed what was once a challenging subject into his current passion! Her teaching brought incredible improvement, from initial struggles to now confidently conversing in Mandarin at home and genuinely enjoying the language.

A special 谢谢 to Zhou Lao Shi for making learning so joyful and effective.

Runningkids Student Care has taken great care of my daughter for the past 2 years. She enjoys the environment, and has made good friends. Her English has improved tremendously in her time there as a result of the in-house tuition provided. They have also been extremely accommodating and responsive with ad-hoc requests across the years and communication with the staff has always been positive. 10/10 would recommend.

My son joined P6 Chinese and Science tuition lessons. My son enjoyed the lessons very much. Teachers are very caring and meticulous in teaching. Materials are great. Am happy with my son’s significant improvement in Chinese and Science grades in prelim PSLE. Highly recommended.

result.name: PlayFACTO School @ Yishun | Student Care Yishun

reviews.text:
My kid joined PlayFACTO Yishun earlier this year and we’re really happy we did. There were a few small hiccups at the start (normal with any new place), but the team sorted them out fast and actually listened to our feedback — not just lip service.

Teachers are warm but firm when they need to be, which is honestly what kids this age need. My child looks forward to going, homework gets done properly, and we always know what’s going on. Big weight off our shoulders as working parents.

Highly recommend 👍
Came for a centre tour today, Playfacto A posh hub is spacious, clean environment. The admin team and teachers are friendly and answered my queries patiently.

Very comfortable environment and friendly teachers. I have my 3rd child with them. All been well taken care off. Now they are shifting to a bigger place at APosh Bizhub, more spacious and nearer to school.

I had a very unpleasant experience with PlayFacto at Yishun (A'Posh Bizhub) today.

The students there seemed to have very little awareness of their surroundings and were extremely noisy in the enclosed lobby area of A'Posh Bizhub. While I understand that this is a student care centre and not a formal school, basic discipline and awareness should still be taught. The teachers should be guiding the students on how to behave appropriately in shared spaces.

One incident in particular was quite concerning. I entered the lift and waited about 3–5 seconds before closing the door. During that time, nobody entered, nor did the teachers said anything. But, the moment the door's about to close, a few kids suddenly dashed towards the lift. If I had not quickly held the door open, one of them could easily have been hurt.

After I held the door, a teacher wearing a dark blue dress guided a few children into the lift. One of the kids even joked, “OMG you almost died,” which clearly shows how undisciplined these childrens are and how little the teachers here cares. Afterwards, the teacher did not address the behaviour at all. Instead, she simply walked in, gave me a hostile stare, and said “shhh” to the children. There was no apology, no thank you for holding the door, and above all else, no attempt to correct the child’s behaviour despite the obvious safety risk.

This kind of response felt extremely irresponsible and unprofessional. If this is the standard of supervision and behaviour being tolerated, I would not feel confident recommending this centre to anyone.

Visited the open house at Playfacto@APosh Bizhub. The centre was spacious, and the staff were friendly and welcoming. Was impressed by the programme offered by the centre and also the facilities available.

Boarded bus 811 at blk 309 and had to alight for a short while outside of northpoint to give way to alighting passengers and was met with a rude condescending tone from the bus driver as if i didnt tap my card earlier when i boarded the bus , honestly i would have let this go but the way he told me off is as if i did not pay up for the ride please check your cctv if needed. Not the first time this has happen probably won’t be the last.
Yishun Bus Interchange, very good for travelers , local as well as foreigner, cool ambiance with retail stores at the side , staff friendly helpful attentive☺️🙏

The bus interchange is clean & easily accessible from North point.
